gmw_00000659.name: Mrk 817
gmw_00000659.overview: Mrk 817 is a nearby Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1012.0592, arXiv:2312.06487] hosting an active galactic nucleus powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2310.01497, arXiv:2312.06487]. The system features a complex emission-line region [arXiv:astro-ph/0607253], an exponential bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0407383], and a clumpy disk wind that drives variable X-ray and ultraviolet obscuration [arXiv:2302.12896].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Markarian 817 as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy with variable intrinsic Ly absorption, limited ionized absorption signatures, and stronger long-term X-ray variability than UV variability.

quotes.quote: We present an investigation of the ultraviolet and X-ray spectra of the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy Markarian 817. The ultraviolet analysis includes two recent observations taken with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph in August and December 2009, as well as archival spectra from the International Ultraviolet Explorer and the Hubble Space Telescope . Twelve Ly absorption features are detected in the 1997 GHRS and 2009 COS spectra -- of these, four are associated with high-velocity clouds in the interstellar medium, four are at low-significance, and the remaining four are intrinsic features, which vary between the GHRS and COS observations. The strongest intrinsic absorber in the 1997 spectrum has a systemic velocity of 4250 . The corresponding feature in the COS data is five times weaker than the GHRS absorber.

The factor of five change in the intrinsic Ly absorber is most likely due to bulk motions in the absorber, since there is no drastic change in the UV luminosity of the source from the GHRS to the COS observations. In a study of variability of Mrk 817, we find that the X-ray luminosity varies by a factor of 40 over 20 years, while the UV continuum/emission lines vary by at most a factor of 2.3 over 30 years. The variability of the X-ray luminosity is strongly correlated with the X-ray power-law index, but no correlation is found with the simultaneous optical/UV photometry.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 817 as a Sy 1.2 AGN with equatorial-scattering signatures, compares 2014 and 2021 polarization behavior, and reports eight monitoring epochs with no significant continuum or broad-line variability.

quotes.quote: Mrk 817 Mrk 817 ( z=0.031 , RA 14 36 22.1 +58 47 39.4 J2000) is a Sy 1.2 AGN, where the equatorial scattering was discovered in . The data published in that work is presented in Figure filt509_817 , right, where the transmission curves of the two filters 100 width oriented to ``red'' and ``blue'' broad line wings and selected for monitoring are also shown. A broader (FWHM = 250 ) filter was chosen for continuum polarimetry. The spectropolarimetric data demonstrate small changes in the polarization degree P and a violent switch of the polarization angle along the line profile.

Note here that in Figure filt509_817 for the Mrk 817 data, there are visible differences in the polarization parameters between the observations of 2014 and 2021, especially in Sy685 band. In this case, it is important to obtain newer spectropolarimetric data in order to confirm whether such a difference is the result of the influence of external factors (e.g., the variability of atmospheric B-band 6860--6917 ) or internal changes in the spectrum of Mrk 817 in polarized light. During the Mrk 817 monitoring, 8 epochs of observations were obtained using the MAGIC device in the period from December 2020 to August 2021.
Unfortunately, during the monitoring period, Mrk 817 did not show significant variability either in the continuum or in the broad line.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 817 STORM 2 light curves with MEM echo models, linking changing BLR and C 4 responses to UV/X-ray obscuring outflows and comparing against unobscured archival spectra.

quotes.quote: Inspired by the anomalous emission-line responses exhibited by NGC 5548 during STORM 1, we set out to analyze the light curves for Mrk 817 in STORM 2 in a model-independent way. We used the maximum entropy method (MEM) implemented in a code called MEME cho for estimating time delays in reverberation mapping of AGN Horne1991, Horne1994 to obtain a one-dimensional linearized echo model Horne2021 . Our model uses the 1180 \ continuum light curve, C(t) , as the driver, assuming it is a proxy for the ionizing continuum.

A significant element affecting the BLR response in Mrk 817 during STORM2 is the presence of outflowing gas that obscures the X-ray and ionizing continuum (Paper 1). To establish an unobscured baseline, we examine archival spectra of when it was observed in 2009 using the HST COS instrument Winter2011 . During these archival observations, the UV spectrum showed no broad absorption troughs (See Figure 2 of Paper 1 for a comparison between the 2009 archival spectra and the AGN STORM 2 campaign). We use the archival spectra to measure the continuum and C 4 flux, adopting the continuum windows 1493 1511 and 1736 1741.5 and emission line integration limit 1590 1638 as in Paper 2. We find that the unobscured C 4 flux response was significantly higher in 2009 (see the gray star symbols in Figure fig:lc_overlap ).

sources.summary: The paper maps UV/optical accretion-disk temperature fluctuations in the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817, finding slow coherent radial structures, long timescales, and limits on BLR contamination.

quotes.quote: We fit the UV/optical lightcurves of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817 to produce maps of the accretion disk temperature fluctuations T resolved in time and radius. The T maps are dominated by coherent radial structures that move slowly ( v ) inwards and outwards, which conflicts with the idea that disk variability is driven only by reverberation. Instead, these slow-moving temperature fluctuations are likely due to variability intrinsic to the disk. We test how modifying the input lightcurves by smoothing and subtracting them changes the resulting T maps and find that most of the temperature fluctuations exist over relatively long timescales ( 100s of days). We show how detrending AGN lightcurves can be used to separate the flux variations driven by the slow-moving temperature fluctuations from those driven by reverberation.

We analyze the AGN STORM 2 lightcurves of Mrk 817 to produce maps of the temperature fluctuations on the disk. In Section sec:results , we find that the temperature fluctuations are dominated by coherent radial structures that move slowly ( v ) inwards and outwards in the disk. These are in strong conflict with the idea that a central lamppost is the only source of the variability in the disk through reverberation, where fluctuations would only appear as structures moving outward at roughly the speed of light.

sources.summary: The paper treats Markarian 817 as a nearby Seyfert-1.2 AGN with unusual X-ray variability, a measured black-hole mass, renewed 2022 low-state observations showing stronger ionized obscuration, and wind feedback likely impacting its host galaxy.

quotes.quote: Markarian 817 is a nearby, z=0.03145 Seyfert-1.2 AGN (Strauss Huchra 1988; Koss et al. \ 2017; also see Miller et al. \ 2021). When it was studied within the CfA Seyfert Sample (Huchra Burg 1992), its X-ray flux was a factor of 40 lower than when it was observed with XMM-Newton in 2009, though its UV flux was broadly comparable (Winter et al. \ 2011). This behavior is unusual among Seyfert-1 AGN, though it is qualitatively consistent with some aspects of the transient ``obscurers'' seen in, e.g., NGC 5548 and NGC 3783 (Kaastra et al. \ 2014, Mehdipour et al. \ 2017). The mass of the black hole in Mrk 817, M = 8.12^ +0.73 _ 0.67 10^ 7 M_ , has recently been refined through continued optical BLR reverberation mapping (Lu et al. \ 2021).

Figure 4 in Miller et al. \ (2021) shows that wind obscuration episodes are indeed marked by rises in X-ray hardness in Swift/XRT monitoring of Mrk 817. Continued Swift monitoring of Mrk 817 in 2022 revealed that the source was an order of magnitude fainter than even in the intensive campaign in 2017--2018 (Zak et al. \ 2021). On this basis, we obtained additional observations with NuSTAR, XMM-Newton, and Swift (Miller et al. \ 2022), finding that the ionized obscuration had increased by an order of magnitude over any prior measurements.

Future work may reveal even lower filling factors that do not satisfy the 5 threshold, but all of the current estimates, using multiple techniques, point to a level of wind feedback in Mrk 817 that exceeds the threshold for significant impact on its host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Mrk 817 in X-ray and UV/optical bands, finding softer-when-brighter historical behavior and short-timescale lags broadly consistent with standard-disk reverberation.

quotes.quote: Previous X-ray variability studies of Mrk 817 have shown a softer-when-brighter behavior winter11,morales19 . To demonstrate how this changes the hardness ratio, we used xspec arnaud96 to calculate the expected count rates and hardness for power-law spectra with power-law indices ranging from = 2.2 to 1.6 (in steps of 0.2), assuming Galactic absorption of N_ H = 1.5 10^ 20  cm ^ 2 dickey90 and varying the power-law normalization to roughly match the observed trend on the HID (purple triangles in Fig.  fig:hid ). Note that a change in normalization simply shifts the points up and down, while a change in power-law index alters the hardness ratio.

We use UVW2 as the reference band, and assume M = 3.85 10^7  M _ and m _ E = 0.2 for Mrk 817, along with assuming an accretion efficient of = 0.1 and a local ratio of external to internal heating of = 1 . This gives = 0.31 days. Our best-fitting ^ 4/3 relations to the detrended lags range from = 0.23 0.04 ( Javelin ) to = 0.53 0.13 (ICCF), bracketing the expected disk size. Thus, the lags on short timescales are broadly consistent with reverberation from a standard disk.

sources.summary: The paper applies the method to Mrk 817, modeling detrended light curves and finding a broad, uncertain transfer-function peak near 17 days with a 16.7-day mean lag.

quotes.quote: Mrk 817 Mrk 817 was monitored for as long as five years by , but only during the intervening three years were H time lags detected. We use the first-year section of the light curves with the H time lags detected (HJD 24,449,000 24,449,211).

It is apparent from a simple visual inspection that the light curves of the continuum and H line show different long-term trends, with that of the continuum fluxes decreasing more steeply. We therefore include the detrending procedure as described in Section 2.2, in which both the trends of both the continuum and the H line are modeled by a linear polynomial.
The transfer function peaks at around 17 days and bears large uncertainties due to poor data sampling. The resulting mean time lag also has a fairly large uncertainty _ m =16.7 8.3 days.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the nearby Seyfert Mrk 817, finding strong historical X-ray brightening, measured X-ray/UV variability, and no significant X-ray--UV correlation.

quotes.quote: We report the results of long-term simultaneous X-ray and UV monitoring of the nearby ( z=0.03145 ) Seyfert 1.5 galaxy Mrk 817 using the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory XRT and UVOT. Prior work has revealed that the X-ray flux from Mrk 817 has increased by a factor of 40 over the last 40 years, whereas the UV emission has changed by a factor of 2.3. The X-ray emission of Mrk 817 now compares to some of the brightest Seyferts, but it has been poorly studied in comparison.

We find that the X-ray (0.3-10.0 keV) and the UVM2 (roughly 2000--2500 ) fluxes have fractional variability amplitudes of 0.35 and 0.18 , respectively, over the entire monitoring period (2017 Jan. 2 to 2018 Apr. 20). A cross-correlation analysis is performed on the X-ray (0.3-10.0 keV) and UVM2 light curves over the entire monitoring period, a period of less frequent monitoring (2017 Jan. 2 to 2017 Dec. 11), and a period of more frequent monitoring (2018 Jan. 12 to 2018 Apr. 20). The analysis reveals no significant correlation between the two at any given lag for all monitoring periods. Especially given that reverberation studies have found significant lags between optical/UV continuum bands and broad optical lines in Mrk 817, the lack of a significant X-ray--UV correlation may point to additional complexities in the inner or intermediate disk.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 817 with NuSTAR and Swift, linking decoupled UV and X-ray behavior to shielding absorption while finding a standard compact corona and inner disk.

quotes.quote: Over a period of 30 years, however, the X-ray (coronal) flux of Mrk 817 increased by a factor of 40 while its UV (disk) flux remained relatively steady. Recent high-cadence monitoring finds that the X-ray and UV continua in Mrk 817 are also decoupled on time scales of weeks and months. These findings could require mechanical beaming of the innermost accretion flow, and/or an absorber that shields the disk and/or broad line region (BLR) from the X-ray corona.

We have analyzed the first deep X-ray observation of Mrk 817 made with a modern X-ray telescope. The high sensitivity achieved with NuSTAR, in combination with the extended pass band afforded by Swift, reveals that the innermost accretion flow is consistent with a standard compact hard X-ray corona and flat inner accretion disk. This is indicated by the fact that excellent fits to the broadband spectrum are achieved using relativistic disk reflection.

sources.summary: The paper reports year-long AGN STORM 2 monitoring of Seyfert 1 Mrk 817, finding a faint X-ray state, variable obscurer column density and continuum brightness, a flare, and UV/X-ray absorption consistent with a clumpy disk wind.

quotes.quote: The AGN STORM 2 collaboration targeted the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817 for a year-long multiwavelength, coordinated reverberation mapping campaign including HST, Swift, XMM-Newton, NICER, and ground-based observatories. Early observations with NICER and XMM revealed an X-ray state ten times fainter than historical observations, consistent with the presence of a new dust-free, ionized obscurer. The following analysis of NICER spectra attributes variability in the observed X-ray flux to changes in both the column density of the obscurer by at least one order of magnitude ( N_ H ranges from 2.85 +0.48 0.33 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 to 25.6 +3.0 3.5 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) and the intrinsic continuum brightness (the unobscured flux ranges from 10^ 11.8 to 10^ 10.5 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ). While the X-ray flux generally remains in a faint state, there is one large flare during which Mrk 817 returns to its historical mean flux. The obscuring gas is still present at lower column density during the flare but it also becomes highly ionized, increasing its transparency.

Correlation between the column density of the X-ray obscurer and the strength of UV broad absorption lines suggests that the X-ray and UV continua are both affected by the same obscuration, consistent with a clumpy disk wind launched from the inner broad line region.

sources.summary: The paper reports AGN STORM 2 monitoring of Mrk 817, finding unexpected X-ray and UV obscuration, reverberation lags, and a temporary decoupling of UV continuum and emission-line variability.

quotes.quote: We present the first results from the ongoing, intensive, multi-wavelength monitoring program of the luminous Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817. While this AGN was, in part, selected for its historically unobscured nature, we discovered that the X-ray spectrum is highly absorbed, and there are new blueshifted, broad and narrow UV absorption lines, which suggest that a dust-free, ionized obscurer located at the inner broad line region partially covers the central source. Despite the obscuration, we measure UV and optical continuum reverberation lags consistent with a centrally illuminated Shakura-Sunyaev thin accretion disk, and measure reverberation lags associated with the optical broad line region, as expected. However, in the first 55 days of the campaign, when the obscuration was becoming most extreme, we observe a de-coupling of the UV continuum and the UV broad emission line variability. The correlation recovers in the next 42 days of the campaign, as Mrk 817 enters a less obscured state.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 817, identified as a z=0.031455 Seyfert 1 galaxy behind HVC Complex C, as a high-S/N HST/COS background source for foreground absorption and depletion measurements.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Galactic coordinates of Mrk 817 ( l =100.3 , b =53.5 )

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we present an extremely high S/N HST/COS spectrum of Mrk 817 , an AGN lying behind Complex C, and use a UV depletion analysis to show that Complex C does contain dust, with 0.3--0.5 dex of depletion seen in the refractory elements Si, Al, and Fe.

Mrk 817 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy at z_ em =0.031455 that by good fortune lies directly behind HVC Complex C. This alignment allows a precision analysis of the cloud's chemical composition. Mrk 817 was observed with the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph COS; green2012 onboard HST 165 times in a 15-month period beginning 2020 November 24 under the reverberation mapping program AGN STORM 2 (HST Program ID 16196). Each visit consisted of four 60s exposures with G130M/1222 (one at each FP-POS position), exposures of 175s and 180s with G160M/1533 (at FP-POS 1 and 2, respectively), and two 195s exposures with G160M/1577 (at FP-POS 3 and 4).

sources.summary: The paper predicts frequency-resolved lags for the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817, adopting AGN STORM II source parameters and presenting simulations for future testing.

quotes.quote: The target for the AGN STORM II campaign is the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 817, whose mass is similar to that of NGC 5548 but more luminous. Two essential CHAR model parameters of Mrk 817 are from : the black-hole mass M_ BH = 3.85 10^7 \ M_ , and the Eddington ratio L/L_ Edd = 0.2 . We run a similar simulation with the redshift z= 0.031455 for Mrk 817 Strauss1988 . For the simulated light curves, the time duration in each band is 200 days with a cadence of 0.1 days. We present the lag-frequency predictions for Mrk 817 in Fig. fig:Mrk 817 , and these simulated results can be tested by the Mrk 817 AGN-STORM II observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 817 as a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy whose BLR and NLR are complex, with a two-component BLR and outflowing narrow-line gas.

quotes.quote: In this contribution, we have reported the results of the emission line regions study of the Seyfert 1.5 galaxy Mrk 817. The Seyfert 1.5 galaxies are convenient for exploring the narrow and broad line emission regions since those galaxies have very strong both narrow and broad emission lines. In our work we have used several sets of different spectral observations with high spectral resolution. Our main finding is that both NLR and BLR are complex emission regions, where they are composed of at least two kinematically separated regions. We made a scheme of the emission line regions of Mrk 817 (Figure fig09 ). We found that the BLR of Mrk 817 can be described with the two-component model, where the core of the line is coming from a spherical region with isotropic velocity distribution, and wings are probably affected by a low inclined accretion disc. The ratio of the fluxes coming from the disc and from the spherical region did not depend on the parameters of the fit, it was almost the same in evert fit and equal to unity . In the NLR, we found that an outflow is present. In principle we can conclude that emission line region of Mrk 817 is complex, heaving at least four kinematically and physically different regions.

sources.summary: The COS composite-spectra paper discusses Mrk 817 as a low-redshift Seyfert 1.5 galaxy with narrow intrinsic absorption, prominent far-UV emission-line spectra, weak lines, and blue-shifted wind-dominated features.

quotes.quote: Several AGN exhibit narrow intrinsic absorption lines (e.g., Mrk 817, Mrk 290, Mrk 1513, PG 1115+407).

We also detect a number of weaker lines, known from atomic data and clearly seen in Figure 2 in high-S/N spectra of Mrk 817 and Mrk 1513. These include line blends of \ 1304 and \ 1308 , \ 1640 , ] 1605 , \ 1335 , \ 1085 , \ 1126 , and ] 1486 .

Figure 2 shows two outstanding examples of far-UV spectra taken by COS, labeling prominent emission lines in the low-redshift Seyfert 1.5 galaxies, Mrk 817 and Mrk 1513. According to the classification scheme proposed by Richards \ (2011), Mrk 817 is a ``wind-dominated AGN" with weak , , and \ emission lines and blue-shifted \ and ] .

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 9412 as a distant spiral Seyfert-2 whose [O III]/H ratio declines with aperture but retains Seyfert classification because AGN features dominate.

quotes.quote: UGC 9412 : This spiral Seyfert-2 galaxy is the second most distant galaxy in the sample. It moves towards lower values of [ O iii ] 5007/H with increasing aperture, an effect diminished when applying starlight subtraction. Despite the decreasing [ O iii ] 5007/H values, the galaxy preserves its Seyfert classification. Combined with the fact that it is relatively distant, the galaxy's AGN features seem to be most dominant and least affected by the host galaxy's starlight.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 817 as requiring a large NLR correction, double-Gaussian O I removal, and an exponential rather than r1/4-law bulge profile.

quotes.quote: Note the exponential bulges in Mrk 590 and Mrk 817.
Also, in Mrk 817 we have a rather large correction for NLR emission, which to our knowledge was not considered by mcluredunlop02 .
Mrk 817 -- OI 8446 was removed with double Gaussian fit. The bulge is better fit by a exponential than an ^ 1/4 law.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 817 as having negligible reflection but a measurable soft excess, suggesting ionised absorption or relativistic absorption may explain its soft X-ray features.

quotes.quote: The object Mrk 817, which has negligible reflection but a measurable soft excess, may well require a different model such as ionised absorption to account for its soft features. Indeed, mention an epoch in this source where absorbing winds were detected in UV spectroscopy of this source from 1997 and 2009, albeit without the X-ray edges due to Oxygen expected at 0.73 and 0.87 keV from such outflows. One potential explanation may be that the >2 keV X-ray continuum in this source is absorbed by a highly relativistic ionised absorber, which may tally with the absorption signatures in the UV.

sources.summary: The paper fits Mrk 817 time lags as one of four local Seyferts with KYNXiltr, exploring spin, colour correction, corona height, and accretion-power transfer parameters.

quotes.quote: We use KYNXiltr to fit the observed time lags obtained from intense monitoring of four local Seyfert galaxies (NGC 5548, NGC 4593, Mrk 817, and Fairall 9). We consider various combinations of black hole spin, colour correction, corona height, and fraction of accretion power transferred to the corona. The model fits well the overall time-lags spectrum in these sources (for a large parameter space).

sources.summary: For Mrk 817, the paper reports slightly larger JAVELIN lags and broader chi-square scatter, but consistent light-curve shapes and lag distributions support reliable H time lags.

quotes.quote: For Mrk 817, the results are shown in Figure 6 and Figure 7. The JAVELIN lag of the whole lightcurves is slightly larger than others and the ^2 lag distributions have larger scatters compared to other methods. But the similar shape of the extracted H , lagged g and lagged SRM H lightcurves 2021ApJ...918...50L for both the whole and part of lightcurves, as well as the consistent lag distributions from at least two methods, can indicate the reliability of the H time lags for Mrk 817.

gmw_00000660.name: PKS1614+051
gmw_00000660.overview: PKS1614+051 is a quasar at a redshift of approximately 3.2 [arXiv:1202.2193, arXiv:1507.07919] that features a resolved core and an inner radio jet [arXiv:1202.2193]. The host galaxy resides in an interacting system with at least four companion galaxies and a bridge of Lyman-alpha emitting gas [arXiv:1507.07919].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1614+051 as a z=3.212 quasar whose 8.4 GHz image resolves a core and inner jet, with consistent two-component fits and polarized jet/core emission.

quotes.quote: 1614+051 This quasar, at a redshift of 3.212 barthel1990 , has been observed by fey2000 with the VLBA at 2.3 and 8.6 GHz, who observed the jet to lie in position angle 158^ . Our 8.4 GHz image shows the source clearly resolved into a core and inner jet, extending in roughly this same position angle. Both our 5 and 8.4-GHz data are best fit with two components, whose positions agree well at the two frequencies. One polarized component was model-fit at 5 GHz (Table 4); its position does not completely agree with the positions of either of the two I components, although it clearly corresponds to jet emission. Polarization from both of the I components is detected at 8.4 GHz (Table 4), as well as another region of polarized emission between them.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS1614+051 as a z=3.2 quasar in an interacting high-redshift system with companion galaxies, Ly emission, and gas likely shaped by gravitational interaction.

quotes.quote: We present MUSE science verification data on one such interacting system consisting of the well-studied z=3.2 PKS1614+051 quasar, its AGN companion galaxy and bridge of material radiating in Ly between the quasar and its companion. We find a total of four companion galaxies (at least two galaxies are new discoveries), three of which reside within the likely virial radius of the quasar host, suggesting that the system will evolve into a massive elliptical galaxy by the present day. The MUSE data are of sufficient quality to split the extended Ly emission line into narrow velocity channels. In these the gas can be seen extending towards each of the three neighbouring galaxies suggesting that the emission-line gas originates in a gravitational interaction between the galaxies and the quasar host. The photoionization source of this gas is less clear but is probably dominated by the two AGN.

sources.summary: The note says the compact double loses its northwest component at 15 GHz and VLBI observations find a very compact core.

quotes.quote: J1616+0459: The NW component of this compact double is not visible in the 15 GHz image. Global VLBI observations by O'Sullivan et al. (2011) and by Pushkarev Kovalev (2012) find the core to be very compact.

gmw_00000661.name: AGN A
gmw_00000661.overview: AGN A is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus companion to the quasar PKS1614+051 [arXiv:1507.07919]. The two galaxies are close enough to interact and are connected by a Lyman-alpha emission line bridge spanning 50 physical kiloparsecs [arXiv:1507.07919].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGN A as a narrow-line companion to PKS1614+051, close enough to interact, connected by a 50 kpc Ly bridge, and showing active-galaxy kinematics.

quotes.quote: The PKS1614+051 quasar has at least four companion galaxies: the previously known narrow line AGN A, galaxy E - hitherto identified as a candidate z=3.2 Ly emitter (LAE) based on narrow-band imaging Hu&Cowie87,Hu91 and two new LAEs/LBGs: G and BH. For these newly identified sources, we extend the galaxy labeling scheme of Hu&Cowie87 but we do not find galaxies at the positions of their C and D objects. The spectra of these companion galaxies are shown in more detail in Fig. spectra_A spectra_companions . Three of the companion galaxies (A, E and G) are close enough to be interacting with the quasar as defined by Ellison08 and are within the expected virial radius of the quasar's host dark matter halo suggesting they will eventually merge with the quasar host to form a massive elliptical galaxy (perhaps at the centre of a group or cluster) by the present day.

We have obtained a detailed, high signal-to-noise spectrum of the quasar, companion A and, for the first time, the emission line bridge. Fig. peak shows the distribution of gas in the central region around the quasar. The emission line bridge extends over 50 physical kpc from A to the quasar and slightly beyond the quasar in the other direction (Fig. peak b), with a width of around 10 kpc. Fig. peak shows the red-most and blue-most (5 \ wide or 300 km s ^ 1 ) components of the Ly line, showing how the high surface brightness emission connects the quasar to companion A and also extends towards the two other nearby galaxies (E and G), denoted by crosses. Fig. peak d shows the dispersion of the Ly line as a function of position, found from fitting a Gaussian to the Ly line in each spatial pixel. The Ly emission from the bridge has a relatively low dispersion, as does the Ly emission from companions E and G, whilst the emission from the quasar and companion A have larger velocity dispersions as expected for active galaxies (see Table results ).

gmw_00000662.name: galaxy E
gmw_00000662.overview: Galaxy E is a Lyman-alpha emitting companion galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.2 [arXiv:1507.07919]. It is close enough to be interacting with the quasar PKS1614+051, and its continuum emission suggests that star formation drives its Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1507.07919].

sources.summary: The paper treats galaxy E as a z=3.2 Ly-emitting companion whose photometry, narrow Ly line, continuum emission, and proximity support interaction with PKS1614+051.

quotes.quote: The PKS1614+051 quasar has at least four companion galaxies: the previously known narrow line AGN A, galaxy E - hitherto identified as a candidate z=3.2 Ly emitter (LAE) based on narrow-band imaging Hu&Cowie87,Hu91 and two new LAEs/LBGs: G and BH. For these newly identified sources, we extend the galaxy labeling scheme of Hu&Cowie87 but we do not find galaxies at the positions of their C and D objects. The spectra of these companion galaxies are shown in more detail in Fig. spectra_A spectra_companions . Three of the companion galaxies (A, E and G) are close enough to be interacting with the quasar as defined by Ellison08 and are within the expected virial radius of the quasar's host dark matter halo suggesting they will eventually merge with the quasar host to form a massive elliptical galaxy (perhaps at the centre of a group or cluster) by the present day. Although the spectrum of E may be affected by scattered light from the quasar, its broad-band photometry is consistent with a Lyman break and its Ly line is significantly narrower than the quasar's, suggesting it is a separate z=3.2 galaxy. E is unlikely to be driven solely by fluorescence from the quasar e.g. Cantalupo05,Kollmeier10 as the presence of continuum emission suggests star formation can create the Ly emission (the rest-frame equivalent width is only 15 ).

The Ly emission from the bridge has a relatively low dispersion, as does the Ly emission from companions E and G, whilst the emission from the quasar and companion A have larger velocity dispersions as expected for active galaxies (see Table results ).

gmw_00000663.name: G
gmw_00000663.overview: G is a newly identified Lyman-alpha and Lyman-break companion galaxy near the quasar PKS1614+051 [arXiv:1507.07919]. It is close enough to be interacting with the quasar and is connected to it by a low-dispersion Lyman-alpha emission bridge [arXiv:1507.07919].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G as a newly found LAE/LBG companion near PKS1614+051, within the likely interaction region and traced by nearby Ly bridge emission.

quotes.quote: The PKS1614+051 quasar has at least four companion galaxies: the previously known narrow line AGN A, galaxy E - hitherto identified as a candidate z=3.2 Ly emitter (LAE) based on narrow-band imaging Hu&Cowie87,Hu91 and two new LAEs/LBGs: G and BH. For these newly identified sources, we extend the galaxy labeling scheme of Hu&Cowie87 but we do not find galaxies at the positions of their C and D objects. The spectra of these companion galaxies are shown in more detail in Fig. spectra_A spectra_companions . Three of the companion galaxies (A, E and G) are close enough to be interacting with the quasar as defined by Ellison08 and are within the expected virial radius of the quasar's host dark matter halo suggesting they will eventually merge with the quasar host to form a massive elliptical galaxy (perhaps at the centre of a group or cluster) by the present day.

Fig. peak shows the red-most and blue-most (5 \ wide or 300 km s ^ 1 ) components of the Ly line, showing how the high surface brightness emission connects the quasar to companion A and also extends towards the two other nearby galaxies (E and G), denoted by crosses. Fig. peak d shows the dispersion of the Ly line as a function of position, found from fitting a Gaussian to the Ly line in each spatial pixel. The Ly emission from the bridge has a relatively low dispersion, as does the Ly emission from companions E and G, whilst the emission from the quasar and companion A have larger velocity dispersions as expected for active galaxies (see Table results ).

gmw_00000664.name: IGR J11079+7106
gmw_00000664.overview: IGR J11079+7106 is an active galactic nucleus identified with the soft X-ray source Swift J1107.8+7107 [arXiv:2003.07199]. Optical spectroscopy of this extended object reveals it is a Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of z=0.059 [arXiv:2003.07199].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IGR J11079+7106 with Swift J1107.8+7107 and classifies its extended optical counterpart as a Seyfert 1 AGN at z=0.059.

quotes.quote: According to the Swift archival data, only one soft X-ray source, Swift J1107.8+7107 (Evans et al. 2014), falls into the IBIS/INTEGRAL error circle. It was localized with a high accuracy (see Table 1) from the XRT/Swift data. We obtained the XRT X-ray spectrum of Swift J1107.8+7107 and combined it with the IBIS/INTEGRAL spectrumof the hard X-ray source IGR J11079+7106 (Fig. 1). It turned out that the fluxes from the sources in the soft and hard X-ray bands agree well between themselves, while the broadband spectrum could be fitted ( ^2=0.65 per degree of freedom for 16 degrees of freedom) by a power law with a slope (photon index) of 1.70 0.05 (Fig. 1). Such a spectral model is typical primarily for active galactic nuclei (AGNs).

Using an accurate localization of the assumed soft X-ray counterpart and the PanSTARRS sky survey data, we managed to determine the optical counterpart of the source (Fig. 1). This is an extended object with an apparent magnitude 17.0 . We performed its spectroscopy with the RTT-150 telescope of the TUBITAK Observatory. Its spectrum (Fig. 1) clear shows a broad (FWHM = 3272 km s ^ 1 corrected for the spectral resolution of the instrument), redshifted H emission line and a number of other characteristic emission lines (in particular, [ OIII ] and [ SII ] ), which unambiguously point to the fact that this object is a Seyfert 1 AGN at redshift z=0.059 0.001 ( D_ L 272.6 Mpc).

gmw_00000665.name: NGC 4250
gmw_00000665.overview: NGC 4250 is an active galaxy located at a photometric distance of approximately 31.8 Mpc [arXiv:2003.07199]. It is classified as an absorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy with LINER-like spectral features and a high absorption column density [arXiv:2003.07199].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4250 as the likely counterpart of IGR J12171+7047 and classifies it as an absorbed Seyfert 2 galaxy with LINER-like spectral features.

quotes.quote: The bright ( 12.61 from the PanSTARRS data) galaxy NGC 4250 (Fig. 2), which we consider as the most probable hard X-ray source, falls into the IBIS/INTEGRAL error circle. Moreover, previously the XRT/Swift telescope has already recorded soft X-ray emission from it. In the Swift source catalog this object is designated as Swift J1217.6+7047 (Evans et al. 2014). We performed the object's spectroscopy with the RTT-150 telescope. Narrow, redshifted H (FWHM = 539 km s ^ 1 , completely determined by the resolution of the instrument), [ NII ] , [ SII ] , and [ OIII ] lines are clearly distinguishable in the spectrum of the central part of the galaxy (Fig. 2). The measured redshift is z=0.007 0.001 and the corresponding photometric distance is D_ L =31.8 Mpc.

Its spectrum reveals a similarity both with the spectra of Seyfert 2 galaxies and with the spectra of LINER galaxies. To establish the true class of the object, we estimated the line flux ratios [ NII ] /H = 1.27 and [ SII ] /H = 0.88, the lower limit [ OIII ] /H > 2.7 , and used the well-known BPT diagram (Baldwin et al. 1981; Kewley et al. 2006). The values obtained correspond to the transition region between the LINER and Seyfert 2 galaxies. However, since the H line in the source's spectrum is not statistically significant and since only a lower limit was obtained for the ratio [ OIII ] /H , the investigated object is more likely a Seyfert 2 galaxy. The total X-ray spectrum of IGR J12171+7047 and its putative counterpart Swift J1217.6+7047 is well fitted ( ^2=1.2 per degree of freedom for 5 degrees of freedom) by a power law with a low energy cutoff due to photoabsorption. In this case, the slope is 1.8 and the absorption column density is N_H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . Given the distance to the object (see above), the hard X-ray luminosity of the source is 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 (Table 2). All of this suggests that we are dealing with a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a large absorption column density.

gmw_00000666.name: SWIFT J2059.6+4301B
gmw_00000666.overview: SWIFT J2059.6+4301B is a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.0656 [arXiv:2003.07199]. It exhibits infrared colors typical of active galaxies and displays X-ray absorption characteristic of its Seyfert 2 classification [arXiv:2003.07199].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SWIFT J2059.6+4301B as the AGN component of IGR J20596+4303, with infrared colors, spectroscopy, redshift, and X-ray absorption consistent with Seyfert 2 activity.

quotes.quote: According to the Swift data, there are two X-ray sources (Fig. 4) known as SWIFT J2059.6+4301A/1SXPS J205915.6+430105 https://swift.gsfc.nasa.gov/results/bs105mon/1095 and SWIFT J2059.6+4301B/1SXPS J210000.9+430208 https://swift.gsfc.nasa.gov/results/bs70mon /SWIFT J2059.6p4301B in the immediate vicinity of this object (Baumgartner et al. 2013). SWIFT J2059.6+4301B is slightly closer ( 4.1' ) to the position measured by the IBIS/NTEGRAL telescope, falling into the 2 error circle. SWIFT J2059.6+4301A is 4.7' away from IGR,J20596+4303 and falls into the 3 error circle with a radius of 6.3' . An accurate localization of SWIFT J2059.6+4301B based on the XRT data (Table 2) allows its optical counterpart to be unambiguously determined. This is an object with optical/IR magnitudes 20.37 , W1=12.63 0.03 (from the PanSTARRS and WISE data, respectively) and with infrared colors ( W1-W2 0.73 ) typical for AGNs (Stern et al. 2012). Previously, this object has already been noted as an AGN candidate (Edelson and Malkan 2012). Our AZT-33IK spectrum shows that this is a Seyfert 2 galaxy (FWHM _ H = 546.6 km s ^ 1 , completely determined by the resolution of the instrument) at redshift z=0.0656 0.0010 (Fig. 5 on the left).

If this difference is attributed to the object's variability, then the total spectrum of the pair IGR J20596+4303/SWIFT J2059.6+4301B can be described ( ^2=0.31 per degree of freedom for 8 degrees of freedom) by a power law ( 1.8 ) with absorption N_H=2.6 0.7 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , typical values for Seyfert 2 galaxies.

gmw_00000667.name: Cen 6
gmw_00000667.overview: Cen 6 is a dwarf irregular galaxy located on the outskirts of the Cen A group [arXiv:0707.3771]. Its stellar population includes sparsely populated blue and red supergiant branches, and it has experienced at least two distinct star formation episodes [arXiv:0707.3771].

sources.summary: The paper describes Cen 6 as a dwarf irregular on the outskirts of the Cen A group, with sparse supergiant branches and ancient plus recent star-formation episodes.

quotes.quote: The dIrr galaxy Cen 6 is situated on the outskirts of the Cen A group. We can see sparsely populated blue and red supergiant branches, AGB stars and upper red giant branch at the CMD. At least two star formation episodes can be distinguished (right panel): the ancient one, about 12--13 Gyr ago with low metallicity and more recent about 10--800 Myr ago with the metallicity probably up to solar.

gmw_00000668.name: KK 197
gmw_00000668.overview: KK 197 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that contains a well-populated, old red giant branch and intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:0707.3771]. The galaxy exhibits red giant branch color scatter, which is attributed to an active star formation episode of very high metallicity about 0.4 to 1 Gyr ago [arXiv:0707.3771].

sources.summary: The paper describes KK 197 as a dwarf spheroidal with an old RGB, intermediate-age AGB stars, unusual RGB color scatter, and unusually high recent metallicity.

quotes.quote: The CMD of the dSph galaxy KK 197 contain well populated old RGB and some intermediate age AGB stars. The galaxy has unusual RGB color scatter, which reflects active star formation episode of very high metallicity level about 0.4--1 Gyr ago, whereas ancient burst of star formation has low metallicity.

Second, several dwarfs under study have unusual rate of metal enrichment, with the metallicity level of recent star formation episodes of about solar and probably higher (like KK 197 and ESO 269--066).

gmw_00000669.name: J1835+620
gmw_00000669.overview: J1835+620 is a giant Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.518 [arXiv:astro-ph/9909201]. It features two distinct pairs of radio lobes that indicate separate phases of core activity [arXiv:astro-ph/9909201, arXiv:2007.14815].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1835+620 as a giant FR II radio galaxy whose double components and active hot spots indicate distinct, not fully interrupted, phases of core activity.

quotes.quote: The radio source J1835+620 belongs to a new sample of large angular size radio galaxies (Lara et al., in preparation) selected from the NRAO VLA Sky Survey . We have observed this source with the VLA in its B- and C- configurations at 1.4, 4.9 and 8.5 GHz, and with the MPIfA 2.2m optical telescope in Calar Alto (Spain). The outstanding aspect of J1835+620 at radio wavelengths is the existence of two symmetric bright components (N2 and S2) within a typical FR II structure (see Fig. fig1 ).

With an angular size of 3.88' and a redshift of z=0.518, implying a total source length of 1.12 Mpc (H _0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ; q _o =0.5), J1835+620 belongs to the group of giant radio galaxies. We can interpret the peculiar structure of J1835+620 as the result of two distinct phases of core activity. However, the existence of a hot-spot in component N1 together with spectral aging arguments indicate that N1 and S1 are still supplied by fresh particles, implying i) the existence of an underlying jet connecting the core with the outer components and ii) that the activity in J1835+620 did not stop completely.

In consequence, the new components N2 and S2 would represent the result of a new ejection propagating through the primary underlying jet. The parallel configuration of the magnetic field in components N2 and S2 (Fig. fig1 , bottom panel) is consistent with a ``second-phase'' jet which is overdense with respect to the ``first-phase'' one .

sources.summary: The paper uses DDRG J1835+6204 as a reference source, describes its two pairs of radio lobes, and ties model interruption timing to its observed outburst history.

quotes.quote: As a reference source, we have chosen the DDRG J1835+6204 to compare our results with.

J1835+6204 has two clearly distinct pairs of radio lobes: the outer North-Western and the South-Eastern lobes, referred to as NW1 and SE1, respectively; and the inner North-Western and the South-Eastern radio lobes (closest to the central engine situated at the plus-sign in the center of the image), referred to as NW2 and SE2, respectively.

The interruption time for all three models is chosen to be 4.5 per cent of the injection time of the initial jet, in correspondence with J1835+6204 which is estimated to have an interruption of 1 Myr and an active phase of the previous outburst of 22 Myr.

gmw_00000670.name: 3C338
gmw_00000670.overview: NGC 6166 is a central cD elliptical galaxy in the Abell 2199 cluster [arXiv:1511.08493, arXiv:2110.07373] that hosts the Fanaroff-Riley type I radio source 3C 338 [arXiv:astro-ph/9909201, arXiv:2110.07373]. The galaxy features a massive central black hole [arXiv:2110.07373], an exceptionally rich globular cluster system [arXiv:1511.08493], and a dust lane crossing its nucleus [arXiv:0712.0595].

sources.summary: The paper presents deep HST photometry of NGC 6166, describing it as Abell 2199’s central cD galaxy with an extended halo and an exceptionally rich globular-cluster system.

quotes.quote: We present new deep photometry of the globular cluster system (GCS) around NGC 6166, the central supergiant galaxy in Abell 2199. HST data from the ACS and WFC3 cameras in F475W, F814W are used to determine the spatial distribution of the GCS, its metallicity distribution function (MDF), and the dependence of the MDF on galactocentric radius and on GC luminosity. The MDF is extremely broad, with the classic red and blue subpopulations heavily overlapped, but a double-Gaussian model can still formally match the MDF closely. The spatial distribution follows a S ersic-like profile detectably to a projected radius of at least R_ gc = 250 kpc. To that radius, the total number of clusters in the system is N_ GC = 39000 2000 , the global specific frequency is S_N = 11.2 0.6 , and 57 of the total are blue, metal-poor clusters.

NGC 6166 is a classic cD galaxy with an extremely extended halo hereafter B15 bender_etal2015 and is the central supergiant galaxy in Abell 2199. Early detections of its rich GCS with ground-based imaging were done by pritchet_harris1990 from the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope, bridges_etal1996 from the William Herschel Telescope, and blakeslee_etal1997 with the MDM Observatory. With ground-based imaging, however, only the brightest magnitude or two of the GC luminosity function and approximate mean color indices could be measured, yielding very uncertain estimates of the spatial extent or specific frequency of the system. By contrast, the HST cameras are extremely well suited to imaging of GCSs in giant galaxies at distances of 100-200 Mpc where the field sizes of either the ACS or WFC3 arrays correspond to linear diameters near 100 kpc, while the bright half of the GC luminosity function (GCLF) can be well measured in just a few orbits of exposure time. In this paper, we present the first comprehensive two-color photometric study of the NGC 6166 system.

sources.summary: NGC 6166 is treated as the dominant Abell 2199 cD galaxy and 3C 338 host, with deeper ACS images revealing dust, nuclear emission, extended ionized filaments, and correlated gas and dust.

quotes.quote: NGC 6166 --- Fig. 1(g) : NGC 6166, host of the powerful radio source 3C 338, is the dominant cD galaxy in Abell 2199. A wealth of information on this galaxy is available in the literature over all wavelength domains. Here, we compare our data with past ground and HST imaging at visible wavelengths. Continuum images acquired with HST's WF/PC-1 and WFPC2 cameras in the F555W/ V and F702W/ R filters are presented in Lauer et al. (1995), Martel et al. (1999), and de Koff et al. (2000). Our new ACS images are significantly deeper and reveal previously undetected dust and ionized filaments.

Most of the emission-line flux of NGC 6166 comes from its nuclear regions. Indeed, using ground-based narrow-band imaging, Morganti, Ulrich, Tadhunter (1992) did not detect extended line emission in this object. On the other hand, our deep ACS images reveal several low-surface brightness filaments out to radii of 8 kpc ( 13 ) in the SW quadrant.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C338 as an FR I radio source in NGC 6166 with active and diffuse relic regions, parsec-scale jets, and possible restarted activity.

quotes.quote: 3C338 ( z=0.030 ) is a FR I radio source, associated with the multiple nuclei cD galaxy NGC 6166 at the center of the cooling flow cluster of galaxies A2199. We are undertaking a program of VLA and VLBI observations of 3C338 in order to study its strong flux density variability, its large scale properties and the structural variations at parsec scales . The large-scale structure of 3C338 can be separated in two regions with very different properties: an active region, which includes the core, two symmetric jets and two faint hot spots at the jet ends, and a diffuse region, displaced to the south, showing a jetlike filament and low-brightness extended emission (Fig. fig3 ).

If the radio core stopped or decreased its activity at some time, it could have left a steep-spectrum aged radio jet behind (relic emission). Burns et al. (1983) suggested two possible explanations for the existence of two separated regions in this peculiar source: (1) the ram pressure of a highly asymmetric cooling flow onto the cD galaxy or (2) the motion of the radio core within the cD galaxy. Alternatively, the relic emission could have been produced by past activity in one of the other nuclei in the cD galaxy.

At parsec scales, 3C338 has an unusual structure consisting of a compact core and two symmetric jets, with moving components in both directions (Fig. fig4 ). We present observational results on two very different radio galaxies, the giant FR II J1835+620 and the FR I 3C338, both with radio structures showing evidence of distinct phases of activity during their lifetimes.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC6166 as a multiple-nucleus cD in a rich cluster; earlier outer rotation and rising dispersion are not confirmed, but central rotation is measured.

quotes.quote: NGC6166: This is a classic multiple nucleus cD galaxy in a rich cluster. The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986). Carter et al. \ (1999) found the velocity dispersion of this galaxy to increase from 325 km s ^ 1 at the centre to 450 km s ^ 1 at 35 arcsec along the major axis.

They also found the galaxy to show modest major-axis rotation (45 km s ^ 1 at 40 arcsec). The profiles derived here do not extend out to the radii necessary to confirm the positive velocity dispersion slope found by Carter et al. \ (1999). Rotation of the order of 31 km s ^ 1 is found in the centre of the galaxy although this is not a very clear rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 338.0/NGC 6166 as a cD elliptical in Abell 2199 with a massive central black hole and FR I radio jets and lobes.

quotes.quote: 3C 338.0 (NGC 6166) is an elliptical (cD2 pec) galaxy in the Abell 2199 cluster. It lies 490 million light years away in the constellation Hercules. It has a supermassive black hole at its center with a mass of nearly 30 10^9M_ based on dynamical modeling . NGC 6166 is classified as an Fanaroff-Riley type I source, which powers two symmetric parsec-scale radio jets and radio lobes. Jets ans lobes are caused by the infall of gas into its center according to a cooling with the rate of 200 M_ of gas per year .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C338 as an unusual radio and X-ray source in A2199, with offset radio plasma, active two-sided jets, and enhanced X-ray emission near its filament.

quotes.quote: 3C338 in A2199 is an unsual source in both radio and X-rays. Figs. 3 and 4 show that the radio source and inner X-ray plasma seem to avoid each other. The X-ray gas shows an enhanced region to the north of the AGN, while the radio source is offset to the south of the core. The radio source again has active, two-sided pc-scale jets, which continue to kpc scales and then grow very faint. The region between the core and the bright radio filament to the south is filled (at least in projection) with enhanced X-ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper places B2 1626+39 in A2199, describes its cooling-flow environment and disrupted young radio source, and uses only the first HRI observation for analysis.

quotes.quote: B2 1626+39 lies in a cluster (A2199), with a prototypical cooling flow. Owen Eilek (1998) conclude that the radio source is relatively young and has been disrupted by the surrounding gas. The ROSAT HRI data set for this source consists of 2 OBIs roughly 7 months apart. Only in the second observation does the source appear extended, with two adjacent peaks. We have taken this to be due to errors in the aspect correction or processing effects and therefore have used only the first OBI in our analysis.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies 3C 338 with Abell 2199/NGC 6166, describing an FR I source in a dense environment with prominent companions and a disturbed elliptical host crossed by dust.

quotes.quote: 3C 338 (Abell 2199; NGC 6166); z=0.030354 An FR I radio source in a dense environment. Two companions are prominent on the NICMOS image. The elliptical host galaxy is disturbed, with a dust lane crossing the nucleus, faintly visible in the IR. First published by jensen+01 .

sources.summary: 3C 338 requires a two-temperature model for cluster gas, which interacts with the radio galaxy; the authors do not detect its AGN or ISM in the bright ICM.

quotes.quote: 3C 338 ( z=0.030 ) : Unlike other extended sources for which deprojection is possible but which can be fit by an isothermal model to find an average temperature (most of the other galaxies in our sample), 3C 338 has sufficient signal to require at least a 2-T model to find average temperatures (signifying the presence of hotter and cooler gas) and these temperatures are roughly in line with the outer and inner temperatures of the johnstone02 deprojection analysis which also agrees with our own (we report the 2-T fit for consistency). Although the cluster gas is interacting with the radio galaxy, it is otherwise smooth and we take it to be a single large ellipsoid for the purpose of measuring ellipticity and position angle of the ICM. Notably, we do not detect the AGN or the ISM amidst the very bright ICM. This observation is studied more thoroughly in johnstone02 , including the unusual radio bridge parallel to the jets.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6166 for its uppermost black-hole model, noting its stellar-kinematic mass measurement is comparable to the model limit but remains unconfirmed.

quotes.quote: For the uppermost value ( M_ 9 =40 ) we use NGC 6166. So far, it is the only source with a measured black hole mass based on stellar kinematics which is comparable to that limit 1998AJ....115.2285M . However, its mass has not yet been confirmed by other studies. For galaxy models 8 9 the bulge mass of NGC 6166 is rounded upwards to M_ dyn [ M_ 9 ] =2000 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6166 as a comparison cD galaxy whose two spheroids and high-dispersion outer envelope resemble the interpretation pursued for NGC 3311.

quotes.quote: Recently, 2015ApJ...807...56B dealt with the above question in cD galaxies, in the study of NGC 6166. They used a combination of deep long-slit observations with deep photometry and required that the photometric decomposition reproduced the radial gradients of the four LOSVD moments, i.e. V , , h_3 , h_4 . As a result, the best kinematic photometric model identified two spheroids with low S ersic indices ( n<4 ), with very different surface brightnesses and radii, and also different systemic velocities and velocity dispersions. Because of the large value of its effective radius, the outer component was identified with the cD envelope of NGC 6166, and its large velocity dispersion supports the idea that its stars were stripped from galaxies orbiting in Abell 2199.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1-594493 as one of the three highest-NVSS radio-flux type-1 AGNs, classifies it as a LERG, and reports FIRST-image radio-jet structure in a host-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: The three AGN with the highest NVSS radio flux density shown in Figures fig:LoiiivsLRadio fig:butiglionini as blue stars in circles are 1-594493, 1-604860, and 1-71872, were classified as LERGs. Figure fig:Radiojets is a mosaic showing their corresponding FIRST images. The images show clear evidence radio jet structures powered by the active nucleus, in agreement with the definition of LERGs. The three galaxies with radio jets are of the host-dominated type according to our empirical classification.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA and VLBA monitoring of radio galaxy 3C 338, describing its association with NGC 6166, powerful radio emission, bidirectional jets, and steep-spectrum lobes.

quotes.quote: Kassim 7 1 Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of New Mexico, Albuquerque, NM 87131, USA 2 Department of Physics, Universidad Simon Bolivar, Sartenejas, Venezuela 3 National Radio Astronomy Observatory, Socorro NM 87801, USA 4 Dipartimento di Astronomia, Universita' di Bologna, 40127 Bologna, Italy 5 Istituto di Radioastronomia - INAF, 40129 Bologna, Italy 6 Kavli Institute for Particle Astrophysics and Cosmology, Stanford University, Stanford, CA 94305-4060, USA 7 Naval Research Lab, Code 7213, Washington, DC, 20375, USA We present the analysis of VLA and VLBA observations of the radio source 3C 338, associated with the cD galaxy NGC 6166, the central dominant galaxy of the cluster Abell 2199.
This galaxy hosts the relatively powerful radio source, 3C 338, which emits a total power at 330 MHz of 4.4 10 ^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 .

These observations are used to study the motions of the inner bidirectional jets in 3C 338. On kiloparsec scales 3C 338 has two symmetric extended radio lobes, characterized by a steep spectrum ( 1.7), and misaligned with the central emission.

sources.summary: 3C 338 is identified as an A2199 radio galaxy with W-shaped FIRST morphology; the paper summarizes models involving cooling-flow ram pressure or intermittent nuclear motion leaving a steep-spectrum jet.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy 3C 338, (four-component FCG J162838.2+393304, Group 611594, Figure typical_ws ), a member of cluster A2199, also of the Taylor sample, also has a W-shaped radio morphology in FIRST. Burns83 discussed two models for 3C 338. One model involved ram pressure produced by a cooling accretion flow and the other invoked motion of an intermittent radio nucleus about the barycenter of the cluster/galaxy potential leaving behind a steep spectrum jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies A 2199 as an outlier BCG without ongoing star formation, while its BCG NGC 6166 hosts the radio source 3C 338.

quotes.quote: Among all the BCGs, A 2199 seems to be an oddball in that the most-likely model does not call for a ``young'' stellar component at all.

Even though A 2199 is the weakest cool-core cluster in the sample with a mass deposition rate of 72^ +2 _ 2 paperIII , the BCG of A 2199, NGC 6166, harbours a moderately powerful radio source, 3C 338, with symmetric parsec-scale jets e.g. and references therein Gentile2007 .
Therefore there is no ``ongoing'' star formation rate in A 2199.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 6166 as a comparison BCG, noting its very populous GC system, large measurement radius, possible intracluster contribution, and need for standardized re-evaluation.

quotes.quote: Both are at the very top end of the most populous known GCSs, and comparable with NGC 6166, which was found to have N_ GC 39000 2000 within a 250 kpc radius Harris2016 . However, as discussed by Harris2016 , this total for NGC 6166 goes well beyond the R_ GCS limit we adopt here and may include a large contribution from the galaxy cluster's intracluster medium. This system, and the other BCGs in the literature, should also be re-evaluated using the R_ GCS = 0.1R_ vir standardization proposed here.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6166 as Abell 2199's central cD galaxy, notes X-ray emission peaks on it, and says it hosts the peculiar radio source 3C338.

quotes.quote: Abell 2199 is a nearby rich cluster of galaxies, the central dominant galaxy of which is the cD galaxy, NGC 6166. The intracluster gas is of intermediate temperature (4.7 keV; EdgeStewart91 ) and gives rise to X-ray emission which is strongly peaked on the central galaxy, leading to the suggestion of a strong cluster cooling flow, e.g., Peresetal98 . NGC 6166 hosts the radio source 3C338 which has been known for nearly 20 years to have a peculiar morphology Burnsetal83 .

sources.summary: The cluster radio galaxy 3C 338 has complex asymmetric X-ray structure and unusual radio properties, so the authors use an inner-region decomposition for the core.

quotes.quote: 3C 338 This radio galaxy, located in the cluster A2199, shows a complex and asymmetrical X-ray structure (Owen Eilek 1998) and is also unusual in the radio. Radial profiling is clearly not particularly appropriate, but a simple application to the inner regions of the source gives the decomposition tabulated here, which represents our best estimate of the core contribution.

gmw_00000671.name: NGC3923
gmw_00000671.overview: NGC 3923 is an E4-5 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1105.1755, arXiv:astro-ph/0611858] that serves as the brightest member of a small galaxy group [arXiv:1105.1755, arXiv:1703.10835]. It is a shell galaxy containing up to 42 concentric stellar shells [arXiv:1505.07146] and a large patch of dust [arXiv:astro-ph/0703588].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3923 as an E4-5 group elliptical with shell structure, evidence for a past starburst or merger, hot gas, and rich globular clusters.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923 This is an E4-5 galaxy within a small group and exhibits a well known shell structure ( MC1980 ). These kind of structures are frequent in ellipticals and S0 galaxies not belonging to galaxy clusters ( SchwS92 ), and they are usually identified as the result of an interaction or merger (e.g. TW1990,Q1984 ). These shell structures may be long-lived and therefore an interaction/merger event may have occurred long time ago. From spectroscopic data, TMBO05 and DTT2005 derive a central galaxy age of 3.4 and 2.6 Gyrs respectively. Thus about 3 Gyrs ago, the galaxy appears to have experienced a central starburst (perhaps induced by the same event that created the shells).

The galaxy has a halo of hot gas (e.g. OFP2001 ). Using the ROSAT satellite, BC1999 fit a model to the X-ray surface density profile out to 500 arcsec and derived a power-law slope of --1.76 0.02. FBPOK2006 fitted a double model to the X-ray surface brightness and found values of 0.598/0.314 for the less/more extended component.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3923 as a shell elliptical testing MOND potentials, finding its known shell system consistent with models and predicting one new faint outer shell around 210-220 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923 (Fig. fig:N3923 ), which is the main subject of this paper, is decorated by the biggest known shell in the Universe. Its radius is more than 100 kpc (Table tab:shpos ) and, according to the MONDian model of the galaxy NGC 3923 from bil13 , it is exposed to a gravitational acceleration of a_0/5 from the host galaxy. In this work, we theoretically predict a new -- currently not yet observed -- shell at a distance of about 220 kpc, which would extend to the acceleration of a_0/10 .

We tested the consistency of the shell radial distribution observed in NGC 3923 with a wide variety of MOND potentials of the galaxy. The potentials differed in the mass-to-light ratio and in distance to the galaxy. We considered different MOND interpolation functions, values of the acceleration constant a_0 , and density profiles of the galaxy.

Our method reliably predicts that exactly one new outermost shell exists at a galactocentric radius of about 1900 ^ ( 210 kpc) on the southwestern side of the galaxy. Its estimated surface brightness is about 28 mag arcsec ^ 2 in B - a value accessible by current instruments.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3923 as a shell elliptical whose shell radii, MOND potential, merger history, and predicted undiscovered giant shell can test galaxy gravity.

quotes.quote: The elliptical NGC 3923 (Fig. fig:N3923 ), which is the main subject of this paper, possess the biggest known shell in the Universe. Its radius is more than 100 kpc (Table tab:shpos ) and, according to the MONDian model of the galaxy NGC 3923 from B i lek et al. , it is exposed to gravitational acceleration of a_0/5 (Table tab:shpos ). We predicted theoretically the presence of a new -- yet undiscovered -- shell at the distance of about 220 kpc , which would extend down to the acceleration of a_0/10 .

We tested the compatibility of MOND with the radial distribution of shells in a famous, well studied shell galaxy NGC 3923 (Fig. fig:N3923 ) . It is a relatively nearby ( 23 Mpc) elliptical with many record properties. Besides the mentioned extraordinary shell radial range, it also hosts the biggest known shell with the radius of 120 kpc. It is also the galaxy with the highest known number of shells, namely 27 (Table tab:shpos ).

Applying the method of shell identification to a large number of possible MOND potentials of NGC 3923, we concluded that a new, yet undiscovered and the biggest shell of NGC 3923 must exist at the distance of 1900 ^ south-west from the galactic center .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3923 as a known Type-I shell galaxy, fitting its Spitzer surface-density profile and adopting shell radii to estimate MOND velocity scalings.

quotes.quote: In this section, we roughly estimate the asymptotic circular and shell expansion velocities for several real Type-I shell galaxies assuming MOND gravity. The shells in galaxies NGC 3923 (e.g., prieur88 ) and NGC 7600 (e.g., turnbull ) have been known for a long time. In spite of their prominence, the shells in NGC 410 and NGC 3099 have not been reported yet. For obtaining the surface density profile of NGC 3923, we fitted the profile to its near-infrared image taken by the Spitzer space telescope. We used the same data, M/L ratio, and the galaxy distance from Earth as in bil13 .

For NGC 7600, NGC 410, and NGC 3099, we started from their SDSS (Sloan Digital Sky Survey, see, e.g., sdssdr10 ) images in the g , , and i bands. We fitted each of them by the one-component profile in Galfit Penggalfit . The integrated magnitudes in the g , , and i bands, and the corresponding color indices were determined from these data. The approximate M/L ratios were obtained from the color indices using the tables of sdssml . We assumed a constant M/L across the whole volume of the galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3923 as a shell galaxy and group brightest member, then models its profile with an upward inflection and an inner Sersic plus outer exponential component.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923 NGC 3923, first studied by Malin80 , is one of the best examples of a ``shell galaxy'', with a vast system of peculiar, concentric shells. Sandage94 found at least seven dwarf ellipticals in the field centered on NGC 3923, which is the brightest member of this small group. Carter98 found that the galaxy has minor axis rotation. This could be either the result of a kinematically decoupled core, or evidence for a triaxial geometry. Buote98 , by studying the X-ray isophotes of NGC 3923, found evidence for a dark matter distribution more flattened and extended than the optical luminosity distribution.

Specifically, we can distinguish the profiles of NGC 1399, NGC 5044 and NGC 5846 which show a downward inflection, from those of NGC 3923, NGC 4365 and NGC 4472, which instead have an upward inflection. According to our multi-component modelling, all the upward inflections are the result of a transition from a high- n inner component to a larger, lower- n , outer component. Adopting the an empirically-motivated two-component approach most common in the literature we find that four of our six galaxies (NGC 3923, NGC 4365, NGC 4472, NGC 1399) can be modelled by an inner S e rsic component and an outer exponential, while the other two objects (NGC 5044 and NGC 5846) are best fitted with a double S e rsic model.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep imaging of shell elliptical NGC 3923, detecting 42 shells and tidal features while rejecting a predicted MOND shell.

quotes.quote: We detected up to 42 shells in NGC 3923. This is by far the highest number among all shell galaxies. We present the description of the shells and other tidal features in the galaxy.

bil14a (hereafter B14) predicted the existence of an as yet unobserved shell in the elliptical galaxy NGC 3923 at 2000 southwest (around 200 kpc) from the galaxy's center. The prediction was made supposing the modified Newtonian dynamics (MOND) milg83a , a modification of Newtonian dynamics for low accelerations that eliminates the need for dark matter. This prediction was based on the shell radii tabulated by prieur88 and sikkema07 . If the shell were to be discovered, it would be the first discovery of an object predicted by MOND, a test of MOND to exceptionally low gravitational acceleration for an elliptical galaxy, and the discovery of the biggest shell ever. Here we present the new deep observation of the galaxy we obtained to search for the predicted shell.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3923 as an elliptical shell galaxy, attributing its shells to a minor merger and testing whether their distribution is consistent with MOND.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxy NGC 3923 is surrounded by numerous stellar shells that are concentric arcs centered on the galactic core. They are very likely a result of a minor merger and they consist of stars in nearly radial orbits. For a given potential, the shell radii at a given time after the merger can be calculated and compared to observations.

hq87 claimed that the shell distribution of NGC 3923 contradicted MOND, but milgsh found several substantial insufficiencies in their work. We test whether the observed shell distribution in NGC 3923 is consistent with MOND using the current observational knowledge of the shell number and positions and of the host galaxy surface brightness profile, which supersede the data available in the 1980s when the last (and negative) tests of MOND viability were performed on NGC 3923. Using the 3.6 m bandpass image of NGC 3923 from the Spitzer space telescope we construct the mass profile of the galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 3923 is identified as the prototype type I shell galaxy, with mapped interleaving shells, inner shells revealed by HST, and unusually high dust mass in a large patch.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923 This is probably the most studied shell galaxy and the prototype of the Type I shell class, where shells are placed in interleaving order along the major axis. prieur , using ground based CCD data and photographic plates, mapped the whole shell system.

The HST imaging has revealed shells in the inner two kpc for two of the type I shell galaxies: NGC 3923: = 8 = 1.8 kpc (at an assumed distance of 20.0 Mpc) and NGC 5982: = 8 = 1.7 kpc (at an assumed distance of 41.9 Mpc).

While the dust content of NGC 1344 and NGC 5982 is quite low (several time 10^2 M_ , Table 4) and corresponds to such a scenario, the dust mass of NGC 3923 is an order of magnitude higher. Most (80 ) of the visible dust in NGC 3923 belongs to a large patch, visible in the NE direction at 50 arcsec from the centre (see Figure D.1). This patch was shown to be part of NGC 3923 and also emits small amounts of H + [ NII ] pence .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3923 as a system with multiple UCDs that likely formed as giant globular clusters, extending its blue globular-cluster sequence and matching the bright end of its GCLF.

quotes.quote: Using these clues we find that the UCDs of NGC3923 are almost certainly GGCs: NGC3923 has multiple UCDs (at least 2, with 1 more highly likely). As in Cen A (see Fig. fig:1 and ) several NGC3923 objects previously classified as GCs smoothly transition between GC-like and UCD-like mass-size behaviour. The UCDs smoothly extend the colour-magnitude relation of the blue GCs. All three possible UCDs have long apparent dynamical friction decay timescales ( > 7 Gyr). The UCDs are all consistent with the bright end of the GC luminosity function (GCLF, see Fig. fig:1 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3923 has a typical bimodal globular-cluster color distribution, a high specific frequency, and a shallow cluster density profile compared with its galaxy light.

quotes.quote: We find that the colour distributions for NGC 3923 and NGC 5982 have a bimodal form typical of bright ellipticals, with peaks near V-I=0.92 0.04 and V-I=1.18 0.06 .
As already known, in NGC3923, which has a high S_N of 5.6, the radial density distribution is more shallower than the diffuse galaxy light.

NGC 3923 and NGC 5982 have been studied before. NGC 3923 has been observed from the ground in the Washington system ( ). Like us, they found a bimodal distribution, which, converting their data into V-I ( ) and correcting to our extinction scale, give colours of 1.01 ( 0.05 ) and 1.21 ( 0.05 ), an offset of +0.06 with respect to our data.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3923 as a nearby early-type galaxy test case, showing that fringe-pattern correction supports meaningful VIMOS-IFU kinematic and line-strength analysis.

quotes.quote: Here we only show our results for the galaxy NGC 3923, which are used to illustrate our correction method.
In Fig. fig:linespectra , we therefore zoomed into the spectral range of two absorption features, H and Mg b , used for the line-strength analysis of the stellar populations in NGC 3923.

Our proposed empirical method for minimizing the effects of the fringe-like pattern enables a meaningful kinematical and line-strength analysis for nearby early-type galaxies as demonstrated for the case of NGC 3923. The combination of several exposures into a combined cube also mitigates the fringe-like pattern, although, our newly proposed method working on individual exposures before combination, provides better results.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3923 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This an E4-5 galaxy in a group of 8 galaxies 2006MNRAS.370.1223B . We detect a cylindrical velocity field along an intermediate direction between the minor and the major axes, which rotate at 75 . The velocity dispersion is constant around 235 . Long-slit kinematics from 1998MNRAS.294..182C inside 1 shows that NGC 3923 does not rotate significantly along the photometric major axis for R 0.5 , but it displays a weak rotation ( 20 ) along the photometric minor axis.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3923 as a nearby elliptical benchmark, noting its shell morphology, normal X-ray properties, old stellar population, and smaller effective radius than the successful AGN-heated model.

quotes.quote: In addition, Fig. \ cmr-fig demonstrates that the colour and magnitude for Model 2 are consistent with the CMR of the Coma cluster ellipticals and roughly reproduce those for NGC 3923 NGC 3923 is known as a shell elliptical. However, their X-ray properties seem to be normal, and old stellar population is dominant e.g. mp04 . Note that the effective radius of NGC 3923 ( R_ e ,B 6.6 kpc) is smaller than that for Model 2, which might affect the comparison of the X-ray properties, because we adopted the aperture radius of 4 R_ e ,B .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3923 as an E4 early-type central galaxy with shell structures, regular X-ray surface brightness, and point-source complications.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923, z=0.0069 The central galaxy of this group, NGC 3923, is a nearby early-type galaxy classified as E4 (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991). This galaxy has numerous shell structures (Malin Carter 1983) indicating past merger activity but shows no signs of an AGN. The X-ray surface brightness of this group has a regular appearance and the X-ray emission can be traced to about 5^ . This group shows the presence of numerous point sources which somewhat complicates the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3923 as a large early-type galaxy with oval VLA radio morphology, possible ghost X-ray cavities, shells, dust, and central H emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 3923* The biggest early-type galaxy, NGC 3923, located in the optical group (LGG 255) with only galaxy-scale X-ray halo has an oval-shaped radio morphology in our new VLA A configuration data (Figure fig:chandra_vla_ps_b a), while the archival C and CD configuration data show no radio emission. This source was observed in the 5 GHz survey done by . Chandra data have shown potential X-ray surface brightness decrements in the form of `ghost' cavities without corresponding radio emission from radio jets and lobes in this giant elliptical galaxy (Plšek et al.

sources.summary: The paper explains that NGC3923 lacks IFS data, has negligible central rotation from slit observations, and is analyzed using planetary-nebula kinematics because its angular momentum comes from the outskirts.

quotes.quote: For 2/32 ePN.S ETGs, that is NGC3923 and NGC4742, IFS kinematic data are not available. Major and minor axis slit data from show that NGC3923 has negligible rotation in the center, therefore most of the contribution to its sAM comes from the outskirts. Therefore for NGC3923 we only use the PN kinematics.

gmw_00000672.name: NGC4546
gmw_00000672.overview: NGC 4546 is a field lenticular galaxy [arXiv:2002.02765] hosting a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.10070] and a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2005.13697]. The system features asymmetric central dust [arXiv:2002.02765], a counter-rotating gas disk [arXiv:2210.10070], and a complex globular cluster population likely shaped by a past interaction [arXiv:2002.02765].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4546 as a field lenticular with four light components, asymmetric central dust, complex globular-cluster subpopulations, an unusually high GC count, and evidence for recent interaction.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric study of the field lenticular galaxy NGC 4546 using Gemini/GMOS imaging in g''i'z' . We perform a 2D image decomposition of the surface brightness distribution of the galaxy using galfit , finding that four components adequately describe it. The subtraction of this model from our images and the construction of a colour map allow us to examine in great detail the asymmetric dust structures around the galactic centre. In addition, we perform a detailed analysis of the globular cluster (GC) system of NGC 4546. Using a Gaussian Mixture Model algorithm in the colour-colour plane we detected hints of multiple groups of GC candidates: the classic blue and red subpopulations, a group with intermediate colours that present a concentrated spatial distribution towards the galaxy, and an additional group towards the red end of the colour distribution.

We estimate a total GC population for NGC 4546 of 390 60 members and specific frequency S_N=3.3 0.7 , which is relatively high compared to the typical value for galaxies of similar masses and environment.
