We suggest that the unusual GC population substructures were possibly formed during the interaction that led to the formation of the young ultra-compact dwarf (NGC 4546-UCD1) found in this system. Finally, we estimate the distance modulus of NGC 4546 by analyzing its luminosity function, resulting in (m-M)=30.75 0.12 mag (14.1 Mpc).

sources.summary: The paper uses NIFS and HST data with JAM modeling to measure NGC 4546's SMBH mass, mass-to-light ratio, anisotropy, and central velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: In this work, we used new AO assisted Near-Infrared Integral Field Spectrometer (NIFS) observations and also photometric data from the Hubble Space Telescope Legacy Archive of the galaxy NGC 4546 in order to determine its SMBH mass. To do this, we applied the Jeans Anisotropic Modelling (JAM) method to fit the average second velocity moment in the line of sight ( v^2_ los ) of the stellar structure. In addition, we also obtained (M/L)_ TOT and the classical anisotropy parameter _z =1--( _z / _R ) ^2 for this object within a field of view of 200 200 pc ^2 .

Using JAM, the best-fitting model for v^2_ los of the stellar structure was obtained with (M/L)_ TOT = 4.34 0.07 (Johnson's R band), M_ BH = (2.56 0.16) 10 ^8 M _ and _z = --0.015 0.03 (3 confidence level). With these results, we found that NGC 4546 follows the M_ BH relation. We also measured the central velocity dispersion within a radius of 1 arcsec of this object as _c = 241 2 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes ionized gas in the inner 200 pc of NGC 4546, finding AGN-like nuclear ionization, a counter-rotating gas disk, non-circular motions, and weak outflow feedback.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the ionised gas distribution and kinematics in the inner 200 pc of NGC 4546, host of a Low Luminosity Active Galactic Nucleus (LLAGN). Using GMOS IFU observations, with a spectral coverage of 4736 6806 and an angular resolution of 0.7 arcsec, we confirm that the nuclear emission is consistent with photoionisation by an AGN, while the gas in the circumnuclear region may be ionised by hot low-mass evolved stars. The gas kinematics in the central region of NGC 4546 presents three components: (i) a disc with major axis oriented along a position angle of 43^ 3^ , counter rotating relative to the stellar disc; (ii) non-circular motions, evidenced by residual velocities of up to 60 km s ^ 1 , likely associated to a previous capture of a dwarf satellite by NGC 4546; and (iii) nuclear outflows in ionised gas, identified as a broad component ( 320 km s ^ 1 ) in the line profiles, with a mass outflow rate of M _ out = 0.3 0.1 M _ yr ^ −1 and a total mass of M_ out = (9.2 0.8) 10^3 M _ in ionised gas, corresponding to less than 3 per cent of the total mass of ionised gas in the inner 200 pc of NGC 4546.

We used the AGN bolometric luminosity estimated by for the NGC 4546 galaxy by using the relation between L_ bol and [ O iii ] luminosity ( L_ bol / L_ O III 584 ). found a value of order of L_ bol 8.7 10^ 42 erg s ^ −1 that gives us a kinetic efficiency (defined as the ratio between the kinetic power of the outflow and the AGN bolometric luminosity) of about 0.1 per cent. This value is much smaller than outflow coupling efficiencies required for AGN winds being efficient in suppressing star formation in the host galaxy, of at least 0.5 per cent Hopkins_Elvis_2010,Harrison_2018 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the UCD of NGC4546 as a young stripped nucleus from a minor merger, citing its youth, short dynamical-friction time, overluminosity, and counterrotating gas.

quotes.quote: The UCD of NGC4546 is almost certainly a stripped nucleus: This UCD is young ( 3 Gyr old), whereas NGC4546 has a uniformly old ( 10 Gyr) stellar population. It does not seem to be associated with an equivalently young GC population. Its apparent dynamical friction decay timescale is short ( 0.5 Gyr). It is not explainable as the bright extension of the GCLF of NGC4546 (see Fig. fig:1 ). Even when the UCD has aged to 13 Gyr it will be significantly overluminous relative to the expectations of a model where it is the most luminous member of NGC4546's GC system. Both the UCD and the gas of NGC4546 counterrotate relative to the stellar disk, suggesting a common external origin for them both.

sources.summary: The paper reports a nuclear velocity-dispersion peak in NGC 4546, aligned with its kinematic center and AGN, and interprets the elongated peak as unresolved stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: The nuclear peaks detected in ESO 208 G-21, IC 1459, IC 5181, and NGC 4546 should correspond, in part, to unresolved stellar rotation. This interpretation is not new at all (see e.g., 1987IAUS..127...17K,1988ApJ...327L..55F ). First, the peaks of velocity dispersion agree, within the errors, with the positions of the kinematic centre and also with the positions of the AGNs of these four objects. Also, note that in IC 1459, IC 5181 and NGC 4546, the shape of the peaks are elongated perpendicularly to the direction of the rotation. The 1D profiles confirm this statement, since the velocity dispersion decreases slowly along this direction. A possible interpretation for this characteristic is that it is caused by a stellar structure in rotation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4546 as an AGN candidate from EW, with diagnostic ratios consistent with dusty AGN photoionization and fast shock models.

quotes.quote: If EW( ) is also used as a proxy for nuclear activity 2011MNRAS.413.1687C , then the galaxies NGC 1533 and NGC 4546 also have AGNs, since both have EW( ) > 6 .

In the / versus / diagram, one may see that most of the type 1 objects and three nuclei without a broad component (NGC 1316, NGC 4546 and NGC 6868) have line ratios that may be only explained by the dusty AGN models.
However, the / versus / diagram reveals that six objects (NGC 1411, NGC 1553, NGC 1052, NGC 4958, NGC 4546 and NGC 2217) have / ratios that require fast shock models.

gmw_00000673.name: XID 5230
gmw_00000673.overview: XID 5230 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.32 [arXiv:1111.2447]. Its individual EPIC pn spectrum displays a clear line feature at an observed energy of 3.00 keV, which corresponds to 6.95 keV in the rest-frame of the galaxy [arXiv:1111.2447].

sources.summary: The paper singles out XID 5230 as a z=1.32 galaxy whose observed EPIC pn line could dominate the high-Eddington-ratio stack, though the remaining sources still show high-ionization Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: Prior inspection of individual spectra of the brightest sources in this subset shows that a single source, XID 5230 might dominate the line feature. The EPIC pn spectrum of this source, as observed, shows a clear line feature at an observed energy of 3.00 0.03 keV, which corresponds to 6.95 keV in the rest-frame of this galaxy ( z=1.32 ). Therefore this source was excluded from the stack (i.e. the spectrum in Fig. 10 was constructed excluding XID 5230). However, despite the exclusion of XID 5230, the stacked spectrum of the remainder of the sources still shows a line feature at 6.89^ +0.02 _ 0.03 keV with EW =0.19 0.10 keV. The line flux is comparable ( 75 ) to that solely from XID 5230.

gmw_00000674.name: UGC10667
gmw_00000674.overview: UGC10667 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2309.11340] that has been identified as a candidate host for the fast radio burst FRB 20190425A [arXiv:2212.00201]. The galaxy exhibits a modest star formation rate [arXiv:2309.11340], contains extended radio emission [arXiv:2212.00954], and is dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:2309.11340].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC10667 as the proposed FRB190425 host, describing it as a spiral galaxy and reporting PS1 and radio non-detections of counterpart emission.

quotes.quote: find that UGC10667 is a spiral galaxy with a modest star formation rate and luminosity dominated by an old stellar population. They also searched for transient radio emission in and around the galaxy at 2.5 yrs post-burst that could be associated with either the \ or \ and found no convincing radio transient emission in VLA 6 Ghz data taken in September and October 2021.

In the context of FRB190425 and its most likely host galaxy \ (RA= , DEC= ), we covered the position of this galaxy with PS1 within a few hours of both the FRB and the GW signals. A single 45 sec band image was taken at 58598.5957604, which is \ after GW190425 merger time. The image is of good quality, taken at an airmass of 1.04 and with image FWHM of 0.97 . The image is shown in Figure fig:UGC10667_PS1 along with the PS1 3 reference image 2016arXiv161205560C and the difference image created from subtracting the latter from the target image. There is no transient source visible in the PS1 images either in or around .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes UGC10667 as the probable FRB host, detecting extended radio emission and using optical spectra to examine emission-line regions, star formation, and gas-phase metallicity.

quotes.quote: Targeted VLA observations We obtained radio observations centered on UGC10667 with the VLA on 2021 September 9 and 2021 October 16 (program ID: 21B-343, PI: Goodwin). Since the archival observations showed no radio detection, our initial observation on 2021 September 9 was taken at 4--8 GHz (C-band) to search for any radio emission from a possible remnant or outflow that may have been produced, as well as any diffuse radio emission from star formation in the galaxy. The galaxy was detected in our observation at 4--8 GHz, motivating the follow-up observation on 2021 October 16 at 1--12 GHz (L-, S-, C-, and X-band with central frequencies 1.5 GHz, 3 GHz, 6 GHz, and 10 GHz) to characterise the radio spectrum of the galaxy and search for any transient emission associated with the event.

To study the processes that give rise to the emission lines present in the spectrum of UGC10667, we select four regions of interest (ROI) in the galaxy (Fig. fig:emissionmap ) representing regions of the disc (1, 3 and 4) and nucleus (2). Integrated spectra are extracted from each of these apertures (central panel of Fig. fig:emissionmap , with the inset spectra showing the strong lines in the red and blue regions of the optical spectrum for each region). We also include the SDSS spectrum used for the full-spectrum fitting procedure in the analysis, which integrates light from a circular aperture with a diameter of 3 arcsec .

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 10667 as the only candidate host within the FRB localization redshift limit, noting its offset, redshift consistency with GW190425/FRB 20190425A, and low expected background galaxy count.

quotes.quote: We search for a host galaxy inside the reported error ellipse of FRB 20190425A's central localization (see Methods). We identify one candidate in the NASA Extragalactic Database (NED) The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is funded by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and operated by the California Institute of Technology. within a redshift range 0.001 < z < 0.08 consistent with the BNS merger redshift range: UGC 10667 (Figure fig:host-assoc ). This galaxy has a redshift consistent with the BNS merger and the FRB DM. UGC 10667 has an offset of 5.05 arcminutes from the optimal FRB location and a redshift of z=0.03136 0.00009 . The expected number of galaxies within the search volume is N_ gals 0.173 (see Methods) assuming a galaxy density of _ gals =2.35 10^ 3 Mpc ^ 3 and a maximum luminosity distance of d_ L = 255.85 Mpc .

gmw_00000675.name: Q1228+3128
gmw_00000675.overview: Q1228+3128 is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of z=2.2218 [arXiv:2111.06409, arXiv:2310.09445]. It is embedded in a bright Lyman-alpha nebula and hosts a massive molecular outflow aligned with an inner radio jet [arXiv:2111.06409], alongside an extended molecular gas reservoir spanning over 100 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2111.06409, arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.summary: The paper presents Q1228+3128 as a z=2.2218 radio-loud QSO with a massive molecular outflow, extended 100 kpc CGM gas, and jet-linked cooling or enrichment.

quotes.quote: As part of the SUPERCOLD-CGM survey (Survey of Protocluster ELANe Revealing CO/ \ in the Ly Detected CGM), we present the radio-loud QSO Q1228+3128 at z=2.2218 , which is embedded in an enormous Ly nebula. ALMA+ACA observations of CO(4-3) reveal both a massive molecular outflow, and a more extended molecular gas reservoir across 100 kpc in the CGM each containing a mass of M _ H2 4 5 10 ^ 10 M _ . The outflow and molecular CGM are aligned spatially, along the direction of an inner radio jet. After re-analysis of Ly data of Q1228+3128 from the Keck Cosmic Web Imager, we found that the velocity of the extended CO agrees with the redshift derived from the Ly nebula and the bulk velocity of the massive outflow. We propose a scenario where the radio source in Q1228+3128 is driving the molecular outflow and perhaps also enriching or cooling the CGM.

Here we present observations sensitive to low-surface-brightness emission of molecular gas, CO(4-3), of Q1228+3128 at z=2.2218 . Q1228+3128 is the most extended and brightest Ly nebula in our sample of ELANe.

We observed Q1228+3128 (12:28:24.97, 31:28:37.70) at z=2.2218 for 1.5 hrs on-source during ALMA cycle-7 on 20 and 23 Nov 2019 and 4 Jan 2020 with a compact configuration(C43-2) of the 12m Array (51 antennas), which baselines range from 15 to 314m, as well as for 12.4 hrs between Oct 2019 \ Mar 2020 with the ACA 7m Array (12 antennas).

sources.summary: The paper uses Ton 618 as an extremely massive SMBH system, adopting its mass and spin lower limit and showing its contribution to light-boson exclusion regions.

quotes.quote: There are some even more massive black holes. OJ 287 has the dynamically measured mass M_ bh =(1.8348 0.0008) 10^ 10 M _ and spin a_ * =0.381 0.004 . The masses of Ton 618 and SDSS J140821.67+025733.2 are estimated to be 6.6 10^ 10 M _ and 1.96 10^ 11 M _ , respectively. The allowed parameter space of the BH mass and spin within the thin disc accretion model has been examined in Ref. , giving lower limits of the spin of about 0.6 for Ton 618 and 0.97 for SDSS J140821.67+025733.2. These SMBHs will also be included in this study.

The orange (blue) bands are those for the most massive SMBHs OJ 287, Ton 618, and SDSS J140821.67+025733.2. Our results extend remarkably the constraints compared with earlier approaches . Panel (b) of Fig. scalar_vector summarizes the combined exclusion mass regions for all the SMBHs used in this work. We can see that the mass range of 10^ 22 10^ 17 eV can be effectively constrained by these sources, particularly for the vector boson scenario due to its faster superradiance rate compared with the scalar scenario (see Eq. ( eq:4 ) and Eq. ( eq:5 )).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q1228+3128 as a radio-loud QSO whose very high ALMA continuum SNR caused dynamic-range limits, requiring self-calibration and exclusion from the stacking analysis.

quotes.quote: The object Q1228+3128 (previously identified as a radio loud QSO in Li2021 ) was detected with very high ( 307 ) signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in the ALMA continuum observations such that the image was dynamic range limited and benefited from self-calibration. To self-calibrate we use the central source to correct for changes in phase. We solved for phase in intervals of scan length following ALMA NAASC recommended self-calibration procedure ( Brogan2018 ). However, even with self-calibration we determined the observation to not have enough dynamic range to detect the Jy level signal of the tSZE. Q1228+3128 was therefore excluded from the analyses.

Only one of the quasars (Q1228+3128, which was not included in our stacking analysis) is currently radio loud, however there could be intermittent jet activity e.g. 2020ApJ...905...74N and it has been show that non radio-loud quasars can still have radio mode feedback that effect the QSO environment on scales of 10-100 kpc ( VillarMartin2021 ).

sources.summary: Q1228+3128 has the most extended CO reservoir and brightest continuum in the sample, is the only radio-detected quasar, and shows evidence for massive outflow.

quotes.quote: Q1228+3128 : This object reveals the most extended CO reservoir and brightest continuum emission from the QSO in our ALMA observations. In addition, it is the only radio-detected quasar among our sample of 10 z 2 QSOs. Fig. fig:spectrum1 reveals an extended reservoir of CO spreading more than 100 kpc in NE direction. The tapered spectrum of the QSO was fitted by a double Gaussian, a narrow component to fit the quiescent gas in the QSO, and a broad component which is missing in the untapered spectrum. The broad component has FWHM = 1166 km s ^ 1 which is significantly larger than what is typical for high- z QSOs and Sub-Millimeter Galaxies Carilli_Walter2013 and could suggest the presence of a massive outflow Li2021 .

gmw_00000676.name: RBS 0679
gmw_00000676.overview: RBS 0679 is a point-like radio source and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.273 [arXiv:1410.1709]. It is classified as a BL Lacertae object that exhibits variable flux and spectral index characteristics in the GeV energy range [arXiv:1410.1709].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RBS 0679 as a redshift 0.273 BL Lac source detected by Fermi at very high gamma-ray energies, with variable GeV behavior and H.E.S.S. limits.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) detection of Very High Energy (VHE; E_ >100 GeV) ray emission from the BL Lac object RBS 0679. 5.3 years of LAT observations revealed the presence of three VHE photon events within 0 ^ .1 of RBS 0679, with a subsequent unbinned likelihood analysis finding RBS 0679 to be a source of VHE photons at 6.9 standard deviations ( ). An unbinned likelihood analysis of the 0.1-100 GeV data, binned in 28-day periods, finds both the flux and spectral index to be variable, with a `softer-when-brighter' trend in the global ray characteristics.

RBS 0679, also known as BZB J0543-5532, is a point like radio source, at a redshift of z =0.273 ( redshift ). Detected as a bright X-ray source with the ROSAT satellite ( xray ), RBS 0679 has been optically identified as a BL Lac object ( opt ). Discovered as a source of rays in Fermi LAT observations ( gamma ), RBS 0679 is included in both the 1 and 2 year LAT AGN catalogues (1LAC, ; 2LAC ), as well as the recent E_ >10 GeV LAT catalogue ( hecat ).

gmw_00000677.name: NGC 3959
gmw_00000677.overview: NGC 3959 is a galaxy that hosts two counter-rotating stellar disks of comparable size [arXiv:1410.1888]. Its younger, more metal-poor secondary stellar component rotates in the same direction as the galaxy's ionized gas [arXiv:1410.1888].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3959 among galaxies with comparable-size counter-rotating stellar disks, finding a younger, more metal-poor secondary component aligned with the ionized gas.

quotes.quote: Here, we describe the results obtained for a sample of galaxies that host counter-rotating stellar disks of comparable size: NGC 3959, NGC 4183, NGC 4550, and NGC 5719. Observations were obtained with the VIMOS integral field unit at the VLT (Chile), except for NGC 4138, which was observed with the B C spectrograph at the 1.22-m telescope at Padova University (Italy). In all the studied galaxies, we detect the presence of an extended secondary stellar component, which is counter-rotating with respect the more luminous and massive stellar component. In addition, the secondary component is rotating along the same direction as the ionized gas. The measurements of the equivalent width of the absorption line features (H , Mgb, Fe5270, and Fe5335) reveal that the secondary stellar component is always younger than the main stellar component, and it is more metal poor.

gmw_00000678.name: NGC 4183
gmw_00000678.overview: NGC 4183 is a galaxy that hosts two counter-rotating stellar disks of comparable size [arXiv:1410.1888]. Its secondary stellar component is younger and more metal-poor than the main disk, and it rotates in the same direction as the galaxy's ionized gas [arXiv:1410.1888].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4183 among galaxies with comparable-size counter-rotating stellar disks, finding a younger, more metal-poor secondary component aligned with the ionized gas.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4183 is already fit well by MOND, but adding an EFE lowers chi-squared further and matches the field from NGC 4138.

quotes.quote: (13) NGC 4183 : The overall form of the MOND RC without an EFE agrees well with the observed data with a reduced ^2 value of 1.01. The rotation curve fit is improved by the addition of an EFE (with the best-fitted value of a_e=0.265^ +0.110 _ 0.130 a_0 for the external filed) and leads to a lower ^2 value of 0.27. NGC 4138 n4138-md, n4138-dm, n3521-lu is the most important galaxy which can gravitationally affect NGC 4183 n2998-dm, n2998-md . With a luminosity of L_V=1.93 10^ 10 L_ and angular distance of 0.4217^o to NGC 4183, this galaxy produces an external field of 3.29 10^ 3 a_0 (with the maximum and minim values of 9.64 10^ 2 a_0 and 4.27 10^ 4 a_0 , respectively) which is compatible with theoretical prediction within the 95 confidence interval.

gmw_00000679.name: NGC 4550
gmw_00000679.overview: NGC 4550 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1210.0535, arXiv:2107.02226] classified as a slow rotator [arXiv:astro-ph/0703533, arXiv:2311.13747] that contains two co-spatial, counter-rotating stellar disks of similar size and mass [arXiv:1403.1263, arXiv:2107.02226]. The secondary stellar disk is younger [arXiv:1210.0535, arXiv:1410.1888] and rotates in the same direction as the galaxy's ionized gas [arXiv:1410.1888, arXiv:2107.02226].

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 4550 into counter-rotating stellar discs, finding a younger secondary disc linked to gas and favoring unusual gas accretion over separatrix crossing.

quotes.quote: In order to try and understand its origins, we present high-quality long-slit spectral observations of the counter-rotating stellar discs in the strange S0 galaxy NGC 4550. We kinematically decompose the spectra into two counter-rotating stellar components (plus a gaseous component), in order to study both their kinematics and their populations. The derived kinematics largely confirm what was known previously about the stellar discs, but trace them to larger radii with smaller errors; the fitted gaseous component allows us to trace the hydrogen emission lines for the first time, which are found to follow the same rather strange kinematics previously seen in the [ OIII ] line. Analysis of the populations of the two separate stellar components shows that the secondary disc has a significantly younger mean age than the primary disc, consistent with later star formation from the associated gaseous material.

With regret, we are therefore forced to abandon the elegance of the separatrix-crossing model entirely, and conclude that NGC 4550 formed through one of the other scenarios. The ages inferred in Section sec:Stellar Populations then tell us something about the process. If formed through a carefully-controlled merger of fully-formed galaxies, these ages just reflect the ages of the progenitors. In the gas-rich accretion scenario, which now seems more natural, the 2.5 Gyr age of the secondary disc tells us how long ago this gas was accreted, with the residue of this accreted gaseous material still rotating along with this component, albeit in a somewhat non-circular manner.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4550 as an anomalous slow rotator dominated by tangential dispersion, with major-axis kinematic signatures naturally explained by two counterrotating stellar components.

quotes.quote: However two galaxies, NGC 4473 and NGC 4550, stand out for being dominated by tangential dispersion. Understanding what makes some object special is key to understand the normal galaxies. In fig:models we show the data versus model comparison for the galaxy NGC 4550, while the same comparison for NGC 4473 was shown in Fig. 2 of cmd05 . Along the major axis both galaxies show a peculiar decrease in the V field and a corresponding increase in the field.

As already pointed out by rub92 and rix92 , for the case of NGC 4550, a natural explanation for this behaviour is the presence of two counterrotating stellar components, as indicated by the observed double-peaked line profiles. In fig:integral_space this explanation is confirmed from the solution of the Schwarzschild models, which shows two major kinematically distinct stellar components, rotating in opposite directions.

Remarkably, only two galaxies fall significantly below the magenta line for 0.3 . These are the fast-rotator NGC 4473 (marginally) and the special slow-rotator NGC 4550. This was expected from the fact that these two galaxies are dominated by tangential anisotropy ( fig:anisotropy ), due to the presence of two counterrotating disks ( fig:integral_space ).

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4550 among lower-mass star-forming early types, emphasizing prominent 8 micron structure, highly asymmetric molecular gas, centralized star formation, and a roughly 6 Gyr young SSP age.

quotes.quote: Widespread Star-Forming Fast Rotators In Figure sfrmstar , the \ galaxies (NGC 3032, NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150, NGC 4550) are seen to be those star-forming early-type galaxies with lower stellar masses. Given the comparable molecular content of all star-forming galaxies in our sample (see Table tbl:sfr ), the \ galaxies are consequently also characterized by higher gas fractions and specific star formation rates. As a result, the current star formation event dominates the single stellar population (SSP) ages, which are measured to be young throughout these systems ( < 1 3 Gyr; Kuntschner et al. in prep ). This is consistent with the location of the \ galaxies in Figure ks ; these systems have molecular gas and star formation surface densities similar to those found in late-type galaxies undergoing global star formation.

Lower SFRs in a \ galaxy also correlates with an increasingly irregular star formation and gas distribution, from a regular, disk-like morphology (NGC 3032) to disturbed ionized and molecular gas distributions (NGC 3489; Figure Main-SF and Crocker et al. in prep ) to highly asymmetric molecular gas distributions (NGC 4550; Cro+09 ). These increasingly irregular morphologies additionally correspond to more centralized star formation at lower SFR (compare NGC 3032, NGC 3156, and NGC 4550 in Figure profiles a ). In the absence of a replenishing gas reservoir, \ galaxies will experience declining SFRs, causing them to move to the left in Figure sfrmstar ; the system with the most intense star formation (NGC 3032) will therefore be expected to eventually move towards the other \ galaxies. Likewise, there is evidence that the \ galaxies with lower SFRs previously had more intense star formation events that extended to larger radii, implying earlier positions to the right of their current location in Figure sfrmstar . In particular, the young SSP ages (1 3 Gyr in NGC 3156, NGC 3489, NGC 4150; 6 Gyr in NGC 4550; Kuntschner et al. in prep ) found throughout these galaxies, beyond the star-forming regions observed in PAH emission, indicate that star formation activity occurred out to larger radii in the past than in the present.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4550 as a counter-rotating system likely shaped by an older gravitational torque, with nearby NGC 4552 and velocity offsets as observables.

quotes.quote: 2007A&A...463..883V suggest that NGC 5719 will eventually evolve into a galaxy like NGC 4550 in which half the stars have been found to be counter-rotating 1992ApJ...394L...9R with the counter-rotating stars younger at 2.5 Gyr and the main stellar disk being older at 11 Gyr 2013MNRAS.428.1296J . Retrograde motion in the ionized gas and half the stars in NGC 4550 has been explained by gas accretion 1992ApJ...394L...9R . We suggest that the origin of the counter-rotating matter would have been in a gravitational torque and similar to NGC 5719 - however the interaction would have happened more than 2.5 Gyr ago.

We examine NGC 4550 in terms of observables. A companion galaxy NGC 4552 is found to be located at a projected distance of 64 kpc from NGC 4550. A difference of 54 kms ^ 1 between the optical and radio heliocentric velocities is found for NGC 4550. The companion galaxy NGC 4552 is an elliptical which shows rotation in the ionized gas 2009APS..OSF.P1005K . No HI 2012MNRAS.422.1835S or molecular gas 2011MNRAS.414..940Y have been detected from either NGC 4550 or NGC 4552. Thus the torque acting on NGC 4550 has resulted in a counter-rotating component and a kinematic offset between its gas and stellar disks. No signature on the companion galaxy could be found but more data on NGC 4552 are required to search for signatures of this interaction on its morphology or kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4550 as a showcase double-peaked LOSVD galaxy, comparing extracted major-axis spectra with earlier results and highlighting regularization effects.

quotes.quote: The last case we analysed is NGC 4550, another classical showcase galaxy with prominent double-peaked LOSVD profiles. We have extracted LOSVDs from \ spectra emsellem04 . We performed our calculations in the wavelength range between 4800 5300 \ and a S/N=150 per spectral pixel. We analysed the results along the major axis of the galaxy at three of the locations presented in rw92 .

Since their data was not in electronic form, we digitised it using the WebPlotDigitiser https://automeris.io/WebPlotDigitizer tool WebPlotDigitizer and then fitted the LOSVDs with double Gaussian profiles for best reproduction. Figure fig:ngc4550 (top panel) shows the F555W/F814W color image from the Hubble Space Telescope.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4550 as a comparison LINER 2, noting an offset radio source, no Chandra nuclear detection, and no clear AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: One object (the LINER 2 NGC 4550) has a flat spectrum radio source at a position significantly offset from the optical nucleus. Source Detection An X-ray nucleus is seen in all the galaxies except for NGC 4550 and NGC 5866. Power Source of LINERs An X-ray nucleus is detected in all the objects except for NGC 4550 and NGC 5866.

On the other hand, NGC 4550, which does not possess a radio core, shows no evidence for the presence of an AGN and all the three possibilities discussed above are viable. NGC 4550 (L2) . --- This source is not detected with the present observation. If this source is indeed the nucleus, our non detection by ( F_ 0.1-2.4 keV <6 10^ 15 ) indicates time variability.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4550 among galaxies with comparable-size counter-rotating stellar disks, finding a younger, more metal-poor secondary component aligned with the ionized gas.

quotes.quote: Here, we describe the results obtained for a sample of galaxies that host counter-rotating stellar disks of comparable size: NGC 3959, NGC 4183, NGC 4550, and NGC 5719. Observations were obtained with the VIMOS integral field unit at the VLT (Chile), except for NGC 4138, which was observed with the B C spectrograph at the 1.22-m telescope at Padova University (Italy). In all the studied galaxies, we detect the presence of an extended secondary stellar component, which is counter-rotating with respect the more luminous and massive stellar component. In addition, the secondary component is rotating along the same direction as the ionized gas. The measurements of the equivalent width of the absorption line features (H , Mgb, Fe5270, and Fe5335) reveal that the secondary stellar component is always younger than the main stellar component, and it is more metal poor.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4550 as the known lenticular benchmark for counter-rotating stellar disks, confirming its double-peaked LOSVD and uniqueness among the observed S0 sample.

quotes.quote: Such a counter-rotating population was first discovered by Rubin et al. (1992) in the Virgo lenticular NGC 4550. This system was found to contain a disk in which half of the stars circulate in one direction while the other half orbit in the opposite direction (Rix et al. \ 1992).

As a guide to the eye, we first show the result for the galaxy NGC 4550 in which the signature of counter-rotating disks is clearly seen as a splitting of the line profile into two approximately equal strength peaks (Fig. fig-ngc4550 ). This figure demonstrates that our data and analysis technique are indeed capable of revealing counter-rotating components, in agreement with the double-gaussian analysis of this galaxy's spectra by Rix et al. (1992). Figure fig-losvds displays the stellar LOSVDs for the remainder of our sample of objects as derived from the UGD method. It is apparent from Fig. 1 that NGC 4550 is unique among the galaxies in our sample as none displays a comparable LOSVD.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4550 as an E7/S0 with two similar counter-rotating stellar disks, using it to test external accretion, mergers, and internal bar-dissolution scenarios.

quotes.quote: The E7/S0 NGC 4550 hosts two cospatial counter-rotating stellar disks, one of them is corotating with the gaseous disk Rubin+1992, Johnston+2013, Coccato+2013 . The two stellar disks have similar luminosities, sizes, and masses Rix+1992 although one is thicker than the other Cappellari+2007 .
An alternative to the external-origin scenarios has been proposed by Evans+Collett1994 for NGC 4550, and it involves the dissolution of a bar or a triaxial stellar halo.

On the contrary, the formation through a major galaxy merger cannot be completely ruled out for NGC 3593 and NGC 4550, which are both quite isolated and undisturbed galaxies Coccato+2011, Coccato+2013 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4550 as the classic E7/S0 counter-rotating disk galaxy, with co-spatial disks of similar size, luminosity, and mass but different thickness, one linked to ionized gas.

quotes.quote: The first detection of two large-scale counter-rotating stellar disks was done by by studying the shape of the absorption lines in the long-slit spectra of the E7/S0 galaxy NGC 4550. Its counter-rotating disks are co-spatial and have similar sizes, luminosities, and masses rixNGC4550Laboratory1992, johnstonDisentanglingStellarPopulations2013 , but different thickness cappellariSAURONProjectOrbital2007 . Moreover, one of them is associated with a disk of ionized gas rubinCospatialCounterrotatingStellar1992,sarziSAURONProjectIntegralfield2006,coccatoSpectroscopicEvidenceDistinct2013 .
These values are consistent with those measured at 1 r_ e by kuntschnerSAURONProjectXVII2010 for the most widely studied counter-rotating galaxy NGC 4550.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4550 with Keck/OSIRIS, finds central velocity-dispersion evidence for an SMBH, and notes it as a slow rotator challenging the axisymmetric Jeans models.

quotes.quote: We observed our other galaxy in the sample, NGC4550, using the Keck telescope. We used Optical Spectrograph and InfraRed Imager System OSIRIS, Keck .

Galaxies NGC 4458, 4379, 4550 and 4262 show an increased velocity dispersion near the centre of the galaxy, indicative of the presence of an SMBH. The JAM models only show this rising velocity dispersion if a SMBH is included, whereas the models without a SMBH have decreasing velocity dispersions towards the centre.
However, there are galaxies within our sample that are not fully axisymmetric as assumed by the Jeans models: NGC 4458 and 4550 are both slow rotators, and additionally, NGC 4458 is misaligned.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4550 as a galaxy with two similar-mass counter-rotating stellar disks, strong decoupling, and lower h4 outside five arcseconds along the major axis.

quotes.quote: This object has two counter-rotating stellar disks of similar mass ( Rix92 Rix92 ) which, at the spectral resolution of , produce a large region with a near zero mean velocity V and a dispersion increasing outwards along the major-axis. Note the extraordinary decoupling revealed by , the outer disk being counter-rotating with respect to the main body of the galaxy further away from the equatorial plane, also noticed by Afa02 . h_4 is significantly lower outside 5 \ along the major-axis.

gmw_00000680.name: NGC 5719
gmw_00000680.overview: NGC 5719 is an interacting, early-type Sab galaxy with a skewed dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0611426, arXiv:1308.4406]. It features a secondary stellar disk and gas component that counter-rotate relative to its main stellar disk, likely accreted during ongoing tidal interactions with its companion galaxy, NGC 5713 [arXiv:1410.1888, arXiv:1308.4406].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5719 among counter-rotating disk galaxies and highlights its especially pronounced stellar-population differences and direct evidence for ongoing acquisition.

quotes.quote: Here, we describe the results obtained for a sample of galaxies that host counter-rotating stellar disks of comparable size: NGC 3959, NGC 4183, NGC 4550, and NGC 5719. Observations were obtained with the VIMOS integral field unit at the VLT (Chile), except for NGC 4138, which was observed with the B C spectrograph at the 1.22-m telescope at Padova University (Italy). In all the studied galaxies, we detect the presence of an extended secondary stellar component, which is counter-rotating with respect the more luminous and massive stellar component. In addition, the secondary component is rotating along the same direction as the ionized gas. The measurements of the equivalent width of the absorption line features (H , Mgb, Fe5270, and Fe5335) reveal that the secondary stellar component is always younger than the main stellar component, and it is more metal poor.
Such a difference in stellar population is particularly pronounced in NGC 5719 (Figure fig:5719 ).
In the case of NGC 5719 we have a direct of the on-going acquisition process Vergani+07 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5719 as a counter-rotating galaxy whose gas disk spin and H I axis were altered by torque from nearby NGC 5713.

quotes.quote: Large quantities of hydrogen has been detected from the galaxies and the gas has been found to be counter-rotating with respect to the main stellar disk in NGC 5719 2007A&A...463..883V . HI is also detected from between the galaxies 2007A&A...463..883V . The old stellar disk in NGC 5719 is in retrograde rotation with NGC 5713 while the counter-rotating gas disk and 20 of stars in NGC 5719 are in prograde rotation with NGC 5713. We suggest that the torque acting on NGC 5719 due to NGC 5713 has modified the spin angular momentum of NGC 5719 leading to a change in direction of rotation of the gas disk.

Moreover the HI major axis of NGC 5719 (PA= 121^ ) makes an angle of 25^ with the optical disk (PA= 96^ ) 2007A&A...463..883V . As an effect of the counter-torque on NGC 5713, we find that there is an offset of 65 kms ^ 1 in it optical and radio heliocentric velocities listed by Hyperleda. Thus, we infer that the torques have led to counter-rotation in NGC 5719 and change in its gas spin axis ie change in its spin angular momentum whereas a torque on NGC 5713 has led to a decoupling in the systemic motions of the gas and stellar disks. The age of stars in the counter-rotating disk of NGC 5719 is between 300 Myr and 700 Myr 2005ApJ...619L..91N,2011MNRAS.412L.113C . On the other hand the age of stars formed in the tidal features external to the galaxies is between 2 Myr and 200 Myr 2005ApJ...619L..91N .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5719 as an interacting edge-on Sab galaxy whose acquired gas and ionized hydrogen counter-rotate and appear to have formed a counter-rotating stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 5719, an almost edge-on Sab galaxy with a prominent skewed dust lane, shows a spectacular on-going interaction with its face-on Sbc companion NGC 5713. Observations of such interacting systems provide insight into the processes at work in assembling and reshaping galaxies. Studies were made of the distribution and kinematics of neutral hydrogen in the NGC 5719/13 galaxy pair and the ionised gas and stellar kinematics along the major axis of NGC 5719. Two \ tidal bridges that loop around NGC 5719 and connect to NGC 5713, and two \ tidal tails departing westward from NGC 5713 were detected.

The neutral and ionised hydrogen in the disc of NGC 5719 are counter-rotating with respect to the main stellar disc. The counter-rotating stellar disc contains about 20 of the stars in the system, and has the same radial extension as the main stellar disc. This is the first interacting system in which a counter-rotating stellar disc has been detected.

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 5719 as the first interacting disk galaxy with detected counter-rotation, tying its younger metal-varying counter-rotating population to interaction-fed gas.

quotes.quote: This has been recently done for the counter-rotating stellar disks of NGC 3593 Coccato+2013 , NGC 4550 Coccato+2013, Johnston+2013 , and NGC 5719 Coccato+2011 . In all of them, the counter-rotating stellar disk rotates in the same direction as the ionized gas, and it is less massive, younger, more metal poor, and more enhanced than the main stellar disk.

NGC 5719 is the first interacting disk galaxy in which counter-rotation has been detected (Fig. fig:n5719 , bottom panels). The age of counter-rotating stellar population ranges from 0.7 to 2.0 Gyr and metallicity changes from subsolar ( [ Z/ H ] 1.0 dex) in the outskirts to supersolar ( [ Z/ H ] 0.3 dex) in the center, where as the main stellar component has ages ranging from 2 to 13.5 Gyr and nearly solar metallicity. The youngest ages and highest metallicities are found in correspondence of the star forming regions. The enhancement of the counter-rotating component indicates a star formation history with a time-scale of 2 Gyr (Fig. fig:n5719 , middle panels).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5719 as a dust-lane early-type with CO emission and counter-rotating gas and stars, likely accreted through interaction with NGC 5713.

quotes.quote: The sample is dominated by red, dusty spirals such as NGC 2861, 3907B, 4030, 5478, 5690, 5740, CGCG 013-010, 018-077; but also contains blue low-surface-brightness disks (NGC 5496, 5584, 5691, 5705, UGC 09215), dust-lane early-types (NGC 5719, 5750), and the starburst/LIRG NGC 5713. We detect line emission in either \ or \ in most of the sample, including three of the four early-type galaxies (NGC 5719, 5750, SDP 4), as shown in Table tab:aperdata . NGC 5719 is noteworthy since its warped dust lane and counter-rotating gas and stellar disks indicate that dust and gas were accreted from an interaction with NGC 5713 Vergani2007,Coccato2011 . Our \ moment map shows that rotation of the molecular gas follows that of the counter-rotating disk and is therefore also likely to have been accreted.

gmw_00000681.name: NGC 3593
gmw_00000681.overview: NGC 3593 is a lenticular [arXiv:2110.08476] or early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1405.3873, arXiv:1403.1263] associated with the Leo Triplet [arXiv:2303.00763]. It hosts two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:1405.3873, arXiv:1402.5346], a circumnuclear molecular gas disk [arXiv:2110.08476], and a central starburst region [arXiv:2303.00763].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3593 as a counter-rotating early-type galaxy whose gas, dust, UV color, SED, morphology, and magnitude are best matched by an equal-halo encounter history.

quotes.quote: NGC 3593 This galaxy, classified as SA(s)0/a, is known to host two large counter-rotating stellar disks Bert96 together with a gaseous disk. It shows a chaotic pattern of dust patches throughout its main body. It is very rich in both neutral (HI) and molecular (H _2 ) gas. Their total masses are 5.1 10 ^8 M_ and 2.3 10 ^8 M_ , respectively Bet01 .

NGC 3593 is a very bright IR source and contains 5.6 10 ^5 M_ of dust Bet01 . The galaxy hosts an inner nuclear ring COM . suggested that an accretion event occurred between 2 and 3.6 Gyr ago, i.e., 1.6 Gyr after the formation of the main galaxy disk. We found that the galaxy has a FUV-NUV color equal to 1.83, and a NUV- = 4.5, placing it in the GV.

The global properties of this galaxy, i.e, its SED, morphology and B band absolute magnitude, match well with a galaxy encounter between two equal halos with total mass 8 10^ 11 M _ , semi major axis of 597.2 kpc and perpendicular spins.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 3593's SMBH with ALMA CO kinematics, describing its lenticular host, circumnuclear molecular gas disk, spiral/ring structure, cold molecular core, and nuclear star cluster.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We therefore adopt this centre (R.A. =11^ h 14^ m 37 1 , decl. =+12 49 05 6 , V_ sys =629 ) as the centre of NGC 3593.

quotes.quote: As part of the Measuring Black Holes in Below Milky Way-mass (M ^ ) galaxies (MBHBM ^ ) Project, we present a dynamical measurement of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) mass in the nearby lenticular galaxy NGC 3593, using cold molecular gas ^ 12 CO(2-1) emission observed at an angular resolution of 0 3 ( 10 pc) with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA). Our ALMA observations reveal a circumnuclear molecular gas disc (CND) elongated along the galaxy major axis and rotating around the SMBH. This CND has a relatively low velocity dispersion ( 10 ) and is morphologically complex, with clumps having higher integrated intensities and velocity dispersions ( 25 ). These clumps are distributed along the ridges of a two-arm/bi-symmetric spiral pattern surrounded by a larger ring-like structure (radius 10 or 350 pc).

Using dynamical modelling, the molecular gas kinematics allow us to infer a SMBH mass M_ BH =2.40_ 1.05 ^ +1.87 10^6 \ (only statistical uncertainties at the 3 level). We also detect a massive core of cold molecular gas (CMC) of mass M_ CMC =(5.4 1.2) 10^6 \ and effective (half-mass) radius r_ CMC,e =11.2 2.8 pc, co-spatial with a nuclear star cluster (NSC) of mass M_ NSC =(1.67 0.48) 10^7 \ and effective radius r_ NSC,e =5.0 1.0 pc (or 0 15 0 03 ). The mass profiles of the CMC and NSC are well described by S e rsic functions with indices 1-1.4 . Our \ and \ estimates for NGC 3593 agree well with the recently compiled - \ scaling relation. Although the M_ NSC uncertainty is twice the inferred M_ BH , the rapid central rise of the rotation velocities of the CND (as the radius decreases) clearly suggests a SMBH.

sources.summary: NGC 3593 is described as a Leo Triplet-associated starburst galaxy with overlapping halo geometry, HI-only structures excluded, and two assigned low-mass satellite candidates.

quotes.quote: NGC 3593 and 3627 (M66 Group) NGC 3593 and NGC 3627 (M66) have overlapping R_ 200 halos (Figure fig:virgo_field_sky_distribution ) and are at a derived three-dimensional distance of 547 kpc. NGC 3593 is frequently associated with the Leo Triplet (M66) Group where M66 is the primary. The other two spiral members of the Triplet, NGC 3623 (M65) and NGC 3628, are located at >10 Mpc, and more than 1 Mpc away from the primary and are not included in our host sample due to the HI sensitivity limitations ( sec:data ). Less than 1.5 Mpc from the Leo Ring, the Leo Triplet field also contains HI-only structures (see, e.g., S09) that are excluded from our dwarf galaxy satellite sample. NGC 3593 is a starburst galaxy with a relatively centered star-forming HII region hunter_star_1989 classified as SA(s)0/a.

Both satellite candidates shared between the virial halos of NGC 3593 and 3627, AGC 202256 and 210220 (IC 2684), are assigned to 3593 based on the lower values. They both have relatively high F_ d_ 3D among the satellite candidates, well-defined optical counterparts durbala_alfalfa-sdss_2020-1,muller_leo-i_2018 , and relatively low stellar masses M_ * < 10^ 7 M_ .

sources.summary: The review presents NGC 3593 as an Sa galaxy with a secondary counter-rotating stellar disk, gas corotation in the inner kpc, and uncertain merger or accretion origin.

quotes.quote: For example, the Sa NGC 3593 (Fig. fig:n3593 ) is composed by a small bulge, a main stellar disk which contains 80 of the stars ( 1.2 10^ 10 M _ ) and a secondary counter-rotating stellar disk Bertola+1996, Coccato+2013 . The latter dominates the kinematics in the inner kpc and corotates with the disk of ionized and molecular gas Corsini+1998, GarciaBurillo+2000 .

This has been recently done for the counter-rotating stellar disks of NGC 3593 Coccato+2013 , NGC 4550 Coccato+2013, Johnston+2013 , and NGC 5719 Coccato+2011 . In all of them, the counter-rotating stellar disk rotates in the same direction as the ionized gas, and it is less massive, younger, more metal poor, and more enhanced than the main stellar disk. These findings rule out an internal origin of the secondary stellar component and favor a scenario where it formed from gas accreted on retrograde orbits from the environment fueling an in situ outside-in rapid star formation.

On the contrary, the formation through a major galaxy merger cannot be completely ruled out for NGC 3593 and NGC 4550, which are both quite isolated and undisturbed galaxies Coccato+2011, Coccato+2013 .

sources.summary: The paper confirms two counter-rotating stellar disks in NGC 3593 and finds the secondary disk is less massive, co-rotates with the ionized gas, and has a younger, metal-poorer stellar population.

quotes.quote: We successfully separated the contribution of the ionized gas and the two stellar components from the observed spectrum, and confirm the presence of two counter-rotating stellar disks in NGC 3593.

The secondary disk is on average less luminous and less massive than the main stellar disk, and it rotates along the same direction as the ionized gas component. The two counter-rotating stellar components have different stellar populations. The secondary stellar disk is younger, more metal poor, and more enhanced than the main galaxy stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3593 to show reconstructed counter-rotating disk profiles, where a luminous but lower-mass-density secondary component dominates the inner light.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:surf_br we compare the reconstructed images and the surface brightness radial profiles of the counter-rotating disks observed in NGC 3593. The secondary components overshines the main stellar disk within 10'' . A single-component fit would reveal the presence of the secondary component only in the inner regions, by revealing the kinematic signature of a kinematically decoupled core (KDC, e.g. Bender88 ). The combination of the luminosity profile and stellar mass-to-light ratio, as inferred from the stellar population analysis, shows that the secondary component has a lower mass density radial profile than the main component, also in the inner regions where it dominates in light.

gmw_00000682.name: 3C 433
gmw_00000682.overview: The radio galaxy 3C 433 exhibits a hybrid morphology, featuring a Fanaroff-Riley class I jet on one side and a class II lobe on the other [arXiv:0901.0925]. Its active nucleus is obscured by a perpendicular dust lane [arXiv:0907.3478] and shows high near-infrared polarization likely caused by dichroic extinction [arXiv:0907.3478, arXiv:1407.2903].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 433 as a powerful radio galaxy, finding a near-infrared core, dust lane, strong extinction, high polarization, and likely dichroic extinction by a magnetized torus.

quotes.quote: We report the analysis of near-infrared imaging, polarimetric and spectroscopic observations of the powerful radio galaxy 3C 433 ( z=0.1016 ), obtained with the HST and UKIRT telescopes. The high spatial resolution of HST allows us to study the near-nuclear regions of the galaxy ( <1 kpc). In line with previous observations, we find that 3C 433 has an unresolved core source that is detected in all near-IR bands, but dominates over the host galaxy emission at 2.05 m. Our analysis reveals: (1) the presence of a dust lane aligned close to perpendicular (PA =70 5 ) to the inner radio jet axis (PA =-12 2 ); (2) a steep slope to the near-IR SED ( =5.8 0.1 ; F _ ^ ); (3) an apparent lack of broad permitted emission lines at near-IR wavelengths, in particular the absence of a broad Pa emission line; and (4) high intrinsic polarization for the unresolved core nuclear source ( 8.6 1 per cent), with an E-vector perpendicular (PA= 83.0 2.3 ) to the inner radio jet.

We have used HST and UKIRT observations to study 3C 433 at near-IR wavelengths, and have found several interesting features. The near-IR observations suggest that we are viewing the AGN directly through an obscuring dust structure perpendicular to the radio jets, or indirectly via scattering by a region close to the nucleus. On the other hand, the near-IR spectrum of the source shows a lack of broad lines. However, the estimated level of extinction ( 3< A _V<67 mag) and high intrinsic polarization of the nucleus ( 8.6 1 per cent) support the idea that the high polarization may be due to dichroic extinction by a magnetised torus structure in the nuclear region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 433 as a bright radio galaxy with near-IR core polarization, strong AGN contribution in the nuclear aperture, and evidence for dichroic extinction rather than polarized synchrotron contamination.

quotes.quote: A similar configuration is found in 3C 223.1 and 3C 433 at 2.2 m and 2.05 m, respectively: polarisation E-vectors perpendicular to the radio jet axis, in addition to dust lanes orientated perpendicular to the radio jets Antonucci:1990, Ramirez:2009 . This is consistent with a dichroic 1 Dichroic extinction is the selective attenuation of light passing through a medium that contains dust grains aligned by a magnetic field. or scattering origin for the polarisation, since such mechanisms can produce polarisation PA s that are perpendicular to the radio jet orientation. In contrast, the polarisation PA s for any synchrotron emission from the inner jets tend to be parallel to the radio jet orientation e.g. Lister:2001 .

The sources of error are the polarisation measurement itself, and the estimation of the starlight dilution. In most of the cases the AGN contributes < 20 per cent to the light in the nuclear aperture of 0.9 arcsec diameter (6 pixel radius). The starlight dilution is least in 3C 433 ( < 30 per cent). This source falls on the maximum dichroic efficiency curve in Fig. jones , providing evidence for a dichroic origin of the polarisation see Ramirez:2009 .
On the other hand, 3C 285 and 3C 433 are expected to have no contribution from a polarised non-thermal component, given that the synchrotron extrapolation falls below the de-reddened near-IR flux.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 433 as a hybrid-morphology radio galaxy with FR I and FR II structures, absorbed nuclear X-rays, soft diffuse emission, and hard X-ray extension from an inner jet knot.

quotes.quote: We present Chandra observations of the hybrid morphology radio sources 3C 433 and 4C 65.15, two members of the rare class of objects possessing an FR I jet on one side of the core and an FR II lobe on the other. The X-ray spectrum of 3C 433 shows intrinsic absorption (with a column density of N_ H 8 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ), such as is typical of FR II narrow-line radio galaxies. There is excess X-ray emission below 2 keV containing contributions from diffuse soft X-ray emission (likely hot gas with kT 1.2 keV) as well as from the nucleus. The core of 3C 433 is extended in hard X-rays, presumably due to X-ray emission from the inner-jet knot on the FR I side that is apparent in the radio map. It is possible that the X-ray emission from this inner-jet knot is absorbed by the dust known to be present in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 433 as the background radio galaxy whose southern lobe resolves H I absorption and whose host/environment show optical and H-alpha structures.

quotes.quote: Optical narrow and medium band images of 3C 433 were taken with the GTC on 11 Sept 2017 (project GTC48-17B), using the Optical System for Imaging and low-Intermediate-Resolution Integrated Spectroscopy (OSIRIS).
We describe them below, including the optical properties of both the host galaxy of 3C 433 and its environment. We then focus only on the \ absorber in the discussion in Sect. discussion .

Our GTC imaging has revealed, furthermore, interesting alignments between H and radio synchrotron emission in the \ companion and in the host galaxy of the active galactic nucleus as well as in the circumgalactic medium in between. This suggests that the shock ionization of gas by the propagating radio source may happen across a scale spanning many tens of kpc.

sources.summary: 3C 433 is described as a hybrid FR I/II source in an asymmetric environment, with bent secondary lobes and uncertain thermal or power-law X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 3C 433 ( z=0.102 ) : The northern secondary lobe of 3C 433 is strikingly bent compared to the southern secondary vanbreugel83 . miller09 argue that the hybrid FR I/II lobe morphology is due to interaction with the surrounding IGM. In this scenario, the galaxy is a typical FR II source propagating into a very asymmetric environment. miller09 also include in their paper a HST image of the host galaxy whose PA is in good agreement with the parameters we derive from the DSS image. The X-ray emission near the southern lobe is consistent with either thermal emission miller09 or power law emission. We assume the ISM is the relevant medium and attempt to isolate it, but we may also be incorporating some local IGM emission in the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper finds the two unresolved 3C 433 components have consistent ATA RM spectra near 72 rad m^-2, differing from Taylor et al. values but matching single-dish observations.

quotes.quote: The RM spectra toward two, unresolved parts of 3C 433 (J212344+250410 and J212345+250448) are also consistent with each other, showing a single RM component at roughly 72 rad m ^ 2 . This value differs significantly from the RM measured by t09 toward the two sources individually, roughly 125 and 94 rad m ^ 2 t09 . The value seen by the ATA is similar to the value of 73 1 rad m ^ 2 \ seen in single-dish, wide-bandwidth observations s81 . The low-resolution RM spectra presented here have sensitivity to similar Faraday thicknesses as t09 , so their difference (and the consistency of the ATA and single-dish observations) may indicate an error in t09 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 433 as having a bright compact near-infrared nucleus, negligible WISE starlight contamination, and AGN detectability at all IRAC wavelengths.

quotes.quote: Indeed, HST studies of two powerful radio galaxies -- 3C 405 (Cygnus A) and 3C 433 -- have shown unresolved point sources clearly detected at longer near-IR wavelengths Tadhunter:1999,Ramirez:2009 , suggesting a direct detection of the hidden AGN, extinguished by foreground dust.
The bright compact nucleus of 3C 433 dominates the near-IR light, making the underlying host galaxy difficult to detect Madrid:2006,Ramirez:2009 .

Given that the WISE photometric fluxes for 3C 433 are so much higher than the starlight contamination, we can say with certainty that its AGN would be detected at all IRAC wavelengths; therefore we include this object in the 8.0 micron AGN detection statistics.

gmw_00000683.name: SHOC 579
gmw_00000683.overview: SHOC 579 is a nearby Lyman-alpha leaking blueberry galaxy that features a density-bounded central starburst [arXiv:2203.04207, arXiv:2110.13461]. This starburst recently formed over an underlying low surface brightness stellar disk, showing a kinematic misalignment between its stars and gas [arXiv:2203.04207].

sources.summary: The paper studies SHOC 579 as a nearby Ly-emitting blueberry galaxy with an old LSB disk host, recent starburst, massive metal-rich component, and gas-star kinematic misalignment.

quotes.quote: In the current work, we present a CELLs galaxy (viz. SHOC 579 or MaNGA ID: 8626 - 12704 , RA: 17 ^ h 35 ^ m 01.25 ^ s and DEC: +57 ^ d 03 ^ m 09 ^ s , see left panel in Fig. fig:1 ) observed with optical IFU spectroscopy at the Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory MaNGA; Bundyetal2015 . SHOC 579 is the only nearby Ly emitting galaxy (z 0.0472) which is observed in the MaNGA IFU survey.

The blueberry source studied here has recently ( 10 Myr) formed over an underlying LSB disk of its host galaxy. To date, this blueberry galaxy is the most massive and metal-rich one around which we have the direct observational evidence of an old stellar population. Based on the modelling of stacked spectra showing clear detection of Mg i 5173,5167 absorption line, we find the average mass-weighted age of the stellar population to be 5 Gyr and 7 Gyr for the blueberry component and the stellar disk, respectively.

This work reveals the first kinematics of stars and gas of a blueberry galaxy based on locally stacked spectra from MaNGA IFU observation. In that, the studied blueberry galaxy is found to be dispersion dominated in the ionized gas component and mildly rotation supported in stellar component. Furthermore, these stellar and gaseous components are misaligned to each other.

sources.summary: The paper presents SHOC 579 as a nearby Ly-alpha leaking Blueberry galaxy with a density-bounded central starburst, ionization-bounded outer regions, and spatially resolved S II deficiency.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we investigate the spatially resolved distribution of ionization and density-bounded H ii regions in a confirmed nearby (z 0.0472) Ly leaker (viz. SHOC 579) using the IFU Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory MaNGA; Bundy2015 survey. Previously, a detailed study, based on MaNGA IFU observation of SHOC579 by Paswan et al. (2021; under review), reveals that it is a blueberry galaxy and associated with a fain low brightness disk. SHOC 579 was also observed by the Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS) on Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and found to be emitting, f^ esc _ Ly 10 Jaskot2019 and it is the only confirmed Ly leaker observed in the MaNGA survey.

We found that only the central starburst region in the galaxy (as shown by the red circle in Fig. em-morph ) is [ SII ] deficient (see the left panel of Fig. SII-def-LHa ), i.e. density-bounded, and thus is likely to leak ionizing photons. The rest of the galaxy is ionization-bounded, i.e. [ SII ] enhanced, and unlikely to leak ionizing photons.

gmw_00000684.name: 3C 31
gmw_00000684.overview: NGC 383 is a dusty lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1909.05884] that hosts the Fanaroff-Riley class I radio source 3C 31 [arXiv:0809.2829, arXiv:1103.1364]. The galaxy features a relaxed nuclear molecular gas disk [arXiv:1909.05884] and spectacular, decelerating twin radio jets [arXiv:1909.05884, arXiv:astro-ph/0405020].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 31 as a twin-jet FR I radio galaxy whose radio, optical, and soft X-ray emission are jet-fit, while hard X-rays require an ADAF.

quotes.quote: ADAF+jet for moderate Eddington ratio sources 3C 31 is a twin-jet FR I radio galaxy. The X-ray spectrum of the core is quite flat, with a photon index =1.48^ +0.28 _ 0.32 and L_ X (2-10 \ keV)=4.7 10^ 40 erg \ s^ 1 ( 4.4 10^ 6 L_ Edd )(Evans et al 2006).

Figure 2 shows the fitting result of 3C 31. The parameters of the jet are m _ jet =2.7 10^ 5 , _ e =0.2 , _ B =0.02 , and p=2.5 . We find that the radio, optical and even the soft X-rays nuclear emission (e.g., 1 keV) can be well-fitted by a pure jet model.

However, the hard X-rays cannot be fitted by a jet, but can be well-fitted by the ADAF with an accretion rate of m _ out =3.7 10^ 3 . The ratio, m _ jet / m (10 \ R_ S ) , is about 9 . We can see that jet contribution is important at soft X-ray band, while the ADAF contribution is important at the hard X-ray band.

sources.summary: The paper measures the SMBH mass in NGC 0383 using ALMA CO kinematics, describing it as a dusty lenticular radio galaxy with a relaxed nuclear molecular gas disk and AGN emission.

quotes.quote: As part of the mm-Wave Interferometric Survey of Dark Object Masses (WISDOM), we present a measurement of the mass of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) in the nearby early-type galaxy NGC 0383 (radio source 3C 031). This measurement is based on Atacama Large Millimeter/sub-milimeter Array (ALMA) cycle 4 and 5 observations of the ^ 12 CO(2--1) emission line with a spatial resolution of 58 32 pc ^ 2 ( 0 18 0 1 ). This resolution, combined with a channel width of 10 , allows us to well resolve the radius of the black hole sphere of influence (measured as R_ SOI =316 pc = 0 98) , where we detect a clear Keplerian increase of the rotation velocities. NGC 0383 has a kinematically-relaxed, smooth nuclear molecular gas disc with weak ring/spiral features.

NGC 0383 is a dusty lenticular galaxy at a distance of 66.6 9.9 Mpc Freedman2001 . It is the brightest galaxy of its group (the NGC 0383 group), part of the Pisces-Perseus Supercluster Hudson2001 . NGC 0383 hosts a radio-loud active galactic nucleus (AGN) with spectacular radio jets. The coincident radio source is catalogued as 3C 031 Edge1959,Bennett1962 .

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 31 is host-dominated in the IRS band, with optical and radio jets, no infrared evidence for a warm torus, but broad H emission consistent with a broad-line region.

quotes.quote: 3C 31 This source appears to be dominated by emission from the host galaxy in the IRS band. This source contains an optical jet, first noticed by , and a radio jet which identifies as the same structure as in the optical. The optical jet does not contribute substantially to the IR core flux. It is worth noting, however, that detect extended NIR emission from the jet at kpc scales in Spitzer /IRAC images.

As shown in Figure fig:SED-3C31-cdo and in Table tab:3C31_frac_cdo , when a torus component is forced it contributes a significant amount of flux at 15 m and 30 m with best fit fractional contributions of 34.2 and 18.7 respectively. However, as shown in Table tab:BICs , the BIC difference between galaxy models with a forced torus and galaxy-only models is non-negligable (the BIC of the forced-torus model is 22.2 , or 6 , higher) and we notice no obviously unphysical best fit model parameters, therefore we conclude that we do not find evidence for a warm obscuring torus in the infrared spectra of 3C 31. We note, however, that detected broad H emission with a FWHM of 2710 km s ^ 1 , contributing 86 of the combined narrow+broad H flux. This broad H is consistent with the presence of a broad line region and therefore possibly an accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 31 as an FR I radio galaxy with decelerating jets, transverse velocity gradients, and conservation-law profiles of pressure, density, Mach number, and entrainment rate.

quotes.quote: Comparison of models and data The four sources we have modelled are 3C 31 LB02a , 0326+39, 1553+24 CL04 and NGC 315 (Cotton et al., in preparation). In Figs fig:compare1 - fig:compareh we compare model and observed images at the same resolution. Figs fig:compare1 and fig:compare2 show the full modelled areas at moderate resolution and display both total intensity and linear polarization. They demonstrate that we can successfully fit the observations in all cases, limited primarily by small-scale (and often non-axisymmetric) fluctuations.

At the closest distance from the nucleus where we can model the jets, all of their on-axis velocities are close to 0.8 . They all decelerate rapidly over a short distance in the flaring region, but at different distances from the nucleus. NGC 315, 0326+39 and 1553+24 then have roughly constant velocities in the range 0.1 -- 0.4 until the end of the modelled regions. 3C 31 continues to decelerate, but more slowly. There is clear evidence for transverse velocity gradients in 3C 31 and NGC 315.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 383 as the central 3C 31 radio galaxy in a rich group, with two strong jets and debated jet interaction with NGC 382.

quotes.quote: The NGC 383 group is a rich group of galaxies that harbours the central radio source 3C 31 = NGC 383. After presenting results from the X-ray analysis of the extended intra-group medium and the central radio galaxy, we discuss the spatial morphology of X-ray emitting gas as compared to the radio plasma and perform a comparison of the pressures of both components. The rich group of galaxies around NGC 383 at redshift z =0.017 is located in the Perseus Pisces filament (e.g., Zwicky et al. 1961, Arp 1966, Sakai et al. 1994). The brightest member galaxy, NGC 383 = 3C 31, is a moderately bright radio galaxy and has been extensively studied at radio wavelengths in the past (e.g., Klein Wielebinski 1979, Ekers et al. 1981, Condon et al. 1991, Artyukh et al. 1994, Lara et al. 1997). It shows a symmetric edge darkened double-source structure with two strong jets.

The structures of the jets of NGC 383 were interpreted by Blandford Icke (1978) as arising from the tidal interaction with the companion galaxy NGC 382. However, based on optical data Fraix-Burnet et al. (1991) found no evidence for interaction between the two galaxies. Here, we present an analysis of the X-ray properties of NGC 383, the extended intra-group medium (IGM), and a search for radio-X-ray relations on the basis of our ROSAT PSPC observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 383 as a bright cluster galaxy in a dumbbell pair, with a spiral dust disk, nuclear emission, blue point source, and distorted radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 383 (3C 31) NGC 383 is the brightest galaxy in the Zwicky cluster 0107.5+3212 (Sakai 1994). NGC 383 is inside a chain of galaxies and forms a dumbbell pair with Arp 331 (NGC 382) which is at 34'' (11 kpc). NGC 383 has a nearly face-on central dust disk (diameter 2.3 kpc/ 7.4'' ) which shows a spiral structure with a counter-clockwise orientation. The spiral structure becomes flocculent at the outer edges. Martel (1999) detected a dust disk in the companion NGC 382 as well.

NGC 383 has a central unresolved bright \ emission peak with low-surface-brightness emission extending out to 1 '' (300 pc) around it. Owen (1990) detected a rotating extended emission disk from a spectroscopic study. NGC 383 has a nuclear point source clearly bluer than its surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C31 as the archetypal flaring-jet FR-I source, fitting its intrinsic properties, host environment, age, jet power, and 608 MHz surface-brightness distribution.

quotes.quote: In this work, we will examine the flaring jet FR-Is, the archetypal example being 3C31 e.g. 3C31 Leahy+2013 . The synchrotron-emitting electrons in these sources are assumed to undergo a single instance of acceleration at the jet flare point.

The intrinsic properties of 3C31 are fitted using the Bayesian framework described by Turner+2016 , based upon the observed source size, luminosity and break frequency arising from the oldest electrons, in addition to properties of the host environment. Heesen+2014 combined high-resolution observations of 3C31 from the Low Frequency Array (LOFAR), the Very Large Array (VLA) and Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) to produce radio SEDs as a function of position within the radio lobe.

We derive a jet power for both jets of 10^ 37.2 W , source age of 200 Myrs and an equipartition factor of 0.31, consistent with the range found by Croston+2008 . The resolved emissivity model (Section sec:SPATIALLY RESOLVED LOBE LOSSES ) is used to predict the emissivity from the electrons of various ages in 3C31 for the best fit jet power, source age and magnetic field strength. These electrons are distributed within the observed volume occupied by 3C31 i.e. an approximately helical shape; Leahy+2013 ; the pressure-limited expansion model we assume from the growth of flaring jet radio sources dictates the radial location of the electrons of different ages.

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C 31 as an archetypal FRI radio galaxy, deriving its jet power, black-hole mass, X-ray halo temperature, and hot-gas density needed for Bondi fueling.

quotes.quote: The only source to have a published jet power derived from this method is 3C 31 (Laing Bridle 2002b) but since 3C 31 is one of the archetypes of its class, and has a relatively powerful jet for an FRI, we begin by considering it in more detail.

For 3C 31 Laing Bridle (2002b) quote a jet power Q 10^ 37 W. From the K-band 2MASS observations (here and subsequently we use the total K-band magnitude provided by the NASA Extragalactic Database, NED) and the M_ BH K relation we infer a black hole mass of 1.1 10^9 M _ . Hardcastle \ (2002, hereafter H02) studied the small-scale X-ray halo of the host galaxy of 3C 31 (the dominant galaxy of a rich group) and measured a central gas temperature of 0.66 keV.

If we assume an efficiency of conversion of accretion power to jet power of = 0.1 [ following Allen : Nemmen \ (2006) have recently argued that efficiencies of this order are possible in various jet-formation models provided that the central black hole is rapidly spinning ] then we can infer that an electron density at the Bondi accretion radius (assuming = 1.13n_ e m_ p as Allen \ do) n_ e,A 6 10^5 m ^ 3 is required to power the jet. H02 give a central density from their model fit of n_ e 2 10^5 m ^ 3 , which is already consistent within the uncertainties on M_ BH : however, we know from the H02 fits to the inner 1.5 arcsec (0.5 kpc) of the 3C 31 X-ray emission that the density is higher in that region than the model would predict, and in fact the mean density inferred from spectral fitting is around 5 10^5 m ^ 5 , while clearly if there is any density gradient in this inner region of the source the density at the Bondi accretion radius ( r_ A = 50 pc) will be higher than the mean value. Thus there is no difficulty in producing the jets of 3C 31 by accretion of the hot phase of the IGM.

sources.summary: The paper uses new X-ray and radio constraints for 3C 31 to test lobe pressure models, favoring entrainment-heated thermal material over leptons, magnetic fields, or relativistic protons.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate in detail the observational constraints on models for the particle and energy content of low-power radio galaxies, by considering how the non-radiating and radiating components of the jets in the well-studied radio galaxy 3C 31 must evolve with distance in order to maintain pressure balance and produce the observed radio emission. We use new deep X-ray data and high-resolution radio data to place tight constraints on the external pressure and internal pressure from radiating particles and field within the radio jets and plumes of 3C 31 . We consider in detail the constraints this result provides for what particle population or magnetic field structure dominates the source energetics, and also carry out a pressure comparison for the cluster-centre source Hydra A as a preliminary test of the generality of our results.

Finally we have demonstrated that a simple entrainment model is consistent with the external pressure constraints and the evolution of radio emissivity, with regions of entrainment corresponding to locations of jet bending/disruption and changes in the external pressure profile. Such a model requires a high temperature for the entrained component, and an increasing temperature with distance, consistent with a rapidly decreasing kinetic energy flux of the jet being converted to particle and magnetic field internal energies. The results presented here are based on consideration of a single object, for which the highest quality radio and X-ray data on the scales of interest are available.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 383 as a large jet system whose AGN energy can offset cooling and may transport energy to large radii in its group.

quotes.quote: These three systems (NGC 383, NGC 741, NGC 4261) have jets which transport the AGN energy out to large radii where the gas density may be too low to allow the energy to be radiated in less than a Hubble time. This could lead to steepening of temperature and entropy profiles in these systems. We find that if the three groups with large radio galaxies are excluded from the sample the correlations are no longer significant.

To investigate whether AGN output is indeed sufficient to balance cooling, we consider the case of NGC 383 (3C 31). This source has been well studied and is one of the few in our sample that has an estimated total jet power. We define the cooling radius as the radius at which the cooling time is equal to the Hubble time, which for NGC 383 is 25 \ kpc .

sources.summary: The paper treats 3CR 31 in detail as an FR-I radio galaxy with a central molecular gas disk traced by double-horn CO lines and confirmed by interferometric observations.

quotes.quote: This galaxy has been observed in more details than the rest of the galaxies. It is an FR-I radio galaxy at a distance of 71 Mpc hosted by an elliptical D galaxy in the Zwicky cluster 0107.5+3212. A double horn profile of the CO(1-0) and CO(2-1) lines was found by giving a first indication of a central molecular gas disk (see Fig. 3c31-30m ). The Nobeyama Millimeter Array (NMA) CO(1-0) interferometer observations by confirmed the presence of this molecular gas disk. Our PdBI observations are about the same spatial resolution than the NMA ones but with a higher sensitivity as shown by the CO(1-0) integrated intensity map on Fig. pdbi-3c31 .

sources.summary: The paper uses a hydrodynamical FRI jet simulation for 3C 31 and links recollimation-shock synchrotron emission to its observed large-scale jet X-rays.

quotes.quote: (PM07 hereafter) performed a simulation aimed to test the FRI jet evolution paradigm b84 and the model by for the FRI jet of the radiogalaxy 3C 31. The simulation was done using a numerical code for relativistic hydrodynamics, based on High-Resolution-Shock-Capturing schemes, to which it was added an equation of state that allows for a specific treatment of two families of particles, leptons and baryons, and computes the adiabatic index in terms of the composition of each cell. The jet was injected in the numerical grid at 500 pc from the active nucleus, with a radius of 60 pc .

This X-ray synchrotron emission concentrated around the recollimation shock is compatible with the large-scale jet X-ray emission found in 3C 31 by . If a strong recollimation shock is indeed the origin of these large-scale jet X-rays, then the hypothesis that jet disruption in 3C 31 is caused by shock triggered instabilities is favored against stellar wind mass-load (as proposed by lb02 ; see also PM07).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 383 as the dominant D galaxy hosting 3C 31, and uses GMRT images to trace bent jets and extended radio tails to 900 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 383 NGC 383 is the dominant D galaxy, a part of the Arp 133 system, at the core of the poor group WBL 25, also known as IV Zw 38. It is also a part of the Pisces-Perseus supercluster (an optical analysis can be found in Miles et al. 2004).
