This galaxy has long been known to host the famous, twin-jet FR I source 3C 31, which belongs to the same class of giant radio galaxies as NGC 315 (Sect. sec:ngc315 ). 3C 31 has been studied in detail both on large and small scales by many authors (e.g., Burch 1977; Blandford Icke 1978; Fomalont et al. 1980; Strom et al. 1983; Andernach et al. 1992; Laing Bridle 2002; Laing et al. 2008, and references therein). Fig. fig:ngc383 presents our new GMRT image at 610 MHz (left), overlaid on the XMM--Newton image.

sources.summary: The paper reports Spitzer infrared detection of the 3C 31 jet, interprets multiwavelength spectra as synchrotron emission, and finds spectral changes with distance.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of infrared emission from the jet of the nearby FR I radio galaxy 3C 31. The jet was detected with the IRAC instrument on Spitzer at 4.5 , 5.8 , and 8.0 out to 30 (13 kpc) from the nucleus. We measure radio, infrared, optical, and X-ray fluxes in three regions along the jet determined by the infrared and X-ray morphology. Radio through X-ray spectra in these regions demonstrate that the emission can be interpreted as synchrotron emission from a broken power-law distribution of electron energies. We find significant differences in the high energy spectra with increasing distance from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper applies its turbulent-jet model to 3C31, matching centerline synchrotron intensity and estimating jet transit time, efficiency, and possible cosmic-ray acceleration energy.

quotes.quote: An astrophysical test can be performed on a typical distance of 15 kpc relative to the jets in 3C 31, see Figure 2 in .

( bx ) in order to have an analytical expression for the centerline intensity I(x)= B_ 0 ^ p/2-1/2 I_ 0 B_ 0 ^ 2 x_ 0 x ^ ^ p/4+1/4 and Figure intensity_3c31 shows the theoretical synchrotron intensity as well the observed one in 3C31, see Figure 8 in .

The application to the measured intensity of 3C31 yields an efficiency over all the jet's length of 87.56 , see Figure intensity_3c31 . A test on the magnetic field of 3C273 in the pc region can be seen in Figure bfield_3c273 . The presence of a law for the magnetic field allows fixing the Hillas plot for the maximum energy which can reached during the process of acceleration of the cosmic rays, which in the case of 3C31 is 10^ 21 \ eV, see the caption of Figure cosmicrays .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 31 as a well-studied radio galaxy with complex outflows, a faint diffuse cocoon, and possible extended halo emission southwest of the host.

quotes.quote: GRG J0107+3224 This radio galaxy was extensively studied in the past in many domains while mostly at radio wavelengths. There are more than 480 publication dealing with this source in the ADS. 3C 31 was observed in great detail by Laing et al. (2008a,2008b), who showed that this radio galaxy produces complex outflows in the form of multiple jets and tails. Fig. 2 shows that the entire complex is embedded in a diffuse faint radio cocoon. The appearance of the diffuse structure around the bright north-south oriented radio structure can be a bit surprising, as it is not visible in any other map known to date.

sources.summary: The paper applies turbulent-jet energy-flux models to 3C31, comparing synchrotron-intensity calculations, magnetic-field behavior, jet transport times, and large-scale jet structure with observations.

quotes.quote: Astrophysical applications are made to the centerline intensity of the synchrotron emission and to the evolution of the magnetic field in the case of the radio-galaxy 3C31.

An astrophysical test can be performed on a typical distance of 15 kpc relative to the jets in 3C 31, see Figure 2 in . On inserting x=15000 pc =15 \ kpc, x_0=100 \ pc, and v_0=10000 \ km s ^ 1 we obtain a transit time of t_ tr =2.488 10^7 \ yr.

We now report the large scale structure and jets of 3C31, see Figure gray_3c31 . } To make a comparison with the observed profile of intensity, we choose the first 10 kpc of 3C31, see Figures 1 and 8 in ; Figure intensityrel_3c31 shows the theoretical synchrotron intensity, as well as the observed one.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of the FRI radio galaxy 3C 31, resolving hot gas around NGC 383 and detecting a one-sided synchrotron X-ray jet tied to particle acceleration.

quotes.quote: We have used a deep Chandra observation of the central regions of the twin-jet FRI radio galaxy 3C 31 to resolve the thermal X-ray emission in the central few kiloparsecs of the host galaxy, NGC 383, where the jets are thought to be decelerating rapidly. This allows us to make high-precision measurements of the density, temperature and pressure distributions in this region, and to show that the X-ray emitting gas in the centre of the galaxy has a cooling time of only 5 10^7 years. In a companion paper these measurements are used to place constraints on models of the jet dynamics. A previously unknown one-sided X-ray jet in 3C 31, extending up to 8 arcsec from the nucleus, is detected and resolved. Its structure and steep X-ray spectrum are similar to those of X-ray jets known in other FRI sources, and we attribute the radiation to synchrotron emission from a high-energy population of electrons.

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 31 as a low-power radio galaxy whose jet flow, thermal atmosphere, deceleration, buoyancy, and brightness profile are modeled with mass entrainment.

quotes.quote: More recently, model fits using ( eq:betasb ) and ( eq:betaspecnorm ) to X-ray measurements of the thermal atmosphere of 3C 31, coupled with results from high-dynamic range radio mapping (Fig. fig:radio31 ), have led to a self-consistent model of the flow. The kinematic model of 3C 31 involves three distinct regions in the jet: an inner 1 kpc long section of narrow jet where the flow is fast (relativistic) and the opening angle of the jet is small, a flaring region of 2 kpc during which the jet broadens rapidly and brightens in the radio, and an outer region in which the jet expands steadily with a smaller opening angle than in the flaring region.

Here the jet decelerates steadily from a moderate Mach number as it picks up mass from the external medium or from the mass lost by stars embedded in the jet . Buoyancy forces are important for much of the outer flow, and the jet becomes sensitive to local changes in the density of the intracluster medium, and hence is liable to deflect from straight-line motion. While this model provides a good description of 3C 31, which displays a smoothly-varying radio brightness profile and so is amenable to simple fitting for flow speed, and is based simply on the basic conservation laws for mass, momentum, and energy , it represents only the overall deceleration of the jet from entrainment.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 31 as an FR I radio galaxy, deriving its jet inclination and using RM structure and depolarization to associate Faraday rotation with surrounding group gas.

quotes.quote: 3C 31 is an FR I radio galaxy at a redshift of 0.0169 (0.344 kpc/arcsec for H _0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ). Our models of the inner jets give an inclination of = 52^ . Our RM image, derived from 6-frequency observations between 1.365 and 8.4 GHz, is shown in Fig. fig:3c31 (a), with an I image at the same resolution for comparison in Fig. fig:3c31 (b). The E vector position angle is accurately proportional to ^2 everywhere, indicating foreground rotation. There is structure in the RM image on a range of spatial scales from 5 to > 50 arcsec and the RM fluctuations are higher by a factor of 2 -- 3 on the counter-jet side (Fig. fig:3c31 and d). There is a small amount of depolarization on the counter-jet side. We estimate this by making a first-order linear approximation to the variation of degree of polarization, p , with ^2 : p( ^2)/p(0) 1 + [ p^ (0)/p(0) ] ^2 (Fig. fig:3c31 e). We model the thermal X-ray emission as the sum of two components: one associated with the galaxy (core radius r_c = 3.6 arcsec), the second with the surrounding group, with r_c = 154 arcsec (Hardcastle et al. 2002). The Faraday rotation variations are therefore plausibly associated with the group component.

sources.summary: The review cites 3C 31 as the FRI source whose dynamical modeling self-consistently explains jet deceleration, entrainment, and pressure balance, while later quasar entrainment estimates challenge that model.

quotes.quote: For one source so far, 3C 31, excellent self consistency has been found . This lends confidence to an understanding of the basic flow behaviour of these sources.

The model for 3C 31 demonstrates that entrainment of the external medium explains the jet dynamics in the deceleration region, and pressure balance can be achieved by adding relativistic protons (with neutrality preserved by balancing proton and electron number densities) or extending the electron spectrum to lower energies (if electron-positron charge balance is enforced). Recent work has claimed a greater pressure imbalance in FRI jets that are more in contact with external gas (less in contact with the plumes or lobes of older jet plasma), and speculates that the pressure is balanced by heated entrained material, with an entrainment rate or a heating efficiency that is higher where jets are in greater direct contact with the X-ray-emitting atmosphere. This seems in conflict with the entrainment model for the quasar PKS 1136-135 in the context of the beamed iC-CMB model, where a standard model would have the jets heavily embedded in old lobe plasma and yet where the estimated entrainment rate is an order of magnitude higher than for 3C 31 .

sources.summary: The appendix summarizes a WISDOM ALMA CO measurement of NGC 383, deriving a precise SMBH mass from high-resolution gas kinematics and noting possible central warps or non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: The SMBH mass of the nearby lenticular galaxy NGC 383 is measured by using ALMA observations of the ^ 12 CO(2-1) emission line with a synthesised beam FWHM of 16 8 pc ^2 ( 0 051 0 025 ). This angular (and thus spatial) resolution is 4 times better than that of the previous intermediate-resolution measurement by and it spatially resolves the SOI by a factor of 23 . The observations yield V_ max 634 \ (corresponding to a deprojected velocity V_ 1040 ), 1.8 times higher than that of , as well as evidence for a mild position angle warp and/or non-circular motions within the central 0 3 . By forward modelling the mass distribution and ALMA data cube, infer a SMBH mass of (3.59 0.20) 10^9 M _ ( 1 confidence interval), more precise (5 ) but consistent with ( 1.4 smaller than) the measurement by .

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 31's jet-base spectrum flattens with distance, shows low-level transverse structure, and includes arcs whose spectra are flatter than surrounding emission.

quotes.quote: Our initial work on the jet spectra in 3C 31, NGC 315 and 3C 296 (summarized in Girdwood ) confirmed Young 's result that the jet base spectral indices can be significantly higher than 0.5 and consistently found a value of 0.62 near the flaring points where the jets brighten abruptly.

Firstly, the spectrum of the main jet in NGC 315 flattens away from its axis LCCB06 as well as with distance from the AGN; the same effect is visible in its counter-jet (Fig. fig:images b) and, at a low level, on one side of each of the jets in 3C 31 Fig. fig:images of the present paper 3c31ls .
The two brightest arcs in 3C 31 have slightly ( 0.05 ) flatter spectra than the surrounding emission 3c31ls .

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 383 as a literature-supported FR I LERG, deriving its jet inclination from detailed modelling and using HST imaging for a unique jet-disc misalignment.

quotes.quote: To improve the statistics of jet--disc orientations, we have also extended our analysis to two other FR I LERGs for which CO and jet inclination data of comparable quality are available in the literature, NGC 383 and NGC 3665.
The jet inclination for NGC 383 is derived from the detailed modelling carried out by Laing02,Laing14 .

For NGC 3557, IC 4296, NGC 383 and NGC 3665 the available archival Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical imaging allows us to identify the side of the CO disc pointing towards the observer unequivocally (Paper I; Onishi17,North19 ) and we can derive unique values of _ dj .

sources.summary: The FRI radio galaxy 3C 31, identified with NGC 383, has extended PSPC emission but HRI emission dominated by a point-like nuclear component.

quotes.quote: 3C 31 Our results for this FRI radio galaxy (NGC 383) are consistent with Trussoni \ (1997). PSPC images show extended emission, but the HRI detects only point-like emission, with a component coincident with 3C 31's nucleus. Emission coincident with NGC 380 and NGC 379, other members of 3C 31's group, is also detected.

gmw_00000685.name: NGC 315
gmw_00000685.overview: NGC 315 is a nearby giant elliptical cD galaxy [arXiv:2012.14154, arXiv:2006.09388] that hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2102.05809, arXiv:2101.06579] and a giant Fanaroff-Riley Class I radio source [arXiv:2012.14154]. The galaxy features a central dust disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9909256, arXiv:1503.01836] and two-sided radio jets that transition from parabolic to conical shapes on sub-parsec scales [arXiv:2206.05803, arXiv:2012.14831].

sources.summary: NGC 315 is discussed as a giant FR-I radio galaxy with redshifted H I absorption, possible infalling clouds, and a broad component influenced by the AGN on parsec scales.

quotes.quote: One interesting case is seen NGC 315. Like in 4C 31.04 and B2352+495, a deep, narrow \ absorption line, redshifted by about 500 \ from the systemic velocity, was discovered by Dressel83 using Arecibo against the inner regions of this giant radio galaxy. The presence of fainter, broader \ absorption closer to the systemic velocity was later revealed by deeper observations Morganti09 . Interestingly, this component is also redshift, albeit only of only 80 \ compared to the systemic velocity (see Fig. \ fig:InfallingClouds . When traced by the VLBI (i.e. \ parsec-scales), the two absorption systems have very different structure. The narrow, redshifted absorption smoothly covers most of the central continuum (the core and the inner 10 pc of the jet) and does not show any morphological or kinematic features. This suggests that this component is likely caused by clouds falling into NGC 315, not necessarily located extremely close to the AGN.

Interestingly, the broad component centred on the systemic velocity shows a velocity- as well as a column density gradient (on the scale of only 10 pc) suggesting that the gas has been influenced by the AGN and that it is located physically close to it. Because of these properties and other considerations, it has been suggested that this gas is also involved in fuelling the AGN. If NGC 315 is representative, these data indicate that only observations at high spatial resolution (pc-scales) are able to determine whether gas is close to the AGN and falling into the SMBH.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 315 in gamma rays and models its SED, finding SSC jet emission explains lower-energy data better than RIAF hadronic emission.

quotes.quote: The result from modeling the SED of NGC 315 with the jet scenario is displayed in the left panel of Figure fig:NGC315 . The X-ray spectrum and the lowest LAT energy bins ( E<5 GeV) are adequately reproduced with a SSC component. However, the highest energy LAT emission ( E>5 GeV) cannot be explained by jet SSC emission and could be from yet another component which we have not taken into account.

The right panel in Figure fig:NGC315 indicates the best RIAF model found for NGC 315 and the corresponding parameters are listed in Table tab:RIAF_parameters . It is known that the compact radio emission in LLAGN is underpredicted by models which only incorporate a thermal distribution of electrons in the RIAF Yu2011,Liu2013,Nemmen2014 and we find the same behavior here for NGC 315 as well as NGC 4261. By including the synchrotron emission of a relativistic jet modeled following , the radio emission can be accounted for, as the figure illustrates. The RIAF leptonic emission is able to broadly account for the 1 m observations and fits the X-ray continuum emission very well. However, the associated hadronic emission fails to reproduce the Fermi LAT observations, underpredicting the ray luminosity by about two orders of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper uses multifrequency VLBI and archival data to show NGC 315 has a jet collimation break, gradual acceleration to Lorentz factor about 3, and limb-brightening in the resolved jet.

quotes.quote: We study the collimation and acceleration of the jets in the nearby giant radio galaxy NGC 315, using multifrequency Very Long Baseline Array observations and archival High Sensitivity Array and Very Large Array data. We find that the jet geometry transitions from a semi-parabolic shape into a conical/hyperbolic shape at a distance of 10^5 gravitational radii. We constrain the frequency-dependent position of the core, from which we locate the jet base. The jet collimation profile in the parabolic region is in good agreement with the steady axisymmetric force-free electrodynamic solution for the outermost poloidal magnetic field line anchored to the black hole event horizon on the equatorial plane, similar to the nearby radio galaxies M87 and NGC 6251. The velocity field derived from the asymmetry in brightness between the jet and counterjet shows gradual acceleration up to the bulk Lorentz factor of 3 in the region where the jet collimation occurs, confirming the presence of the jet acceleration and collimation zone.

The nearby giant elliptical galaxy NGC 315, at a redshift of 0.01648 Trager2000 which corresponds to a scale of 0.348 kpc arcsec ^ 1 for our adopted cosmology ( H_0 = 67.4 \ km \ s^ 1 , _m = 0.315 , Planck2020 ), is a good laboratory for studying jet collimation and acceleration. It hosts a Fanaroff-Riley Class I FR I, FR1974 radio source whose two-sided jets extend out to several hundred kpc from the galaxy e.g., see also Figure fig:images Bridle1976, Laing2006 . Its large black hole mass of 1.6 10^9 \ M_ , estimated from the stellar velocity dispersion of _v 350 \ km \ s^ 1 Faber1989, Ene2020 and the latest M_ BH _v relation Sexton2019 , makes it easier to resolve the putative jet collimation and acceleration region of this source.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 315 as an elliptical LLAGN with a supermassive black hole, using RIAF emission for high frequencies and jet emission for the radio SED.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 is another elliptical galaxy at 64 Mpc of distance, located at Pisces constellation. It contains a SMBH of 7.8 10^8 M_ Woo2002 .
The SED data is available in table tab:NGC315 . presented data points and similar modeling for the same galaxy. NGC 315's SED data points and the best fit are in figure fig:315-sed .

For NGC 4261 and NGC 315, our models were more focused on the RIAF component, especially for higher frequencies. In our model, the innermost regions of the RIAF produce most of the X-ray emission. We used the jet component to explain mostly the radio region of the SED. We did not consider the jet emission to explain the observed high energy emission (optical, UV, or X-ray bands).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 315 as a nearby giant FR I radio galaxy whose VLBI jet acceleration, collimation, power, and opening-angle profiles constrain thermal and magnetic acceleration scenarios.

quotes.quote: As initial template for the physical conditions, we use the radio galaxy NGC 315 as prototype. NGC 315 is a nearby z = 0.0165 , Trager_2000 , giant FR I e.g., Laing_2006 radio galaxy whose acceleration and collimation properties have been extensively studied by employing very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) observations. Such observations showed the jet in NGC 315 to accelerate up to v (0.8-0.9) , and to collimate on similar scales, with the highest Lorentz factor 4 reached at 2 pc Boccardi_2021, Park_2021, Ricci_2022 . Other physical properties such as the magnetic field strength, the jet power and the jet width have been estimated Boccardi_2021, Ricci_2022 and are also used as initial conditions in our models.

As seen in Figs. fig:Lorentz_all_models and fig:opening_all_models , a number of models can reproduce both the Lorentz factor and opening angle profiles inferred for NGC 315 employing cm- and mm-VLBI observations. From the comparison with the observed jet speed in NGC 315, we suggest certain models are favored over others when it comes to modeling such a source. On the one hand, jets with power larger than 10^ 44 erg/s show Lorentz factors at 3 pc which are remarkably larger than the observed one, giving an indication of a possible upper limit on the jet power of NGC 315. On the other hand, we highlight how different models with jet powers 10^ 43 erg/s match the different acceleration profiles obtained across the multi-epoch observations at 5 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper reviews and reanalyzes NGC 315 X-ray data, finding a mildly absorbed power-law plus thermal component, jet emission, flux changes, and behavior consistent with RIAF-like accretion.

quotes.quote: worrall94apj:ngc315 first suggested the presence of an active galactic nucleus at the center of NGC 315 using \ data. This assumption was later confirmed by matsumoto01pasj:ngc315 when studying a 37 ks ASCA observation (see also terashima02apjs:LLAGNASCA ). The authors fit the hard 2-10 keV spectrum with a power-law and find a photon index of 2 and a luminosity of 3.1 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 . A resolved X-ray jet emission was first reported by worrall03mnras:ngc315 when studying the \ snapshot observation (obs. ID: 855).

Our analysis of the two \ observations and the \ one gives similar results to all of the above studies with a simultaneous fit to the different extracted spectra where a combination of a mekal ( kT 0.5 keV) and a mildly absorbed ( N_ h 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 ) power-law ( between 1.5 and 2) were used. The increase in the power-law slope from 1.5 0.1 during the \ observation to 2.1_ 0.2 ^ +0.1 during the \ one is accompanied by a decrease in the 2-10 keV flux from 9.8 10^ 13 to 4.6 10^ 13 erg s ^ 1 . This behavior (see accmode ) is typical of X-ray emission originating in a RIAF structure which is believed to be the accretion mechanism responsible for the bulk of energy from radio to X-rays in NGC 315 wu07apj:riaf .

sources.summary: The paper uses multi-frequency VLBA data to model NGC 315, describing it as a nearby giant FR I radio galaxy whose parsec-scale jet favors electron-positron plasma and shows a counter-jet absorber.

quotes.quote: We applied Bayesian analysis to multi-frequency VLBA observations of radio galaxy NGC 315 and found that its parsec-scale jet is well described by the inhomogeneous conical model. Our results favour electron-positron jet. We also detected a component in a counter jet. Its position implies the presence of an external absorber with a steep density gradient at close ( =0.1 pc) distance from the central engine.

We choose radio source NGC 315 1976Natur.262..179B which is a close ( z=0.0165 ) giant FR I 1974MNRAS.167P..31F radio galaxy. It resides in elliptical galaxy and optically classified as broad-lined LINER 1997ApJS..112..391H . Flux density monitoring of NGC 315 with Westerbork synthesis radio telescope at 5 GHz from 1974 to 1980 revealed constant flux 1983A&A...120..297E .

sources.summary: The paper applies recent NGC 315 multi-frequency core-shift measurements, adopts Boccardi et al. data, and finds gravitational-scale magnetic-field estimates consistent with magnetically arrested-disc estimates.

quotes.quote: For NGC 315 we use the recent results by Ricci22 . In particular, multi frequency measurements are consistent with k_ =0.57 0.17 at 22-8 GHz, and k_ =0.92 0.01 at 8-5 GHz. This result is in agreement with formula ( k43 ) k_ =4k/3 , with 4k/3=0.6 for k=0.45 Boccardi_2021 . We adopt the core shift measurement by Boccardi_2021 between the frequencies 15.4 and 8.4 GHz.

For NGC 315 both the obtained values B_ g ^ par =21 and 480 G can be compared with the independent estimate based on an assumption of magnetically arrested disc launching a jet Ricci22 . The estimate for k_ =2k is in excellent agreement with B_ MAD =125-480 G at the lower limit event horizon radius. On the other hand, the magnetic field, obtained assuming k_ =4k/3 , when recalculated to the upper limit magnetosphere radius 10.6 r_ g , has the value 2.5 G, which correspond very well to the independent magnetic estimate 5-18 G at this scale.

sources.summary: The paper presents jet models for NGC 315, fitting radio/polarization maps, modeling only its flaring region, and finding roughly constant velocities plus transverse velocity gradients.

quotes.quote: Comparison of models and data The four sources we have modelled are 3C 31 LB02a , 0326+39, 1553+24 CL04 and NGC 315 (Cotton et al., in preparation). In Figs fig:compare1 - fig:compareh we compare model and observed images at the same resolution. Figs fig:compare1 and fig:compare2 show the full modelled areas at moderate resolution and display both total intensity and linear polarization. They demonstrate that we can successfully fit the observations in all cases, limited primarily by small-scale (and often non-axisymmetric) fluctuations.

NGC 315, 0326+39 and 1553+24 then have roughly constant velocities in the range 0.1 -- 0.4 until the end of the modelled regions. 3C 31 continues to decelerate, but more slowly. There is clear evidence for transverse velocity gradients in 3C 31 and NGC 315.

sources.summary: The paper reports a central dust and emission-gas disk in NGC 315, additional dust patches, broad-line nuclear emission, and a well-studied two-sided radio jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 NGC 315 is part of Zwicky cluster 0107.5+3212 (Zwicky 1961) which is located in the Perseus-Pisces filament. We detect a central dust disk 820 pc ( 2.5'' ) in diameter which is close to but not a perfect ellipse; the north tip of the disk has a small extension. In addition several mottled patches of dust are detected southwest of the nucleus out to 5'' ( 1.5 kpc). The central part of the dust disk forms a small bright emission-gas disk which extends into low-level emission throughout the dust disk. There is also a low-level emission feature adjacent to the southeast side (i.e., counter-jet side) of the dust disk which is elongated in the direction of the dust disk.

HI was detected in absorption redshifted by 400 \ with respect to the systemic velocity (van Gorkom 1989, Knapp 1990). Ho (1997) found a nuclear H broad-line emission component with FWHM 2000 \ in addition to the narrow-line emission.

sources.summary: NGC 315 is discussed as an FRI jet with resolved X-ray structure, a coherent oscillatory filament, possible shear-layer knots, and downstream deceleration by entrainment.

quotes.quote: A particularly interesting example is NGC 315 . Here the diffuse emission contains a knotty structure in the radio and X-ray that appears to describe an oscillatory filament (Fig. fig:n315jet ). Although the structure could be the result of a chance superposition of non-axisymmetric knots, the level of coherence led to suggestions that the knots might be predominantly a surface feature residing in the shear layer between the fast spine and slower, outer, sheath plasma. If this interpretation is correct, we might expect the X-ray spectra of the knots to be similar across the transverse width of the jet.

It is in this final region, beyond that shown for NGC 315 in Figure fig:n315jet , that the jets are modelled as decelerating steadily as they collect mass from the external medium or stellar winds . Buoyancy forces are then important for much of the flow further downstream, as the jets adjust to changes in the density of the external medium, causing deflections from straight-line motion.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 315 as a bright cD elliptical GRG with HI absorption/emission, exceptionally short depletion time, and molecular-gas deficiency relative to scaling relations.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 is the brightest elliptical (cD) galaxy of a group Sullivan2015 , and has also been detected in HI- absorption and emission Morganti2009 .

The R1-1 detected spiral galaxy has a long t _ dep of 5.5 Gyr, and the tentative detection R2-9 has t _ dep of 3.15 Gyr, and the two detected GRG (NGC 315 and 3C 236) have an exceptionally short t _ dep of 120 and 280 Myr, as well as two upper limits that are more typical of starbursts. When normalised to the galaxy surface, the R1-1 spiral galaxy and other outliers fall much closer to the main sequence in the KS diagram, except for NGC 315 and NGC 6251, which are still exceptionally deficient in molecular gas.

The GRG targets follow the main-sequence galaxies of the xCOLD GASS sample, two of which are gas-poor, NGC 315 and NGC 6251. Together with the gas fraction, SFR depends on the efficiency of star formation with respect to the amount of fuel, that is, SFR/M _ mol , which is the inverse of the depletion time t _ dep . The latter is plotted versus sSFR in the right panel of Figure. \ fig:fgas-tdep . Here again, most of our targets follow the MS scaling relations, except for two, NGC 315 and NGC 6251, which appear to be too efficient to form stars because they lack molecular gas. Their t _ dep (the inverse of star formation efficiency) is shorter than expected from the scaling relation. h Radio power and fueling The nuclear activity in galaxies is thought to be triggered when the central supermassive black hole accretes a significant mass of gas from its interstellar reservoir.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 315 favors a significant torus component, consistent with polarized broad H emission and a compact low-luminosity AGN seen after subtracting stellar infrared light.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 This source favors a torus component and that torus component is a significant part of the best fit, contributing 70 of the flux at 15 and 30 m and 50 at 60 m. There is possible degeneracy between the torus component and NLR component but we doubt that this is a significant concern in this case due to the fact that the BIC will penalize the torus model more harshly due to its higher number of parameters. We therefore see no particular reason to doubt the presence of an obscuring warm torus-like structure in this AGN. The presence of a torus component in the spectral energy distribution of NGC 315 is consistent with the discovery of broad polarized H emission in this source by .

Similarly, detected a low-luminosity compact AGN in IRAC and MIPS images of NGC 315 after removal of stellar IR emission and accounting for emission from a central dusty disk. They make no claims about the nature of this AGN however its presence is consistent with our result that the IR spectrum of NGC 315 is not entirely dominated by emission from the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 315 as a giant radio galaxy whose sub-parsec jet accelerates in a parabolic region and whose disk magnetic flux matches a MAD scenario.

quotes.quote: In this paper we use a multi-frequency and multi-epoch data set to study the giant radio galaxy NGC 315, with the goal to explore the properties of its accretion disk and sub-parsec jet. We analyze the source maps with a pixel-based analysis and we use theoretical models to link the observational properties of the jet to the physical state of the accretion disk. We propose that the bulk flow in NGC 315 accelerates on sub-pc scales, concurrently with the parabolic expansion.

Along the acceleration and collimation zone, we observe an unexpected spectral behavior, with very steep spectral index values 1.5 ( S_ ^ ) between 22 GHz and 43 GHz. Based on the properties of this region, we predict the black hole of NGC 315 to be fast rotating and the magnetic flux threading the accretion disk to be in excellent agreement with that expected in the case of a MAD. Using a new formalism based on the core-shift effect, we model the magnetic field downstream a quasi-parabolic accelerating jet and we reconstruct it up to the event horizon radius.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 315 to nonradiative jet simulations, using its black-hole mass, Bondi radius, low-luminosity accretion flow, and observed sub-parsec parabolic-to-conical transition.

quotes.quote: For NGC 315, the inferred BH mass ranges from M=3 10^8M_ 2007ApJ...671L.105G to M=3.4 10^9M_ 2003A&A...399..869B . Following 2021A&A...647A..67B , we adopt an intermediate BH mass, M = 1.3 10^9 M_ 2005ApJ...633...86S . We take the Bondi radius to be r_ B = (51 25) \ pc 2020ApJ...894..141I,2021A&A...647A..67B , the ambient medium number density to be n = 2.8 10^ 1 cm ^ 3 2007MNRAS.380....2W,2022A&A...664A.166R , and the jet power to be P_ jet 1.4 10^ 44 \ erg s^ 1 2022A&A...664A.166R .

Because the accretion flow in NGC 315 is in the low-luminosity, radiatively-inefficient regime, L/L_ Edd 5 10^ 4 1 2007ApJ...671L.105G , our energy-conserving model M5 provides the most appropriate comparison. The jet in our model M5 transitions from quasi-parabolic to quasi-conical shape at a factor of a few smaller radius than r_ B . This is very different than what is observed: observations indicate that the parabolic to conical jet transition in NGC 315 happens at sub-pc scales, at r_ tr 5 10^3r_ g 0.6 pc, which is much smaller than the Bondi scale, r_ B 4 10^5r_ g 50 pc 2021A&A...647A..67B .

sources.summary: NGC 315 is described as the cD host of an FR I radio galaxy with jets, LINER-like emission, and evidence favoring a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: 0055 + 300 is an FR I radio-galaxy with an asymmetric two-sided jet and a prominent core; its optical counterpart is the cD galaxy NGC 315, at z = 0.017 . This source has been extensively studied using the VLA and VLBI ( e.g. Venturi et al. 1993; Cotton et al. 1999; Dennett-Thorpe March a 2000) and has been detected at X-ray (Worrall Birkinshaw 2000; Terashima et al. 2002) and infrared wavelengths (Golombek et al. 1988). Spectroscopic observations by Ho et al.(1995) suggest the presence of a LINER with a weak broad \ component.

We confirm the low ionization nature of this object, but were not able to detect any broad Balmer line; however, such a component cannot be excluded by our data. The detection of a broad component would be in agreement with the other AGN-like characteristics of the object, namely the presence of a radio jet and a compact, unresolved, X-ray core. Although a small-scale cooling flow may be present in NGC 315 (Worrall Birkinshaw 2000), we interpret the observed low ionization spectrum as the optical signature of a low luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 315 as a nearby giant low-luminosity radio galaxy whose FR I jets decelerate, recollimate, and have larger physical scales than comparable sources.

quotes.quote: We apply our intrinsically symmetrical, decelerating relativistic jet model to deep VLA imaging of the inner 70 arcsec of the giant low-luminosity radio galaxy NGC 315. An optimized model accurately fits the data in both total intensity and linear polarization. We infer that the velocity, emissivity and field structure in NGC 315 are very similar to those of the other low-luminosity sources we have modelled, but that all of the physical scales are larger by a factor of about 5.

The nearby giant elliptical galaxy NGC 315, whose large-scale radio structure was first imaged by Brid76 , is one of the brightest sources satisfying these criteria. NGC 315 also shows a conical outer region of constant (very small) opening angle, as seen in other FR I jets, but this is beyond the bends which limit our ability to fit an axisymmetric model directly to the observed data. Our results for NGC 315 therefore give a more detailed picture of the initial propagation of an FR I jet.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes B2 0055+30 as an extended radio source with redshifted H I absorption, a small H I emission cloud, and an asymmetric jet/lobe structure.

quotes.quote: B2 0055+30 (NGC 315): \ results of this sources have been published by mor09_NGC315 . The absorption profile contains a broad component slightly redshifted from the systemic velocity as well as a narrow component redshifted by about 460 \ see also hec83 . mor09_NGC315 favour the idea that the broad component may represent gas that is falling into the nucleus, while the narrow component is likely an \ cloud at larger distance from the centre. A small cloud of \ emission is also detected within the host galaxy at roughly the same velocity as the narrow absorption component mor09_NGC315 . B2 0055+30 is the most extended \ radio source in our sample and has an asymmetric jet/lobe structure with a peculiar bend at one end see and references therein lai06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 315 as an FR I radio galaxy with jet structures at arcsecond and milliarcsecond scales and radio spectra measured from prior 49 to 3.6 cm observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 This immense radio galaxy has been the focal point of many radio studies in the past willis81AA:ngc315, venturi93apj:ngc315, cotton99apj:ngc315, canvin05mnras:ngc315, laing06mnras:ngc315 . The radio morphology of NGC 315 indicates a Fanaroff-Riley type 1 (FR I) nucleus with jet structures at both arcsecond and milliarcsecond resolutions. willis81AA:ngc315 present arcsecond resolution observations at 49 and 21 cm of this large radio galaxy. They derive a 49--21 cm constant jet spectral index of =-0.6 . venturi93apj:ngc315 studied NGC 315 at 18, 6, and 3.6 cm using VLBI networks and found the same spectral index as above between 18 and 6 cm, but it steepens at lower wavelengths (see also laing06mnras:ngc315 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 315 as a giant radio galaxy whose jet spectral index decreases with distance, has unusual transverse gradients, and is flatter than most sample jets.

quotes.quote: In a detailed study of the spectrum of the brighter jet in the giant radio galaxy NGC 315 LCCB06 , we found that the jet spectral index decreases with distance from the AGN, contrary to the expectations of simple synchrotron-loss models, and that there is significant transverse spectral structure, with a higher spectral index (steeper spectrum) on the jet axis than at its edges.

The pronounced transverse spectral gradient in NGC 315 therefore seems to be unusual, although we cannot rule out similar effects at a lower level in other sources. This gradient extends over many beam areas at high signal-to-noise (Fig. fig:images b).

NGC 315 A detailed study of the spectral-index distribution was given by LCCB06 . The spectrum of the jets is unusually flat, although less extreme than in our earlier work after correction of the flux-density scale.

sources.summary: NGC315 is reviewed as a giant radio galaxy with a massive black hole whose jet changes from parabolic to conical collimation at sub-parsec scales and remains conical to kiloparsec scales.

quotes.quote: NGC315 is a giant radio galaxy with a black hole mass of 1.3 10^9 M_ , whose sub-parsec scale structure has been studied recently by Boccardi2021 and Park2021 . Boccardi2021 have observed it with higher resolution extending to 86.2 GHz using the Global Millimeter-VLBI Array (GMVA). Both groups find a transition from a parabolic to a conical shape, although Boccardi2021 find it to occur closer to the central engine at a distance of 0.58 0.28 pc or 5 10^3 Schwarzschild radii (Fig. f:NGC315 ). This is much smaller than the Bondi radius which has been estimated to be 92 pc from x-ray observations. The transition appears to occur at sub-pc scales, after which it remains conical to kpc scales.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 315 as a 4FGL radio galaxy whose VLBA jet motion and jet-to-counterjet ratio imply limited Doppler boosting, matching the authors estimate.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 was first identified as a radio galaxy in 4FGL abd20 . par21 employed VLBA observation to investigate the jet collimation and acceleration of NGC 315. They found an apparent outward motion ( =1.85 0.44c ) of the core of NGC 315, then combined the jet-to-counterjet ratio to constrain the jet viewing angle of 50 . They suggested that the flux density of NGC 315 from the Doppler boosting effect is at most by a factor of about two. Our Doppler factor estimation for NGC 315 ( _ =1.31 ) is suggesting that the flux density of NGC 315 is not strongly enhanced by a Doppler factor, in accordance with par21 .

sources.summary: The paper reports a resolved X-ray jet in the giant FRI radio galaxy NGC 315 and relates its jet pressure, radio structure, and nuclear absorption to the surrounding X-ray gas.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report the detection of an X-ray jet in the inner parts of the giant FRI radio galaxy NGC 315, at z=0.0165 . The observation was made as part of our \ programme of imaging and spectroscopy of FRI radio galaxies from the B2 bright sample (Colla et al. 1975; Ulrich 1989), aimed at studying the validity of the unification of B2 radio galaxies and BL Lac objects and at testing physical and dynamical models for the X-ray and radio-emitting structures. \ is one of the X-ray brightest B2 sources studied with \ (Canosa et al. 1999), and consequently only a short \ exposure time was needed to meet our criterion of uniform data quality for the sample. NGC 315 has a two-sided radio structure extending roughly one degree on the sky (Bridle et al. 1979).

If this is the case, the low X-ray column density of NGC 315, whose jets may lie 35^ from the line of sight (see 1), says little about the presence or absence of a nuclear torus, although a second, heavily-absorbed, component might appear in a deeper X-ray observations.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 315 as a nearby giant FR I radio galaxy, measuring jet spectra, Faraday rotation, magnetic structure, and the surrounding hot-plasma halo.

quotes.quote: We present images of the jets in the nearby radio galaxy NGC 315 made with the VLA at five frequencies between 1.365 and 5 GHz with resolutions between 1.5 and 45 arcsec FWHM. Within 15 arcsec of the nucleus, the spectral index of the jets is = 0.61 . Further from the nucleus, the spectrum is flatter, with significant transverse structure.

The giant FR I FR74 radio source NGC 315 was first imaged by Brid76 , who showed that it has an angular size of nearly 1 ^ . The source is associated with a giant elliptical galaxy at a redshift of 0.01648 Trager , giving a scale of 0.335 kpc/arcsec for our adopted cosmology (Hubble constant H_0 = 70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 , _ = 0.7 and _M = 0.3 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 315 as a giant FR I radio galaxy with detectable RM fluctuations whose amplitudes and jet asymmetry are consistent with tenuous group gas.

quotes.quote: The giant FR I radio galaxy NGC 315 is at a redshift of 0.01648 (0.335 kpc/arcsec). We infer an angle to the line of sight of 38 ^ (Canvin et al. 2005). Our RM images, derived from 5-frequency observations in the range 1.365 - 5 GHz, are shown in Fig. fig:ngc315 , together with profiles along the jet axis. The mean RM and a linear gradient along the jet are almost certainly Galactic. After removing these components, we detect RM fluctuations on scales of 10 -- 100 arcsec, but the typical amplitudes are only 2 rad m ^ 2 (Fig. fig:ngc315 e and f). The thermal X-ray emission from NGC 315 has a very small core radius, r_c = 1.55 arcsec (Worrall, Birkinshaw Hardcastle 2003). No X-ray emission has yet been detected from the galaxy group associated with NGC 315 (Miller et al. 2002), but the small amplitude of the RM fluctuations and the fact that they are larger for the receding jet are both consistent with an origin in a tenuous group gas component with a scale size 200 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper reports no significant long-term spectral variations for NGC 315 in Chandra or XMM-Newton data, with only low-confidence hard-band light-curve variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 : From the simultaneous analysis of Chandra data, variations are not found (i.e., SMF0) in a three years period. The annular region contributes with 3 in Chandra data. When comparing with XMM -Newton data, variations of the parameters do not improve the fit within the five years period. The analysis from one of the Chandra light curves shows variations in the hard band at 1.6 confidence level.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 315 with a single summed Swift spectrum, favoring an absorbed power law plus hot gas component consistent with previous models.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 315 could be produced from the available Swift data. Our best-fitting model consists of an absorbed power law, plus a hot gas component ( ^2_R = 1.02 ). Previous spectral modelling of NGC 315 has employed very similar models e.g Matsumoto2001,Terashima2002, Younes2011 . The photon index we measure is very similar to that found by Matsumoto2001 and Younes2011 ( = 1.94 ; slightly higher than that found by Terashima2002 ).

sources.summary: The merged map of NGC 315 recovers its radio morphology, western jet bending, eastern lobe extension, and flux missed by the NVSS map.

quotes.quote: GRG J0057+3021 The merged map of NGC 315 (Fig. 1), similarly as in the case of NGC 6251, shows the extent and morphology of the source to a similar degree as the map at 325 MHz from Mack et al. (1997). Although the continuity of the western jet, as well as its bending are traced more clearly than in the NVSS map, the ``backflow'' part seems to be disrupted, as if the lobe emanated directly from the central source. The eastern lobe and its extension to the north has been reproduced to a significant level in the merged map. Due to both size and morphology of the source, the NVSS map lacks as much as 52 of the total flux restored from the single-dish map.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 315 as a nearby low-accretion radio galaxy whose Fermi-band gamma rays can arise from proton synchrotron emission in a magnetically arrested disk.

quotes.quote: They emit gamma-rays via hadronic processes, which can account for the observed gamma-rays for M87 and NGC 315.
These energies are lower than the Hillas energy, E_ Hil eBR(V_A/) 35 EeV for M87 and 52 EeV for NGC 315.

For NGC 315, the proton synchrotron produces the gamma-rays in the Fermi band, but the TeV gamma-rays by the Bethe-Heitler pairs are completely absorbed, and we cannot detect them even with CTA. Detecting the MeV gamma-rays by the planned satellites is also challenging, although sub-GeV photons are detectable.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 315 as a radio galaxy whose jet collimation ends on sub-parsec scales, with a parabolic-to-conical transition far inside the Bondi radius.

quotes.quote: In this article we present a multi-frequency and multi-scale analysis of the radio galaxy NGC 315, including the use of mm-VLBI data up to 86 GHz, aimed at revealing the evolution of the jet collimation profile. We then consider results from the literature to compare the jet expansion profile in a sample of 27 low-redshift sources, mainly comprising radio galaxies and BL Lacs, classified based on the accretion properties as low-excitation (LEG) and high-excitation (HEG) galaxies. The jet collimation in NGC 315 is completed on sub-parsec scales. A transition from a parabolic to conical jet shape is detected at z_ t =0.58 0.28 parsecs or 5 10^3 Schwarzschild radii ( R_ S ) from the central engine, a distance which is much smaller than the Bondi radius, r_ B 92 pc , estimated based on X-ray data. The jet in this and in few other LEGs in our sample may be initially confined by a thick disk extending out to 10^3 10^4 R_ S .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 315 as a nearby LLAGN source, combining XMM-Newton, Swift, radio, X-ray, and Fermi-LAT data and finding that SSC alone fits its gamma-ray data only to 1.6 GeV.

quotes.quote: We have shown that SSC emission from the sub-parsec scale jet can well explain the broadband spectral energy distributions of these four LLAGNs, while external Compton emission from the kilo-parsec jet is required to explain the ray emission beyond 1.6 GeV and 0.6 GeV in the case of NGC 315 and NGC 4261, respectively.
NGC 315 NGC 315 is a nearby elliptical galaxy, located at a redshift of 0.01648 ( trager ).

We have analysed the X-ray data recorded by XMM-Newton in 2017 from NGC 4486, XMM-Newton data taken in 2019 and Swift data from 2017 to 2018 for NGC 315, and compiled the archival X-ray data available on NGC 4261 and NGC 1275 (see details in Section sec:xray ). In addition, we analysed 12 years of data, collected over the period of 2008 to 2020. Due to low spatial resolution in gamma-ray energy band, the distinction between the emission from the sub-parsec scale and kilo-parsec scale jet cannot be made. The radio and X-ray data points of the extended jets of NGC 315 and NGC 4261 were obtained from observations by Very Large Array (VLA) and Chandra observatories respectively owing to their high spatial resolution ( worrall315 , worrall4261 ).

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC315 as a giant radio galaxy in a gas-rich environment with infalling H I, a possible AGN-fueling absorption component, and H I emission.

quotes.quote: NGC315 has been studied in detail by Morganti09 and emonts10 . Morganti09 suggest that the narrow component which is infalling with a velocity of 490 km s ^ 1 is not close to the AGN but due to clouds falling into NGC315 which appears to be in a gas-rich environment. The relatively broad absorption component (FWZI 150 km s ^ 1 ) is redshifted by 80 km s ^ 1 , and could be fuelling the AGN. H i has also been detected in emission towards NGC315 and its mass is 6.8 10^7 M_ emonts10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 315 to test jet magnetic-field models, finding symmetrical transverse profiles, a 38 degree viewing angle, and mixed longitudinal and toroidal field components.

quotes.quote: A comparison between our best-fitting model and VLA data for the first arcminute of the jets in NGC 315 is shown in Fig. fig:ngc315ivec (Canvin et al. \ 2005).

Fig fig:ngc315transverse shows average transverse profiles of total intensity and degree of polarization for the jets in NGC 315 (Canvin et al. \ 2005). Our modelling shows that the angle to the line of sight is 38^ and that the field must have a a mixture of longitudinal and toroidal components. The profiles are extremely symmetrical, especially in the counter-jet, so we can rule out an ordered helical field at least in this source.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 315 as a giant elliptical hosting B2 0055+30, with GMRT mosaic observations targeting its large FR I radio structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 NGC 315 is a giant elliptical at the core of the poor group WBL 22 in the Group Evolution Multiwavelength Study (GEMS) sample (Forbes et al. 2006), which is part of the Pisces-Perseus supercluster. This galaxy is the optical counterpart of the well-known giant FR I radio galaxy B2 0055+30, which has been studied at many frequencies and angular resolutions (e.g., Bridle 1976, 1979; Willis et al. 1981; Venturi et al. 1993; Mack et al. 1997, 1998; Xu et al. 2000; Laing et al. 2006; Worrall et al. 2007). Given the large angular size of B2 0055+30, the GMRT observations were carried out in mosaic mode to obtain uniform sensitivity, covering the region of interest with two fields centered on RA=00h57m30.0s, DEC= +30^ 24^ 00^ and RA=00h59m00.0s, DEC= +30^ 10^ 00^ .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 0315 as a low-density radio-galaxy environment, noting Faraday-rotation constraints imply intracluster magnetic fields as low as 0.1 G and weaken the modeled ALP signal.

quotes.quote: In Sec. sec:results we will investigate central magnetic between 0.1 G (as found around NGC 0315 which is situated in a galaxy poor environment ) and 10 G as determined for richer environments such as Hydra A .

Such low values for the magnetic field have been inferred from Faraday rotation measure observations of NGC 0315 situated in a low density galaxy group while the high central magnetic field values have been determined in massive cool-core clusters, such as the Hydra A or the Persues cluster .
The same is true if the source is located in a low-density type environment as observed around the radio galaxy NGC 0315 where the intra-cluster magnetic fields can be as low as 0.1 G .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 315 as a giant cD radio galaxy in a cluster, with resolved two-sided radio jets, X-ray jets, a dust disk, and optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 (L1.9) The host galaxy of NGC 315 is a giant cD radio galaxy located in the Zwicky cluster 0107.5+3212 Zwicky96 . Two-sided, well-resolved radio jets are shown in both VLA and VLBI observations Cotton99,Venturi93 . Nagar05 reported an unresolved core in addition to the radio jet at VLBI resolutions.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 315 as a giant elliptical FR I group-central radio source with symmetric jets, knots, a southeastern lobe, and a bent northwestern plume.

quotes.quote: NGC 315 NGC 315 is well-known giant elliptical galaxy classified as a FR I radio source fanaroff1974 , which resides in the central region of the NGC 315 (WBL 22) group. The radio emission has been thoroughly studied at various radio frequencies by many research groups ; and references therein bridle1979,willis1981,klein1994,mack1997, laing2006,worrall2007,giacintucci2011 and has the form of relatively symmetric radio jets with several wiggles and knots, which are inflating wiggles and knots, which extend almost 200 kpc. The SE jet is inflating a radio lobe, while the NW jet undergoes an apparent 180 degree bend and forms an extended plume (Figure fig:chandra_vla_a ).

sources.summary: The paper locates NGC 315's rotation-curve turnover at 5, flags an anomalous velocity point at PA 40, and removes six outer data points from the model.

quotes.quote: NGC 315. The turnover radius of the rotation curve is easily located at 5 by inspection. At 27 on PA 40 the rotation velocity is more than 3 from the mean. Since we do not know if this is a real effect, we eliminate the total of six data points outside of 20 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 315 as a cD galaxy with exceptional isotropy, cautions that its circular-velocity rise may be a modeling artifact, and notes deviant dynamical M/L behavior.

quotes.quote: The rise of the CVC of NGC 315 seen near R_ max in Fig. vcall may not be real; a flat CVC appears also consistent with the kinematic data (see discussion in K+2000).
These two are NGC 315 (thick solid line), a cD galaxy, and NGC 7626 (thick long dashed line), which is classified as E pec.
Only two objects are particularly deviant, NGC 315, where the dynamical value is a factor 2 larger than the stellar population estimates based on Terlevich Forbes ( ) and Trager et al.

gmw_00000686.name: Mrk 324
gmw_00000686.overview: Mrk 324 is a nucleated blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1403.6294, arXiv:astro-ph/0703165] powered by a luminous central starburst knot [arXiv:astro-ph/0703165]. This central star-forming region is kinematically decoupled from the rest of the galaxy [arXiv:1403.6294] and is surrounded by a large-scale expanding rim [arXiv:astro-ph/0703165].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mk 324 as a BCD with a kinematically decoupled central star-forming region, enhanced metallicity, and no evidence that its burst is sustained by a nearby companion.

quotes.quote: A good example in this respect is Mk 324 (Fig. fig:Mrk324_prop , left panel), where the bright, star-formation region, between 4 and 1 arcsec along the major axis seems to be almost completely kinematically decoupled from the rest of the galaxy -- it has lower velocity dispersion and even a solid body rotation profile. The other, less prominent star-formation region around + 2 arcsec also has a lower velocity dispersion. From the colour maps (Fig. fig:BmI ) we do not see a significant young population.

Based on these tests, we conclude that the mass fraction of young stars in Mk324 should be less than 1 per cent. Moreover, it is worth noticing that the individual band images (see Sect. app:photometry ) are showing a pronounced burst, even visible in the J band image (Fig. fig:imaJ ). From our spectroscopic analysis we see that the regions of star formation seem to have significantly higher metallicity, 0.4 dex versus the 1.4 dex of the galactic body.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 324 as a nucleated blue compact dwarf dominated by a young central knot, with diagnostic diagrams tracing its major starburst and expanding rim.

quotes.quote: Mrk 324 is a galaxy classified as a Blue Compact Dwarf BCD, see Thuan81 and is included as a Nucleated BCD in the morphological classification of Cairos et al. (2001), as it is basically powered by a luminous central knot. The star formation history of the galaxy fits well with an instantaneous star formation law with a Salpeter IMF ( =2.35 ) and a range of masses between 1 M/M_ 100, in which a central knot of 4.4 Myr and 1.4 10^5 M _ in stars dominates 95 of the emission flux of the galaxy see Ismael06 . Figure Mrk324diag (upper row of panels) shows the results from the single gaussian fit to the full H SCORPIO data set in the vs I _ peak , vs _c , and _c vs I _ peak diagrams for Mrk 324.

These points in the emission-line peak intensity map (last frame) display their location in the galaxy, which indeed coincides with the major burst of star formation in Mrk 324. A major burst with an estimated mass in stars 4 10^5 M _ and an age of about 4.4 Myr see Ismael05,Ismael06 that causes an H II region with a radius of 250 pc. On the other hand, the data belonging to the inclined bands with a higher velocity dispersion and a lower intensity in the vs I _ peak diagram (see third row of panels in Figure Mrk324diag ) appear well scattered in _c and arise from a large-scale (600 pc in thickness) expanding rim that surrounds the central emitting knot, as shown in the last panel of the third row.

gmw_00000687.name: Mrk 370
gmw_00000687.overview: Mrk 370 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1009.2519, arXiv:2207.11923] characterized by a chain morphology [arXiv:2207.11923, arXiv:astro-ph/0703165] and a heavily warped inner gaseous disk [arXiv:1009.2519]. It features multiple central starburst regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0703165], polar gaseous structures [arXiv:2207.11923], and shock waves indicated by high velocity dispersion [arXiv:2207.11923].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 370 as a blue compact dwarf with a warped gaseous disk, external emission knots, and polar inner gas likely produced by accretion or merging.

quotes.quote: The gaseous disk in Mrk 370 has a more complex structure with a heavily warped innermost part.

Cairos et al. found that the current star formation with the age of 3-6 Myr took place in numerous knots in this luminous ( M_B=-17.20 ) BCD galaxy. Our FPI data show that external emission knots rotate in a plane roughly coinciding with the stellar disk of the galaxy. At smaller radii ( <700-800 pc), circular orbits in the ionized gas disk change orientation abruptly (see Fig. Moiseev_fig1 ). The intrinsic orientation of the disk changes through i=55-70^ (it depends on the accepted outer disk inclination). We suppose that a coherent warped disk observed in Mrk 370 is a small-scale analogue of strongly twisted disks recently found in the galaxies NGC 2685 and NGC 3718 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 370 as a blue compact dwarf with chain morphology and polar gaseous structures; its high-velocity-dispersion points indicate shock-related non-stellar ionization.

quotes.quote: Mrk 370 belongs to Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxies. BCDs are low luminosity (M_ B 18 mag) and compact (starburst diameter 1 kpc) galaxies. Mrk 370 has a chain morphology. Moiseev (2010) [ 8 ] has considered the presence of polar gaseous structures in Mrk 370 due to current burst of star formation and external gas accretion or merging.

Mrk 370: The points with high velocity dispersion are interesting to study. These points indicate the presence of non-stellar mechanisms for the excitation of ionized gas, and the correlation between and confirms the presence of shock waves in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 370 as a chained blue compact dwarf with coeval clusters, structured ionized gas, multiple central star-forming bursts, and expanding gas around its star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Mrk 370 This BCD galaxy belongs to the Chained morphological type see Cai01b , in which the star formation takes place along a privileged direction. It follows the same star formation law as Mrk 324, with 25 young and massive exciting clusters hosted over an underlying stellar component with ages larger than 5 Gyr, ( see Cai02 ). The clusters have an average age of 6.6 Myr with a dispersion of 0.9 Myr, suggesting a coeval and global star formation event.

The ionized gas in Mrk 370 (see the H image in Figure Mrk370diag ) presents much more structure than Mrk 324. However, its vs I _ peak diagram looks almost identical to that shown in Figure Mrk324diag for Mrk 324. The only exception is perhaps the low intensity data points with values of below the supersonic lower limit imposed by the horizontal band ( 21 km s ^ 1 , see first panel in Figure Mrk370diag ). The structure of the photoionized gas in Mrk 370 begins to be disentangled when one selects the data from the horizontal band in the vs I _ peak diagram (see Figure Mrk370diag , second row of panels). Such a data set appears clumped at three different _c values in the vs _c diagram. The brightest one sits at the rest wavelength of the galaxy (6580.1 ). There is a second one at 6579.4 \ and a third one between these two at 6579.8 . Two of them are much more apparent in the _c vs I _ peak diagram where they clearly end up as pointed structures with different intensity (factors of 2) and different central wavelengths. The emission-line peak intensity map tracing the selected points (last frame in the second row) shows two major bursts of stellar formation and two adjacent much less intense, associated to the massive central stellar clusters with masses that range between (8 50) 10^4 M _ see Ismael05,Ismael06 , also resolved by using the INTEGRAL spectrograph with a single gaussian fit to the emission lines. All of these, although near the center of the galaxy, are kinematically resolved as well detached structures. This multiplicity of structures would have been missed if one had only use the vs I _ peak diagram as a diagnostic for this galaxy (see first panel in Figure Mrk370diag ), as the brightest points present almost the same supersonic gaussian profiles. These massive regions also match the area of the galaxy that shows the highest ionization values (see the H emission-line and the [ O III ] /H maps in Cai02 ). The third row of panels in Figure Mrk370diag displays the data from the inclined bands with low intensity and large values in the vs I _ peak diagram. This data set, as the corresponding one in Mrk 324, presents a large range of values of _c , around the systemic velocity of the galaxy. The spread of the data is also noticeable in the _c vs I _ peak plane. The bulk of the data arises from a large galactic volume shown in the emission-line peak intensity map as an expanding rim of gas around the main centers of star formation all over the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper compares model color predictions with resolved Mrk 370 observations, finding its continuum colors well reproduced and its host component redder than the BCD burst population.

quotes.quote: On the other hand the data by cai02 of resolved locations in Mrk 370, shown in the figure as open green triangles are very well reproduced by our continuum colors. The data of Mrk 370 are based on UBVRI broadband and H narrow band observations. In this case, the authors subtracted the contribution of the underlying continuum from the old stellar population, removing the contribution from emission lines and correcting for extinction, measuring in this way true colors of the young star-forming knots. This way while open green triangles correspond to the observed Mrk 370 colors, including the emission lines contribution and the underlying stellar population, the full green triangles are the stellar population colors after correction of extinction, emission lines and old stellar populations.
The colors of the isolated host galaxy (blue stars) show redder colors than the BCD component, in agreement with QBCDs, Mrk 370 host and model colors with ages older than 10 Myr.

gmw_00000688.name: III Zw 102
gmw_00000688.overview: III Zw 102 is an interacting extended blue compact galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0703165] classified as a polar-ring system [arXiv:0808.1696, arXiv:1808.08525]. It features an inner stellar disk and an inclined outer ring, where colliding gas clouds generate shock fronts and trigger star formation [arXiv:1808.08525, arXiv:1401.7912].

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 212 as a peculiar galaxy with kinematically distinct inner and outer gas subsystems, interpreting its warped outer disk as a polar ring likely formed by gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Moiseev The Fabry--Perot scanning interferometer mounted on the 6-m telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory of the Russian Academy of Sciences is used to study the distribution and kinematics of ionized gas in the peculiar galaxy Arp 212 (NGC 7625, III Zw 102). Two kinematically distinct subsystems---the inner disk and outer emission filaments---are found within the optical radius of the galaxy. The first subsystem, at galactocentric distances <3.5 kpc, rotates in the plane of the stellar disk. The inner part of the ionized-gas disk ( <1.5 -2 kpc) exactly coincides with the previously known disk consisting of molecular gas. The second subsystem of ionized gas is located at galactocentric distances 2--6 kpc.

Hence Arp 212 can be classified as a galaxy with a polar ring (or a polar disk). The observed kinematics of the ionized and neutral gas can be explained assuming that the distribution of gravitational potential in the galaxy is not spherically symmetric. Most probably, the polar ring have formed via accretion of gas from the dwarf satellite galaxy UGC 12549.

sources.summary: The paper treats III Zw 102 as an interacting extended blue compact galaxy with widespread young knots, massive nuclear star-forming centers, expanding gas, and H II regions along its arms.

quotes.quote: III Zw 102 This galaxy is believed to be the result of an interaction event see Vor59,Vor77 and has been considered by some authors to belongs to the polar-ring galaxy type Whitmore90 . It is morphologycally considered as an Extended Blue Compact Galaxy Cai01b , in which 65 star-forming regions were identified spreaded over the whole main body of the galaxy, including the arms. The age of the knots and their low age dispersion (6.1 0.6 Myr) suggest a coeval starburst event see Ismael06 .

The galaxy has a plethora of small H II regions spread along two arms as well as several massive centers of stellar formation in the densest nuclear region with masses between 10^5-10^6 M _ , see Ismael05,Ismael06 . All of these, as well as the large galactic volumes undergoing a supersonic expansion leave their signature in the diagnostic diagrams. In this way, the horizontal band with supersonic values at all possible intensities noticeable in the vs I _ peak diagram (see Figure 3, 2 ^ nd row of panels) is spread over a large range of _c values in the vs _c diagram and into at least four pointed structures (at _c = 6596.7 , 6597.25 , 6598 , and 6600 ) of different intensities in the _c vs I _ peak diagram. All of these in the emission-line peak intesity map are well resolved as giant structures within the galactic nuclear region. On the other hand, the larger points at all peak intensities in the vs I _ peak diagram arise from gas expanding around the nuclear zone (see third row of panels in Figure 3). Among this data set is the inclined structure between I _ peak =1100-1400 which is produced by gas between the four major centers of stellar formation in the nuclear region. The broad line structure appears at the center of the galaxy with a _c very similar to the systemic velocity of III Zw 102. Finally, the lowest and lowest intensity data points in the vs I _ peak diagram (last row of panels in Figure 3) appear, as in the case of Mrk370, as a set of well detached vertical stalactites in the vs _c diagram, hanging from the bulk of the data at very distinct _c values. The latter as shown in the last panel are produced by the numerous small H II regions located along the galactic arms.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 212 as a polar-ring system with distinct gas subsystems, a wide inclined outer ring, infalling gas that induces star formation, and shock fronts from cloud collisions.

quotes.quote: Arp 212 -- a Polar Ring Galaxy Arp 212 is a peculiar galaxy in which two rotating gas subsystems that are kinematically different have been discovered: an internal disk of the 3.5-kpc size and outer H II regions whose orbits are inclined at a significant angle to the stellar disk Mois2008 . The observed picture was explained in the assumption that the gas (mostly neutral) in the outer regions of the galaxy is located in a wide ring of a diameter of about 20 kpc rotating in the plane almost orthogonal to the disk. As the radii of the gaseous-cloud orbits decrease, their inclination angle decreases too; and at a radius of 2--3 kpc, the gas from the ring begins to fall out onto the plane of the galaxy inducing a burst of star formation. It is the region with the highest observed velocity dispersion reaching 80--100 (see Fig. diag4 ).
For Arp 212, our approach allowed to confirm the previously assumption in on the direct collision of gaseous clouds on inclined orbits with the main disk of the galaxy generating shock fronts.

sources.summary: The paper uses III Zw 102 to demonstrate VIRUS-P and STARLIGHT analysis, correcting spaxel-by-spaxel stellar absorption and recovering the H flux distribution in the BCD.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in Fig. fig:IIIZw102 preliminary results from the analysis of our VIRUS-P data for the BCD III Zw 102 (Cair os et al., in prep.). We used the STARLIGHT code CFG+04,CFM+05 to generate a stellar continuum fit at each position. Figure fig:IIIZw102 shows the H flux before and after correction for underlying stellar absorption using the best-fitting stellar model for each spaxel. It can be seen that the artifical depression in the H flux distribution in southeastern and northwestern direction could be efficiently rectified this way (Papaderos et al., in prep.).

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 212 as showing direct ISM collisions between the main galaxy plane and inclined polar ring, with shock signatures and triggered star formation.

quotes.quote: The more intriguing case is the Arp 212 galaxy which provides the evidence of direct collisions between the ISM in the main galaxy plane and in the internal part of the inclined polar ring: a peculiar dust lane is observed in the region where the orbits (calculated from FPI velocity field analysis) intersect each other 2008AstBu..63..201M . The integral-field observations of this galaxy also point to a possible contribution of shocks into the gas ionization, as it follows from the emission-line ratio maps [ N ii ] /H 2008ApJ...677..201G and [ O iii ] /H 2012A&A...547A..24C for the region of the matter collision. In this case the shock fronts related with the interaction between multi-spin gaseous components provoke the formation of dense molecular clouds and trigger the burst of star formation observed in Arp 212 now.

sources.summary: The paper finds Arp 212 has a center stable inside 6 arcseconds before substantial changes, double-peaked Fourier behavior, and coefficients decreasing from A1 to A4.

quotes.quote: Arp 212: The centre co-ordinates x0, and y0 remain constant in the inner 6" and then change substantially. All A coefficients show (nearly) double-peaked behaviour. The values of the A coefficients decrease in the order: A _1 > A _2 > A _3 > A _4 .

gmw_00000689.name: B2 0648+27
gmw_00000689.overview: B2 0648+27 is an early-type radio galaxy [arXiv:0807.1541, arXiv:astro-ph/0606303] that formed from a major merger approximately 1.5 Gyr ago [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:0911.4531]. The system features a distorted optical morphology with a broad tidal arm [arXiv:0807.1541], a post-starburst stellar population [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:astro-ph/0508438], and a massive, rotating neutral hydrogen ring [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:1001.5009].

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0648+27 as a nearby radio galaxy evolving from a major merger into an early-type radio galaxy, with a giant H I ring, tidal arm, stellar tail, and delayed starburst/AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We present the detection of a low surface-brightness stellar counterpart to an enormous (190 kpc) ring of neutral hydrogen ( ) gas that surrounds the nearby radio galaxy B2 0648+27. This system is currently in an evolutionary stage between major merger and (radio-loud) early-type galaxy. In a previous paper we investigated in detail the timescales between merger, starburst and AGN activity in B2 0648+27, based on its unusual multi-wavelength properties (large-scale \ ring, dominating post-starburst stellar population and infra-red luminosity). In this Research Note we present deep optical B- and V-band imaging that provides further evidence for the merger origin of B2 0648+27.

The host galaxy shows a distorted optical morphology and a broad tidal arm is clearly present. A low surface-brightness stellar tail or partial ring curls around more than half the host galaxy at a distance of up to 55 kpc from the centre of the galaxy, following the large-scale, ring-like \ structure that we detected previously around this system. The gas and stars in this ring are most likely tidally expelled material that slowly fell back onto the host galaxy after the merger event.

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0648+27 as a compact radio post-merger with a 190 kpc H I ring, distorted optical body, post-starburst population, and delayed radio-AGN activity.

quotes.quote: B2 0648+27: We studied this galaxy in great detail in emo06,emo08_0648 . The \ gas is distributed in a massive, regularly rotating ring-like structure with a diameter of 190 kpc. Deep optical imaging shows a distorted optical morphology and a faint stellar tail or partial ring that follows the \ ring emo08_0648 . The stellar light across the host galaxy is dominated by a 0.3 Gyr post-starburst stellar population emo06 .

We argued that B2 0648+27 formed from a major merger event roughly 1.5 Gyr ago, after which \ gas that was expelled from the system during the merger had time to fall back and settle around the host galaxy emo06 . The radio source is compact, with a minimum estimated age of only about 1 Myr gir05a . The current phase of radio-AGN activity has therefore started late in the lifetime of the merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0648+27 as a rich radio galaxy whose old merger signature is paired with a 0.2 Gyr post-starburst population and a much younger compact radio source.

quotes.quote: For the rich radio galaxy B2 0648+27 we confirm such a merger origin through the detection of a post-starburst stellar population that dominates the visible light throughout this system.

The first radio galaxy in our sample for which we did such an analysis is B2 0648+27, as shown in Fig. 2 (Emonts et al. 2005 in prep.). The early-type host-galaxy is dominated by a post-starburst stellar population of only about 0.2 Gyr old. The offset in time between the early stage of the merger (roughly 2 Gyr ago as concluded from the \ distribution) and the ignition of the starburst in B2 0648+27 can be explained when the progenitor galaxies already contained a bulge component, that stabilized the central region from forming gravitational instabilities until the second encounter of the progenitor galaxies (see models by e.g. ).

Radio sources The radio continuum structure of two of the rich radio galaxies in our sample (B2 0258+35 and B2 0648+27) has been studied in detail at VLBI-scale by . They estimate that the radiative age of these sources is about 1 Myr, although there are also regions of likely much older continuum-emission, which suggests that these sources could have been confined for a significant time.

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0648+27 as an advanced major merger whose post-starburst population, faint stellar tail, and rotating H I ring trace merger, starburst, and AGN stages.

quotes.quote: As is published in Emonts et al. 2006 emo06 , a detailed spectroscopic stellar population analysis of this system shows that a 0.3 Gyr post-starburst stellar population is present throughout this system, which could have given B2 0648+27 the appearance of an (Ultra-) Luminous Infra-Red Galaxy in the first epoch after the starburst was triggered. New deep optical imaging of this system (see Fig. fig:B2detections left ) shows that a faint stellar tail is present in the \ ring. The morphology of the \ ring, the faint stellar tail and the presence of a post-starburst stellar population in B2 0648+27 suggest that this galaxy is an advanced major merger. The merger event in this system must have occurred roughly 1.5 Gyr ago, after which \ and stellar tails -- which were expelled during the merger -- had time to fall back onto the host galaxy and settle in the rotating ring-like structure that we observe. B2 0648+27 is an excellent example of a galaxy in which the different stages of merger, starburst and AGN activity can be traced (see Emonts et al. 2006 emo06 for more details).

sources.summary: The paper presents B2 0648+27 as an early-type radio galaxy with a large H I ring from a 1.5 Gyr merger, a 0.3 Gyr starburst, and delayed radio-AGN activity.

quotes.quote: In this paper we use neutral hydrogen ( ) and optical spectroscopic observations to compare the timescales of a merger event, starburst episode and radio-AGN activity in the radio galaxy B2 0648+27 . We detect a large ring-like structure of \ in emission around the early-type host galaxy of B2 0648+27 ( M_ = 8.5 10^ 9 M_ , diameter = 190 kpc). We interpret this as the result of a major merger that occurred 1.5 Gyr ago. From modelling optical long-slit spectra we find that a young stellar population of 0.3 Gyr, indicative of a past starburst event, dominates the stellar light throughout the galaxy. The off-set in time between the merger event and the starburst activity in B2 0648+27 suggests that the starburst was triggered in an advanced stage of the merger, which can be explained if the gas-rich progenitor galaxies contained a bulge.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0648+27 as a radio galaxy with a massive extended ring or disc and optical merger signatures, contrasting it with AGC 203001.

quotes.quote: The other case is that of the radio galaxy B2 0648+27. It has very large amount of mass ( 1.1 10^ 10 M _ ) distributed in the form of a large ring/disc extending 160 kpc and centered on the host galaxy Morganti03a , as against AGC 203001.
