Moreover, the host galaxy shows clear signs of a major merger in the deeper optical image due to a distorted optical morphology and tidal arms Emonts08 . This again is different from the case of AGC 203001 presented here.

sources.summary: The paper classifies B2 0648+27 as a gas-rich post-merger radio galaxy whose large-scale ring and optical tail formed after a merger about 1.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: We classify B2 0648+27 (Fig. fig:HIdiscs left ) as a gas-rich post-merger system, given the distorted optical morphology of the system and the presence of a dominating post-starburst stellar population throughout the host galaxy see emo06,emo08_0648 . We argue that the large-scale \ ring which has a faint optical counterpart in the form of a long tail or partial stellar ring; emo08_0648 formed as a result of this merger, which must have started roughly 1.5 Gyr ago emo06 . Since then, gas and stars that were expelled during the merger have had the time to fall back onto the host galaxy and settle into the regularly rotating ring that we observe today.

sources.summary: The appendix describes B2 0648+27 as a high CO line-ratio galaxy whose H I ring, optical tails, and young post-starburst population imply a major merger 1.5 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: B2 0648+27: It is detected in CO(1-0) CO(2-1) with a line ratio of 2.82 -the highest of all-. Observed this galaxy with the VLA-C, where they detected HI in emission and in absorption noticing that it is distributed in a large and massive ring-like structure. This distribution and kinematic of the HI gas, together with the presence of faint tails in deep optical imaging Heisler94 and the detection of galaxy-scale post starburst young stellar population, imply that this galaxy formed from a major merger event that happened 1.5Gyr ago.

sources.summary: B2 0648+27 lacks GALEX data, but the paper finds a young population improves its SED fit, identifies it as a HEG, and models MIR/FIR dust.

quotes.quote: B2 0648+27. Unfortunately, no GALEX data are available, but an additional young population does improve the fit (see Fig. fig1 ). Moreover, spectral data show that this galaxy is a HEG (see Emonts et al. ). MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mixture of dust with temperatures of 60 K (dust mass M_d = 1.3 10^6 M_ ) and 130 K ( M_d = 2.9 10^4 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that B2 0648+27 has a more-than-100 kpc H I structure, links it to a merger from a post-starburst population, and notes its low-power radio source may not bore through local ISM.

quotes.quote: For B2 0258+35, B2 0648+27 and NGC 3894 the \ structure is more than 100 kpc in size and contains at least about the \ mass of the Milky Way.
For B2 0648+27 we confirmed a merger origin through the detection of a post-starburst stellar population emo06 .

Although it is not clear how much gas is needed to confine a radio source see e.g. the discussion by hol03 , Giroletti et al. argue that the relatively low power radio sources in NGC 4278 and B2 0648+27 can not bore through the local ISM.

gmw_00000690.name: NGC 4861
gmw_00000690.overview: NGC 4861 is an inclined irregular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1301.5847] classified as a blue compact dwarf [arXiv:2304.12800, arXiv:2105.02302] with a comet-like morphology [arXiv:2105.02302]. It features a highly asymmetric structure where intense star formation is concentrated in a bright southern knot [arXiv:2304.12800, arXiv:astro-ph/0311030], and it shows signs of interaction with its companion, NGC 4861B [arXiv:2307.13738].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4861 is an inclined irregular dwarf whose observed rotation curve is overpredicted by MOND unless implausibly different distance or inclination values are adopted.

quotes.quote: NGC 4861 is a irregular dwarf galaxy with a luminosity L_ B =7.9 10^ 8 L_ and shows no evidence for spiral structure (van Eymeren et al. 2009b). This galaxy was studied in H i by Wilcots et al. (1996), Thuan et al. (2004) and van Eymeren et al. (2009a,b). For _ ^ B =0.3M_ /L_ , the total baryonic mass is 9.1 10^ 8 M_ . It is one of the most inclined galaxies in our selected sample.

The galaxy NGC 4861 The left panel of Figure fig:model_NGC4861 shows the observed rotation curve of NGC 4861 from van Eymeren et al. (2009b) together with the predicted MOND rotation curve (solid lines), for the simple and the standard interpolating functions. The adopted distance was D=7.5 Mpc. We have modelled only the inner 7.5 kpc from the dynamic center because beyond this radius the observed rotation curve is affected by uncertainties caused by the sparsely filled tilted rings. For the stellar disk, we have assumed ^ R _ =0.3M_ /L_ . We see that MOND overestimates the observed rotation velocities at any radius.

sources.summary: The paper presents integral-field spectroscopy of NGC 4861, characterizing its cometary starburst, blue compact dwarf nature, young stellar populations, He II emission, and gas-rich environment.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: From IPAC NED (ned.ipac.caltech.edu), the J2000 co-ordinates of NGC 4861 (the disk galaxy centre) are R.A. 12h59m02.34s and Dec +34d51m34.0s, and the heliocentric recession velocity is 835 km s^ 1 with redshift z=0.00279 , while the velocity relative to the CMB 3K background is given as 1079 km s^ 1 , which would correspond to z=0.003599 or 15.9 Mpc.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4861 was listed in the Arp Atlas of Peculiar Galaxies (Arp 1966) as number 266, in the category of a `galaxy with irregular clumps', and was noted to have a `bright knot at S end'. Wakamatsu et al. (1979) described the galaxy as `resembling the shape of a comet'. The cometary `head', at the southern end of NGC 4861 has a much higher surface brightness than the rest of the galaxy (a relatively faint `tail'), and has been separately catalogued as Markarian 59 (Mrk 59). Wakamatsu et al. (1979) performed spectroscopy of Mrk 59 and found it to be centred on a giant HII region, a nebula undergoing intense star-formation, as evident from its very strong emission lines, especially H and OIII 5007 for which they estimated equivalent widths (EWs) >1000 .

NGC 4861 appears to be a low surface brightness disk galaxy which formed relatively recently, 1 to 2 Gyr ago. The SFR then declined but reactivated 10^8 years ago until the present day, with continuous starbursts, throughout the galaxy where it created hotspots, and especially at its southern end, where it formed a giant nebula and millions of stars. This major starburst event evolved the galaxy into an asymmetric `green pea' or `blueberry' galaxy (depending on where these are divided by mass), or blue compact dwarf.

sources.summary: The survey groups UGC 8098/NGC 4861 with cometary systems whose displaced, intense star-forming centers contrast with smoother old stellar distributions.

quotes.quote: UGC 7326 (classified Im), UGC 7866 (an IABm) and UGC 8098 (an SBm, also named NGC 4861; see also Conselice et al. who present ground-based and Hubble Space Telescope imaging of this galaxy) all show highly asymmetric or `cometary' morphologies (Fig. Figd ) in their continuum-subtracted H + [ N ii ] images, with extremely intense star formation centres which are displaced very significantly from the centres of the old stellar distributions. This is reflected in long central troughs in the EW curves, with sharp rises to very high values when the star formation centres are reached (all growth curves are centred on the peaks of the old stellar light in the R band images).

The decoupling between the smooth old stellar distribution and the highly clumped and asymmetric sites of ongoing star formation is particularly marked in these galaxies. The overall EW values for these three galaxies lie in the range 5--20 nm, much higher than is typical for even late-type spiral galaxies. Local EW values in apertures centred on the H ii complexes lie in the range 50-100 nm for these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies ionized gas structures in NGC 4861 with deep H imaging and echelle spectroscopy, comparing shell and filament expansion velocities with dark-matter-halo escape velocities.

quotes.quote: We want to investigate whether the ionized gas detected in two irregular dwarf galaxies (NGC 2366 and NGC 4861) stays gravitationally bound to the host galaxy or can escape from it by becoming a freely flowing wind. Very deep H images of NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 were obtained to detect and catalog both small and large scale ionized gas structures down to very low surface brightnesses. Subsequently, high-resolution long-slit echelle spectroscopy of the H line was performed for a detailed kinematic analysis of the most prominent filaments and shells. To calculate the escape velocity of both galaxies and to compare it with the derived expansion velocities of the detected filaments and shells, we used dark matter halo models. We detected a huge amount of both small scale (up to a few hundred pc) and large scale (about 1--2 kpc of diameter or length) ionized gas structures on our H images.

sources.summary: The paper predicts NGC 4861 as a LyC leaker candidate, comparing it visually with NGC 2366 and linking its possible leakage to supernova-driven feedback and disrupted gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4861 is another galaxy that our model predicts to be a leaker, although it is less confident about this galaxy compared to NGC 2366. NGC 4861 is similar to NGC 2366 both visually and the fact that it contains an outflow likely driven by supernova feedback vEymeren2009 . Once again, no escape fraction has been measured for this galaxy but it is known from simulations that strong stellar feedback is needed to facilitate the escape of ionising photons and it is encouraging that our model has selected these two galaxies, both of which show evidence for being disrupted by feedback. NGC 1705 is another example of a galaxy selected to be a leaker by our model.

sources.summary: For NGC4861, the authors reject the FAT rotation curve for mass modelling, favor BBarolo, and find inner stellar/dark-matter dominance with gas becoming comparable outward.

quotes.quote: For one galaxy, NGC4861, which is an irregular dwarf galaxy and has a signature of the interaction with its close companion NGC4861B n4861_paper1 , we are unable to get a satisfactory fitting to the rotation curve obtained from FAT while doing the mass modelling. For this source, the rotation curve obtained using FAT gets flattened with a rotation velocity V_ flat 50 kms ^ 1 but the rotation curve obtained from BBarolo does not reach the flat part and keeps increasing with a rotation velocity V_ max 70 kms ^ 1 which is more consistent with the maximum velocity inferred from the moment one map ( 60 kms ^ 1 , see figure 6, biswas2022 ). Besides that, for this source, we compared the moment one residual maps obtained in kinematic modelling using FAT and BBarolo and decided to finalize the rotation curve obtained from BBarolo. The finalized rotation curve for this source that is obtained from BBarolo is shown in figure fig:fat_rotc_bb_ngc4861 and as well as in figure 4a of http://www.physics.iisc.ac/ nroy/garcia_web/kinematics the kinematics section of GARCIA website .

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4861 in the Mrk 59 complex, identifies it with a bright southern knot, and notes a nearby barely extended radio counterpart.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4861 is part of the Mrk 59 complex, together with the dwarf irregular galaxy IC 3961. In particular, NGC 4861 refers to the bright knot in the southern region of the complex (see electronic Fig. ngc4861 ). It has been suggested that it is an HII region powered by massive early type stars. The proposed radio counterpart is a barely extended radio source very close to it.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4861 as a low-metallicity outlier whose [C II] and [O I] SFR relations diverge while [O III] tracks SFR, suggesting ionized gas and less efficient photoelectric heating.

quotes.quote: The behavior of NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 is mainly divergent in the SFR- L_ [ CII ] and SFR- L_ [ OI ] relations, whereas the [ O iii ] _ 88 emission correlates remarkably well with the SFR, suggesting that the gas around massive star clusters is mostly ionized and that PDRs only occupy a limited volume of the ISM.

Low-metallicity galaxies often have warm FIR colors (e.g. 1999ApJ...516..783T,2004ApJS..154..211H,2009A&A...508..645G,2011A&A...532A..56G,2013A&A...557A..95R ), which could explain why galaxies such as NGC 2366 and NGC 4861 show an offset in the SFR- L_ [ CII ] relation compared to more metal-rich galaxies.

Higher values of [ O i ] _ 63 / [ C ii ] + [ O i ] _ 63 occur preferentially in galaxies of lower metal abundance (e.g. NGC 2366, NGC 4861), which might suggest that the importance of the photoelectric effect diminishes in dust deficient environments and other heatings mechanisms (e.g. soft X-ray heating) become more efficient.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4861 as a blue compact, Magellanic-type irregular dwarf, noting its comet-like morphology, blue color, high UV continuum, and star-forming head-tail structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4861 (Mrk 59) is classified as a Magellanic-type irregular galaxy at a distance of 9.95 Mpc 2013AJ....146...86T and is called a blue compact dwarf galaxy because of its blue color and high UV continuum emission 1980ApJ...240...41F, 1981ApJ...247..823T, 1998ApJ...497..601K, 1999ApJ...511..639I, 2006A&A...459...71I, 2009ApJ...700..654E . NGC 4861 was also classified as the first example of a "comet-like" galaxy by . The "comet's head" at the one end is characterized by a high surface brightness star-forming region and elongated low surface brightness area, the "tail", suggesting an irregular dwarf galaxy seen edge on.

gmw_00000691.name: ESO 364-G029
gmw_00000691.overview: ESO 364-G029 is a nearby Magellanic dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:0704.3050] with a morphology characterized by a large dominant bar and a one-sided spiral or tidal arm [arXiv:0704.3050]. Its neutral hydrogen disk extends well beyond the optical boundary [arXiv:0704.3050] and exhibits a mildly asymmetric distribution [arXiv:1301.5847].

sources.summary: The paper presents optical and H I observations of PGC 018396, describing its Magellanic dwarf morphology, asymmetric gas distribution, solid-body inner rotation, H I mass, and possible shells or holes.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the center coordinates were fixed at ( =6^ h 05^ m 43 4, =-33 04 31 ; J2000)

quotes.quote: As part of an effort to increase the number of Magellanic dwarfs with detailed observations, we focus in this paper on the relatively nearby Magellanic dwarf irregular galaxy ( PGC 018396 ). was first reported by , who classified it as a ``dwarf irregular or emission nebulae'' using the ESO (B) Atlas. By combining optical data from the UK Schmidt plates with Parkes observations, concluded that is a dwarf irregular galaxy, although designate the object as ``dwarf irregular or reflection nebula'' in their optical search for nearby dwarf galaxy candidates in the Southern hemisphere.

We presented the analysis of optical imaging observations and radio synthesis observations of the dwarf irregular galaxy . The optical BVI imaging data reveal a morphology characteristic of Magellanic-type spirals and irregulars, with a large dominant bar and a one-sided spiral or tidal arm, although the absolute magnitude of (for a distance D=10.8 1.4 Mpc) is more than a magnitude fainter than that of the . While poorly-defined, the azimuthally-averaged surface brightness profiles show an exponential disk with a possible break at large radii and colors typical of late-type disk galaxies.

The radio synthesis observations reveal an disk extending well outside of the optical extent. The total mass is M_ =(6.4 1.7) 10^8 , yielding a (distance independent) mass-to-blue-luminosity ratio M_ /L_B=(0.96 0.14) / , significantly more rich than the .

sources.summary: The paper highlights ESO 364-G029 as a promising MOND target with Holmberg-II-like baryonic mass, higher inclination, symmetric rotation curve, and observed acceleration below MOND expectations.

quotes.quote: The galaxy ESO 364-G029 is a promising target because its baryonic mass and rotation curve are similar to Holmberg II but presents a higher inclination. Deeper photometric and H i observations of ESO 364-G029, together with further decreasing systematic uncertainties, may provide a strong test to MOND.

ESO 364-G029 has a luminosity of L_ B =6.6 10^ 8 L_ for a distance of D=10.8 Mpc (Kouwenhoven et al. 2007). The models of Bell de Jong (2001) predict a stellar mass-to-light ratio of 0.2 - 0.6 in the blue band, implying a stellar mass of (1.5-4) 10^ 8 M_ . The H i distribution is mildly asymmetric and roughly follows the stellar brightness distribution. The H i mass is 6.4 10^ 8 M_ and the rotation velocity, assuming i=70^ , reaches a value of 42 km s ^ 1 at a distance of 7 kpc. Despite the asymmetric appeareance of the H i , the rotation curve is fairly symmetric.

gmw_00000692.name: ESO 358-51
gmw_00000692.overview: ESO 358-51 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy that recently fell into the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1906.08187]. It features a lopsided stellar disc [arXiv:2302.11895], central star formation [arXiv:1906.08187], and a newly identified one-sided neutral hydrogen tail shaped by ram pressure [arXiv:2302.11895].

sources.summary: The survey finds ESO 358-51 has a newly identified one-sided H I tail, radial orientation consistent with ram pressure, and a lopsided disturbed stellar disc.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).

For example, ESO 358-51 hosts a lopsided stellar disc, which thickens on the side closer to the cluster centre, has an up-bending stellar-light radial profile and shows an isophotal twist (its molecular gas distribution is disturbed, too; zabel2019 ).

sources.summary: FCC 263 is described as a late-type barred spiral recent infaller with perturbed central isophotes, rotating low-dispersion stellar disc, central star formation, and irregular ionised gas.

quotes.quote: FCC 263 (ESO 358-G051) -- Fig. fig:FCC263map FCC 263 is a late-type barred spiral on the north-eastern side of the cluster at R_ proj =0.8^ in the transition from high to low-density regions. It is a recent infaller with perturbed central isophotes. The stellar disc has a maximum rotation velocity v_ max 90 km s ^ 1 at R 30 arcsec and small and constant velocity dispersion at all radii ( 20 km s ^ 1 ). The galaxy shows strong central H emission powered by star-formation. The ionised-gas distribution and kinematics are not entirely regular, with emission extending towards the north, consistent with the molecular gas data Zabel2019 , and suggests that either a tidal encounter or a minor-merger occurred in the past.

gmw_00000693.name: ESO 358-63
gmw_00000693.overview: ESO 358-63 is a late-type spiral galaxy located within the Fornax cluster [arXiv:2302.11895, arXiv:1906.08187]. It features a newly identified one-sided neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:2302.11895], faint outer stellar tails [arXiv:2302.11895, arXiv:1906.08187], and a stellar disc dominated by star-forming H-alpha emission [arXiv:1906.08187].

sources.summary: The survey finds ESO 358-63 has a newly identified one-sided H I tail, radial orientation consistent with ram pressure, and disturbed outer stellar structure.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).

Both ESO 358-63 and NGC 1437B have an up-bending radial profile, a boxy, flaring outer disc, an irregular distribution of dust and star forming regions, and outer stellar tails (not coincident with the \ tail).

sources.summary: FCC 312 is described as the most luminous late-type spiral inside Fornax’s virial radius, with faint tidal tails, a rotating stellar disc, star-forming H emission, and gas consistent with recent infall.

quotes.quote: FCC 312 (ESO 358-G063) -- Fig. fig:FCC312map FCC 312 is a late-type spiral and the most luminous LTG inside the virial radius of the Fornax cluster. It is located in the low-density region at distance R_ proj =1.7^ from the centre of the cluster on its eastern side. Deep FDS imaging shows faint tidal tails warping out from the stellar disc Raj2019 , which has maximum rotation velocity of v_ max 120 km s ^ 1 at R 90 arcsec. At the same distance the velocity dispersion is 30 km s ^ 1 .

The disc is dominated by H emission powered by star-formation, although some highly-excited material protrudes out of the disc near, but not exactly along, the direction of the galaxy minor axis. Star formation together with detected neutral hydrogen Schroder2001 and molecular gas Zabel2019 are consistent with this galaxy being a recent infaller into the cluster potential.

gmw_00000694.name: FCC 306
gmw_00000694.overview: FCC 306 is a small, late-type galaxy [arXiv:2302.11895] that contains gas and ongoing star formation distributed throughout its structure [arXiv:2005.01532]. It exhibits a disturbed stellar morphology [arXiv:2302.11895] and a long, one-sided neutral hydrogen tail pointing away from the Fornax cluster center [arXiv:2302.11895].

sources.summary: The survey identifies FCC 306 as a small late-type Fornax galaxy with a one-sided H I tail, radial orientation, and disturbed stellar morphology.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).
Finally, FCC 306 --- the smallest galaxy in our sample --- exhibits an elongated blue region contained within a redder, circular and slightly offset body.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 306 as the sample galaxy outside the core tile, with gas throughout, blue colors from ongoing star formation, and a likely misclassified off-center NSC candidate.

quotes.quote: The remaining galaxy, FCC 306, lies within the R_ vir /2 , within the NGFS Tile 6. Additionally, 10 of the galaxies show no evidence of gas, while FCC 207 shows evidence of gas emission at its core and FCC 306 contains gas distributed throughout the galaxy.

The exception to this trend is the host galaxy of FCC 306, which shows a weakly decreasing magnitude with wavelength indicating that it is bluer than the rest of the sample. Since this galaxy contains gas emission and ongoing star formation throughout its structure, this bluer colour is unsurprising.
However, the velocity measurement shows that it does not lie in the kinematic centre of the galaxy, thus indicating that this potential NSC has likely been misclassified.

gmw_00000695.name: NGC 1427A
gmw_00000695.overview: NGC 1427A is a massive irregular galaxy located near the center of the Fornax cluster [arXiv:2102.01185]. It features a broad, starless neutral hydrogen tail pointing away from the cluster center, which is likely shaped by ram pressure [arXiv:2102.01185, arXiv:2302.11895].

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 1427A as a massive irregular Fornax detection with a long H I tail, central cluster location, high velocity, and uncertain cluster dynamical state.

quotes.quote: NGC 1427A is classified as Im-III by . The morphology shows a very long tail pointing to the south-east in projection (opposite direction to the centre of the cluster - we refer the reader to for a detailed study about the origin of the tail). NGC 1427A has a = (2.1 0.4) 10 ^9 M _ and a = (2.3 0.5) 10 ^9 M _ . This is the second most massive galaxy of our sample, which has an content comparable to that of non-cluster (see Fig. fig:himfraction ). NGC 1427A is the closest detection to the centre of the cluster (0.2 R_ vir ).

Due to its high velocity (see Fig. fig:phsp ) it is not clear whether NGC 1427A is already virialised in the cluster or not. If it is virialised, it may decrease its velocity while reaching its apocentre. It is the only / upper limit with >3 10 ^9 M _ in Fig. fig:hionh2 .

sources.summary: The survey revisits NGC 1427A, finding its broad H I tail belongs to a radial, starless tail sample whose geometry supports ram pressure.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).
The clearest exception is that of NGC 1427A, whose tail's width is larger than the size of the host galaxy.

gmw_00000696.name: NGC 1437A
gmw_00000696.overview: NGC 1437A is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:2302.11895] that is a recent infaller to the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1906.08187, arXiv:2302.11895]. It exhibits active star formation [arXiv:1906.08187], an arrow-shaped stellar morphology [arXiv:2302.11895], and a newly detected neutral hydrogen tail shaped by ram pressure [arXiv:2302.11895].

sources.summary: FCC 285 is described as a very late-type spiral recent infaller with regular stellar rotation despite irregular morphology, tiny dispersion, widespread H emission, co-rotating gas, and active star formation.

quotes.quote: FCC 285 (NGC 1437A) -- Fig. fig:FCC285map FCC 285 is a very late-type spiral located on the south-eastern side at a distance of R_ proj =1.2^ from the cluster centre. Despite its irregular morphology, the galaxy shows a stellar velocity map with a regular rotation pattern with v_ max 20 km s ^ 1 at R 20 arcsec. The stellar velocity dispersion is extremely small ( 5 km s ^ 1 ) at all radii. Intense H emission is detected over the entire extent of the galaxy. It mainly consists of HII-regions all along the spiral structure.

The ionised gas co-rotates with the stellar disc. FCC 285 is one of the recent infallers in the low-density region of the cluster. This is consistent with the active star formation and presence of neutral hydrogen Schroder2001 .

sources.summary: The survey reports a newly detected H I tail in NGC 1437A, with radial orientation and arrow-shaped stellar morphology supporting tidal disturbance followed by ram-pressure shaping.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).
NGC 1427A and NGC 1437A have a strongly lopsided, arrow-shaped stellar body, with a concentration of star-forming regions on the head side of the ``arrow''.

gmw_00000697.name: NGC 1437B
gmw_00000697.overview: NGC 1437B is an edge-on, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2102.01185, arXiv:1906.08187] with a rotating stellar disc [arXiv:1906.08187] and a southern gas elongation [arXiv:2102.01185]. It features pervasive central star formation [arXiv:1906.08187] and a faint candidate neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:2302.11895], possibly indicating interaction with FCC 323 [arXiv:2102.01185].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1437B as an H I-rich edge-on Sd galaxy with southern gas and molecular elongation, optical tidal-tail evidence, and possible interaction with FCC 323.

quotes.quote: NGC 1437B is classified as Sd (edge-on) by . The distribution shows an elongation to the south, while the peak corresponds to the optical centroid (Fig. fig:allmorph ). This galaxy is located on the south east of the cluster, close to FCC 323 both on the sky and in velocity (see Fig. fig:distrib and Fig. fig:phsp ). Given the small separation of only 120 kpc with a difference of 10 in velocity, they might be part of an interacting subgroup of galaxies. NGC 1437B has a = (2.4 0.6) 10 ^8 M _ and a = (5.00 0.01) 10 ^9 M _ and it is a rich galaxy.

Indeed, although the scales are different, we found consistency between the southern elongation of NGC 1437B and the elongation of the molecular morphology detected by . Thus, the agreement between its / ratio and the xGASS scaling relation might be due to a slower evolution with respect to that taking place in NGC 1436. did not observed any hint of optical morphological transition from the late-type spiral structure, which supports the hypothesis of a recent infaller.

sources.summary: FCC 308 is described as a late-type spiral intermediate infaller near FCC 306, with dust-perturbed central regions, a rotating disc, central star-forming ionised gas, and inner spiral HII regions.

quotes.quote: FCC 308 (NGC 1437B) -- Fig. fig:FCC308map FCC 308 is a late-type spiral which is close to FCC 306 at R_ proj =1.8^ from FCC 213 (NGC 1399). It is one of the intermediate infallers located in the low-density region on the south-eastern side of the cluster. The central regions are perturbed by dust, which also affects the stellar kinematic maps. The disc has a maximum rotation velocity of v_ max 50 km s ^ 1 at R 50 arcsec.

The velocity dispersion is 50 km s ^ 1 at all radii and tends to increase along the minor axis. Ionised-gas emission is detected throughout the central region, where star-formation is pervasive. The ionised-gas distribution is characterise by the presence of several HII-regions associated to inner spiral structure.

sources.summary: The survey finds NGC 1437B has the weakest candidate H I tail, radial orientation, and disturbed outer stellar structure in the Fornax tail sample.

quotes.quote: Six of our detections stand out for showing long, one-sided \ tails: ESO 358-51, ESO 358-63, FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B. All are late-type spirals (from Scd to Irr; raj2019 ) and all were already known to host . In fact, they represent six of our nine brightest \ detections so far (all with \ >10^8 ) --- the other three being NGC 1365, NGC 1436 and ESO 358-60. With the exception of NGC 1427A leewaddell2018 , none of these six galaxies was previously known to host an \ tail.

In ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 (both in the northern part of Fornax) the tails point towards the cluster centre, while in FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B (all in the southern part of Fornax) they point away from it. Considering that rich galaxies are thought to be recent arrivals in Fornax e.g., loni2021 and that most galaxies enter clusters on fairly radial orbits e.g., mamon2019 , the tail's orientation is consistent with them being shaped by ram pressure. Furthermore, it suggests that ESO 358-51 and ESO 358-63 are already past pericentre, while FCC 306, NGC 1427A, NGC 1437A and NGC 1437B are still on their way towards it (more on this below).
The most extreme case is that of NGC 1437B, where we only detect two faint clouds south east of the galaxy, making this the only feature whose exact tail nature could be questioned.

gmw_00000698.name: SBS0335-052
gmw_00000698.overview: SBS 0335-052 is an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy pair embedded in a shared neutral hydrogen cloud [arXiv:2202.08231]. The system is undergoing a vigorous starburst [arXiv:0807.1098] that drives a giant ionized outflow cone and large-scale filaments through its halo [arXiv:2212.01239].

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 335-052 as a young, metal-deficient damped Ly absorber whose weak diffuse Ly emission is regulated by large static H I coverage and dust absorption.

quotes.quote: Damped absorption system: SBS 335-052 HST/GHRS spectroscopy thuan97a has revealed these metal-deficient BCDs to be damped Ly \ absorbers. They don't show, indeed in our images, any direct emission but a low surface brightness diffuse halo. The Ly \ photons manage to escape from these galaxies after multiple scattering events. Figure sbs_plots shows how Ly \ is related to different physical parameters. We see, for SBS 335-052 , in the first frame, a weak diffuse component which accounts for the whole Ly \ emission.

Very Large Array 21 cm observations thuan97b show the BCD to be embedded in a large H i \ cloud. The H i \ column density in the GHRS aperture derived by these authors is very large, N (H i ) = 7.0 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 . According to mashesse03 and the evolutionary models of tenorio99 , this galaxy is a very young starburst of which the age of the stellar population is too small to have ionised the whole surrounding medium, leading to a great amount of neutral gas covering the massive stars.

sources.summary: The review describes SBS 0335--052 as a pair of interacting blue compact dwarfs in a common H I cloud, with vigorous star formation and extremely low metallicity.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335--052 and IZw 18 (Mrk 116) : The first target consists of a pair of two BCDs, SBS 0335--052W and SBS 0335--052E (see Figures fig6 and fig7 ), embedded in a common H i cloud and exhibiting vigorous star formation. This includes the presence of young SSCs, possibly a consequence of their interaction. The most interesting aspect of this system ( D 55 Mpc; M_ V 16 ^ M . 8, ) is its extremely low metallicity. For the brighter eastern source find 12 + log(O/H) = 7.11 -- 7.32 in different H ii regions. In the fainter, western one projected 22 kpc away from SBS 0335--052E they obtain for the main source 12 + log(O/H) = 7.22 0.07. However, in three less conspicuous ionized bubbles, the metallicity is exceedingly low, with values of 7.01 0.07, 6.98 0.06 and 6.86 0.14.

Thus SBS 0335--052 is a clear candidate for the class of 'young dwarfs' discussed in Section S5.1 . The spectral energy distribution (SED) of SBS 0335--052E (see Figure fig8 ) covering six orders of magnitude in wavelength shows the typical peaks due to stellar emission near optical wavelengths and the dust emission in the infrared, as well as additional free-free emission at longer wavelengths . Noteworthy is that even at 870 m ( 345 GHz) almost 90 of the emission appears to represent free-free emission, likely because the ISM is affected by (1) a high level of star formation and (2) a low dust-to-gas mass ratio (see also Section S3.3 ). CO remains undetected .

sources.summary: The paper studies SBS 0335-052E as a compact metal-poor dwarf with 16 kpc ionized filaments, offset neutral gas, and a giant star-formation-driven outflow cone through its halo.

quotes.quote: The onset of kiloparsec-scale ionised filaments in the halo of the metal-poor compact dwarf SBS 0335-052E was previously not linked to an outflow. In this paper we investigate whether these filaments provide evidence for an outflow. We obtained new VLT/MUSE WFM and deep NRAO/VLA B-configuration data of the galaxy.

We report the prolongation of a lacy filamentary ionised structure up to a projected distance of 16 kpc at SB _ H = 1.5 10^ 18 erg s cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . The filaments exhibit unusual low H /H 2.4 and low [ O iii ] /H 0.4 - 0.6 typical of diffuse ionised gas. They are spectrally narrow ( 20 km s ^ 1 ) and exhibit no velocity sub-structure.

The morphology and kinematics of H I and H II reveal how star-formation-driven feedback interacts differently with the ionised and the neutral phase. We reason that the filaments are a large-scale manifestation of star-formation- driven feedback, namely limb-brightened edges of a giant outflow cone that protrudes through the halo of this gas-rich system.

sources.summary: The review says SBS0335-052 rivals IZw18 in very low oxygen abundance, has complex H I and HST morphology, and is probably old despite earlier claims of youth.

quotes.quote: SBS0335-052 For a long time I Zw18 seemed to play in its own league, with no other BCG coming really close to its low oxygen abundance in the H ii gas. However the entrance of SBS0335-052 on the stage changed the situation. This galaxy was found in the Second Byurakan Survey (SBS, Markarian and Stepanian 1983).

A number of papers from 1990 and onwards have shown it to be a galaxy with an oxygen abundance comparable to that of IZw18 (Izotov et al. 1990, 1997b; Melnick et al. 1992). Melnick et al. (1992) and Izotov et al. (1997b) both find an oxygen abundance of 1/40 of the solar value. The analysis by Melnick et al. (1992) suggest that the O abundance may be a factor of two higher in a north-western H ii region. Thuan et al. (1997) argues that the oxygen abundance in the neutral gas may be even smaller by a factor 100. Mid infrared observations with ISO revals the presence of dust, and a gas to dust ratio typical for more metal rich BCGs (Thuan et al. 1999). VLA observations have revealed an H i mass of 2 10^9 M_ and a dynamical mass a factor of a few larger (Pustilnik et al. 1999).

Thus, SBS0335-052, like IZw18, is probably not a genuinely young galaxy. In some sense SBS 0335-052 is more extreme than IZw18 in that despite being almost as metal-poor as IZw18, it is more luminous ( M_B = -16.7 ) and thus lies further off the metallicity--luminosity relation for dIs, see Fig. 10.

sources.summary: The paper studies SBS 0335-052 as a low-metallicity blue compact dwarf undergoing a vigorous starburst, with massive clusters whose age spread suggests successive cluster formation.

quotes.quote: In an effort to investigate massive star cluster formation in an environment similar to that which might be found in primordial galaxies during the time ancient globular clusters were prolifically formed, we have targeted the galaxy SBS 0335-052 (E) for a detailed multi-wavelength study. SBS 0335-052 is a remarkable blue compact dwarf galaxy at a distance of 54 Mpc (NED NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database ) that is well-known for its extremely low oxygen abundance of 1/40Z_ Izotov90,Melnick92,Izotov01 . Unlike I Zw 18 and SBS 0335-052W, which have slightly lower metallicities, SBS 0335-052 is undergoing a vigorous starburst with a star formation rate 1 M_ yr ^ 1 and hosts extremely massive young clusters, making it ideal for this study Thuan97, Thuan99, Hunt01, Dale01, Plante02, Hunt04, Houck04 . Here we present new near-infrared and archival optical high-resolution observations of SBS 0335-052 with the goal of studying SSC formation and evolution at low metallicity.

We have already established a significant age spread between the SSCs in SBS 0335-052 ( agesec ). In this section, we investigate the possibility of successive cluster formation in the galaxy. Thuan97 demonstrated a systematic increase in the [ F569W ] [ F791W ] color away from SSC 1.

sources.summary: The paper discusses SBS 0335-052 as a nearby starburst with broad absorption, higher extinction, high column density, and dust-enhanced photon destruction in a static ISM.

quotes.quote: Four of the eight \ galaxies observed with GHRS/HST by kunth98 show broad \ absorption profiles: II Zw 70, Mrk 36, SBS 0335-052, and \ studied here. As already noted by these authors, these objects clearly differ from those with \ in emission by very low velocity shifts between the interstellar absorption lines and the systemic velocity Two of the objects with \ in absorption, \ and SBS 0335-052, have also been observed with FUSE. with a large aperture. The measurements of grimes08 confirm the earlier finding of low velocity shifts, now also on a much larger aperture. . An essentially static ISM appears therefore as one of the main factors leading to \ absorption, as already concluded by these authors and as supported by our radiation transfer modeling. Furthermore, among the \ absorbers, SBS 0335-052 and II Zw 70 show clearly higher extinction, with E(B-V) = 0.18 and 0.15 respectively (less than 0.02 for Mrk 36, izotov98 ).

For SBS 0335-052 \ reaches high values, up to 9.4 10^ 20 2 \ Pustilnik01 . Similarly, Mrk 36 shows a high column density peak up to 2.4 10^ 21 2 \ bravo04 . Of course, depending on the efficiency of dust destruction, some spatial regions with diffuse \ emission may be expected; however, this is not necessarily the case. For example, for SBS 0335-052 we know that \ absorption is observed over a large area, showing that absorption by dust must be important atek08 .

sources.summary: The paper models SBS 0335-052 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf with a young compact starburst whose dust, FIR luminosity, dust temperature, and H2 abundance can be explained together.

quotes.quote: To gain insight into primeval galaxies at high redshift, we examine the dust content of the nearby extremely low-metallicity galaxy SBS 0335-052 which hosts a very young starburst ( 10^7 yr). In young galaxies, the dust formation rate in Type II supernovae governs the amount of dust, and by incorporating recent results on dust production in Type II supernovae we model the evolution of dust content. If the star-forming region is compact ( 100 pc), as suggested by observations of SBS 0335-052, our models consistently explain the quantity of dust, far-infrared luminosity, and dust temperature in this low-metallicity object.

We focus on the comparison with the extremely metal-poor (1/40 Z_ ) BCD SBS 0335-052, one of the few possible local counterparts of a primordial galaxy in the nearby Universe (Vanzi et al. \ 2000). Because of its young age, the contribution of winds from late-type stars to dust formation is assumed to be negligible. We consider the dominant (brightest) star-forming complex, and assume that grains produced by SNe II are accumulated within this region.

Finally, we conclude that if the star forming region of SBS 0335-052 is compact ( r_ SF 100 pc) and the gas density is large ( 10^3 cm ^ 3 ), the young age, the dust amount, the FIR luminosity, dust temperature, and the 2 abundance are mutually consistent and explained by our models.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 0335 052 as an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf in a shared HI envelope, with bright super-star clusters and patchy low-surface-brightness emission.

quotes.quote: The relative proximity of these three BCDs [ D = 54.3 Mpc for SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W (TIL); D = 12.6 Mpc for I Zw 18 Ostlin2000 ] , their extremely low metallicities [ 0.025Z _ for SBS 0335 052 Izotov1999a , 0.020Z _ for SBS 0335 052W Lipovetsky1999 , and 0.020Z _ for I Zw 18 Izotov1999a ] , and high neutral gas content see Pustilnik2001 for SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W and vanZee1998 for I Zw 18 make them the best local approximations to primordial young galaxies. Their relatively bright apparent magnitudes ( m_B=17.0 mag for SBS 0335 052, m_B=19.4 mag for SBS 0335 052W and m_B=15.8 mag for I Zw 18) make them much easier to study than the very faint and small building-block dwarf galaxies at high-redshift. HI VLA mapping Pustilnik2001 shows SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W to be embedded in a common very large HI envelope with an overall size of 66 22 kpc. There are two prominent HI peaks separated in the east-west direction by 22 kpc (84 ). The eastern peak is associated with SBS 0335 052, while the western peak is about a factor of 1.3 brighter in the HI line and is associated with the fainter SBS 0335 052W.

Optical HST imaging shows that nearly all star formation in SBS 0335 052 occurs in six very blue super-star clusters (SSCs) with absolute V luminosities between -11.7 and -14.7 mag, and surface brightnesses 100 times those of clusters and associations in normal HII regions.
On larger scales, the light of SBS 0335 052 is dominated by a patchy and filamentary very blue low surface brightness (LSB) emission.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SBS 0335-052E's lower-mass cluster population, noting its six massive young clusters, very low metallicity, young candidate clusters, and high cluster formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335-052E The nuclear region of this extreme metal-poor galaxy is dominated by 6 massive young star clusters which have been referred to in the literature as super star clusters (SSCs). These SSCs have been extensively studied before (e.g., 2008AJ....136.1415R ; 2009ApJ...691.1068T ; A2010b among the most recent published works on this subject). In the present work we explore the lower mass cluster population of the galaxy.

The photometric properties of the final cluster population are shown in the color-color diagram in Figure  mas-age . The filter combination corresponds to a U-B (F330W F435W) versus a B-V (F435W F550M) color. The colors are not dereddened but clearly suggest that the cluster candidates are not older than 100 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper presents SBS 0335--052E as an extreme metal-deficient blue compact dwarf with unusually strong mid-infrared and hot-dust emission, used as the benchmark for red WISE colors.

quotes.quote: The WISE mission has an advantage because it is directed to produce an all-sky photometric survey at wavelengths 3.4 m ( W1 ), 4.6 m ( W2 ), 12 m ( W3 ) and 22 m ( W4 ) with a sensitivity at 12 - 24 m that is 1000 times higher than that of the Infrared Astronomical Satellite ( IRAS ) and has an angular resolution of 6 \ at 3.4 m. T99 first showed (from ISO spectroscopy) that one of the most metal-deficient BCDs, SBS 0335--052E e.g. I90 , is extraordinarily bright in the MIR range, implying a large amount of warm ( 100 - 300K) dust. Later, H04 based on Spitzer spectra have confirmed the presence of warm dust in SBS 0335--052E and found that the dust emission peaks at a wavelength of 28 m, much shorter than that for the bulk of star-forming galaxies.

This longward rising of MIR emission implies the presence of hot (up to 1000K) dust emission. The properties of SBS 0335--052E in the MIR range are very different from that in another extremely metal-deficient BCD with similar metallicity, I Zw 18.

sources.summary: The paper presents SBS 0335 052E as a very metal-poor BCD with low cold dust mass, no direct old population evidence, and disputed dust-to-gas estimates.

quotes.quote: The two most-metal deficient galaxies, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335 052E are among the galaxies of the Herschel sample with the lowest cold dust mass (Table tab1 ). In Fig. fig10 a, they have low M_ cd / M_* mass ratios of 10 ^ 4 , which are lower by a factor of 10 than the average value for the sample.

There is no direct evidence for an old stellar population in SBS 0335 052E. P04 inferred from the integrated light of SBS 0335 052E that the stellar-to-total baryon mass fraction in this galaxy is 0.035.

H13 derived a similar dust-to-gas mass ratio for I Zw 18, but at a value 5000 times higher for SBS 0335 052E. The difference in the case of SBS 0335 052E is in part due to that H13 derived a dust mass, which is a factor of 40 higher than the value in this paper (Table tab1 ), the latter being consistent with RR13 .

sources.summary: SBS 0335-052E is part of an interacting XMP pair in a common H I envelope, with extended gas perpendicular to the optical extension and a velocity gradient along a tidal tail.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335-052E and SBS 0335-052W From Ekta, Chengalur Pustilnik (2009; their Fig. 1, 4 and 5), with the GMRT. The low resolution ( 40 ) map shows this interacting pair of XMP galaxies ( D 20 kpc), enveloped in a common H 1 structure (PA _ HI 175 ^ ), with each H 1 peak corresponding to a galaxy. At intermediate ( 20 ) resolution, the individual single-peaked H 1 structures are distinguishable. The elongated disks are oriented along approximately the same direction (PA _ HI 145 ^ for the Eastern, and PA _ HI 150 ^ for the Western, component).

In the case of SBS 0335-052E, the extended H 1 emission is approximately perpendicular to the Northwestern extension seen in the optical image (PA _ opt 45 ^ ). The velocity gradient in SBS 0035-052W changes from the center (approximately North-South) to the outskirts (Northeast-Southwest). For SBS 0335-052E, the velocity gradient is along the tidal tail (Northeast-Southwest), but deviates from a standard disk. High resolution ( 9 ) observations show that SBS 0335-052W maintains the overall H 1 position angle seen at lower resolutions, with an extension to the Northeast. The direction of the velocity gradient is approximately Northwest-Southeast. SBS 0335-052E reveals extended emission (PA _ HI 20 ^ ), roughly aligned with the optical cometary tail.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 0335-052 as an XMP interacting galaxy pair with adopted H I masses, no MPA-JHU SFR estimate, and low star-formation efficiency behavior.

quotes.quote: The Dwarf Galaxy Survey (DGS; Madden et al. 2013) contains 18 sources in common with the original XMP sample, only three of which possess CO observations that yield H _2 mass upper limits (Cormier et al. 2014): SBS 0335-052, IZw 18 and VII Zw 403 (UGC 6456). The integrated measurements for these XMPs are included in the subsequent analysis, where _ H_2 (Spitzer, APEX Atacama Pathfinder Experiment , Mopra 22-m and IRAM Institut de Radioastronomie Millim etrique 30-m data), _ HI (see Madden et al. 2013 for details) and _ SFR (Spitzer data) are sampled within r_ dust 50 -- 90 or r_ CO 10 -- 30 (the dust and CO aperture, respectively).

Two of these sources, the XMPs SBS 0335-052 and IZw 18 (see Filho et al. 2015 and references therein for details; see also the Appendix), are documented interacting galaxies, and UM 461 is suspected to be interacting (Doublier et al. 1999). These sources could represent a transient, perhaps less advanced phase of the interaction process, whereby the HI (and possibly the H _2 ) content is high, but they have not begun forming stars at the appropriate rate.

SBS 0335-052 is a well-known interacting pair of galaxies (SBS 0335-052 East and SBS 0335-052 West; Izotov et al 1990; Pustilnik et al. 2001), and (individual total) HI mass values (Filho et al. 2013 and references therein) have been adopted, which are consistent with the (total) HI mass quoted by Cormier et al. (2014; log(M _ HI /M _ ) 8.6). A MPA-JHU SFR estimate is not available for this interacting pair.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBS0035-052E among the lowest-sulphur-abundance galaxies and compares its sulphur and oxygen abundances for different knots, finding a factor-of-two S/O discrepancy for knots 1+2.

quotes.quote: On the other extreme, the lowest sulphur abundances are found for objects in the \ Gal sub-sample, with the lowest values, of about only 2 of the solar one, found for SBS0035-052E and IZw18. In these two cases the comparison with the abundances derived by the authors of the articles of reference is difficult.

The situation is similar for SBS0035-052E. There is a large difference between the value of T _e ( [ SIII ] ) given by 2009AandA...503...61I , 18704 K, and the one obtained here, 13360 K, for knots 1+2 that translates into larger values of the total sulphur abundance in the latter case by 0.29 dex. Since the oxygen abundance derived by both 2009AandA...503...61I and the present work is identical (7.28 0.01), the difference in the sulphur abundance implies a difference in the S/O abundance of about a factor of 2. On the other hand, as in the former case, the results obtained for the other region analysed in this galaxy, knots 4+5, are fully consistent in the two works.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS0335-052 as a very metal-poor dwarf whose MIR spectrum lacks UIBs, shows dust absorption and recent star formation, and contains super star clusters.

quotes.quote: In Figure cvfs we show ISOCAM spectra covering 5 to 17 m for 3 galaxies from our [ CII ] survey, IIZw40, NGC 1140 and NGC 1569 along with that of the notoriously metal-poor SBS0335-052 . While the UIBs are not obvious in the spectra of IIZw40 and SBS0335-052, and are only very weakly present NGC 1569, they can be distinguished in the spectrum of NGC 1140. In SBS0335-052, a very low metallicity galaxy (1/40 solar), A _ v 20 deduced from the absorption in the ISOCAM MIR spectra (Figure cvfs ) .

The presence of a significant amount of dust in such a low metallicity galaxy is surprising, since star formation in SBS0335-052 began as recently as 100 Myr ago . These galaxies contain evidence for Wolf-Rayet stars and super star clusters have been detected in NGC 1140 , NGC 1569 and SBS0335-052 . Likewise we see the destruction of the UIBs in the beam- averaged spectrum of the entire galaxy of IIZw40 and SBS0335-052 (due to our lack of spatial resolution we do not see the details within these galaxies in the MIR).

sources.summary: The paper includes SBS 0335--052 among the very metal-deficient galaxies, reanalyzing its helium abundance with lower preferred T(He ii) values than earlier work.

quotes.quote: In sections 4 to 7 we redetermine the He ^+ /H ^+ values for I Zw 18, NGC 2363, Haro 29, and SBS 0335--052, based on the observations of izo97 and izo99 and on photoionization models for these objects. These four objects were selected from their sample based on the following criteria: the two objects with the lowest heavy element abundances and the two objects with the smallest observational errors pei01 . We derive He ^+ /H ^+ , N_e (He  ii ), T (He  ii ), and (3889) self-consistently based on all the observed He  i line intensities, with the exception of those strongly affected by underlying absorption. By combining the He ^+ /H ^+ values of these four objects with that of NGC 346 we derive a new Y_p (nHc) in section 8.

The Y_p (nHc) value derived by us is significantly smaller than the value derived by izo98 , from the Y - O/H linear regression for a sample of 45 BCGs, and by izo99 , from the average for the two most metal deficient galaxies known (I Zw 18 and SBS 0335--052), that amount to 0.2443 0.0015 and 0.2452 0.0015 respectively. Our T_e (He  ii ) determinations, that we prefer, produce Y values that are about 0.007 smaller than those derived by izo98 and izo99 , a small but significative difference, as we will see below. It should be noted that the abundances for NGC 2363, SBS 0335--052, Haro 29, and I Zw 18 obtained by both groups are based on the same observations.

sources.summary: The paper presents SBS 0335 052 as a local low-metallicity dwarf starburst with an unusual MIR-bright dust SED, reproduced by the unmixed dust model but not the mixed case.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335 052 is a local galaxy ( 54 Mpc ) with SFR = 1.7 M_ yr ^ 1 and extremely low metallicity Z = 1/41 Z_ . This galaxy is known to have an unusual IR SED and strong flux at N--MIR. It has a very young starburst ( age < 5 Myr ) without significant underlying old stellar population.

We present the model and the observed SED of SBS 0335 052 in Figure fig:sed_sbs . The very strong N--MIR continuum of SBS 0335 052 is well reproduced by the SED of unmixed case, but the mixed case seriously underpredicts its MIR continuum.
The model roughly reproduced the observed SED of a local low-metallicity dwarf SBS 0335 052 which has a peculiar strong and MIR-bright dust SED.

sources.summary: The paper applies a young-galaxy dust-emission model to SBS 0335 052, reproducing its strong near-to-mid-infrared continuum and lack of cold far-infrared emission.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335 052 \ is a local galaxy ( 54 Mpc ) with SFR = 1.7 M_ yr ^ 1 hunt01 and extremely low metallicity Z = 1/41 Z_ . This galaxy is known to have an unusual IR SED and strong flux at N--MIR. It has a very young starburst ( age 5 Myr ) without significant underlying old stellar population vanzi00 .

We show the model SEDs for SBS 0335 052 \ in Figure fig:sed_sbs . The very strong N--MIR continuum of SBS 0335 052 \ is well reproduced. Our present model SED naturally reproduces the strong N--MIR continuum and the lack of cold FIR emission of SBS 0335 052.

sources.summary: The paper finds SBS 0335-052 has a significantly decreased derived temperature and helium abundance, with solutions sensitive to assumptions and self-absorption uncertainty.

quotes.quote: However, the derived temperatures for SBS 0335-052 and SBS 940+544N both decreased significantly (16,200 to 10,700 K and 18,500 to 12,800 K respectively) and showed corresponding helium abundance decreases. The newly derived temperatures are significantly different from those indicated by the [ O III ] emission lines.

Note that there are two objects where the difference caused by the new assumption is noticeable: SBS 0335-052 and NGC 346. In the case of SBS 0335-052, we note that the solution changes with almost every small change in assumptions that we test. This spectrum indicates a significant amount of self absorption in the helium lines, and, for large values of self-absorption our model is very uncertain.

sources.summary: SBS 0335--052 E has extremely long K-band exposure but very little compact K flux; the paper finds B-band light more extended than the stellar-mass-tracing K band.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335--052 E The K band exposure time for this galaxy is the longest one in the entire sample ( 5 hours with NOTCAM), yet we detect very little K band flux. The surface brightness quickly drops to _K 22.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 within the central 2 kpc ( 4 arcsec), beyond which radius the errors become too large ( 1 mag). We performed a sanity check using the 2 hour SOFI K band image, but the result was the same and we could only reach _K 22.0 mag arcsec ^ 2 . The K band flux is concentrated in the compact regular elliptical region visible in the contour plot, with no measurable flux beyond this region. Considering this it is difficult to speak of a host galaxy in the traditional sense of the term since the B band, which contains contributions from both stars and gas, seems to be much more extended than the K band, which is considered a good proxy for stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper reports MUSE discovery of ionised halo filaments around SBS 0335-052E, linking them to starburst feedback, dispersion-dominated kinematics, and possible Lyman-continuum escape channels.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of ionised gas filaments in the circum-galactic halo of the extremely metal-poor compact starburst SBS 0335-052E in a 1.5 h integration with the MUSE integral-field spectrograph. We detect these features in H and [ O iii ] emission down to a limiting surface-brightness of 5 10^ 19 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . The filaments have projected diameters of 2.1 kpc and extend more than 9 kpc to the north and north-west from the main stellar body.

Similar to high- z star-forming galaxies, the ionised gas in this galaxy is dispersion dominated. We argue that the filaments were created via feedback from the starburst and that these ionised structures in the halo may act as escape channels for Lyman continuum radiation in this gas-rich system.

sources.summary: The paper maps SBS0335 052E as a clumpy EMPG with redshifted and blueshifted high-dispersion regions likely connected to outflows, plus a more settled southern area.

quotes.quote: 3 SBS0335 052E : The gri image shows that SBS0335 052E consists of a large blue clump (thick cyan arrow) and a western subclump (thin cyan arrow) . In the velocity map, we confirm that SBS0335 052E has a redshifted ( +20 km s ^ 1 ) region at northeast (red circle) and a blueshifted ( 30 km s ^ 1 ) region at northwest (blue circle) . The redshifted and blueshifted regions seem connected to the northeast and north filaments identified with the wide-field MUSE H observations Herenz2017 , respectively . Both regions have relatively high velocity dispersions of 60 km s ^ 1 , which agree with the scenario that the 2 structures are created by outflows Herenz2017 . The southern area of SBS0335 052E generally shows a relatively low velocity dispersion of 30 km s ^ 1 , which indicates that the southern area is relatively settled .

sources.summary: The paper uses IFU spectroscopy for J0337-0502 and highlights its broad UV emission-line profiles, double-peaked doublet profile, and fitted UV emission-line example.

quotes.quote: For the remaining galaxies of the sample (J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, and J0144+0453), we used integral field spectroscopy data, when available, or long-slit spectroscopy, instead of SDSS. Specifically, we used VLT/VIMOS integral field unit (IFU) from james09 for J0127-0619, MMT Blue Channel Spectrograph spectra from senchyna19a for J0144+0453, Keck/KCWI IFU spectra from rickardsvaught21 for J0934+5514, and VLT/MUSE IFU spectra for the remaining three galaxies. MUSE spectra are also available for the galaxies J0021+0052 (PI: Göran Östlin), J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (PI: Dawn Erb). We used these data to retrieve emission line-ratios involving faint auroral lines, if undetected ( S/N<3 ) in SDSS spectra. Finally, for the galaxies J0808+3948, J0944-0038, J1148+2546, J1323-0132 and J1545+0808, Multi-Object Double Spectographs (MODS) data from the LBT telescope, presented in arellano-cordova22 , are also available ( Paper V arellano-cordova22 hereafter). Information on each of the optical datasets is provided in the following paragraphs. Concerning the IFU data available for J0021+0052, J0036-3333, J0127-0619, J0337-0502, J0405-3648, J0934+5514, J1044+0353 and J1418+2102, we extracted a spectrum from a 2.5" aperture centred at the same coordinates of COS observations to match the COS aperture (see also berg22 and Paper V arellano-cordova22 ).

However, after a visual inspection we did notice a clear broad profile in the emission lines of J1044+0353 and J1418+2102 (see also berg21a ), J1016+3754, J0337-0502, J1323-0132 and J1545+0858. For these objects we fitted the 1640 and 1661,6 with two components. We tested a two-Gaussian component fitting also on the \ doublet, without finding a significant improvement in our results. This is due to the very small wavelength separation of the \ doublet lines, which results in degenerate line centroids that make it difficult to use multiple components. Interestingly, for J0337-0502, J1044+0353, J1418+2102, J1323-0132 (see Fig. fig:uvfitcarbon ) we also observed a doubled-peak profile in the doublet.
As an example, in Fig. fig:examplefit we show the UV emission-lines fitted by our fitting routine for the galaxy J0337-0502 (i.e., SBS0335-052 E).

sources.summary: The paper observes SBS 0335-052E with Keck/LRIS, finds no significant [NeV] temporal variability, and compares its ionizing spectrum and X-ray behavior with IMBH scenarios.

quotes.quote: We have searched for low-metallicity dwarf galaxies with multiple observations of [ Ne v ] 3426 lines and have found a galaxy dubbed SBS 0335-052E. The [ Ne v ] 3426 emission lines of SBS 0335-052E are observed in September 2003 and February 2004 Izotov2009, 2005ApJS..161..240T . The extinction corrected emission line ratios of Ne \ v ] 3426/ [ Ne \ iii ] 3869 are 0.0387 0.0068 and 0.0306 0.0026 for each observation. We find that the two ratios are consistent within 1-2 sigma, and we find no significant temporal variability in the [ Ne \ v ] 3426 lines.

There is a possibility that X-ray binaries (XRBs) having the stellar-mass BHs produce the hard ionizing radiation 2024ApJ...960...13G,Schaerer+19 , although a famous dwarf galaxy SBS 0335-052E, which shows strong He ii 4686 emission, has an X-ray luminosity lower than expected from XRBs.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0335-052 as an extreme dwarf galaxy with very low line ratios, extended He II emission, disfavored HMXB ionization, and stellar-population models requiring very low metallicity.

quotes.quote: Compared to the values of C3He2-O3He2 spanned by the local dwarf galaxies, our high-redshift composite spectrum is offset to the lower-left region, close to the two well-known dwarf galaxies with the lowest / \ ratios, SBS 0335-052 and I Zw 18. Our high-redshift composite spectrum overlaps better with the region spanned by the z 2 - 4 \ emitters. The offset from the region spanned by most dwarf galaxies might suggest that reionization-era galaxies generally have harder ionizing radiation and are capable of producing more \ ionizing photons ( >54.4 eV) than those typical dwarf galaxies. The two extreme dwarf galaxies, SBS 0335-052 and I Zw 18, might be more similar to the high-redshift galaxies and could provide some hints at the \ ionizing sources. In particular, the extended \ 4686 emission has already been observed in these two galaxies Kehrig2015,Kehrig2018,RickardsVaught2021 .

For example, ionization from high-mass X-ray binaries is also disfavored by comparing the observed X-ray luminosity to \ luminosity in SBS 0335-052 Kehrig2018 and I Zw 18 Kehrig2021 . Stellar-population models that include the effects of binary stars and their evolution BPASS; Eldridge2017,Stanway2018 might be able to explain the observed \ fluxes in the SBS 0335-052 Kehrig2018 , but these models require extremely low metallicities ( Z=0.0005 \ Z_ ).

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS0335-052 as a very metal-poor dwarf whose DUSTY-modeled SED indicates heavy dust absorption, a deeply embedded super star cluster, and larger surviving grains.

quotes.quote: The SEDs of SBS0335-052, (1/40 solar metallicity), and NGC 5253 (1/6 solar metallicity) have been modeled using DUSTY plante+02 vanzi+04 . SBS0335-052 was shown by ISOCAM to harbor a significant amount of dust, due to absorption of the 9.7 m silicate feature, accounting for A_ V = 30 to 40 thuan+99 . These mid infrared (MIR) results alone point to a deeply-embedded SSC. Applying the DUSTY model to these two galaxies (Figure fig:sbs , Figure fig:ngc5253 ), the authors note that the observations require dust with a flatter size distribution, favoring larger grain sizes than those in our Galaxy.
While silicate dust is obviously present in SBS0335-052, model results point to the absence of silicate grains in NGC 5253, where only carbon-based grains are used.

sources.summary: The paper presents GMRT observations of SBS 0335--052E as one component of an extremely metal-poor interacting dwarf pair, emphasizing tidal features, gas-rich merger morphology, and triggered clustered star formation.

quotes.quote: We present Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), \ observations of SBS 0335--052E and SBS 0335--052W, a close pair of dwarf galaxies, which are further unusual in being the most metal-poor star-forming galaxies known.

The highest angular resolution \ images show that the ionized superbubble, identified by Thuan, Izotov Lipovetsky (1997), in the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) images of SBS 0335--052E, is extended along one of the diffuse tidal features, and that there is a high-density \ clump at the other end of the superbubble. The star formation in SBS 0335--052E occurs mainly in a group of superstar clusters (SSCs) with a clear age gradient; the age decreases as one approaches the dense \ clump. We suggest that this propagating star formation is driven by the superbubble expanding into a medium with a tidally-produced density gradient.

sources.summary: The paper highlights SBS 0335-052E as the object with the largest helium-abundance change, caused by a broad likelihood minimum and a higher temperature from the O III prior.

quotes.quote: The most significant difference in the helium abundance is for SBS 0335-052E, where the abundance likelihood has a very broad, shallow bottom and a weak second minimum. As a result, the previous solution for SBS 0335-052E was determined by an average of abundances that spanned this large range in y ^ + , including the weak secondary minimum with a low abundance and temperature. Therefore, the new solution, consistent with the T(O III) prior, is much higher. In the three cases with the largest shifts in helium abundance, SBS 0335-052E, SBS 0940+544N, and NGC 2363A, the helium abundance increased due to an increase in the temperature, which results from the use of the T(O III) prior.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 0335-052 as an extreme low-metallicity, dust-deficient outlier whose weak cooling lines and short-wavelength SED complicate FIR-continuum and line SFR estimates.

quotes.quote: The weak trends for [ O i ] _ 63 and [ O iii ] _ 88 are mainly driven by two galaxies of extreme low metal abundance, I Zw 18 and SBS 0335-052, in which the [ O i ] _ 63 and [ O iii ] _ 88 lines do not seem to add significantly to the overall gas cooling. Since the SFR is unlikely to be overestimated for these galaxies based on FUV +MIPS 24 m (see comparison with other SFR tracers in Section DGScompare of the Appendix), the offset of these extremely dust deficient galaxies might suggest that other lines dominate the cooling processes (e.g. Ly ). The gas heating might, alternatively, be dominated by heating mechanisms other than the photoelectric effect (e.g. soft X-ray heating, mechanical heating, cosmic rays), which could disperse the link between the emission of cooling lines and the SFR.

The best fitting line and dispersion (0.28 dex) in this plot is mainly dominated by three galaxies (SBS 0335-052, Tol 1214-277, Haro 11) which have the peak of their SED at very short wavelengths and, therefore, the 24 m band will overestimate the SFR while the 70 m emission will underestimate the SFR.

sources.summary: The CLASSY notes place J0337-0502 in metal-poor SBS 0335-052, focus the coadd on the E region, and describe young clusters within SBS 0335-052E.

quotes.quote: J0337-0502 : J0337-0502 is part of the well-known, metal-poor galaxy SBS 0335-052 that has two main star-forming clusters, E and W. The CLASSY coadded spectrum only contains light from the E region. It also combines data from COS FUV detector lifetime positions 3 and 4. SBS 0335-052E consists of six young ( 10< Myr) star clusters adamo10 , four of which fall within the COS aperture wofford21 .

sources.summary: The paper newly observes SBS-0335-052E as a bright EMPG, measures multiple emission lines including Fe III, and uses its abundance ratios in the chemical-evolution analysis.

quotes.quote: We select three EMPGs, SBS-0335-052E 2009A&A...503...61I , J2314+0154 2020ApJ...898..142K , J0125+0759 2020ApJ...898..142K , that are observable in the given Keck/LRIS nights. These are bright EMPGs whose faint emission lines, especially [ Fe iii ] 4658, can be potentially detected.

Although many lines of Ar, S, Ne, N, and O emission are identified in the spectra, only two out of three EMPGs, SBS-0335-052E, and J0125+0759, have a significant detection of [ FeIII ] emission. We hereafter use SBS-0335-052E and J0125+0759 in our analysis.

sources.summary: The paper says SBS 0335-052 is extremely oxygen-poor, has similar abundances in unrelated knots, and has colors compatible with several-Gyr ages rather than requiring youth.

quotes.quote: Another illustration that O/H in itself is by no means an indication of youth is that SBS 0335-052 (one of the most O/H deficient galaxies) has a similar O/H abundance in two knots that are completely unrelated causually (Lipovetsky et al., 1999).

In Fig. 1 we compare published halo colours for two young galaxy candidates: Tololo 65 and SBS 0335-052 with the predictions from spectral synthesis models (PEGASE.2 by Fioc and Rocca-Volmerange, 1999). For Tololo 65 the agreement is good when comparing models with extended (i.e. not SSP) star formation history. For SBS 0335-052, U-B gives a lower estimate, still the data can be fit with an age of several Gyr. There is nothing from the photometrical point of view that requires these galaxies to be young.

sources.summary: The paper cites SBS 0335-052E as an extremely metal-poor galaxy whose circumgalactic filaments are detected in H and [O iii] beyond its small diameter.

quotes.quote: Herrenz17 detect filaments of ionised gas in the CGM of the extremely metal-poor galaxy SBS 0335-052E. The features are detected in H and [ O iii ] 5007 down to a limiting surface brightness of 5 10^ 19 . The galaxy diameter is around 3 kpc, with the filaments extending more than 9 kpc and connecting seamlessly in velocity space to the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper cites SBS 0335-052 as a Dwalin-19 galaxy with UV P-Cygni evidence for massive-star winds, lower terminal wind velocity than Tol 1214-277, and smaller inferred outflow/loading rates.

quotes.quote: We stress that some of the galaxies in the Dwalin-19 sample have been studied individually in separate works. ThuanIzotov97 found P-Cygni profiles in the stellar UV absorption lines of SBS 0335-052 and Tol 1214-277, suggesting the presence of a stellar wind from massive stars in these two systems. The terminal velocities measured for the stellar winds in SBS 0335-052 and Tol 1214-277 were 500 and 2000 , respectively. Interestingly, while in our study we do not infer terminal velocities, our estimates for mass outflow rates and loading factors in Tol 1214-277 are about one order of magnitude larger than for SBS 0335-052.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 0335 052 as a very metal-poor blue compact dwarf whose compact, dense star-forming region, super star clusters, dust extinction, and warm dust define the active star-formation mode.

quotes.quote: Two classes of star-formation activity in BCDs have recently emerged observationally, as proposed by Hunt et al. \ ( ) and Hunt Hirashita (2006, in preparation). They argue that the star-formation modes in the two most metal-poor galaxies, SBS 0335 052 and I Zw 18 , are very different, in spite of their similar metallicities ( 12+ ( O/H )=7.3 and 12+ ( O/H )=7.2 , respectively; Skillman Kennicutt ; Izotov et al. \ ). The major star-forming region of \ is compact and dense (radius r_ SF 40 pc, number density n 600 cm ^ 3 ; Dale et al. \ ; Izotov Thuan ). Moreover, \ hosts several super star clusters (SSCs), detectable H _2 emission lines in the near-infrared (NIR) (Vanzi et al. \ ), a large dust extinction ( A_V 16 mag; Thuan et al. \ ; Hunt et al. \ ; Plante Sauvage ), and high dust temperature (Hunt et al. \ ; Dale et al. \ ; Takeuchi et al. \ ).

gmw_00000699.name: SBS 0335-052W
gmw_00000699.overview: SBS 0335-052W is an extremely metal-poor [arXiv:0904.4797], low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9911094] that forms an interacting pair with SBS 0335-052E [arXiv:1501.06709, arXiv:0904.4797]. The pair is embedded in a shared neutral hydrogen envelope [arXiv:1501.06709, arXiv:astro-ph/0401349], where SBS 0335-052W exhibits modest star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/9911094] and a central velocity gradient likely associated with its original disk spin [arXiv:0904.4797].

sources.summary: SBS 0335-052W is part of an interacting XMP pair; its component has a small southwestern nodule, offcenter H I peak, changing velocity gradient, and high-resolution extension.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335-052E and SBS 0335-052W From Ekta, Chengalur Pustilnik (2009; their Fig. 1, 4 and 5), with the GMRT. The low resolution ( 40 ) map shows this interacting pair of XMP galaxies ( D 20 kpc), enveloped in a common H 1 structure (PA _ HI 175 ^ ), with each H 1 peak corresponding to a galaxy. At intermediate ( 20 ) resolution, the individual single-peaked H 1 structures are distinguishable. The elongated disks are oriented along approximately the same direction (PA _ HI 145 ^ for the Eastern, and PA _ HI 150 ^ for the Western, component).
