SBS 0335-052W displays a small nodule of emission to the Southwest. The H 1 peaks are slightly offcenter ( D 10 ) from the peaks of optical emission. In the case of SBS 0335-052E, the extended H 1 emission is approximately perpendicular to the Northwestern extension seen in the optical image (PA _ opt 45 ^ ). The velocity gradient in SBS 0035-052W changes from the center (approximately North-South) to the outskirts (Northeast-Southwest). For SBS 0335-052E, the velocity gradient is along the tidal tail (Northeast-Southwest), but deviates from a standard disk. High resolution ( 9 ) observations show that SBS 0335-052W maintains the overall H 1 position angle seen at lower resolutions, with an extension to the Northeast. The direction of the velocity gradient is approximately Northwest-Southeast.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0335 052W as a very metal-poor companion in the shared HI envelope, fainter optically than SBS 0335 052 but brighter in X-rays.

quotes.quote: The relative proximity of these three BCDs [ D = 54.3 Mpc for SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W (TIL); D = 12.6 Mpc for I Zw 18 Ostlin2000 ] , their extremely low metallicities [ 0.025Z _ for SBS 0335 052 Izotov1999a , 0.020Z _ for SBS 0335 052W Lipovetsky1999 , and 0.020Z _ for I Zw 18 Izotov1999a ] , and high neutral gas content see Pustilnik2001 for SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W and vanZee1998 for I Zw 18 make them the best local approximations to primordial young galaxies. Their relatively bright apparent magnitudes ( m_B=17.0 mag for SBS 0335 052, m_B=19.4 mag for SBS 0335 052W and m_B=15.8 mag for I Zw 18) make them much easier to study than the very faint and small building-block dwarf galaxies at high-redshift. HI VLA mapping Pustilnik2001 shows SBS 0335 052 and SBS 0335 052W to be embedded in a common very large HI envelope with an overall size of 66 22 kpc. There are two prominent HI peaks separated in the east-west direction by 22 kpc (84 ). The eastern peak is associated with SBS 0335 052, while the western peak is about a factor of 1.3 brighter in the HI line and is associated with the fainter SBS 0335 052W.
Although SBS 0335 052W is 2.4 mag fainter in B than SBS 0335 052, it is 2.8 times brighter in the X-ray.

The point source in SBS 0335 052W has an unabsorbed X-ray luminosity in the range (5.2--8.5) 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . The spectrum of the fainter component point source ( 2) in SBS 0335 052W is also acceptably fit by both the absorbed power-law or thermal plasma models.

sources.summary: SBS 0335--052 W is described as the companion-field peak with a record-low metallicity optical counterpart, very little compact K-band flux, and barely resolved K-band data.

quotes.quote: SBS 0335--052 W This galaxy was first discovered as a peak in the maps of the companion galaxy. 1997AstL...23..308P detected an optical counterpart, and 2005ApJ...632..210I measured its record--low metallicity, 10 lower than the previous record--holder I Zw 18. This galaxy is in the same field of view as SBS 0335--052 E, so the same remarks on K band exposure time apply here. Similarly, we find very little K band flux, again located only in the small compact region visible in the contour plot. The K band profile reaches _K 22.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 but we can barely resolve the galaxy in this filter -- there are only 3 reliable data points in the profile.

sources.summary: The review identifies SBS 0335-052W as a physical companion of SBS0335-052, with even lower oxygen abundance, low surface brightness, modest star formation, and shared H I environment.

quotes.quote: Like IZw18, SBS 0335-052 also has a physical companion, SBS 0335-052W at a projected distance of 22 kpc (Pustilnik et al. 1999). Lipovetsky et al. (1999) found an oxygen abundance of 12+log(O/H)=7.2, even lower than that of SBS 0335-052, and fully comparable to IZw18. This companion, though blue is not very compact and it has evidently avoided inclusion in any BCG catalogue.

Its central R surface brightness and SFR is rather low, comparable to a ``normal'' dI. The fact that both SBS0335-052 and its companion resides in the same H i cloud but at least 22 kpc apart, poses a problem for the youth hypothesis since it would require a coordinated ignition of star formation in the two clouds.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0335--052W as an extremely metal-poor dwarf in a gas-rich interacting pair, with high-resolution H I kinematics suggesting residual disk spin and tidally affected star formation.

quotes.quote: We present Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), \ observations of SBS 0335--052E and SBS 0335--052W, a close pair of dwarf galaxies, which are further unusual in being the most metal-poor star-forming galaxies known.

However, for SBS 0335--052W at least, at high angular resolution, one sees a central velocity field that could be associated with the spin of the original undisturbed disc. The two galaxies have very similar \ masses, but very different optical properties and current star formation rates. A possible reason for this is the differing amounts of tidally-induced star formation, because of the different spin orientations of these interacting galaxies.

sources.summary: SBS 0335 052W is discussed as an extremely low-metallicity galaxy with lower-metallicity regions, higher-abundance bright regions, and a luminosity-weighted abundance of 7.12.

quotes.quote: We note that metallicities in regions 3 and 4 of the galaxy SBS 0335 052W are even lower I09 . However, the [ O iii ] 4363 emission line is not detected in spectra of these regions and their oxygen abundances are derived by the semi-empirical method. Furthermore, two other brighter regions 2 and 1 of SBS 0335 052W have higher oxygen abundances (Fig. fig4 ) indicating an abundance gradient in this galaxy. This gradient results in a higher luminosity-weighted oxygen abundance of 7.12 averaged over the entire galaxy SBS 0335 052W.

gmw_00000700.name: ESO 235-G58
gmw_00000700.overview: ESO 235-G58 is a peculiar polar-ring-related galaxy featuring an edge-on central disk and a low-inclination ring structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0402126]. The ring component contains a high amount of neutral hydrogen and shows evidence of recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0402126].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies ESO 235-G58 as a polar-ring-related peculiar galaxy with an edge-on central disk, blue low-inclination ring structure, recent ring star formation, and substantial H I.

quotes.quote: We present the near-infrared and optical properties of the peculiar galaxy ESO 235-G58, which resembles a late-type ringed barred spiral seen close to face-on. However, the apparent bar of ESO 235-G58 is in reality an edge-on disk galaxy of relatively low luminosity. We have analyzed the light and color distributions of ESO 235-G58 in the NIR and optical bands and compared them with the typical properties observed for other morphological galaxy types, including polar ring galaxies. Similar properties are observed for ESO 235-G58, polar ring galaxies, and spiral galaxies, which leads us to conclude that this peculiar system is a polar-ring-related galaxy, characterized by a low inclined ring/disk structure, as pointed out by Buta Crocker in an earlier study, rather than a barred galaxy.
The ring structure seems to be much younger than the central galaxy: the last episode of star formation is recent and it may be not older than 10^8 yr.
Furthermore, this peculiar galaxy is characterized by a high amount of neutral hydrogen, mainly associated with the ring component, as is also usually observed in many PRGs (van Gorkom et al.

gmw_00000701.name: AGC 110482
gmw_00000701.overview: AGC 110482 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with a fuzzy, disky appearance [arXiv:0808.1789] and is associated with the NGC 672 galaxy group [arXiv:1402.3723]. It contains asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1501.07313] and two HII regions that generate nearly half of its total H-alpha emission [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

The Environment Around AGC 110482, AGC 111164, AGC 111946, AGC 111977, AGC 112521: The NGC 672 and NGC 784 Groups There are six SHIELD galaxies located in region (4) in Figure  fig:all_groups and the final plot of Figure  fig:subgroups . With the exception of AGC 748778 (discussed in the previous subsection), all of the SHIELD systems have been previously associated with two galaxy groups, namely the NGC 672 and NGC 784 groups e.g., Karachentsev2004 . Specifically, AGC 110482, AGC 111946, and AGC 112521 have been identified as part of the NGC 672 group. The heliocentric radial velocities of the 3 SHIELD galaxies range from 274 367 km s ^ 1 compared with the higher velocity of 429 km s ^ 1 for NGC 672.

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 110482 as a dwarf irregular with a fuzzy disk, two star-formation bursts, and two HII regions producing nearly half of its H emission.

quotes.quote: AGC 110482: This object shows a fuzzy disky appearance with a brighter center and north-east side. The first star burst in this dIrr galaxy took place more than 10 Gyr ago and the second burst only 6 Myr ago, forming 70 10 of the stars we observe today. Two HII regions can be identified in this galaxy, with line fluxes of 1.07 10^ 14 erg/cm ^ 2 /s and 7.82 10^ 15 erg/cm ^ 2 /s respectively; these produce 48 of the total H flux from this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses AGC 110482 as an example SHIELD dwarf with composite imaging, helium-burning and AGB populations, and recent star formation approximately matching its lifetime average.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:image shows an example composite image from the data of AGC 110482, reproduced from the previous study measuring the distances to the galaxies see their Figure 1 McQuinn2014 .
Some of CMDs also show somewhat populated helium burning sequences, including AGC 110482, AGC 111977, AGC 731457, AGC 749237, which is a further indication of recent star-formation activity.
Asymptotic giant branch (AGB) populations, indicative of intermediate age star-formation activity, are also observed in a subset of the CMDs, most notably in AGC 110482, AGC 174605, and AGC 182595.

gmw_00000702.name: AGC 111164
gmw_00000702.overview: AGC 111164 is a dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 784 group [arXiv:1402.3723, arXiv:1501.07313]. Its nearest neighbor is the more massive dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 784, situated 0.14 Mpc away [arXiv:1501.07313], and its recent star formation rate is half of its lifetime average [arXiv:1501.07313].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

AGC 111164 and AGC 111977 have been identified as part of the NGC 784 group. The radial velocities of these two SHIELD galaxies are 163 and 207 km s ^ 1 , which bracket the measured 193 km s ^ 1 velocity of NGC 784.

sources.summary: The paper places AGC 111164 near NGC 784, notes its recent SFR is half its lifetime average, and links its low H I mass to low recent activity.

quotes.quote: Further, the three galaxies that show somewhat lower levels of recent star-formation activity compared with their lifetime averages (AGC 111164, AGC 111977, and AGC 112521) are part of the galaxy associations of the NGC 784 and NGC 672 groups mentioned above.
The nearest neighbor to AGC 111164 is the more massive dwarf starburst galaxy NGC 784 at a distance of 0.14 Mpc.
However, despite being separated by only 0.14 Mpc, the recent SFR in AGC 111164 is half its lifetime average.

gmw_00000703.name: AGC 111977
gmw_00000703.overview: AGC 111977 is a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1402.3723, arXiv:1501.07313] located in the NGC 784 group [arXiv:1402.3723]. It features a fan-shaped blue optical image [arXiv:0808.1789] and exhibits evidence of multiple star-formation bursts [arXiv:0808.1789], supported by populated helium-burning sequences [arXiv:1501.07313] and diffuse H-alpha emission [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 111977 as a galaxy needing multiple star-formation bursts, with diffuse H emission and a fan-shaped blue optical image.

quotes.quote: AGC 111977: the models indicate that 65 10 of the light is produced by stars formed in a first SF burst 10 Gyr ago, and the rest in a relatively recent one 27 Myr ago. The following nine fits show a possible recent burst 1 Myr ago; this is further supported by models allowing more than two bursts. It seems that at least three SF bursts are needed in order to describe well the colours of this galaxy. No specific HII regions are identified, yet we measure F(H )=1.74 10^ 15 erg/cm ^ 2 /s from the object. The blue optical image is fan-shaped, wider in the North part that in the South, with the center appearing brighter than other locations on the galaxy image.

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

AGC 111164 and AGC 111977 have been identified as part of the NGC 784 group. The radial velocities of these two SHIELD galaxies are 163 and 207 km s ^ 1 , which bracket the measured 193 km s ^ 1 velocity of NGC 784.

sources.summary: KK 16 demonstrates ACS improvement over WFPC2: the WFPC2 TRGB detection lies near the photometric limit, while ACS gives a more reliable CMD and a larger inferred distance.

quotes.quote: An example of the enhanced capability is provided by the dwarf galaxy KK 16 which was observed both with WFPC2 (program 8601) and ACS (program 10210). In the case of the WFPC2 observations (exp = 600s in both F606W and F814W), we find F814W _ TRGB = 24.62 0.10 (D = 5.0 Mpc) (lower panel Fig. f:acswfpc ). Definitely, this TRGB detection is near the limit for a WFPC2 snapshot observations. The ML procedure picks up the TRGB near the photometric limit where the photometric errors and bias are substantial. By contrast, the observations of the same galaxy with ACS (and slightly longer exposures of 1226s in F814W and 934s in F606W) give a very reliable CMD sampling with F814W _ TRGB = 24.78 0.04 (D = 5.7 Mpc) (upper panel Fig. f:acswfpc ).

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 111977 as a nearby SHIELD dwarf with deep CMD photometry, helium-burning stars, low recent activity, and a location near AGC 112521.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:cmd presents an example CMD for one of the farthest galaxies from the SHIELD sample, AGC 749237, plotted to the 50 completeness level as determined by the artificial star tests, and one of the closest galaxies, AGC 111977.
For the 7 galaxies that are located between 5-8 Mpc, the depth of photometry is similar to AGC 111977, reaching approximately 2 mag below the TRGB.
Some of CMDs also show somewhat populated helium burning sequences, including AGC 110482, AGC 111977, AGC 731457, AGC 749237, which is a further indication of recent star-formation activity.

gmw_00000704.name: AGC 112521
gmw_00000704.overview: AGC 112521 is a dwarf galaxy located in the NGC 672 group [arXiv:1402.3723]. The galaxy exhibits a lower level of recent star-formation activity compared to its lifetime average [arXiv:1501.07313], and it is situated 0.65 Mpc away from AGC 111977 [arXiv:1501.07313].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

sources.summary: The paper updates AGC 112521 H I measurements, groups it with low recent-activity SHIELD galaxies, and places it 0.65 Mpc from AGC 111977.

quotes.quote: Note the \ masses reported for AGC 112521 and AGC 182595 have been updated.

Further, the three galaxies that show somewhat lower levels of recent star-formation activity compared with their lifetime averages (AGC 111164, AGC 111977, and AGC 112521) are part of the galaxy associations of the NGC 784 and NGC 672 groups mentioned above.

AGC 111977 is located 0.65 Mpc from AGC 112521, which is also separated by a comparable distance in the opposite direction from four members of the NGC 672 group, namely NGC 672, IC 1727, AGC 111945, and the SHIELD galaxy AGC 110482.

gmw_00000705.name: AGC 182595
gmw_00000705.overview: AGC 182595 is an isolated dwarf galaxy with no known neighbors within a 1 Mpc radius [arXiv:1402.3723]. It contains asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1501.07313] and exhibits recent star-formation activity that is approximately equivalent to its lifetime average [arXiv:1501.07313].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

Truly Isolated: AGC 174605, AGC 182595, AGC 748778, AGC 749237 AGC 749237 is located in region (1), AGC 174605 and AGC 182595 are located in region (3), and AGC 748778 is located in region (4) in Figure  fig:all_groups . No known galaxies were identified within a 1 Mpc radius of these four galaxies. The four SHIELD galaxies have properties (i.e., late morphological type, low luminosity, significant gas content) consistent with other known isolated galaxies Karachentsev2011 . The closest systems identified are as follows: AGC 174605: Closest neighbors are the pair of galaxies DDO 47 and KK 65 at a distance of 3.2 Mpc that are part of the 14+19 association. AGC 182585: Closest neighbor is the galaxy LSBCD564 0 at a distance of 1.3 Mpc. AGC 748778: Closest neighbor is the galaxy UGC 00685 at a distance of 2.4 Mpc. AGC 749237: Closest neighbor is the galaxy IC 3308 at a distance of just over 1 Mpc. Note that three of these systems are at distances greater than 10 Mpc. Thus, their isolation may be due to our incomplete knowledge of the galaxy population at these larger distances.

sources.summary: The paper notes updated H I masses for AGC 182595, identifies AGB populations, finds recent star formation near its lifetime average, and calls it isolated.

quotes.quote: Asymptotic giant branch (AGB) populations, indicative of intermediate age star-formation activity, are also observed in a subset of the CMDs, most notably in AGC 110482, AGC 174605, and AGC 182595.
Note the \ masses reported for AGC 112521 and AGC 182595 have been updated.
Finally, we examine the three galaxies whose recent star-formation activity is approximately equivalent to their lifetime averages, namely AGC 110482, AGC 182595, and AGC 749241.

gmw_00000706.name: AGC 748778
gmw_00000706.overview: AGC 748778 is a low-mass transition dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1501.07313, arXiv:2009.09145] located at a distance of 5 to 12 Mpc [arXiv:1402.3723]. It is highly isolated, with no known galaxies within a 1 Mpc radius [arXiv:1402.3723], and it exhibits only weak, diffuse H-alpha emission [arXiv:1501.07313].

No known galaxies were identified within a 1 Mpc radius of these four galaxies. AGC 182585: Closest neighbor is the galaxy LSBCD564 0 at a distance of 1.3 Mpc. AGC 748778: Closest neighbor is the galaxy UGC 00685 at a distance of 2.4 Mpc. AGC 749237: Closest neighbor is the galaxy IC 3308 at a distance of just over 1 Mpc. Note that three of these systems are at distances greater than 10 Mpc. Thus, their isolation may be due to our incomplete knowledge of the galaxy population at these larger distances.

sources.summary: The paper classifies AGC 748778 as a transition dwarf with weak diffuse H emission, measurable resolved-population SFR, discrepant M/L stellar mass, and isolation.

quotes.quote: Specifically, AGC 749241 was not detected in H and AGC 748778 had only weak, diffuse H emission but no \ region.

Thus, AGC 749241 and AGC 748778 meet the criteria of having both detectable amounts of \ and no significant recent SFR as measured by H and can be classified as transition dwarfs dTrans; Mateo1998, Skillman2003, Weisz2011 .
The exception is AGC 748778 whose stellar mass estimate from the M/L ratio is significantly lower than our value.

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 748778 as the closest present-day dwarf to Pisces A, rules out a current tidal role, but considers it as a possible past perturber.

quotes.quote: In fact, the present-day closest galaxy to Pisces A is AGC 748778, a low-mass ( M_ 3 10^6 M _ ) dwarf galaxy originally discovered within ALFALFA see also cannon+2011 cannon+2011 huang+2012 . At a distance of 900 kpc from Pisces A, it is effectively ruled out as the genesis of possible tidal forces. Pisces A may still have been disrupted at some point in the past by AGC 748778 or another galaxy.

gmw_00000707.overview: AGC 749237 is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 5 to 12 Mpc [arXiv:1402.3723]. It is truly isolated with no known galaxies within a 1 Mpc radius [arXiv:1402.3723], and its color-magnitude diagram shows helium-burning stars that indicate recent star-formation activity [arXiv:1501.07313].

AGC 182585: Closest neighbor is the galaxy LSBCD564 0 at a distance of 1.3 Mpc. AGC 748778: Closest neighbor is the galaxy UGC 00685 at a distance of 2.4 Mpc. sources.summary: The paper uses AGC 749237 as a distant SHIELD CMD example, notes shallow TRGB-depth photometry, helium-burning stars, and isolation within 1 Mpc.

quotes.quote: Figure  fig:cmd presents an example CMD for one of the farthest galaxies from the SHIELD sample, AGC 749237, plotted to the 50 completeness level as determined by the artificial star tests, and one of the closest galaxies, AGC 111977.
For the 5 galaxies that are located between 8-12 Mpc, the depth of photometry is similar to AGC 749237, reaching approximately 1 mag below the TRGB, but populated by differing numbers of stars.
Some of CMDs also show somewhat populated helium burning sequences, including AGC 110482, AGC 111977, AGC 731457, AGC 749237, which is a further indication of recent star-formation activity.

gmw_00000708.name: AGC 749241
gmw_00000708.overview: AGC 749241 is a transition dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1501.07313] located at a distance of 5 to 12 Mpc [arXiv:1402.3723]. It contains detectable neutral hydrogen but lacks H-alpha emission [arXiv:1501.07313], and its nearest neighbor is the galaxy IC 3840 [arXiv:1402.3723].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

The Environment Around AGC 749241 AGC 749241 is located in region (2) in Figure fig:all_groups and the second plot of Figure fig:subgroups . This system resides in a more highly populated region with 8 low mass galaxies and one more massive spiral galaxy, NGC 4656. NGC 4656 has been identified as a member of the 14 6 group Tully1988, Tully2008 . The nearest neighbor to AGC 749241 is IC 3840 at a distance of 0.5 Mpc. IC 3840 has an heliocentric radial velocity of 557 km s ^ 1 , compared with a velocity of 451 km s ^ 1 for AGC 749241.

sources.summary: The paper classifies AGC 749241 as a transition dwarf with no H detection, average recent star formation, gas content, and little recent impact from likely perturbations.

quotes.quote: Specifically, AGC 749241 was not detected in H and AGC 748778 had only weak, diffuse H emission but no \ region.

Thus, AGC 749241 and AGC 748778 meet the criteria of having both detectable amounts of \ and no significant recent SFR as measured by H and can be classified as transition dwarfs dTrans; Mateo1998, Skillman2003, Weisz2011 .
Finally, we examine the three galaxies whose recent star-formation activity is approximately equivalent to their lifetime averages, namely AGC 110482, AGC 182595, and AGC 749241.

gmw_00000709.name: AT2019cuk
gmw_00000709.overview: AT2019cuk is a Seyfert 1 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2201.11633] that exhibits changing-look active galactic nucleus behavior [arXiv:2205.06275, arXiv:2302.12847] and optical broad emission line profile variations [arXiv:2401.17318]. It is a candidate host for an imminent supermassive black hole binary merger [arXiv:2201.11633, arXiv:2208.11968], displaying chirping optical and X-ray variability [arXiv:2201.11633, arXiv:2205.06275] alongside day-long hard X-ray flares [arXiv:2302.12847].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ143016.05+230344.4 as a Seyfert 1/elliptical AGN whose chirping optical and X-ray variability is modeled as an imminent eccentric SMBH binary merger.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ143016.05+230344.4 (hereafter SDSSJ1430+2303) is known as a Seyfert 1 galaxy at redshift 0.08105 , which shows typical AGN-like narrow emission line ratios yet somewhat unusual blueshifted broad \ emission (see Extended Data Fig.  sdsspec ) in its optical spectrum from Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS). In particular, in the past three years the optical luminosity of SDSSJ1430+2303 has shown an unprecedented time-dependent variation. The g and band light curves from Zwicky Transient Facitity (ZTF) display an oscillation pattern since early 2019, which has completed at least 3 cycles up to August 2021, with a decreasing oscillation amplitude and period (see Figure  lc ). To our best knowledge, such a chirping AGN with simultaneously rapid damping amplitude and period has never been reported in the past.

Unfortunately, the target is invisible in the subsequent months because it is too close to the solar direction. We have triggered the Neil Gehrels Swift X-ray telescope (XRT) monitoring on this intriguing target since Nov. 24, 2021 (see details of Swift observations in Methods) and discovered a further shortened periodic variation in X-ray bands, whose period is approximately one month at the end of 2021.

The host galaxy of SDSSJ1430+2303 appears as a typical elliptical galaxy with stellar mass of 1.5 10^ 11   \ (see host galaxy part in Methods). In the standard framework of galaxy formation and evolution in a hierarchical universe, giant elliptical galaxies such as SDSSJ1430+2303 are considered to be the product of major mergers of two or more less massive galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI imaging of SDSS J143016.05+230344.4, a Seyfert I SMBHB candidate, finding a compact parsec-scale radio component that may be a jet or corona with no current radio outburst.

quotes.quote: Recently, SDSS J143016.05+230344.4 (J1430+2303) was reported to be a supermassive black hole binary (SMBHB) in the final coalescence phase. It is probably the first SMBHB coalescence event observable in human history. Radio observations of J1430+2303 before and after coalescence will provide a unique diagnosis of the energetics and environment of the SMBHB.

A compact component is detected in all three VLBI images. It has a brightness temperature of >10^8 K, an unresolved morphology with a size <0.8 pc, and a flat radio spectrum. These observational features are inconsistent with large opening-angle outflows or winds, but indicate that this compact component might be a jet or a corona. Nearly 60 of the emission is resolved by VLBI and may come from remnant lobes of previous radio activities, the outer layers of a structured jet, or shocks formed by the disc winds in the narrow line region. Current VLBI images do not yet show signs of radio outbursts.

sources.summary: SDSSJ1430+2303 is a nearby Seyfert/AGN hosting a candidate merging SMBH binary, with chirping flares, strong X-ray variability, relativistic reflection, and a variable warm absorber.

quotes.quote: We observed SDSS J1430+2303 with XMM-Newton , NuSTAR , Chandra, and Swift spanning the first 200 days since its discovery. X-ray variability, up to a factor of 7, has been detected on a timescale of a few days. The broadband spectrum from 0.2--70 keV can be well fitted with a model consisting of a power law and a relativistic reflection covered by a warm absorber. The properties of the warm absorber changed dramatically, for example, with a decrease in the line-of-sight velocity from 0.2c to 0.02c, between the two XMM-Newton observations separated by only 19 days, which can be naturally understood in the context of the SMBHB; although, the clumpy wind scenario cannot be completely excluded.

Recently, we have discovered a possible candidate SMBHB close to the merger stage in a nearby galaxy SDSS J143016.05+230344.4 (hereafter SDSSJ1430+2303) at z=0.08105 Jiang2022 . SDSSJ1430+2303 was initially noticed during our systematic search for mid-infrared (MIR) outbursts in nearby galaxies (MIRONG, Jiang2021 ). A careful check of its optical light curves from the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) then revealed unique chirping flares, with a reduced period from about one year to only three months by the end of 2021. The flares can be ideally interpreted as emissions from plasma balls that are kicked out from the primary SMBH accretion disk by an inspiraling secondary SMBH during disk crossings.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS J1430+2303 as a Seyfert galaxy hosting an imminent SMBH binary candidate, estimating its gravitational-wave detectability and parameter constraints for Taiji configurations.

quotes.quote: Recently, it has been reported that the Seyfert 1-type galaxy SDSS J1430+2303 hosts an SMBHB candidate located at a redshift 0.08105 (hereafter, we will refer to this SMBHB as J1430+2303). The optical luminosity variations of the galaxy SDSS J1430+2303 were monitored using the g and band light curves of the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) , which exhibit an oscillation pattern since early 2019. Ref. predicts that the merger time of J1430+2303 is about three years by fitting the trajectory models with the optical light curves.

SDSS J1430+2303 consists of a primary SMBH with a mass of 1.6 10^8M_ and a secondary SMBH with a mass of 4 10^7M_ , with an inclined and highly eccentric trajectory. Despite this, we can use a waveform derived for a circular orbit to approximate the expected signal, since the contribution of the high eccentricity is mainly distributed in the low frequency band which is outside the detection range of the three types of detectors considered here. Here we use ``SEOBNRE" model to compute the waveform with eccentricity in the time domain and the ``IMRPhenomD" method which is a frequency-domain phenomenological model for the GW signal from the inspiral, merger and ringdown (IMR) of black-hole binaries to get the frequency domain signal approximate.

The GW signal emitted by J1430+2303 is strong enough to be directly detected by Taiji pathfinder plus-A. By improving the noise level to 100 pm/ Hz and 60 fN/ Hz and building Taiji pathfinder plus-B, we will produce rich scientific results.

sources.summary: The paper treats Tick-Tock as a Seyfert 1 MBHB candidate at z=0.08105 whose limited detectable waveform causes looser constraints, especially for TianQin.

quotes.quote: Of particular interest are two examples: the extensively studied blazar OJ 287 (OJ287), located at a redshift of z = 0.306 ; and the Seyfert 1-type galaxy SDSS J143016.05 + 230344.4, which hosts a candidate mbhb at redshift of z=0.08105 (referred to `Tick-Tock' hereafter).

Parameter estimation of OJ287 and Tick-Tock In this subsection, we present the constraint results for the OJ287 and Tick-Tock systems, which is presented in Figure fig:OJ287 and Figure fig:Ticktock , respectively. As depicted in Fig. fig:signal , only a portion of the merger and ringdown phases of these two systems can be detected by TianQin and LISA. Thus, this limited detection may have a significant impact on parameter estimation. For the OJ287 system, it can be observed that TianQin's constraint results are slightly looser compared to those of LISA, except for (D_L, , ) . As for the Tick-Tock system, TianQin's constraints for all 11 parameters are looser than those of LISA.

sources.summary: The paper presents spectroscopic monitoring of SDSS J1430+2303, describing it as a Seyfert 1 SMBH-binary candidate with dramatic broad-line profile changes.

quotes.quote: We conducted follow-up optical spectroscopic observations of SDSS J1430+2303 with KOOLS-IFU on Seimei Telescope in May, June, and July 2022, and April 2023.

SDSS J143016.05+230344.4 (hereafter , also known as AT2019cuk and Tick Tock) which is a Seyfert1 galaxy at redshift 0.08105 with AB magnitude 16.5 in `- band, is argued to possess the SMBHB system just before the coalescence jiang2022tick .

It is interesting to note that the optical broad emission line profile of this object also dramatically changed between 2005 and 2022. While the spectrum of taken in 2005 showed only one broad line which is blueshifted ( 2400 \ km \ s^ 1 ) from the narrow line, the spectrum taken in January 2022 showed the drastically changed puzzling profiles of a broad line which is blueshifted ( 900 \ km \ s^ 1 ) from the narrow together with the component which is represented by a pair of two broad lines with symmetry jiang2022tick . Such abrupt changes in the broad line profile are considered quite unique even among the AGNs with the most prominent line profile variations and it's important to monitor the phenomena over a sufficiently long time scale, whether or not it is directly related to the predicted SMBHB coalescence itself.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ1430 + 2303 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy and possible late-inspiralling supermassive binary black hole source for gravitational-wave memory searches.

quotes.quote: The recently observed chirping signature in the light curves of Seyfert 1 galaxy SDSSJ1430 + 2303 could be explained by a late-inspiralling supermassive binary black hole (SMBBH) system in the galactic center, which will merge in the near future (or could have merged already). For the merging SMBBH scenario, SDSSJ1430 + 2303 can be a source of nonlinear gravitational wave (GW) burst with memory (BWM), which may provide a promising target for future pulsar timing array (PTA) observations. In this work, we investigate the prospects for detecting the BWM signal from SDSSJ1430 + 2303 by the International PTA (IPTA) and FAST-PTA in the next 5 years.

The recent observations of Seyfert 1 galaxy SDSSJ143016.05 + 2303 (hereafter SDSSJ1430 + 2303) at various electromagnetic wavelengths, in particular at the optical and X-ray bands, reported periodically varying light curves with decreasing oscillation amplitude and period Jiang:2022 .

sources.summary: The paper follows the tick-tock AGN candidate, describing its shrinking modulation, changing-look behavior, and later nuclear fading in optical photometry.

quotes.quote: Among these, SDSSJ143016.05+230344.4 ('tick-tock' hereafter) is the only candidate with a remarkably well sampled light curve showing a clear reduction of the modulation period and amplitude over three years of observations.

In addition to the chirping optical and X-ray variability that led to the proposed MBHB interpretation, tick-tock exhibits a changing-look AGN behaviour, with a significant increase of optical and infrared luminosity (by more than one magnitude in the different bands) over few years and the brightening of broad hydrogen Balmer lines ticktock .

Our photometry campaign demonstrates that the tick-tock nucleus has fainted during the last observations (MJD 59600 ), by 0.2 magnitudes, when including all the ZTF data and all the photometric points from our NTT and REM follow-ups (the average dimming when only the second daily set of more accurate REM observations is condidered is 0.26 mag).

sources.summary: The paper treats AT2019cuk as a changing-look AGN and binary-SMBH candidate, reporting day-long hard X-ray flares, radio-core evidence, and multiwavelength monitoring.

quotes.quote: Paek SNU Astronomy Research Center, Astronomy Program, Department of Physics and Astronomy, Seoul National University, 1 Gwanak-ro, Gwanak-gu, Seoul, 08826, Republic of Korea Hyun-Il Sung Korea Astronomy and Space Science Institute, 776 Daedeokdae-ro, Yuseong-gu, Daejeon 34055, Republic of Korea Joh-Na Yoon Department of Astronomy and Space Science, Chungbuk National University Observatory, Chungdae-ro 1, Seowon-Gu, Cheongju, Chungbuk 28644, Republic of Korea Megan Masterson mmasters@mit.edu The nuclear transient AT2019cuk/Tick Tock/SDSS J1430+2303 has been suggested to harbor a supermassive black hole (SMBH) binary near coalescence.
In its non-flaring state, AT2019cuk looks like a relatively standard AGN, but it presents the first case of day-long, hard X-ray flares in a changing-look AGN.

These findings illustrate how important high-cadence X-ray monitoring is to our understanding of the rapid variability of the X-ray corona and necessitate further high-cadence, multi-wavelength monitoring of changing-look AGN like AT2019cuk to probe the corona-jet connection.

gmw_00000710.name: Gal. 7
gmw_00000710.overview: Gal. 7 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.9 [arXiv:2105.11572]. It is a strongly lensed source with an intrinsically faint nature, exhibiting a star formation rate and gas mass that are lower than other high-redshift detections by an order of magnitude [arXiv:2105.11572].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Gal. 7 as a strongly lensed, intrinsically faint z=2.9 member with unusually low SFR and gas mass relative to other high-redshift CO detections.

quotes.quote: The outlier of most interest is Gal. 7 because its SFR and gas mass are lower than the bulk of the other z>1 galaxies by an order of magnitude. The fact that it is possible for us to observe this galaxy at all is due to lensing (Gal. 7 is the most strongly lensed source), which allows us to probe intrinsically fainter galaxies. It is the source at the highest magnification, with the "b" image being magnified by a factor of 45 3 , and without lensing, more telescope time than is practical would be required in order to achieve the same SNR as in the presented spectrum. We also note that all of our limits are upper limits, and the true location of the undetected galaxies in the M_ gas SFR parameter space is toward the position of Gal. 7, with lower masses and SFRs than other high-redshift detections.

gmw_00000711.name: FDF-4691
gmw_00000711.overview: FDF-4691 is a compact, high-redshift young starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414, arXiv:astro-ph/0401419] with a modest amount of dust reddening [arXiv:astro-ph/0401419]. It features a double-peaked Lyman-alpha emission line profile [arXiv:0805.3601, arXiv:astro-ph/0702414] with an unusually high line width [arXiv:astro-ph/0702414].

sources.summary: FDF4691 has a high-EW double-peaked Ly-alpha profile; the authors prefer a higher-column, small-b, dust-free shell and require a very broad intrinsic emission line.

quotes.quote: The double-peaked \ profiles (group B) Two of the 11 spectra present double-peaked profiles (FDF4691 and FDF7539). Such line morphologies are a natural outcome of radiation transfer in a static medium Neuf90 , since in such media the \ photons can only escape by diffusing into the red or blue wings, where the opacity decreases rapidly. htb FDF4691 This galaxy has a high EW( ) _ obs 80 . Tapk04 fitted this object with a code using a finite element method Rich01 , and proposed =4. 10^ 17 cm ^ 2 and b=60 \ in an almost static ( =12 ) and dust-free shell as best-fit parameters.

We find a fit of better quality --- the deep gap between the peaks is better reproduced by a smaller b , and the red wing is fitted with more accuracy starting from an input spectrum with a continuum --- with a higher column density ( =8 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 ), and consequently a smaller b=20 Indeed, the location of the peaks were predicted in static media, and depend on a combination of \ and b (see Fig. \ FDF4691_7539 for the fit and Table t_summary for a summary of the parameters). Our best fit is obtained with no dust, and the fit with _a=0.1 is less good than the one with no dust, from which we estimate an upper limit on the dust content of _a 0.1 . This is consistent with the fact that the \ and UV SFR indicators derived from observations Tapk07 are similar. To reproduce the very extended wings of the line, the intrinsic \ emission line has to be very broad.

sources.summary: The paper describes FDF-4691 as a compact high-redshift galaxy with a double-peaked Lyman-alpha profile, unusually high line width, and modelled line formation whose neutral column was poorly constrained.

quotes.quote: The \ profile displays a wide variety of morphologies, from absorption (e.g., FDF-6063), to absorption with a weak emission (FDF-5903), and then to strong emission (e.g., FDF-2384). Most profiles show one asymmetric emission line with a sharp drop on the blue side and a pronounced red wing (e.g., FDF-2384, FDF-5550). This profile type is referred to hereafter as the ``asymmetric line profile''. FDF-4691, FDF-5215, and FDF-7539 show a secondary emission line blueshifted with respect to the main emission line. This profile type is called ``double-peak profile'' hereafter. FDF-1267 FDF-1337 FDF-2384 FDF-3389 FDF-4454 FDF-4691 FDF-5215 FDF-5550 FDF-5744 FDF-5812 FDF-5903 FDF-6063 FDF-6557 FDF-7539 FDF-7683 FDF-8304 The full width half maximum (FWHM) values of the \ emission lines are listed in Table def_lya2 . The distribution of the FWHM values corrected for the instrumental profile is shown in Fig. fig_as_fwhm . The observed values range from 200 \ up to 1500 . For FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, this value refers to the envelope of the profile. Most lines have widths below FWHM 600 , in agreement with Rhoads et al. ( ), Dawson et al. ( ), and Venemans et al. ( ), who analysed LAEs at a redshift of z > 5 and found line widths of FWHM < 500 . Figure fwhm_ewlya shows the \ as a function of the line width of the emission line. The values of the line width show a possible anti-correlation with the \ equivalent widths. The outliers with high line width are FDF-4691 and FDF-7539, both galaxies with double-peak profiles.

The HST/ACS images show the rest-frame UV of the starburst galaxies, which is dominated by the young stars. Moreover, as the surface brightness scales with ( z +1) ^ 4 , the extended regions of the galaxies are not easily detected. The observed diameters of the objects are of the order of 2 kpc, which is typical of Lyman-break galaxies at a redshift of three (Ferguson et al. 2004). The strong \ emitters appear compact (e.g., FDF-4691 or FDF-5215), while the galaxies with a weak (or no) \ emission line are elongated and irregular (e.g., FDF-5903 and FDF-6063). We detected neither (strong) spatial extension of the \ emissions in the two-dimensional spectra nor a spatial offset between the \ emission and the continuum for all of the members of the medium-resolution sample, excepting FDF-2384. Figure images_hst b shows that FDF-2384 has two components, a compact one and a diffuse component, north east of the compact component. The two-dimensional spectrum shows that the continuum is emitted by the compact object, while the \ emission is emitted by the diffuse object.

Therefore, we restricted the finite element modelling to the double-peaked profiles of FDF-4691, FDF-5215, and FDF-7539. A spherical two-component model with a central line emission region surrounded by a shell of neutral HI gas was assumed. For the emission from the central region, we assumed a Gaussian emission profile. The model is mainly characterised by the velocity dispersion of the intrinsic emission line, as well as the velocity dispersion, the neutral column density, and the outflow velocity of the shell. The exact geometrical properties of the model are of minor importance for the computed line profiles (Richling et al. ). In Fig. FDF5215_lyatheo the comparison of the \ profiles of FDF-5215 and FDF-7539 is shown with the theoretical model. The results for FDF-4691 have already been described by Tapken et al. ( ). The derived fit parameters are listed for the three galaxies in Table tab_fem . While N _ HI could not be constrained well for FDF-4691, an upper limit can be found for FDF-5215: N _ HI cannot exceed N _ HI = 2 10^ 16 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper models FDF-4691 as a z = 3.304 young starburst galaxy whose strong Ly emission arises from hot stars, low reddening, and radiative transfer through a neutral shell.

quotes.quote: In order to study the origin of the strong \ emission of high-redshift starburst galaxies we observed and modeled the emission of the z = 3.304 galaxy FDF-4691 (rest-frame EW_ Ly = 103 ). The observations show that FDF-4691 is a young starburst galaxy with a (for this redshift) typical metallicity. The broad, double-peaked profile of the \ emission line can be explained assuming a highly turbulent emission region in the inner part of the starburst galaxy, and a surrounding extended shell of low-density neutral gas with a normal dust/gas ratio and with Galactic dust properties.

Our observations have shown that the Ly galaxy FDF-4691 is a young starburst galaxy with a (for this redshift) normal metallicity and a modest amount of reddening. The Ly flux of the galaxy appears not significantly more attenuated by dust absorption than the UV continuum. According to our models the observed line profile can be explained assuming that a turbulence-produced broad profile of a central emission region is modified by frequency redistribution in a resonance scattering neutral shell around the central H II region.

gmw_00000712.name: NGC 5775
gmw_00000712.overview: NGC 5775 is an edge-on SBc spiral galaxy undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbor, NGC 5774 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509225, arXiv:0810.5393]. This starburst galaxy features widespread star formation [arXiv:0804.3819, arXiv:0810.5393] and an extraplanar halo characterized by an X-shaped magnetic field structure [arXiv:0804.3819, arXiv:1105.5259].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5775 as a well-studied edge-on starburst spiral with widespread star formation, a pronounced dust lane, extraplanar emission, and a gaseous halo linked to magnetic-field structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 5775 has a well-defined disk with evidence of widespread, high-level star formation, as indicated by copious regions of increased stellar density. Its dust lane is similar to that of NGC 4634 and more pronounced than that of NGC 4700. The dust lane is most pronounced in the outer disk regions (i.e. at large galactocentric radii). However, NGC 5775 has a relatively compact nuclear region, that has the bulk of the stellar densities.

NGC 5775 is a well studied edge-on spiral galaxy in the disk-halo context, which was imaged extensively by ground-based observations in H leh95,col00,tul00,ros03b . Extraplanar emission was imaged out to |z| 5 kpc above the galactic plane. It is the galaxy with the highest L_ FIR /D^2_ 25 ratio in our current sample and it is a starburst-type galaxy, although it is morphologically quite different from that of nucleated starbursts. A gaseous halo with individual H spurs or filaments, associated with the X-shaped structure of the large scale magnetic field, was discovered tul00 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5775 as the main observational comparison, describing its edge-on X-shaped polarized halo and comparing modeled polarized emission and rotation measures against the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The polarization vectors of NGC 5775 close to the galactic plane are parallel to the disk, but in the halo they extend up to 10 kpc above the disk forming so called X shaped structure in all four quadrants. Such picture most probably reflects the quadrupole configuration of magnetic field in NGC 5775 . The observed degree of polarization is very high (locally reaching even up to 50 ).

In Fig. fig:model we compare maps of polarized emission for NGC 5775 (the lower panel) and synthetic maps for our model of this galaxy (the upper panel). In both pictures, isolines of polarized intensity are superimposed onto the grayplot of the same quantity, together with the vectors of directions of the apparent magnetic polarization with the length proportional to the intensity. The polarization vectors in the central part of the bodies are parallel to the disk in both modeled and real galaxies. The extended structures, polarized perpendicularly to the disk plane, are visible in both maps as well. However, in the real NGC 5775 the extensions are separated less than in our model.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5775 as an edge-on spiral with an extended radio halo, X-shaped magnetic field, and axisymmetric disk field shaped by wind and dynamo action.

quotes.quote: In order to better determine the large-scale 3D-structure of magnetic fields in spiral galaxies we present a Faraday rotation analysis of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 5775. Deep radio-continuum observations in total power and linear polarization were performed at 8.46 GHz with the VLA and the 100-m Effelsberg telescope. They were analyzed together with archival 4.86 and 1.49 GHz VLA-data. We thus can derive rotation measures from a comparison of three frequencies and determine the intrinsic magnetic field structure. A very extended halo is detected in NGC 5775, with magnetic field lines forming an X-shaped structure.

The rotation measure distribution varies smoothly on both sides along the major axis from positive to negative values. From the derived distribution of rotation measures and the plane-parallel intrinsic magnetic field orientation along the galactic midplane we conclude that NGC 5775 has an even axisymmetric large-scale magnetic field configuration in the disk as generated by an dynamo which is accompanied by a quadrupolar poloidal field. The magnetic field lines of the plane-parallel component are pointing outwards .

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5775 ionized gas, finding a bright extraplanar layer, interaction with NGC 5774, and a vertical azimuthal-velocity gradient in its halo.

quotes.quote: We present imaging Fabry-Perot observations of H emission in the nearly edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 5775. We have derived a rotation curve and a radial density profile along the major axis by examining position-velocity (PV) diagrams from the Fabry-Perot data cube as well as a CO 2--1 data cube from the literature. PV diagrams constructed parallel to the major axis are used to examine changes in azimuthal velocity as a function of height above the midplane. The results of this analysis reveal the presence of a vertical gradient in azimuthal velocity.

NGC 5775 is classified as an SBc galaxy. It is undergoing an interaction with its neighbor, NGC 5774, and a tidal stream of H I connects the two galaxies see irwin94 . coll00 cite a high far-infrared luminosity determined from the IRAS satellite, L_ FIR =7.9 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , and a ``far-infrared surface brightness'' of L_ FIR /D_ 25 ^2=8.4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 (where D_ 25 is the optical isophotal diameter at 25 ^ th magnitude), which is typical of mild starbursts rd03a . The EDIG layer is bright, with a large scale height and filamentary structures coll00 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5775 has a significant halo and flat C-band disk indices, while its L-band field likely contains foreground Galactic polarization from the Fermi bubbles.

quotes.quote: NGC 3556 and NGC 5775 Both of these galaxies are well-known to have significant halos, which we can also see in our data. Moreover, they both show very flat spectral indices at C-band throughout the disk, where the errors in spectral indices are the lowest. Neither data set showed any particular problems and were single pointings. Bearing in mind the summary regarding spectral index in Section sec:alpha_errors , this suggests that thermal emission may be more strongly dominant for these galaxies in their disks.

For NGC 5775 at L-band, some broadscale polarization features have been found in the field that are most likely from foreground Galactic emission (this is more obvious when a larger field than shown in the appendix is displayed). The Galactic coordinates of this source place it almost directly over the Galactic centre, although at reasonably high Galactic latitude. Indeed, the Galactic coordinates of NGC 5775 (l=359.4^ ,b=52.4^ ) indicate that we view it through the northernmost tip of the Fermi bubbles that extend 55 degrees above and below the Galactic centre Ackermannetal2014 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5775 as an interacting starburst-type galaxy with possible tidal interaction, compact southeastern sources, disk ULX candidates, and star formation above the disk.

quotes.quote: ULX 4 in NGC 5775 NGC 5775 was found to be an interacting galaxy with its neighbour face-on spiral galaxy NGC 5774. Estimations based on FIR ratio by Rossa Dettmar (2003) suggested this galaxy to be a starburst-type galaxy. From H images a possible tidal interaction has been suggested to happen, which together with the numerous compact sources at the south-east, presumably HII regions, could be an indication that star formation may be occurring well above the disk (Collins et al. 2000). Sw04 give 4 possible ULXs associated to this galaxy, none of them coincident with the location of the nucleus given by NED. Three Chandra ULXs are located within the galactic disk in the outskirts of the contour emission map (see Figure 3).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5775 has a 2.5-3.5 kpc UV scale height, a flattening halo SED, and a modeled stellar-halo luminosity near 5 x 10^9 solar luminosities.

quotes.quote: We find H 2.5-3.5 kpc for NGC 5775, H 2-3 kpc for NGC 5907, and H 3-5 kpc in NGC 3079. The bins closest to the galaxy follow a typical trend where a central enhancement is visible and the flux declines to larger displacements (except the FUV band in NGC 5775), but higher above the galaxy the profile is much flatter. Notably, the SED of NGC 5775 flattens out at large displacement.

We attempted to determine the stellar halo luminosity in this way for NGC 5907 and NGC 5775, which have some of the best data. For NGC 5907, the model predicts a bolometric stellar halo luminosity of 2 10^9 L_ , and for NGC 5775 about 5 10^9 L_ .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5775 as an edge-on SBc starburst whose tidally triggered disk star formation is associated with outflow and a superwind phase.

quotes.quote: NGC 5775 is a SBc starburst galaxy seen nearly edge-on, i 86 , with star formation extending throughout its visible disk.

As with many interacting pairs (e.g., Kennicutt \ 1987), star formation in NGC 5775, although triggered by external forces, appears to result from tidally induced gas motions within the disk of NGC 5775 rather than by mass transfer between the galaxies.

The observed outflow may be the starburst-driven superwinds from the starburst galaxy, NGC 5775. This indicates that NGC 5775 is in the superwind phase, which can be used as tool to study its evolutionary process.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5775 as a starburst galaxy with FUV halo filaments, multiwavelength extraplanar halos, and UV/H I filaments coincident near the disk.

quotes.quote: Filaments are most easily visible in the FUV, and they are seen in most starburst galaxies (M82, NGC 253, NGC 3079, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, and NGC 5775), as well as two normal galaxies (NGC 4522, NGC 4330). The filaments in the latter two are associated with gas stripped by ram pressure in the Virgo cluster.

The starburst galaxies all have eDIG, soft X-ray halos, extraplanar thermal dust emission (except for NGC 3628), and radio halos. H 1 emission is also seen around all galaxies in the sample with high resolution maps, although in some cases the beamsize makes it unclear how much of the emission is truly extraplanar.

The UV and H 1 filaments do coincide near the disk in the starburst galaxies NGC 253, NGC 3628, NGC 4666, NGC 5775, and NGC 3079 (not all shown in Figure figure.multiwave_sb ). The UV halo is also brighter and more vertically extended on the side of the galaxy with the large H 1 filament in NGC 891, and a similar phenomenon is seen in NGC 3044.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5775 as a worked example for scale-height fitting and finds its C-band halo scale height smaller than earlier work after distance correction.

quotes.quote: The value for NGC 5775 in C-band (which is in both samples) is also smaller than the values given in . However, when corrected to the distance of 26.7 Mpc as taken in , we derive 1.6 0.14 kpc for the C-band halo scale height instead of 2.0 0.2 as given in . The remaining difference may be due to a higher angular resolution of our new maps ( 11 instead of 16 ) and to the more sophisticated method of our new scale height determination which may be important for this small object (in angular size). We already note here that other bright and more nearby galaxies (hence larger in angular and/or physical size) like NGC 891, NGC 4565, and NGC 4631 from the CHANG-ES sample have larger halo scale heights when determined with the same new method (see Schmidt et al., in preparation, Mora-Partiarroyo et al., in preparation) than the galaxies in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper reports that FORS1 spectroscopy traces NGC 5775's DIG halo to at least 10 kpc, with halo rotation dropping toward systemic velocity and ordered magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: Using FORS1 in long-slit mode we could recently establish by spectroscopy (T ullmann et al. 2000) that also the DIG halo in NGC 5775 extents out to at least 10 kpc (Fig. 2), confirming and extending the work by Rand (2000). This finding is in itself of interest in the context of the chemical evolution history of disk galaxies, since the gaseous halo and in particular its hot component could transport and maintain significant amounts of metals.

The deep VLT spectra of NGC 5775 are, however, of interest also with respect to the kinematical information as Fig. 2 shows that the rotational velocity of the DIG halo drops to systemic high above the plane. In addition, it was shown (T ullmann et al. 2000), that the DIG halo is associated with highly ordered magnetic fields, surprising in view of the energy input (i.e., turbulence, flows etc.) from the star formation activity in the underlying disk.

gmw_00000713.name: NGC 5774
gmw_00000713.overview: NGC 5774 is a faint dwarf galaxy that is interacting with its companion, NGC 5775 [arXiv:1105.5259]. This system is connected by two neutral hydrogen bridges in an early merger stage [arXiv:1105.5259], while NGC 5774 itself exhibits a low star formation rate [arXiv:0810.5393].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5774 has low star formation yet hosts three ULXs and five other X-ray binaries, with its X-ray population tied to mass and SFR.

quotes.quote: NGC 5774 has a low SFR of 0.2 \ yr ^ 1 , but the presence of three ULXs and five other X-ray binaries is remarkable. Using the mass and the SFR of NGC 5774 and Equation (4) of Colbert \ (2004) we can determine the total point-source X-ray luminosity of this galaxy, which is (4.15 0.5) 10^ 39 . This is comparable to the observed value and suggests that the X-ray source population of NGC 5774 consists of the older stellar population (including low mass X-ray binaries) and the younger stellar population (including HMXBs), which are directly related to the mass and SFR of NGC 5774 (Colbert \ 2004; Grimm, Gilfanov, Sunyaev 2003b). Three ULXs of NGC 5774 are located in active starforming regions on the spiral arms of this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5774 as a faint dwarf companion interacting with NGC 5775 through H i bridges, with bridge emission suggesting an early-stage merger.

quotes.quote: Indeed, NGC 5775 is known to interact with NGC 5774, a faint dwarf galaxy projected to the north-west of NGC 5775 (Irwin , Gosh et al. ). There are two H i bridges with gas transfer from NGC 5774 to NGC 5775. Optical and radio emission was also detected along these bridges and Irwin suggested that this system may be in an early stage of a merger.

gmw_00000714.name: IC3468
gmw_00000714.overview: IC3468 is a dwarf galaxy in the Virgo cluster that contains an embedded bar or edge-on disc structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0510398, arXiv:0812.3272]. It exhibits complex kinematics with irregular, asymmetric rotation [arXiv:astro-ph/0510398, arXiv:0812.3272] and a central nucleus that is older and more metal-poor than the surrounding disc or bar [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC3468 as a diffuse Virgo galaxy with no young subpopulation, a metallicity gradient, low velocity-dispersion structure, and irregular weak rotation.

quotes.quote: IC3468 Rather low S/N ratio doesn't allow very precise measurements. The results shown are obtained for tessellations with target S/N=10. No young subpopulation is detected.

For a main burst with t=5 Gyr with low metallicity ( [ m/H ] =-0.4) we see some metallicity gradient (from -0.3 in the centre till -0.55 in the outskirts). Velocity dispersion map shows stripe with low values (near the limit of the measurements, about 20 km/s) aligned along the major axis of the isophotes. Radial velocity field is quite irregular and asymmetric, though showing some kind of rotation with the amplitude of 10 km/s.

sources.summary: VCC 1422 contains an embedded bar or edge-on disc, complex IFU kinematics, possible rotation beyond 5 arcsec, and a disc/bar region younger than the periphery.

quotes.quote: VCC 1422 = IC 3468 (Fig figvcc1422 ). An embedded structure is detected in this galaxy interpreted as a bar by or as an edge-on disc by . The IFU spectroscopy reveals complex kinematics with evidences for rotation only beyond 5 arcsec from the centre. There is a hint that stellar population in the region of the disc/bar is somewhat younger than in the periphery of the galaxy ( = 54 4 km s ^ 1 , t=7.2 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] =-0.54 dex), although the difference is on the limit of detection. VCC 1422 is the only galaxy in our sample, where the very central region, dominated by the light of its nucleus exhibits older and more metal-poor stellar population than one of the surrounding disc/bar clearly visible on the maps.

gmw_00000715.name: IC3653
gmw_00000715.overview: IC 3653 is a bright dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0701842]. It hosts an old, embedded stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0701842] that exhibits an inner rotation with an amplitude of approximately 40 km/s [arXiv:astro-ph/0510398].

sources.summary: The paper presents 3D spectroscopy and HST imaging of IC 3653, a bright Virgo dwarf elliptical, finding an old stellar disc and stellar-population structure.

quotes.quote: IC 3653 is a bright dE galaxy belonging to the Virgo cluster (Binggeli et al. 1985). In Table tabic3653params we summarize its main characteristics. IC 3653 was chosen because it is amongst the most luminous dE in Virgo and has a relatively high surface brightness. It is located 2.7 deg from the center of the cluster, i. e. 0.8 Mpc in projected distance.

This is the main observational result from our study of IC 3653, which may be regarded to some extend as an edge-on counterpart of IC 3328, the dE with embedded spiral structure found by Jerjen et al. (2000). The disc of IC 3328 is detected to a larger radius than the one of IC 3653: the spiral structure is seen up to 30 arcsec = 2 r_e , while the disc of IC 3653 is detected only to 3 arcsec = 0.5 r_e . The contrast of the spiral in IC 3328 is a few per cent and in IC 3653 the disc is seen from the colour and only as a weak maximum of ellipticity at the radius of its maximum contrast to the bulge. In both galaxies, the discs are essentially stellar (no sign of star formation) and old.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC3653 has precise central maps, unusual inner disc rotation, declining velocity dispersion, mostly old stellar ages, and a shallow metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: IC3653 Good S/N ratio allows to build precise maps of the parameters till 7'' from the centre of the galaxy. Disc rotation with the amplitude of about 40 km/s is seen in the radial velocity field with the strongest gradient in the inner region, that is quite unusual for objects of such type. Velocity dispersion shows gradient from 90 km/s in the centre till 45 km/s in the outer regions.

Age map is almost flat with a mean age of 7 Gyr, all details seen are not statistically significant except young values in the very centre. Metallicity distribution shows shallow gradient from [ m/H ] =0 in the centre to [ m/H ] =-0.2 at the outskirts with a small metal-rich nuclear region ( [ m/H ] =0.2). There is a marginal detection of younger population t 1 Gyr in the centre which may contain a mass fraction of 1 to 3 percent.

gmw_00000716.name: NGC770
gmw_00000716.overview: NGC 770 is an intermediate-luminosity galaxy experiencing a strong tidal interaction with the giant spiral galaxy NGC 772 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510398]. It hosts a kinematically decoupled, counter-rotating central stellar disc that likely formed during a past dissipative merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0510398].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC770 as tidally interacting with NGC772 and hosting a kinematically decoupled, young, metal-rich counter-rotating central stellar disc from a past dissipative merger.

quotes.quote: NGC770 The galaxy experiences strong tidal interaction with the giant spiral NGC772. Its luminosity (M _B =-18.4) and velocity dispersion (110-120 km/s) place it between giants and dwarfs. Very good S/N ratio allows precise measurements of all the parameters (for example, v 1 km/s ).

Good sampling of LOSVD due to high velocity dispersion allows to measure H3 and H4 Gauss-Hermit coefficients. We see impressive kinematically decoupled core in this object. There are evidences for considering it to be a counter-rotating young metal-rich highly inclined stellar disc aligned almost with the major axis of the galaxy: (1) velocity dispersion map shows larger values on the "switches" of the velocity, there is a stripe with lower values orthogonal to the structures in age/metallicity distributions; (2) map of H3 coefficient shows regions with positive and negative values before and after "switch" of the velocity; (3) age and metallicity distributions clearly show the decoupled structure aligned almost along the major axis having younger age ( t 4.5 Gyr vs 7 Gyr ) and higher metallicity ( [ Me/H ] 0.05 vs 0.2 ) than the surrounding spheroid.

The galaxy probably had a partially dissipative merger event, that induced a burst of star formation some 4.5 Gyr ago producing the central stellar structure that we see now as a counter rotating subpopulation with the high metallicity.

gmw_00000717.name: Messier 85
gmw_00000717.overview: Messier 85 is a peculiar lenticular galaxy in the Virgo Cluster that shows signs of a recent major wet merger, including distorted isophotes, shells, and possible dust patches [arXiv:1811.00018, arXiv:1902.04088]. It hosts a counter-rotating, kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1902.04088, arXiv:astro-ph/0311204] and a complex system of globular clusters with distinct ages and metallicities [arXiv:2010.03041, arXiv:2201.01759].

sources.summary: The paper describes M85-HCC1 as a hypercompact, exceptionally dense stellar system with a young age, soft X-ray source, and likely origin as a tidally stripped galaxy center.

quotes.quote: The second, M85-HCC1, has a size like a typical globular cluster (GC; r_ h 1.8 pc) but is much more luminous ( M_V 12.5 ). This hypercompact cluster is by far the densest confirmed free-floating stellar system, and is equivalent to the densest known nuclear star clusters. From spectroscopy, we find that both objects are relatively young ( 9 Gyr and 3 Gyr, respectively), with metal-abundances that resemble those of galaxy centers. Their host galaxies show clear signs of large-scale disturbances, and we conclude that these dense objects are the remnant nuclei of recently accreted galaxies.

Turning to M85-HCC1, its compactness combined with its luminosity places it nearly in a class of its own among confirmed, free-floating old stellar systems, with a luminosity like the brightest classical UCDs but with a size like the smallest GCs (Figure fig:uber ). Although fading with age would place it close to the previously densest-known star cluster, NGC 4494-UCD1 Foster11 , that object also shows signs of fading youth Usher15 . The mean surface brightness and mass density of M85-HCC1 are 4 10^5 L_ , pc ^ 2 and 6 10^5 M_ pc ^ 2 , which is 10 denser than M59-UCD3. The volume densities are 100 higher: 7 10^4 L_ , pc ^ 3 and 10^5 M_ pc ^ 3 . For additional context, we compare to two classes of object with possible evolutionary connections to UCDs and GCs: young massive clusters (YMCs, generally formed during galactic starbursts), and galactic nuclei---also called nuclear star clusters (NSCs). Stellar masses should be compared, rather than luminosities, since young objects will naturally fade with time (a key issue for the YMCs and NSCs, not so much for the GCs/UCDs). For our new objects' stellar masses, we use spectral model fitting (below). For YMCs and NSCs, N+14 have provided a compilation. These objects have a wide range of densities, making them plausible as progenitors of both GCs and UCDs. However, the maximum reported YMC density is 2.5 10^5 M_ pc ^ 2 --lower than M85-HCC1 by half.
We note that the original motivation for SDSS-III spectroscopy of M85-HCC1 was its X-ray source, which has fairly soft emission and a luminosity of L_ X = 2.6 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 Sivakoff03 .

sources.summary: The paper presents M85 as a peculiar galaxy whose globular-cluster subpopulations have distinct metallicities and kinematics, including disk-like red-cluster rotation and major-axis green-cluster alignment.

quotes.quote: We present a study on stellar population and kinematics of globular clusters (GCs) in the peculiar galaxy M85. We obtain optical spectra of 89 GCs at 8 kpc < R < 160 kpc using the MMT/Hectospec. We divide them into three groups, blue/green/red GCs (B/G/RGCs), with their (g-i)_0 colors. All GC subpopulations have mean ages of 10 Gyr, but showing differences in metallicities. The BGCs and RGCs are the most metal-poor ( [ Z/H ] 1.49 ) and metal-rich ( [ Z/H ] 0.45 ), respectively, and the GGCs are in between.

We find that the inner GC system exhibits a strong overall rotation that is entirely due to a disk-like rotation of the RGC system. The BGC system shows little rotation. The GGCs show kinematic properties clearly distinct among the GC subpopulations, having higher mean velocities than the BGCs and RGCs and being aligned along the major axis of M85.

sources.summary: The paper presents first GC spectroscopy for M85, finding 20 member clusters and strong GC-system rotation; it describes M85 as a peculiar Virgo S0 merger remnant with a wet merger about 4 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the first spectroscopic study of globular clusters (GCs) in M85. We obtain spectra for 21 GC candidates and the nucleus of M85 using the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph on the Gemini North 8.1 m telescope. From their radial velocities, 20 of the GCs are found to be members of M85. We find a strong rotation signal of the M85 GC system with a rotation amplitude of 235 .

These results suggest that M85 experienced a wet merging event about 4 Gyr ago, forming a significant population of star clusters. The strong rotational feature of the GC system can be explained by an off-center major merging. galaxies: abundances --- galaxies: elliptical and lenticular, cD --- galaxies: clusters: individual (Virgo) --- galaxies: individual (M85) --- galaxies: star clusters: general --- globular clusters: general M85 (NGC 4382) is an S0pec galaxy in the northernmost region of the Virgo Cluster and has many interesting and unusual properties. It has the second highest fine-structure index, = 6.85 , in the list of merger remnant elliptical and S0 galaxies in ss92 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies M85 as a peculiar S0 Virgo Cluster galaxy hosting SN 2020nlb and estimates its distance modulus from the supernova light curve, comparing SBF and PNLF distances.

quotes.quote: Supernova 2020nlb exploded in M85 (NGC 4382), a peculiar S0 galaxy 1991rc3..book.....D in the Virgo Cluster. The primary distance indicator for nearby galaxies are Cepheid variables; however, these are not found in passive environments. Therefore, for early-type galaxies, an alternative must be used. The distance to M85 has been estimated using a number of different methodologies.

We use our light curve of SN 2020nlb to estimate the distance to M85. Fitting the light-curve with SNooPy (using `EBV model') yields a distance modulus of _0=30.99 0.19 mag. This distance modulus corresponds to a distance of 15.8^ +1.4 _ 1.3 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents preliminary WIFIS results for Messier 85, showing a central spectrum, continuum image, good spectral-model agreement, and features useful for stellar-population work.

quotes.quote: Given that we are carrying out a large survey of nearby galaxies to study their stellar populations, we present preliminary results of Messier 85 in Figure fig:m85 . The figure shows a telluric corrected spectrum of the galaxy within its central 10 generated from one hour of data on-source and a reconstructed continuum image with the extraction region highlighted. Overplotted on the spectrum is a model spectrum that matches the age and metallicity of the galaxy generated using a spectral synthesis model . The model spectrum has only been scaled by a multiplicative factor to match the spectrum at 1.2 m.
The model agreement is very good. Several detected metal and hydrogen features are highlighted in the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper links VCC 798 to a recent merger, possible dust and a stellar disk, young luminosity-weighted age, and intermediate-age globular clusters.

quotes.quote: VCC 798 (NGC 4382). The morphological feature of this lenticular galaxy shows that it experienced a recent merger 2018ApJ...859..108K . This galaxy shows possible dust patches and likely hosts a stellar disk 2006ApJS..164..334F . 2002MNRAS.330..547T estimated the age of VCC 798 based on the H and [ MgFe ] indices. Its luminosity-weighted age, 1.6 Gyr, points to recent star formation.
2018ApJ...859..108K estimated the spectroscopic ages of 20 GCs with the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph. Among their sample GCs, 11 GCs are classified as an intermediate-age population ( 3.7 Gyr).

sources.summary: The paper selects M85-HCC1 for a tentative UCD emission estimate; although its parameter lies beyond the fitted range, the authors apply the MSP correlation and predict undetectably low Fermi-LAT flux.

quotes.quote: Taking advantage of these similarities, a tentative estimate of the ray emission for a couple of UCDs was performed. To have an idea of their ray luminosities, some of the densest known UCDs were chosen: M59-UCD3 liu2015most and M85-HCC1 sandoval2015hiding and the N_ MSP correlation discussed in section results was applied. Using their central luminosity densities and core radii (the core radius r_ core was assumed here as 1/5 r_ half , the half-light radius, which is very close to the mean value for the ratio r_ core /r_ half in GLOBCLUST catalog) given by , their values for and then L_ were estimated (Table Table3_UCD ).

Despite M85-HCC1 presenting a value for beyond the fitted range of Figure PL_x_line , it was assumed that the correlation was still valid at least as a first approximation. To estimate their energy fluxes, isotropic emission was assumed and the distances to M59 and M85 were taken from . Despite their high luminosity, these compact galaxies are so far away that their energy fluxes are extremely low, not detectable by the Fermi-LAT.

sources.summary: The paper surveys M85's globular-cluster system, describing it as a peculiar early-type Virgo galaxy with merger-induced features, younger red clusters, and evidence for a recent major wet merger.

quotes.quote: M85 (NGC 4382, VCC 798) is the fifth-brightest early-type galaxy in the Virgo Cluster, classified as S0 _ 1 (3)pec in bst85 , SA0 ^ + pec in dev91 , and E2 in kor09 . It is located in the northernmost region of the Virgo Cluster, far from the main body of the Virgo Cluster, where bright early-type galaxies are located. M85 shows peculiar features not only in its structure and kinematics but also in its GC system. It shows several merger-induced features such as distorted isophotes bur79 , shells, ripples ss88 , boxy isophotes fer06 , and a kinematically decoupled core mcd04 .

The mean (g-i)_0 color of the RGCs in M85 is about 0.1 mag bluer than typical values for other Virgo early-type galaxies of similar luminosity, indicating that a significant fraction of the RGCs in M85 may be younger than typical GCs. These results indicate that M85 might have undergone a major wet merger recently.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4382 as a merger remnant with complex globular-cluster subpopulations, young central stellar population, metallicity gradient, and merger-related morphological signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 4382 is a merger-remnant galaxy that has been classified as morphological type E2, S0, and even Sa. In this work, we performed a photometric and spectroscopic analysis of the globular cluster (GC) system of this peculiar galaxy in order to provide additional information about its history.

The photometric analysis of the GC system, using the Gaussian Mixture Model algorithm in the colour plane, reveals a complex colour distribution within R_ gal <5 arcmin (26.1 kpc), showing four different groups: the typical blue and red subpopulations, a group with intermediate colours, and the fourth group towards even redder colours.
