As with UGCA 086, most of the NED `infrared sources' are not matched to SIMBAD sources; NED also contains about 150 times as many stars as does SIMBAD for this galaxy. fig:CS2_Match_Distribution shows that almost all of the classification non-matches between these two galaxies come as a result of a single conflicting object classification: region (HII) as classified by NED matched to stellar association (As*) as classified by SIMBAD. Examination of the original identifications for these objects shows that in fact the original source for both databases is the work by 2009MNRAS.396.2295H .

sources.summary: NGC 2903 is used as a failed-fit example: its high central surface brightness and nearly maximal disk leave little room for dark matter, causing compressed halos to overshoot.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:FailExample shows the rotation curves of NGC 2903 as an example. This galaxy has a high central surface brightness and its disk is nearly maximal. This leaves no much room for dark matter. As a result, the DM halos is strongly compressed and the resulting rotation curve well overshoot the data. This is a general behaviour for high surface-brightness galaxies in the CDM framework. In order to achieve a better fit, one has to reduce the stellar mass-to-light ratio, and perhaps also the concentration of the initial NFW halo.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2903 as a nearby Sbc spiral with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The nearby galaxy NGC 2903 is a Sbc spiral galaxy (morphological type code T = 4.0 0.1). Its inclination angle is i = 65 and the position angle of the major axis PA = 204 deBlok2008 . The optical radius of NGC 2903 is R _ 25 = 5.87 arcmin Walter2008 . The distance to NGC 2903 is d = 8.9 Mpc Drozdovsky2000 that results in the physical optical radius of R_ 25 = 15.21 kpc. The mean value of the estimations of the stellar mass of NGC 2903 (rescaled to the adopted distance) is M _ = 3.33 10 ^ 10 M _ Jarrett2019,Leroy2021 .

sources.summary: NGC 2903 is treated as an isolated, high-mass spiral analog whose ALFALFA satellite selection converges on AGC 191706, while ELVES finds additional lower-mass satellites.

quotes.quote: NGC 2903 NGC 2903 is an isolated SAB(rs)bc galaxy. With a derived halo mass of M_ Halo 1.6 10^ 12 M_ , it is one of the closest MW/M31 analogs (by mass) in our sample. All satellite selection methods defined in subsec:sat_search converge to one gaseous satellite candidate, AGC 191706, with relatively high F_ d_ 3D confidence. AGC 191706 is confirmed as a satellite by irwin_cdm_2009 (deep targeted HI) and carlsten_exploration_2022-1 (ELVES; deep optical), although both works find additional low-mass HI sources that are likely below the ALFALFA detection limit. In particular, ELVES finds three more gaseous/star-forming satellites and two more quenched satellites within R_ 200 , which gives a 33 - relatively low quenched fraction compared to the MW/M31 ( sec:elves_saga_discussion ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a significant AME detection in NGC 2903, with SED fits favoring a 10-100 GHz bump and relatively strong AME emissivity compared with earlier extragalactic measurements.

quotes.quote: Combining with the ancillary data, we investigated the radio-IR spectral energy distribution (SED), while searching for the AME signals in five galaxies. The AME in NGC 2903 was well detected at a significant confidence level, whereas that in NGC 2146 and M82 was marginal. NGC 1068 and Arp 299 indicated no significant hints, and we provided the upper limits for the AME.

Notably, AME played a crucial role within the frequency range of 10-100 GHz, particularly in the case of the AME-detected galaxy, NGC 2903. The signature of AME For NGC 2903, NGC 2146, and M82, the observed data exhibited better agreement with the SED that included a bump in the range of 10–100 GHz, both visually and based on quantitative statistical evaluation, which is suggestive of AME.

The AME emissivity of NGC 2903 was ^ AME _ _ peak = 0.7 0.4 MJy sr ^ 1 (M _ pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 at _ peak and the mean AME emissivity of three detections ^ AME _ _ peak was 0.8 0.3 MJy sr ^ 1 (M _ pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 at _ peak . For comparison, the mean AME emissivity at 30 GHz for our three detections ^ AME _ 30 \ GHz was 0.2 0.1 MJy sr ^ 1 (M _ pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 , which is higher by one order of magnitude than that obtained for AME detection in M31 estimated at 30 GHz ( 0.03 0.00 MJy sr ^ 1 (M _ pc ^ 2 ) ^ 1 ) Battistelli_2019, Bianchi_2022 . This difference may be attributed to the environment where AME was detected as further discussed in the following section. Adopting the dust-to-gas ratio of 0.0074 2017A&A...602A..46J , the AME emissivities in the unit of H-column density were 4.4 2.5 10^ 17 and 4.4 1.5 10^ 17 Jy sr ^ 1 (H cm ^ 2 ) ^ 1 for NGC 2903 and the mean AME emissivity of three detections, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2903 as a starburst galaxy with circumnuclear star-formation peaks, a strong bar velocity gradient, a step in the rotation curve, and molecular gas following the bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 2903: This starburst galaxy shows several peaks of star formation in the circumnuclear region. These peculiar hotspots \ have been identified and described in different ways by various authors in ( 1983A&A...128..140M 1983A&A...128..140M ), in radio and in the infrared by 1985ApJ...290..108W ( 1985ApJ...290..108W ). A strong velocity gradient can be seen along the bar in the velocity map. The PV diagram shows a clear step in the RC. This step-like structure could be related to its strong bar.

sources.summary: The paper states that NGC 2903 rotation data are fit by Newtonian and Carmelian regimes, with tabulated scale radii, masses, and transition radius.

quotes.quote: Figs fig:fig4 and fig:fig5 show both Newtonian and Carmelian models fit the rotation curve data for the galaxies NGC 2903 and NGC 7331. In the high acceleration regime a Newtonian fit is applicable and when the acceleration drops below 2 3 a_ 0 the Carmelian applies. From the Table the best fit determined values of a and M for each galaxy are listed. They are compared with published values of a and masses determined from different methods. In these and all subsequent top (a) figures, the fits for curves 2 and 3, respectively, apply only for accelerations less than and greater than the critical acceleration. In the rotation curve fits, in the top (a) figures, the circular velocities are not added but each apply over their respective regimes. This means the masses determined from the Newtonian fits must be less than those from the Carmelian fits because the Newtonian determined mass must lay within the radius R where the two curves cross. The Newtonian fits occur in the stronger acceleration regimes ( > 2 3 a_ 0 ) close to the galactic center as indicated by all of the bottom (b) figures.

sources.summary: NGC 2903 is presented as a grand-design spiral with a central starburst, bar-aligned radio/H/CO emission, and an H i structure extending beyond the stellar disk with a warp.

quotes.quote: This is a grand-design spiral with a bright central region characterised by a starburst. A high-resolution study of the central region was performed by Saikia et al. ). The radio continuum, H and CO emission are almost identically distributed along a central bar (Jackson et al. ). The H i traces both the inner and outer spiral structures, but also extends far beyond the stellar disk, though with a different position angle. As in the above case, this is indicative of a warp (eg. Wevers et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes CNSFRs in NGC 2903, finding stellar dispersions larger than H gas dispersions and [O III] widths closer to stellar values, with ionization differences between broad and narrow components.

quotes.quote: We have analysed the results derived by H07, H09 and H10 using high spectral resolution data of 17 CNSFRs belonging to three barred spiral galaxies: NGC 2903, NGC 3310 and NGC 3351. The stellar velocity dispersions were measured from the CaT lines at 8494, 8542, 8662 . The gas velocity dispersions have been measured by Gaussian fits to the H 4861 \ and the [ O iii ] 5007 \ emission lines. Stellar velocity dispersions range between 31 and 80 km s ^ 1 . In the case of NGC 2903 and NGC 3351 ( _ between 37 and 64, and between 39 and 66 km s ^ 1 , respectively) these values are about 25 km s ^ 1 larger than those measured for the gas from the H emission line using a single Gaussian fit. For NGC 3310 the stellar (between 31 and 80 km s ^ 1 ) and gas (H ) velocity dispersions are in relatively good agreement, the stellar ones being marginally larger.

Single Gaussian fitting to the [ O iii ] 5007 \ line provides gas velocity dispersions for NGC 2903 and NGC 3351 that are very similar to - or marginally larger than - the stellar ones. The gas velocity dispersion derived from a single Gaussian fit for the [ O iii ] line in NGC 3310 is also comparable to the stellar velocity dispersion but, contrary to what was found in the other two galaxies, its value is very similar also to that derived using H . The best Gaussian fits for H , however, involved two different components for the gas: a ``broad component'' with a velocity dispersion similar to that measured for the stars for NGC 2903 and NGC 3351, and about 20 km s ^ 1 larger than the stellar one for NGC 3310, and a ``narrow component'' with a velocity dispersion lower than the stellar one by about 30 km s ^ 1 . This narrow component shows a relatively constant value for the two emission lines (H and [ ] ), close to 23 km s ^ 1 (with scatter errors of 1.1 and 1.4 , respectively) for all the studied CNSFRs. The [ O iii ] /H ratio distribution shows that the two systems are clearly segregated for the high-metallicity regions of NGC 2903 and NGC 3351, with the narrow component having the lowest excitation.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2903 as a steeply declining THINGS H i rotation-curve case, noting central bar streaming motions and sensitivity of the fitted slope to that inner curve.

quotes.quote: We therefore fit the polyex model to the two most steeply declining H i rotation curves in the THINGS sample deblok08 , namely NGC 4736 and NGC 2903. Unfortunately, for both these galaxies the inner slope is not tightly constrained (for NGC 4736 due to absence of H i in the center; for NGC 2903 due to streaming motions induced by the central bar), leading to some degeneracy between the r_ PE and fit parameters. For NGC 4736 we find values of between 0.001 and 0.002 . When the part of the rotation curve of NGC 2903 that is affected by the bar is not taken into account, we find 0.002 . Fitting the full curve yields 0.018 , though this value is likely affected by the uncertain inner rotation curve and the associated uncertainy in r_ PE .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2903 as an isolated barred spiral whose metallicity diagnostics show bar-related fine structure and generally shallow gradients.

quotes.quote: Specifically, the isolated barred galaxies (NGC 2903 NGC 5713) show very shallow N202-KD02 gradients of 0.33 0.01 in units of dex ( R / ) ^ 1 and -0.023/ 0.003 in units of dex kpc ^ 1 .

The fine structure of the radial profile for the isolated barred galaxy NGC 2903 shows a positive gradient in the nucleus which then flattens then inverts to a negative gradient towards the end of the bar. This pattern is seen using all the diagnostics except those based on N2, which shows opposite behavior in the gradient (see discussion in sec:results-zgas ). The gradient then becomes flatter and smooth in the outer disc beyond the bar.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 2903 as an isolated barred spiral with small orientation variations, low radial velocity, and bar-correlated non-axisymmetric flow.

quotes.quote: NGC 2903 NGC 2903 is an isolated barred spiral galaxy. The TRM detects an inclination angle with small variation in the inner disk, i.e., for R<R_ 25 360'' , while in the outer regions of the galaxy, i(R) varies by < 10^ , whereas the P.A. is almost constant across the whole disk (see Fig. NGC2903 ). Given these small variations, we find that v_t(R) v_c(R) .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2903 among four galaxies whose rotation curves are fit with the modified acceleration law, reporting good agreement and the galaxy's fitted luminous mass and core radius.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 4, we show fits to the four galaxies NGC 1560, NGC 2903, NGC 4565 and NGC 5055 using the modified acceleration law ( coremodel ) including the galaxy core with =2 . As can be seen from the data fitting, the agreement with the core and extended rotation curves data is good for the two fitting parameters M and the core radius r_c . In Fig. 5, we display a 3-dimensional plot of v versus the range of distance 0.1 kpc < < 10 kpc and the range of total galaxy mass M used in the fitting of rotational velocity data.
For NGC 2903 the luminous mass used to fit the data is M=9.95 10^ 10 M_ , while the core radius is r_c=2.75 kpc .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2903 as a barred starburst spiral with bar and arm star formation; CO and H-alpha offsets reveal structured star-forming gas along the bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 2903 . This large starburst galaxy has a large-scale bar visible in CO and in high-resolution RC images. The H images knapen02 show star-formation along the bar and in the arms, mostly in the southern arm, and in the circum-nuclear region. The bar has been studied in detail by , who have compared the distribution of the molecular gas, traced using CO emission, and star formation activity, traced using the H emission line. They have found that the CO emission is brightest along the leading edges of the bar, while the majority of the H emission is offset, with values between 200-800 pc towards the leading side of the CO.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2903 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2903 .SXT4../Sc(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge light is dominated by a flocculent spiral that breaks into clumps in the center. The decomposition yields n_b=0.5 , and the bulge is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =2.2 mag.

sources.summary: The MOND analysis finds ambiguous bulge mass-to-light fits for NGC 2903, overpredicted disk mass-to-light, and added uncertainty from a likely central bar.

quotes.quote: The only three galaxies where the best-fit disk M/L ratio differs from the population synthesis one by more than a factor of two are NGC 2903, NGC 2976, and NGC 7331 (see Fig. colours and Table 2). In NGC 2903 the MOND fit significantly overpredicts the M/L , a phenomenon that was also observed in Newtonian mass models with dark matter (de Blok et al. 2008). Let us note that, if one would follow population synthesis predictions, the predicted stellar disk would be surprisingly very sub-maximum for a massive galaxy with a rapidly increasing rotation curve with maximum velocity 215 km s ^ 1 . In addition, in the central parts of NGC 2903 there is evidence for a bar (e.g. Leroy et al. 2009): the non-circular motions associated to it further increase the uncertainties on the mass modelling results (see also Sellwood Z anmar S anchez 2010).

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 2903 as the high-surface-brightness comparison case, with MOND fitting its 21 cm rotation curve and a near-Keplerian decline to the final velocity.

quotes.quote: These are 21 cm line rotation curves of a LSB, low-surface-brightness, (NGC 1560) and a HSB, high surface brightness galaxy, (NGC 2903), where the points show the observations, the dotted and dashed curves are the Newtonian rotation curves of the observed baryonic components (stars and gas), and the solid curves are the MOND curves resulting from the simple algorithm (with a_0 = 1.2 10^ 10 m/s ^2 ). One should note that the rotation curves have the general forms for LSB and HSB predicted by Milgrom in 1983: in the LSB galaxy the curve slowly rises to its asymptotic form and in the HSB the curve falls in a near Keplerian fashion to the final value. The required mass-to-light ratios (shown on the figure) are quite reasonable for a gas dominated and a star dominated galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2903 as a strongly barred nuclear-burst EMPIRE galaxy, uses Spitzer infrared maps because KINGFISH coverage is absent, and finds HCN prominent in its central bar.

quotes.quote: It also covers strongly barred galaxies (NGC 2903, NGC 3627), flocculent disks (NGC 5055, NGC 6946), strong nuclear bursts (NGC 2903, NGC 4321, NGC 6946), Virgo cluster members (NGC 4254, NGC 4321), and interacting galaxies (NGC 3627, NGC 5194).

NGC 2903 and NGC 5194 lack KINGFISH coverage, therefore we use Spitzer 24 m emission maps Local Volume Legacy, LVL, from DALE09 for NGC 2903 and other Herschel data for NGC 5194 Very Nearby Galaxy Survey, VNGS, from BENDO12 .
The HCN intensity peaks at the center of each target and then appears prominent along the spiral arms (e.g., NGC 5055, NGC 5194 and NGC 6946) and central bars (e.g., NGC 2903 and NGC 3627).

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2903 as barred and reports that its clouds have higher mean virial parameter than most targets, with a steep truncation and a few high-mass clouds.

quotes.quote: Bar - Six of our 10 galaxies have stellar bars (NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 5068, NGC 5643, NGC 6300).
Clouds in NGC 5068 have a lower mean virial parameter than other galaxies whereas clouds in NGC 4826 and NGC 2903 have a higher mean virial parameter.
Some systems show a steep truncation and then a few high-mass clouds (NGC 1637 and NGC 2903).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2903 is enhanced by the Prim field and is one of the main starburst sources populating HS3 in the modeled arrival maps.

quotes.quote: The enhancement happens to M82, NGC 4945, NGC 2903, NGC 2146, and For A.
HS3 is mostly populated by NGC 2903 and NGC 3079 independently of the EGMF model in the case of light and intermediate composition, as shown in figure fig:contribution_HS3 .
The HS3 is mostly populated by SBGs (NGC 2903 and NGC 3019) with a smaller contribution of Vir A, however the results are more dependent on the EGMF model and injected nuclei.

gmw_00000724.name: WLM
gmw_00000724.overview: Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) is an isolated, gas-rich dwarf irregular galaxy located on the outskirts of the Local Group [arXiv:1601.05821, arXiv:2405.09104]. It is seen nearly edge-on [arXiv:2401.14889] and exhibits active, ongoing star formation [arXiv:2405.09104] alongside a stellar population that includes carbon stars [arXiv:2312.04626] and a single old globular cluster [arXiv:1309.0822].

sources.summary: The paper discusses WLM as a low-mass dwarf benchmark whose AMR and sparse old globular-cluster population help interpret possible Milky Way halo cluster progenitors.

quotes.quote: We note that the relative difference in specific SFR Karim11 and metallicity Zahid13 between two galaxies of different mass stays roughly constant with time (back to redshift 3) as they evolve --- at least for masses similar to the MW and the LMC. This suggests that the halo GCs would have formed in dwarf galaxies comparable to the SMC, WLM, or even the LMC and Sagittarius. This is empirically illustrated in Figure fig:gcamrs , where the AMRs for the MW thin disk, as well as those that have been derived for three dwarf galaxies are overlaid We do not show the Sagittarius AMR due to the extreme difficulty in selecting clean, representative samples of RGB stars in this object, however the AMR given by follows a similar shape and lies between that of the LMC and SMC. on the GC AMRs.

These predictions are based on the assumed merger history from the simulations we consider, but suggest that if the MW has experienced 3 WLM-sized mergers, they would have contributed only a total of 4 GCs, while one merger of an LMC-sized system would have contributed 35 GCs While the LMC itself does not contain this many old GCs, dwarfs of similar mass in show a wide range of S_ N values. . This is in close agreement with positions of the halo GCs with respect to the AMRs of the LMC and WLM in Figure fig:gcamrs . Therefore it is plausible that most of the Halo GC system came from 6-7 mergers of WLM- to LMC-sized dwarfs. It is interesting to note that, while the absolute number of merged dwarfs is different for the or subhalo mass distributions, they both suggest that the GC systems were primarily built from the 6-7 most massive mergers.

Indeed WLM itself, with a stellar mass of 5 10^ 7 , has only one old GC, while the LMC and SMC, and particularly Sagittarius are known to have extended cluster AMRs of 3 Gyrs or more Glatt08,Law10,Colucci11 . This could be an indication that less massive dwarfs, which exhaust their gas reservoirs quickly (perhaps due to the efficient SNe induced blowout of gas from their shallow potential wells), form GCs for only a short interval of time.

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM as a representative rotation-curve fit, where the feedback-modified profile fits well and an NFW fit gives a consistent halo mass despite poorer curve shape.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:exrots , we show three example rotation curve fits for CVnIdwA, WLM and NGC 6822, chosen to span the range of stellar masses in our full sample (see Table tab:data ). (We show the full sample, including the `rogues', in Appendix app:rotcurves .) The black contours show the median (black), 68 (dark grey) and 95 (light grey) confidence intervals of our fitted rotation curve models (see sec:massmodel ). The vertical green dashed line shows the projected stellar half light radius R_ 1/2 . The thin vertical black line marks the inner data point used for the fit, R_ min (where this is not marked R_ min = 0 ). The blue and green lines show the rotation curve contribution from stars and gas, respectively. The top three panels of Figure fig:exrots show fits using the \ profile, the bottom three using an \ profile, where we set R_ min to ensure that the outer rotation curve is well-fit (see sec:rotmethod for details of our fitting methodology and priors). As can be seen in Figure fig:exrots , in all three cases the \ profile provides an excellent fit to the data, while the \ profile gives a poor fit, reaffirming the long-standing `cusp-core' problem (see sec:intro ).

The good fits that we find when using the \ profile are particularly striking since, like the \ profile, it has only two free parameters: M_ 200 and (see sec:massmodel ). However, despite the \ profile giving a poor fit to the rotation curve shape, the NFW-derived M_ 200 are actually in good agreement with those from our \ fits. For NGC 6822, we find M_ 200, NFW = 2.0_ 0.2 ^ +0.13 10^ 10 M _ ; for WLM, M_ 200, NFW = 5.2_ 1.2 ^ +2.1 10^ 9 M _ ; and for CVnIdwA, M_ 200, NFW = 0.79_ 0.3 ^ +0.5 10^ 9 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper presents UVIT imaging of WLM, mapping extended FUV/NUV emission, hot star-forming complexes, clumpy hierarchical structure, vigorous southwest star formation, compact regions, and a 0.008 solar-mass-per-year star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: We present a study of star forming regions and its demographics in the nearby dwarf irregular galaxy WLM using the Ultra-Violet Imaging Telescope (UVIT) multi band observations in three filters F148W, N245M and N263M. We find that the UV emission is extended at least up to 1.7 kpc, with the NUV emission more extended than the FUV.

We identify several complexes with temperature T > 17500 K which are likely to be the OB associations present in the galaxy. These complexes show good spatial correlation with the H emitting regions, H I distribution and HST detected hot stars. The hot star forming regions are found to be clumpy in nature and show a hierarchical structure, with sizes in the range of 4 - 50 pc, with a large number with sizes < 10 pc. The south western part of the galaxy shows many hot star forming regions, high level of H emission and low column density of H I which altogether denote a vigorous recent star formation.

WLM is likely to have a large fraction of low mass compact star forming regions with mass M < 10^3 M_ , in agreement with the size and mass of the CO clouds. We estimate the star formation rate of WLM to be 0.008 M_ /yr , which is similar to the average value measured for nearby dwarf irregular galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents WLM as an isolated dwarf irregular dark-matter target with smooth H I kinematics, a coreNFW halo model, a high-precision J factor, and H.E.S.S. non-detection limits on annihilation.

quotes.quote: WLM is a dwarf irregular galaxy located at (l = 75.86 , b = -73.62 ) at 1 Mpc from the Milky Way. It possesses a star-forming region at its center and is isolated from other astrophysical sources. This dwarf possesses excellent HI data with a smooth HI distribution and a well-measured photometry and stellar kinematics with an extension of its halo of r_ halo = 49.4 kpc ( _ halo = 2.89 ). WLM is rotationally supported with no significant non-circular motions in the gas.

Fitting the results of an MCMC on the coreNFW profile parameters, we derive a J factor of _ 10 J ( GeV ^2. cm ^ 5 ) = 16.6 0.037 (Fig. J_factor_fit ) based on the DM profile derived in . WLM represents a very promising target among the dIrrs as it possesses one of the highest J factor with extremely small uncertainties compared to those of some other dIrrs (eg. Aquarius). In 2018, H.E.S.S. collected about 19 hours of data towards WLM with an offset of 0.5 and 0.8 .

As no significant excess has been found towards WLM in the ROI, upper limits on the DM annihilation cross section v at 95 C.L. vs. the DM mass are computed using the loglikelihood ratio method for the b b , ^+ ^- , W^+W^- and Z^+Z^- annihilation channels (fig. UL_sigmav ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative CGM detection around WLM, linking the v-150 absorber to WLM rather than the Magellanic Stream and estimating a substantial silicon reservoir outside its stars and ISM.

quotes.quote: We report a tentative detection of the circumgalactic medium (CGM) of WLM, an isolated, low-mass (log M_*/M_ 7.6 ), dwarf irregular galaxy in the Local Group (LG). We analyze an HST/COS archival spectrum of a quasar sightline (PHL2525), which is 45 kpc (0.5 virial radius) from WLM and close to the Magellanic Stream (MS). Along this sightline, two ion absorbers are detected in Si II, Si III, Si IV, C II, and C IV at velocities of 220 km s ^ 1 (Component v-220) and 150 km s ^ 1 (Component v-150).

To identify their origins, we study the position-velocity alignment of the components with WLM and the nearby MS. Near the Magellanic longitude of PHL2525, the MS-related neutral and ionized gas moves at 190 km s ^ 1 , suggesting an MS origin for Component v-220, but not for Component v-150. Because PHL2525 passes near WLM and Component v-150 is close to WLM's systemic velocity ( 132 km s ^ 1 ), it is likely that Component v-150 arises from the galaxy's CGM.

This results in a total Si mass in WLM's CGM of M_ Si ^ CGM (0.2-1.0) 10^5 M_ using assumption from other COS dwarf studies. Comparing M_ Si ^ CGM to the total Si mass synthesized in WLM over its lifetime ( 1.3 10^5 M_ ), we find 3 is locked in stars, 6 in the ISM, 15 77 in the CGM, and the rest ( 14 76 ) is likely lost beyond the virial radius.

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as a massive Solo dwarf with extensive spectroscopy, aligned stellar and HI structures, slow continuous star formation, and possible two-component profile structure.

quotes.quote: Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte WLM, also known as DDO 221, is one of the more massive dwarfs in the Solo Survey. It has been extensively studied, from its discovery over 100 years ago Wolf1909 to recent work presented in . Several spectroscopic studies have been conducted. For example, RGB stars have been targeted by and blue supergiants have been targeted by . concluded that this dwarf is a highly oblate spheroid.

They found good agreement, kinematically and photometrically, between the position angle and ellipticity of the RGB stars, young carbon stars and HI gas. They concluded that external environmental effects have not had a dominant role in shaping the structure of WLM. Using HST observations, derived a SFH history and found relatively continuous star formation, with no dominant early episode ( Gallart2015 classified the SFH of WLM as "slow"). This dwarf is one of the few with a known globular cluster, in this case a single large globular cluster located just outside the central region.

sources.summary: The paper presents WLM as an isolated Local Group field dwarf whose offset gas and trailing clouds indicate ram-pressure stripping far from the Milky Way and M31.

quotes.quote: Here, we report on new observations of the gaseous content of Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM), an archetype of isolated, gas-rich field dwarf galaxies in the Local Universe, which presents a much more complex situation. Previous studies of its gaseous disk suggest it has perturbed kinematics; here, we identify four trailing, extended gas clouds lying in the direction opposite to WLM's spatial motion, as well as a spatial offset between the WLM gas and stars. Overall, the morphology and kinematics of this gas show that WLM is undergoing ram-pressure stripping, despite being 930 and 830 kpc from the Milky Way and M31, respectively. This finding indicates the presence of an intergalactic, gaseous reservoir far from large galaxies whose evolutionary role in galaxies, both large and small, may not be fully appreciated.

Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) was discovered in 1909 by Max Wolf and then confirmed by Knut Lundmark and Philibert Melotte. It is a nearby example of a field dwarf galaxy and is located near the edge of the Local Group. This dwarf is among the smallest galaxies with a rotational field established in Spitzer Photometry and Accurate Rotation Curves SPARC; Lelli2016 . Given its isolated nature and highly inclined orientation, the dark matter mass of this dwarf is considered to be robustly determined and shows that WLM's dark matter may exceed its baryonic content by a factor close to 90 Read2017 .

sources.summary: The paper derives WLM’s JWST-based star formation history and age-metallicity relation, finding agreement with HST results and an early star-forming phase followed by a post-reionization pause.

quotes.quote: The target is the Local Group star-forming galaxy WLM at 970 kpc. The depth of the color-magnitude diagram (CMD) reaches below the oldest main sequence turn-off with a SNR =10 at M _ F090W =+4.6 mag; this is the deepest CMD for any galaxy that is not a satellite of the Milky Way. We use Hubble Space Telescope (HST) optical imaging that overlaps with the NIRCam observations to directly evaluate the SFHs derived based on data from the two great observatories.

The target selected for the JWST Near Infrared Camera (NIRCam) based SFH is the well-studied galaxy Wolf–Lundmark–Melotte (WLM), a gas-rich, low-mass \ =4.3 10^7 ; McConnachie2012 system located near the outskirts of the Local Group at a distance of =968^ +41 _ 40 kpc; Albers2019 . An overview of WLM's properties is provided in Table  tab:properties . WLM was chosen primarily because of its proximity, isolation and the rich suite of existing data sets.

From the JWST data, WLM shows an early onset to star formation, followed by an extended pause post-reionization before star formation re-ignites, which is qualitatively similar to what has been observed in the isolated galaxies Leo A and Aquarius. Quantitatively, 15 of the stellar mass formed in the first Gyr, while only 10 formed over the next 5 Gyr; the stellar mass then rapidly doubled in 2.5 Gyr, followed by constant star formation over the last 5 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper treats WLM as an isolated, low-metallicity Local Group dwarf irregular and uses ALMA, JWST, and GALEX data to compare CO cores, NIR source clumps, and star-formation timescales.

quotes.quote: Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) is a Local Group dwarf irregular (dIrr) galaxy with a metallicity 13 of solar. At 1 Mpc, the relative isolation of WLM provides a unique opportunity to investigate the internal mechanisms of star formation at low metallicities. The earliest stages of star formation in larger spirals occur in embedded clusters within molecular clouds, but dIrrs lack the dust, heavy metals, and organized structure of spirals believed necessary to collapse the molecular clouds into stars. Despite actively forming stars, the early stages of star formation in dIrrs is not well understood. We examine the relationship between early star formation and molecular clouds at low metallicities.

We utilize ALMA-detected CO cores, JWST near-infrared (NIR) images (F090W, F150W, F250M, and F430M), and GALEX far-ultraviolet (FUV) images of WLM to trace molecular clouds, early star formation, and longer star formation timescales respectively. We compare clumps of NIR-bright sources (referred to as objects) categorized into three types based on their proximity to FUV sources and CO cores. We find objects, independent of their location, have similar colors and magnitudes and no discernible difference in temperature.

sources.summary: The paper reports failed CO detections in the low-metallicity dwarf irregular WLM, gives CO intensity limits, and relates WLM's metallicity, H I content, and star formation to a high CO-to-H2 conversion factor.

quotes.quote: We present ^ 12 CO J = 1 0 and J = 2 1 observations of the low metallicity (12 + log(O/H) = 7.74) Local Group dwarf irregular galaxy WLM made with the 15 m SEST and 14 m FCRAO telescopes. Despite the presence a number of HII regions, we find no CO emission. We obtain low upper limits on the integrated intensity (I _ CO 0.18 K for CO (1 0)). The non-detection is consistent with the result of Taylor, Kobulnicky Skillman ( ), that dwarf galaxies below a metallicity of 7.9 are not detected in CO emission. WLM shows that this trend continues for low metallicity galaxies even as their metallicities approach 7.9.

The WLM galaxy is a dwarf irregular that sits on the edge of the Local Group. Its distance is 0.9 Mpc (Minniti Zulstra ), close enough for detailed studies of the stellar populations both from the ground (Minniti Zulstra ) and from HST (Dolphin ). As is typical for dwarf galaxies, the metallicity of WLM is low, 12 + log(O/H) = 7.74 (Skillman, Terlevich Melnick ), while there is a comparatively large amount of atomic hydrogen (M _ HI = 4.7 10 ^ 7 M _ ; Huchtmeier, Seiradakis Materne ).

sources.summary: The paper presents Ca II triplet spectroscopy of WLM, finding it is an isolated gas-rich dwarf irregular with metallicity gradients and kinematically distinct young central and older halo populations.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the first spectroscopic analysis of the calcium II triplet (CaT) feature in a sample of RGB stars in the dIrr galaxy WLM. WLM is a typical low-luminosity, high gas fraction late-type dwarf irregular galaxy. A summary of its fundamental parameters is listed in Table 1. The nearest neighbour to WLM is the Cetus dSph, which lies 200 kpc away Whiting99 .

WLM's distance to the Milky Way is estimated at 932 33 kpc Alan05 , and its separation from M31 is 820 kpc, therefore this is one of the most isolated galaxies in the Local Group. WLM has a heliocentric velocity of -130 km s ^ 1 Jackson04 and a modest velocity with respect to the Local Group barycentre, 29 km s ^ 1 , implying that it may have recently passed apocentre and is turning around.

Taken together, the kinematics, metallicities, and ages in our sample suggest a young metal-rich, and kinematically cold stellar population in the central gas-rich regions of WLM, surrounded by a separate dynamically hot halo of older, metal poor stars.

sources.summary: The paper measures globular-cluster abundances in WLM and finds its sampled cluster old, metal-poor, alpha-enhanced, and indicative of rapid, efficient early star formation.

quotes.quote: We present detailed chemical abundances of Fe, Ca and Ba for 17 globular clusters (GCs) in 5 Local Group dwarf galaxies: NGC 205, NGC 6822, WLM, the SMC and LMC. These abundances are part of a larger sample of over 20 individual elements measured in GCs in these galaxies using a new analysis method for high resolution, integrated light spectra. Our analysis also provides age and stellar population constraints. The existence of GCs in dwarf galaxies with a range of ages implies that there were episodes of rapid star formation throughout the history of these galaxies; the abundance ratios of these clusters suggest that the duration of these burst varied considerably from galaxy to galaxy. We find evolution of Fe, Ca, and Ba with age in the LMC, SMC, and NGC 6822 that is consistent with extended, lower-efficiency SF between bursts, with an increasing contribution of low-metallicity AGB ejecta at late times.

Our sample of GCs in NGC 205 and WLM are predominantly old and metal-poor with high [ Ca/Fe ] ratios, implying that the early history of these galaxies was marked by consistently high SF rates. Stars of all ages record the chemical enrichment of a galaxy throughout its history. To unravel the complete formation history of a galaxy, one must target both old and young stellar populations. While very young stars are conveniently luminous, and detailed abundances for a handful of stars have been obtained out to distances of 900 kpc , older stars are far too faint for detailed abundance analysis much beyond 100 kpc. To enable measurements in more distant galaxies, we have developed a new method of abundance analysis that allows us to analyze high resolution spectra of the integrated light (IL) of globular clusters (GCs), both old and young. The method has been developed using a ``training set'' of MW and LMC GCs and has been demonstrated to provide abundances as accurate as obtained from individual stars --- 0.1 dex or better for [ Fe/H ] and [ X/Fe ] . Using this method, we can target GC systems in galaxies at distances up to 4 Mpc using 8-m class telescopes to probe their formation histories with the same fidelity that stellar abundances have provided in the Milky Way. In these proceedings, we present partial results for an ongoing survey of detailed chemical abundances of GCs in Local Group dwarf galaxies. Data were obtained for 17 GCs in NGC 205, NGC 6822, WLM, and the Magellanic Clouds with the HIRES- spectrograph on the Keck I telescope, the MIKE spectrograph on the Magellan Clay telescope, and the echelle spectrograph on the du Pont telescope. Our results to date show that these samples of GCs have ages and abundances consistent with the field stars in their respective galaxies (where such abundances are available), demonstrating that these low mass galaxies had episodes of rapid star formation where massive clusters were able to form out of the same gas reservoir as field stars. Results for [ Fe/H ] , [ Ca/Fe ] , and [ Ba/Fe ] for the 17 GCs are listed in Table 1. In Figure 1, we plot these along with abundances taken from the literature for individual stars. In the LMC, our cluster sample has [ Ca/Fe ] values that decrease with both [ Fe/H ] and age. This is consistent with available data for individual stars in the LMC , although few comparisons are available for the SMC. Note that [ Ca/Fe ] is representative of the true [ /Fe ] in the GC integrated light , whereas the commonly-used low-resolution indexes that depend on [ Mg/Fe ] are strongly affected by self-enrichment within the GC stars and therefore not representative of the gas-abundance at the time of formation (see ). The old, metal-poor GC in NGC 6822 has solar [ Ca/Fe ] . The 3 GCs sampled in NGC 205 and the sole GC in WLM are reasonably metal-poor ( [ Fe/H ] <-1 ), and are consistently enhanced in elements ( [ Ca/Fe ] >+0.2 ).

High [ Ba/Fe ] in young GCs and the increasing [ Ba/Fe ] with decreasing age in the LMC is consistent with increasingly significant contributions from low-metallicity AGB-stars at late times in these galaxies (see similar results found in ). While the samples in each galaxy are small, these abundances suggest that the SF histories of Local Group dwarf galaxies are diverse; SF was extended and occurred predominantly at low-efficiency in the LMC, SMC and NGC 6822 in between short bursts of more rapid SF that resulted in some massive, surviving GCs. In NGC 205 and WLM, a larger fraction of the SF was rapid and efficient. We present the first detailed abundances for both old and young GCs in NGC 205, NGC 6822, WLM, the LMC and the SMC using a new analysis method.

sources.summary: WLM is a low-metallicity dwarf target whose maps show a broad north-south emission column and offset line peaks near the northern edge of the footprint.

quotes.quote: We present far-infrared spectral line observations of five galaxies from the \ sample: DDO 69, DDO 70, DDO 75, DDO 155, and WLM. While most studies of dwarfs focus on bright systems or starbursts due to observational constraints, our data extend the observed parameter space into the regime of low surface brightness dwarf galaxies with low metallicities and moderate star formation rates. Our targets were observed with \ at the \ 158 m, \ 63 m, \ 88 m, and \ 122 m emission lines using the PACS Spectrometer. These high-resolution maps allow us for the first time to study the far-infrared properties of these systems on the scales of larger star-forming complexes.

WLM shows a large, wide N-S column of \ spanning the entire map. The \ peak lies just to the NW of the \ and the \ just to the NE, near the north edge of the footprint. The \ detection mainly comes from a region west of the center of the bulk of the , but has a low absolute flux.

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as an isolated dwarf irregular with smooth HI structure and little non-circular gas motion; its rotation curve is well fit with CDM-consistent halo parameters.

quotes.quote: WLM: WLM was first discovered by 1909AN....183..187W and later rediscovered by Lundmark and 1926MNRAS..86..636M - hence the name WLM. It lies some D 1 Mpc from both the Milky Way and Andromeda 2011AJ....141..194G and so is remarkably isolated. It has excellent HI data, photometry and stellar kinematics e.g. 2012ApJ...750...33L,2012AJ....143...47Z,2015AJ....149..180O . Its HI distribution is smooth, apart from the presence of a small HI hole of size 0.46 kpc ( 2007AJ....133.2242K , and see Figure fig:real_rotcurve second row, leftmost panel).

Similarly to NGC 6822, our model gives an excellent match to WLM's rotation curve over its full range from 0-3 kpc. We obtain good constraints on M_ 200 and -- like NGC 6822 -- a concentration consistent with cosmological expectations. As with NGC 6822, it is important that we fit with our \ profile rather than the NFW profile, since the latter leads to rotation curves that rise substantially more steeply than the data (compare the magenta and black lines in the rotation curve data panel).

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA observations of CO clouds in the isolated dwarf irregular WLM, emphasizing its low metallicity, efficient star formation, and tiny CO clouds embedded in larger envelopes.

quotes.quote: Here we report interferometric observations of CO clouds in the local group dwarf irregular galaxy Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) , which has a metallicity that is 13 of the solar value and 50 lower than the previous CO detection threshold. The clouds are tiny compared to the surrounding atomic and H _2 envelopes, but they have typical densities and column densities for CO clouds in the Milky Way.

WLM is an isolated dwarf galaxy at a distance of 985 33 kpc . Like other dwarfs, the relative abundance of supernova-processed elements (``metals'') like Carbon and Oxygen is low , 12+ (O/H)=7.8 , compared to 8.66 for the Milky Way . Low C and O abundances, along with the correspondingly low abundances of other processed elements and dust, make the CO molecule rare compared to H _2 , and this calls into question the standard model of star formation in CO-rich clouds . In fact, the star formation rate compared to the existing stellar mass is actually high in WLM: 0.006 M_ yr ^ 1 of new stars for a total stellar mass of 1.6 10^7 M_ is 12 times higher than in the Milky Way, where the star formation rate is 1.9 0.4 M_ yr ^ 1 and the stellar mass is 6.4 0.6 10^ 10 M_ . Thus WLM forms stars efficiently even with a relatively low abundance of CO.

sources.summary: The paper maps and models WLM, an isolated Local Group dwarf irregular, finding an extended H i disc, better non-circular-motion model fits, and dark-matter-dominated mass distribution.

quotes.quote: We present observations and models of the kinematics and the distribution of the neutral hydrogen (H i ) in the isolated dwarf irregular galaxy, Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM). We observed WLM with the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) and as part of the MeerKAT Early Science Programme, where 16 dishes were available. The H i disc of WLM extends out to a major axis diameter of 30 (8.5 kpc), and a minor axis diameter of 20 (5.6 kpc) as measured by the GBT. We use the MeerKAT data to model WLM using the TiRiFiC software suite, allowing us to fit different tilted-ring models and select the one that best matches the observation.

We model the mass distribution of WLM with a pseudo-isothermal (ISO) and a Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) dark matter halo models. The NFW and the ISO models fit the derived rotation curves within the formal errors, but with the ISO model giving better reduced chi-square values. The mass distribution in WLM is dominated by dark matter at all radii.

sources.summary: The paper studies WLM with UVIT, finding 180 young FUV clumps, small 7-30 pc sizes, preferential southwest-northeast orientation, and especially blue compact clumps in the southern half.

quotes.quote: We present an ultra-violet study of two nearby dwarf irregular galaxies WLM and IC 2574, using the Far-UV and Near-UV data from the Ultra-Violet Imaging Telescope (UVIT). We used the F148W band Far-UV images and identified 180 and 782 young star-forming clumps in WLM and IC 2574, respectively. The identified clumps have sizes between 7 - 30 pc in WLM and 26 - 150 pc in IC 2574.

We noticed more prominent hierarchical splitting in the structure of star-forming regions at different flux levels in IC 2574 than WLM. We found that the majority of the clumps have elongated shapes in the sky plane with ellipticity ( ) greater than 0.6 in both the galaxies. The major axis of the identified clumps is found to show no specific trend of orientation in IC 2574, whereas in WLM the majority are aligned along south-west to north-east direction.

We estimated (F148W N242W) colour for the clumps identified in WLM and noticed that the younger ones (with (F148W N242W) <-0.5 ) are smaller in size ( <10 pc) and are located mostly in the southern half of the galaxy between galactocentric radii 0.4 - 0.8 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper finds WLM has a highly symmetric carbon-star luminosity function, classifies it as SMC-like, and derives a CS distance modulus of 25.03 mag.

quotes.quote: WLM NGC 3109 WLM and NGC 3109 show highly symmetric CS luminosity functions with skew values of s_ binned =-0.10 and s_ MLE =0.01 for WLM and s_ binned =-0.03 and s_ MLE =-0.01 for NGC 3109. All these values are greater than the limit specified in equation eq:dm , therefore, both galaxies are classified as "SMC-like" galaxies. Using the most suitable calibrator for these two galaxies we obtain distance moduli of _0 = 25.03 0.04 (statistical) for WLM and _0 = 25.59 0.03 (statistical) for NGC 3109. Two other studies using CS report a distance modulus for NGC 3109 and three for WLM.

obtained a distance modulus of _0 = 24.954 0.08 (stat) for WLM and _0 = 25.517 0.046 (stat) for NGC 3109, while reports _0 = 24.97 0.05 (stat) and _0 = 25.56 0.05 (stat) for WLM and NGC 3109 respectively. In the case of they find a distance modulus to WLM of _0 = 24.97 0.02 (stat). The results reported in the current study and the papers cited agree within the error bars.

sources.summary: The paper presents WLM as a metal-poor Local Group dwarf irregular with an old-to-recent star-formation history and one detected candidate planetary nebula.

quotes.quote: The Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte ( ) galaxy was discovered by wolf23 and independently by lundmark and by melotte . \ (called also DDO 221, vdb66 ) is a Local Group metal-poor dwarf irregular (12 + log(O/H) = 7.7; skillman ), in a part of the Local Group relatively free of galaxies, at a distance of 925 kpc (vdB00). dolphin00 studied with the Hubble Space Telescope the SFH in a portion of WLM. It appears to have begun no more than 12 Gyr ago, forming more than half of its stellar population by 9 Gyr ago. The SFR has subsequently gradually decreased until a recent increase in activity starting between 1 and 2.5 Gyr ago, but is still continuing to the present time.

In this paper we presented the search for PNe in three dwarf irregular galaxies belonging to the Local Group, or close to it: IC 1613, WLM, and 8. We discovered two new candidate PNe in IC 1613, one in WLM, and none in 8.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WLM as a Local Group dwarf irregular rich in carbon stars, derives its C/M ratio, distance modulus, gas-to-dust ratio, and J-band disk scale length.

quotes.quote: Near-IR view of WLM: Carbon stars and C/M ratio We identify the rich Carbon star population of the Magellanic-type dwarf irregular galaxy WLM (Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte) and study its photometric properties from deep near-IR observations. The galaxy exhibits also a clear presence of Oxygen rich population. We derive a Carbon to M-star ratio of C/M =0.56 0.12, relatively high in comparison with many galaxies.

The spatial distribution of the AGB stars in WLM hints at the presence of two stellar complexes with a size of a few hundred parsecs. Using the H i map of WLM and the derived gas-to-dust ratio for this galaxy N(H i )/E( B V )=60 10 [ 10 ^ 21 at. cm ^ 2 mag ^ 1 ] we re-determined the distance modulus of WLM from the IR photometry of four known Cepheids, obtaining (m M) _0 =24.84 0.14 mag. In addition, we determine the scale length of 0.75 0.14 kpc of WLM disk in J band.

Galaxies: individual: WLM, distance, gas-to-dust ratio, scale length, C/M ratio -- Galaxies: Local Group, irregular -- Stars: AGB, Carbon WLM (Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte; also DDO 221; Wolf , Melotte ) is a dwarf irregular Local Group member.

sources.summary: The paper finds that WLM Cepheids match SMC templates closely, have nearly SMC-like scatter, and yield an adopted distance modulus of 24.95 +/- 0.03.

quotes.quote: Sandage 2 Department of Physics and Astronomy, Univ. of Basel Klingelbergstrasse 82, 4056 Basel, Switzerland g-a.tammann@unibas.ch The Observatories of the Carnegie Institution of Washington, 813 Santa Barbara Street, Pasadena, CA 91101, USA. -- Deceased on November 13, 2010 Received 21 December 2010 / Accepted 22 April 2011 The metal-poor, fundamental-mode (P0) and first-overtone (P1) Cepheids in the dwarf galaxies IC 1613, WLM, Pegasus, Sextans A, Sextans B, and Leo A are compared with the about equally metal-poor Cepheids of the Small Magellanic Cloud (SMC). The P-C and P-L relations of the Cepheids by Pietrzynski:etal:07 , corrected for foreground reddening of E(V I)=0.047 Schlegel:etal:98 , are exhibited in Fig. fig:05 a to where the corresponding relations of SMC are drawn as full lines. With this interpretation, the match of WLM with SMC becomes impressively good. The scatter of the points about the SMC templets is almost the same as that of the SMC Cepheids.

The 45 P0 magnitudes in V and I yield, if compared with the absolute magnitudes from the P-L relation of SMC, individual distances as a function of P (Fig. fig:05 e f). A number-weighted mean of the P0 distances and the somewhat larger P1 distances gives (m-M)^ 0 =24.95 0.03 for WLM, which we adopt.

sources.summary: GBT maps detect WLM over the expected velocity range, find no extended gas skirt along the mapped directions, and measure a total gas mass affected by the incomplete strip map.

quotes.quote: WLM We detected \ emission from WLM between -179 \ to -71 . Similarly, Kepley \ (2007) detected emission from -170 \ to -72 and Huchtmeier \ (1981) from -179 \ to -64 . Our integrated \ map of WLM is shown in Figure fig-wlmm0 and contours are superposed on a V band image in Figure fig-wlmhionv .
However, no extended skirt of \ is seen along the two map directions.

We calculate a total \ mass of 1.1 10^8 M . This is 1.6 more than the mass of 7.0 10^7 M \ reported by Kepley \ (2007), 1.9 the mass of 5.9 10^7 M \ detected by Huchtmeier \ (1981), 3 times higher than the mass of Jackson \ (2004), and 60 higher than Barnes De Blok's (2004) mass ---all corrected to our choice of distance for WLM.

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM as a metal-poor dwarf irregular comparison, giving integrated H I, distance, SFR measurements and noting compact regions dominate its star formation.

quotes.quote: Further included is a comparison with integrated values for (4) the Milky Way (Kennicutt Evans 2012), where _ H_2 and _ HI (see Kalberla Kerp 2009 for details), and _ SFR (see Chomiuk Povich 2011 for details) are sampled within r_ opt 13.5 kpc, (5) the metal-poor dIrr WLM, where the total HI mass (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8) and distance ( D 0.95 Mpc) are from Kepley et al. (2007), the source size from the NED, and the integrated H derived SFR (SFR 0.002 M _ yr ^ 1 ) from Hunter, Elmegreen Ludka (2010), and (6) several dwarf galaxies from the DGS (Cormier et al. 2014) with a range in metallicities (7.7 12+log(O/H) 8.4),

Dicussion on WLM WLM has an integrated H derived SFR (SFR 0.002 M _ yr ^ 1 ; Hunter, Elmegreen Ludka 2010) two orders of magnitude above the H /far-ultraviolet SFR of the individual Northwestern (cloud A) and Southeastern (cloud B) star-forming regions, respectively (SFR _ NW 3.9 -- 4.8 10 ^ 5 and SFR _ SE 1.7 -- 12.6 10 ^ 5 M _ yr ^ 1 ; Elmegreen et al. 2013). Because the star formation in WLM is likely dominated by a few compact, higher efficient star-forming regions, the integrated SFR surface density (dark green triangle; Fig. 1b), is about an order of magnitude below that of the individual star-forming regions (dark green triangles; Fig. 1a).

Similarly, the total HI mass for WLM (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8; Hunter, Elmegreen Ludka 2010) is about two orders of magnitude above the estimated HI mass for the Northwestern component (Elmegreen et al. 2013). By averaging over the whole galaxy, although large-scale HI gas and HI gas from diffuse inter-star-forming regions are included, a significant part of the HI gas may be clumpy and concentrated in the star-forming regions. Hence, lower (by approximately an order of magnitude) integrated _ HI values (red open square and circle, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1b) are obtained relative to the individual star-forming region values (red open squares and circles, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1a).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes WLM as an isolated dwarf irregular with measured coordinates and quiescent central star formation, then reports no H.E.S.S. excess and derives dark-matter annihilation constraints.

quotes.quote: WLM, also known by the names DDO 221, UGCA 444, and LEDA 143, was discovered by Max Wolf in 1909, and identified as a dwarf galaxy by Knut Lundmark and Philibert Jacques Melotte in 1926. This faint galaxy of absolute magnitude -14.7 is located in the constellation of Cetus, 985 33 kpc from the Milky Way at coordinates RA = 00^ h 01^ m 58^ s and Dec = 15 27'39'' (J2000) , corresponding to l=75.86 and b=-73.62 in Galactic coordinates. The optical size of WLM spans about 2.5 kpc (or 0.15 ) at its greatest extent. Its nearest neighbor within the Local Group, the Cetus dwarf spheroidal galaxy, is about 175 kpc away and, therefore, WLM is believed to have developed independently from the influence of other systems . WLM hosts a star-forming region at its center where the star formation rate is about 10^ 3 M_ yr ^ 1 , a low value suggesting the dwarf galaxy is in quiescent phase at the present time.

As we do not observe any significant excess in the direction of WLM, we interpret the result in terms of constraints on the velocity-weighted cross section for dark matter pair annihilation v as a function of the dark matter particle mass for various continuum channels as well as the prompt emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM as one of six isolated Local Group dwarfs with extended stellar-halo observations, fitting ghostly-halo models to infer pre-reionization star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: We compare the model to available observations of stellar haloes in six isolated dwarf galaxies in the Local Group (Leo T, Leo A, IC 10, WLM, IC 1613, NGC 6822) to infer the star formation efficiency in dwarf galaxies at the epoch of reionization. We find an efficiency of star formation in dark matter haloes with masses 10^6-10^8 M _ at z 7 in rough agreement with independent methods using data on the luminosity function of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies but systematically higher by a factor of 3-5. The systematic uncertainty of our results is still large, mainly because available observations of stellar halo profiles do not extend over a sufficiently large distance from the center of the host dwarf galaxy. Additional observations, easily within reach of current telescopes, can significantly improve the accuracy of this method and can also be used to constrain the present day dark matter masses of dwarf galaxies in the Local Group.

KangR:2019 have compiled a list of six dwarf galaxies (Leo T, Leo A, WLM, IC 1613, IC 10, and NGC 6822) located in the Local Group, but outside the virial radii of the Milky Way and Andromeda, showing robust or tentative evidence of the existence of an extended stellar halo. In the collected observational data sets, radial density profiles of stars are provided out to several half-light radii from the central galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper applies GCVD to WLM using its single known globular cluster, correcting the velocity dispersion and adopting a conservative uncertainty.

quotes.quote: WLM The WLM dwarf galaxy is presently only known to host one GC. Its velocity dispersion was taken from and V band magnitude ( V=16.06 ) from . We adopted an extinction of A_V=0.104 Schlafly2011 .

The quoted by is neither a central nor global value but is somewhere between the two, modified by aperture effects. We estimate a correction of order 10 downwards based on the corrections listed in and our corrections for the Milky Way data. do not quote an uncertainty on their measurement, and we adopt a conservative uncertainty of 2 km s ^ 1 . This gives _ gl =8.6 2.0 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The survey found no red supergiants in WLM, but identified a low-metallicity sgB[e] source, no blue or yellow supergiants or LBV candidates, and carbon stars.

quotes.quote: WLM The WLM sample mostly consisted of stars without a priority or a low priority. No RSGs were found in this galaxy, despite recent studies on the RSG content of WLM (i.e. Britavskiy2015,GonzalezTora2021 , as these targets were not in our priority system). We observed one sgB [ e ] (WLM-95), which is the first such source identified in a low-Z galaxy ( 0.14 ) presented by source WLM-1 Maravelias2023 .
No BSGs, YSGs or LBV candidates were identified in WLM.
Other objects of interest Carbon stars were identified only in nearby galaxies (seven in WLM WLM-1120 corresponds to star 10 in Britavskiy2015 . , one in Sextans A, two in NGC 300).

sources.summary: The paper targets WLM with JWST/NIRCam, describing it as an edge-on dwarf irregular with ongoing recent star formation, low metallicity, and predominantly carbon-rich dusty evolved stars.

quotes.quote: To investigate JWST's ability to photometrically classify evolved stars, we target the nearby dwarf irregular galaxy Wolf--Lundmark--Melotte (WLM) as part of the JWST Stellar Populations Early Release Science (ERS) program Boyer+2022, Warfield+2023, Weisz+2023, McQuinn+2024 . wolf1909 discovered the galaxy on photographic plates, and along with K. Lundmark melotte1926 re-discovered the galaxy and noted its similar appearance to NGC 6822, the nearest dwarf irregular galaxy. WLM is seen nearly edge-on to its rotating disk of gas and stars leaman12 .

Like most dwarf irregular galaxies, WLM has ongoing star formation 0.008 M_ yr ^ 1 mondal18 . It also has a star formation history weighted toward recent ages, with the majority of star formation occurring in the last 5 Gyr weisz14,Albers+2019,McQuinn+2024 . WLM's stellar metallicity distribution is typical for dwarf galaxies of its mass, with a mean iron abundance of Fe/H = -1.27 and a shallow radial metallicity gradient Leaman+2009,leaman13 .

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM as a star-forming dwarf-galaxy ERS target, highlighting its faint point-source sample, high crowding, and SNR behavior in ETC comparisons.

quotes.quote: Specifically, we examine a wide range of effects included in the ETC (photometric aperture properties, flat fields, saturation, etc.) and compare them to the multi-band photometric data and artificial star tests (ASTs) from our application of the DOLPHOT JWST/NIRCam module to the three ERS targets: the globular cluster M92, the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy , and the star-forming dwarf galaxy WLM.

By design, each of the three targets of the survey (M92, WLM and ) are suitable for different aspects of this analysis. For instance, M92 has many high-SNR sources close to the saturation limit, WLM has a large sample of faint low-SNR sources, and \ has a very low stellar density that is suitable to benchmark photometric performance in a very uncrowded field.

For instance, for a point source in WLM with F090W =28 , DOLPHOT reports a mean SNR of 36.6 at the same magnitude. With the same WLM observing set up, the ETC predicts a SNR of 13.0 using aperture photometry and the default ETC aperture of 0.1 . Using a 0.03 aperture results in SNR of 28.20.

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as a nearly extinction-free Local Group dwarf with adopted distance and metallicity, comparing recent and earlier C/M AGB-star measurements.

quotes.quote: Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) dwarf galaxy is located on the periphery of the Local Group and it is seen at a high Galactic latitude ( = 76^ , b = -74^ ), thus being essentially extinction free. From the investigation of Dolphin (2000), we adopt (m-M)_0 = 24.90 for its distance and a metallicity for its intermediate-age population of [ Fe/H ] = --1.4. WLM has been the target of a recent NIR study by Valcheva et al. (2007) who identified numerous C and M AGB stars. They determined a C/M ratio for WLM that is quite different from the one calculated by Battinelli Demers (2004) from the (CN - TiO) criterion. As we shall see the difference comes mostly from the different sets of M and C stars.

sources.summary: The paper determines WLM distance and young-population metallicity from blue supergiant spectroscopy, describing it as an isolated faint dwarf irregular with young disk and old halo populations.

quotes.quote: The quantitative analysis of low resolution spectra of A and B supergiants is used to determine a distance modulus of 24.99 0.10 mag (995 46 Kpc) to the Local Group galaxy WLM. The analysis yields stellar effective temperatures and gravities, which provide a distance through the Flux weighted Gravity--Luminosity Relationship ( fglr ). Our distance is 0.07 mag larger than the most recent results based on Cepheids and the tip of the RGB.

WLM is one of the faintest dwarf irregular galaxies ( M_ B 14 ), located in an isolated part of the Local Group. Detailed photometric studies have shown that it consists of a young population concentrated in a disk and an old extended metal poor halo ferraro1989,minniti1997,dolphin00,rejkuba2000,mcconnachie2005 .

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM as a nearby low-metallicity dwarf target, selecting one bright O-type star and reporting its oxygen abundance and unexpectedly high wind strength.

quotes.quote: We present a spectroscopic analysis of VLT/X-Shooter observations of six O-type stars in the low-metallicity ( Z 1/7 Z_ ) galaxies IC 1613, WLM and NGC 3109. The stellar and wind parameters of these sources allow us, for the first time, to probe the mass-loss versus metallicity dependence of stellar winds below that of the Small Magellanic Cloud (at Z 1/5 Z_ ) by means of a modified wind momentum versus luminosity diagram. The wind strengths that we obtain for the objects in WLM and NGC 3109 are unexpectedly high and do not agree with theoretical predictions. The objects in IC 1613 tend towards a higher than expected mass-loss rate, but remain consistent with predictions within their error bars.

The resulting abundances are given in Table tab:basic , and although abundances derived with the strong line method can have uncertainties up to 0.8 dex see e.g. bres09 , our measures agree well with the literature values of 12 + log(O/H) = 7.90 0.08 IC 1613; bres07 , 7.80 0.07 WLM; urba08 and 7.76 0.07 NGC 3109; evan07 derived from B- and A-type supergiants.

In order to observationally constrain stellar and wind parameters at sub-SMC metallicities, we have used the X-Shooter spectrograph mounted on ESO's Very Large Telescope (VLT) UT2 Vernet et al. 2011, in press dodo06 to secure intermediate-resolution spectra of some of the most massive stars in three nearby dwarf galaxies, IC 1613, WLM and NCG 3109, each having a metallicity of Z 1/7 \ Z_ .

sources.summary: The paper measures WLM's star-formation history from deep HST color-magnitude diagrams, finding late-rising star formation and a strong radial age gradient.

quotes.quote: We present the star formation history (SFH) of the isolated ( D 970  kpc) Local Group dwarf galaxy WLM measured from color-magnitude diagrams constructed from deep Hubble Space Telescope imaging. Our observations include a central ( 0.5 r_h ) and outer field ( 0.7 r_h ) that reach below the oldest main sequence turnoff. WLM has no early dominant episode of star formation: 20 of its stellar mass formed by 12.5 Gyr ago ( z 5 ). It also has an SFR that rises to the present with 50 of the stellar mass within the most recent 5 Gyr ( z<0.7 ). There is evidence of a strong age gradient: the mean age of the outer field is 5 Gyr older than the inner field despite being only 0.4 kpc apart.

sources.summary: The paper derives a JAGB distance for WLM from near-infrared JK data and 77 JAGB stars, then compares it with normalized TRGB distances.

quotes.quote: These values average to < _o (TRGB)> = 25.57 0.05 mag. Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte (WLM) JAGB stars: Near-infrared ( JK ) data for 555 stars in the distant Local Group galaxy WLM were obtained by Valcheva et al. (2007) using ESO's New Technology Telescope ( NTT ). The J versus (J-K) CMD is shown in Figure fig:CMDmontage . Only JK data were available for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as hosting one luminous old metal-poor globular cluster whose abundance is below the old field population and agrees with earlier calcium and barium measurements.

quotes.quote: WLM galaxy hosts a single known, but quite luminous ( M_V=-9 ), gc Humason1956,Sandage1985,Larsen2014 . A cmd from the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) shows well-defined giant- and horizontal branches, and confirms the object as an old, metal-poor gc Hodge1999 . Our measurement of the iron abundance, [ Fe/H ] =-1.85 0.03 , is about 0.11 dex higher than that listed in Larsen2014 , which is mainly due to the revised microturbulence prescription and the inclusion of nlte corrections. In any case, the gc in WLM has a metallicity well below that of the general old field population, which has an average [ Fe/H ] =-1.3 Leaman2013 .

sources.summary: The paper finds WLM proper-motion uncertainties are larger than previous work because of its angular size and HST coverage, while its star formation has been steady since early times.

quotes.quote: For three dwarfs (NGC 6822, Peg dIrr and WLM) we obtain larger uncertainties than those obtained by . This is likely because the angular size of the dwarfs on the sky is such that large parts of the dwarfs are outside the HST FOV, as well as the relatively short time baseline in the case of Peg dIrr and WLM. As a result, more stars can be found with the all-sky coverage of Gaia .

This is the case for our sample galaxies IC 1613, Leo I, Leo II, Leo A, Leo T, Phoenix, and WLM (see references provided in subsections below). IC 1613, WLM) star formation has been relatively constant, with the galaxies assembling their stellar mass at a steady rate since early times Albers2019 .

sources.summary: The paper treats WLM as a dwarf irregular and notes HST-based central and outer-field SFHs showing a strong age gradient, with higher recent average SFR than prior work.

quotes.quote: Among the remaining nine galaxies, NGC 6822, IC 1613, WLM, Leo A, Sextans A, and Sextans B, PegasusDwarf are dwarf irregulars, NGC 147 and NGC 185 belong to dwarf ellipticals.

For instance, 2019MNRAS.490.5538A analyzed the SFHs of the central and outer fields of WLM using HST ACS and UVIS observations. They defined the central field as being within 1 kpc of WLM's center and the outer field as 1.4 kpc from the center. Their results indicate a strong age gradient in WLM.

Firstly, it can be seen that the average SFRs of NGC 6822, IC 1613, WLM, NGC 147, and NGC 185 we obtained is greater than the results obtained by 2014ApJ...789..147W , because recent star formation activities have contributed to the overall SFRs of the galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as an isolated gas-rich dwarf irregular and assigns one absorber group along PHL2525 to WLM's CGM while another is attributed to the Magellanic Stream.

quotes.quote: WLM is a gas-rich dwarf irregular (dIrr) galaxy located on the outskirts of the Local Group at a distance of 0.93 0.03 Mpc McConnachie_dwarf . Like IC1613, WLM is an isolated system whose nearest neighbor (Cetus dwarf spheroidal galaxy) is at a distance of 210 kpc. We find one QSO sight line, PHL2525 (ID 54 in Table tb:qso ), that is located at an impact parameter of 45.2 kpc ( 0.8 R_ 200c Note that we adopt a different virial radius definition from what used in their work. ) from WLM. Along this sight line, there are two main absorber groups: one centers at v_ LSR 150 and another at v_ LSR 210 . Our algorithm shows that the absorbers at 150 are associated with WLM, while the other group of absorbers at v_ LSR 200 are associated with the MS, which is consistent with what found in their study of the WLM's CGM.

sources.summary: The review uses WLM as a low-metallicity Local Group dwarf where ALMA resolved small CO clouds whose virial properties resemble low-end Milky Way GMC correlations.

quotes.quote: WLM ( 12+ O/H =7.8 or 0.13Z_ , D=985 kpc): used ALMA to map CO(1-0) in two 300 pc regions of WLM with 6.2   pc 4.3 pc spatial resolution (HPBW) and 0.5 km s ^ 1 velocity resolution (FWHM). They found 10 small clouds with an average radius of 2.5 pc and an average virial mass of 2.8 10^3 M_ (see Figure fig-wlmse ). The individual clouds satisfied the usual correlations for GMCs in the Milky Way larson81 , namely linewidth versus size and mass versus radius, although they occupied the low-mass and low-radius ends of these correlations. The CO clouds are also approximately virialized, considering that the H _2 density required for virial equilibrium is about equal to the density required for CO excitation (i.e., 10^3 cm ^ 3 ), and this density multiplied by the square of the linewidth gives an internal pressure about equal to the cloud boundary pressure derived from the weight of the overlying gas as observed in dust emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep HST/STIS photometry to identify WLM's horizontal branch, measure its distance, and argue that WLM contains an old stellar population formed at high redshift.

quotes.quote: We have obtained V and I-band photometry for 1886 stars down to I=27 and V=28 in the field of the dwarf irregular galaxy WLM, using deep STIS CL and LP band images taken in the parallel mode with the Hubble Space Telescope. The photometry is used to study the horizontal branch identified in WLM. The horizontal branch, extending blueward from the red giant clump, is an unambiguous signature of an old population. We demonstrate that it is possible to reach the horizontal branch of an old population at a distance of 1 Mpc using STIS, with relatively short exposure times. From the VI color--magnitude diagram we obtain an accurate distance modulus (m-M)_0 = 24.95 0.13 for WLM by using the V-magnitude of the horizontal branch, and by adopting E(V-I) = 0.03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as the first blue-supergiant FGLR distance application, finding very low young-population metallicity, patchy extinction, and a distance modulus consistent with TRGB and Cepheid distances.

quotes.quote: The first distance determination of this type has been carried out by urbaneja08 who studied blue supergiants in WLM, one of the faintest dwarf irregular galaxies in the Local Group. The quantitative spectral analysis of VLT FORS spectra yields an extremely low metallicity of the young stellar population in this galaxy with an average of -0.9 dex below the solar value. The interstellar extinction is again extremely patchy ranging from 0.03 to 0.30 mag in E(B-V) (note that the foreground value given by schlegel98 is 0.037 mag). The individually de-reddened FGLR - in apparent bolometric magnitude - is shown in Fig. fig15 yielding a distance modulus of 24.99 0.10 mag in very good agreement with the TRGB distance rizzi07 and the K-band Cepheid distance gieren08 .

sources.summary: The simulation is compared to WLM, whose hot stellar distribution, inferred halo mass, stellar mass, and bursty star formation history are used as observational constraints.

quotes.quote: The best-studied system to date is WLM. Despite being extremely tidally isolated, WLM does indeed have a hot, oblate spheroidal, stellar distribution (with v/ 1 and h/R_d 0.5 ) that is reasonably approximated by an exponential surface brightness fall-off 2012ApJ...750...33L .
The total mass inferred from this analysis by for WLM, M_ tot 2 10^ 10 M _ is therefore accurate, and again very close to our simulated halo mass.
With stellar feedback, however, we form only M_ * 2 10^7 M _ of stars after 1 Gyr, in much better agreement with the observed stellar mass in WLM.

sources.summary: The paper uses WLM's measured star-formation history and dark-matter profile, finding its shallow inner density similar to Fornax and comparing both galaxies' histories until Fornax quenched.

quotes.quote: Two of the dwarfs, WLM and Aquarius, have star formation histories derived from deep colour magnitude diagrams 2000ApJ...531..804D,2014ApJ...795...54C ; the remainder are known to be still forming stars today 2012AJ....143...47Z .

In Figure fig:killer_plot , we show our results for the radial DM density profiles of dSphs with >500 member velocities, and two dIrrs -- WLM and Aquarius -- that have a well-measured SFH (see sec:data ).

This shallow inner density profile for Fornax is remarkably similar to that for WLM (compare the middle and right panels in the middle row of Figure fig:killer_plot ). This is interesting since WLM and Fornax share similar SFHs (see Figure fig:killer_plot , middle row, left panel) up until 2 Gyrs ago when Fornax's star formation quenched.

sources.summary: The WLM galaxy is described as hosting one luminous globular cluster, with the paper deriving its mass and comparing the halo mass to several literature estimates.

quotes.quote: WLM (DDO221) The WLM galaxy has been known for some time to host a single GC with a luminosity of M_V = --8.74 . This corresponds to a mass of 5.04 10^5 . Virial masses in the literature for WLM include 2017MNRAS.467.2019R log M_ 200 = 9.92, 2017A&A...605A..55A log M_ 200 = 10.07 assuming a 2014MNRAS.437..415D DM profile and 10.39 for a coreNFW profile Read+16 , and 2015AJ....149..180O log M_ 200 = 10.09. Our value of the halo mass is within the range of these values and their quoted uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper models WLM HI surface-density and velocity-dispersion profiles, then notes its VSF-law points are slightly detached and more than 3 from the relation.

quotes.quote: The left panel in Fig. fig:wlm_sdhi_vdhi_sfrd shows the azimuthally averaged radial profile of the atomic gas surface density for WLM from , and the polynomial best-fit model (dot-dashed black curve).
The central panel in Fig. fig:wlm_sdhi_vdhi_sfrd shows, for WLM, the observed velocity dispersion of HI (red diamonds) and the best-fit exponential profile (black solid curve).

From a thorough inspection of Fig. fig:vsf , we can see that a few points belonging to three galaxies, DDO 87, DDO 168 and WLM, seem slightly detached from the VSF law. We note that DDO 168 and DDO 87 are actually compatible within the errors with the best-fit relation. The data points of WLM, instead, appear to be slightly more than 3 from the relation, also in light of their relatively small error-bar.

sources.summary: The paper selects WLM as a dwarf irregular target with robust rotation-curve data, high predicted J factors, and nearly degenerate Burkert and NFW dark-matter profile fits.

quotes.quote: According to the observed RCs, NGC6822, IC10 and WLM are the ones with more available data, thus in these cases the fits are more robust and stable than for the rest of objects in their sample. The mentioned three objects also yield the highest J-factor values independently of the selected DM profile or substructure boost values. Taking these findings in into account, we thus decided to include NGC6822, IC10 and WLM in our sample, whose parameters are gathered in Table tab:dIrr .
First, for NGC6822 and WLM, the two DM profiles are almost degenerate for the rotation curve fits, so that all associated ratios are close to one (for all _ values).

sources.summary: The review describes WLM as a bright Local Group dwarf with radially varying rotational support, declining dispersion plus increasing rotation, age-dependent stellar rotation, and a cored prolate dark halo.

quotes.quote: V/ is not necessarily constant with radius: for instance WLM has V/ = 2.5 in the last measured point, to be compared to the global value of 1.1 ( ; see also the case of NGC 147 ). Some systems at the bright end of the LG DG luminosity function, like NGC 147 Geha2010 and WLM Leaman2012 , exhibit declining profiles accompanied by rotational velocities increasing as a function of radius, while for others the trend with radius appears less clearly defined. In the massive LG DGs WLM and NGC6822, whose stellar component is rotating, the amount of rotational support is found to vary with age Leaman2012 or metallicity Belland2020 .
An exception is the dIrr WLM for which a joint analysis of stellar and gas kinematics has provided evidence that the halo is cored and prolate .

sources.summary: The paper describes WLM as a dwarf irregular with young, intermediate, and old populations and a measured TRGB distance.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: WLM (Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte), located at 0 h  1 m  58.1 s , -15^  27'  39''
