quotes.quote: WLM (DDO 221) WLM (Wolf-Lundmark-Melotte), located at 0 h  1 m  58.1 s , -15^  27'  39'' , is a dwarf irregular galaxy, discovered at the start of the 20^ th century by . In recent decades, several groups have analysed the stellar content of this object ( sandage1985,ferraro1989,minniti1997,rejkuba2000 ). derive a mean metallicity of Fe/H = -1.45 0.2 from the colour of the RGB, and show that young and intermediate age stellar populations are present. The discovery of blue horizontal branch (HB) stars by is an unambiguous indicator of an old stellar population.

sources.summary: The WLM galaxy DDO 221 provides a very nearby deep-CMD test case, with HST/WFPC2 photometry and two fields showing close agreement between maximum-likelihood and edge-detection TRGB measurements.

quotes.quote: The WLM galaxy (DDO 221) was included in the sample to provide a very nearby case with a deep CMD. The HST/WFPC2 observation of DDO 221 were made within HST programs 6898 and 6813, and photometry results along with the results of artificial star experiments were taken from J. Holtzman's HST Local Group stellar photometry archive http://astronomy.nmsu.edu/holtz/archival/html/lg.html holt .

The agreement of the two measurements for two fields in DDO 221 are good. The mean difference between two methods is 0.07 mag, whereas the difference between the two fields measured with the same method is 0.05 for the ML and 0.19 for the ED method.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WLM as a Local Group dwarf whose single globular cluster may contain a high fraction of its metal-poor stars despite little evidence for stripping.

quotes.quote: In some dwarfs, such as Fornax, WLM, and IKN, the fraction of metal-poor stars that belong to GCs can be as high as 20 -25 , an order of magnitude higher than the 1 -2 typical of GCs in halos of larger galaxies.
Within the Local Group, the single GC in the WLM galaxy appears to account for a similar high fraction of the metal-poor stars there ( ).

Neither of them is obviously involved in interactions with any other nearby galaxies, and WLM in particular is located on the outskirts of the Local Group, more than 900 kpc from both the Milky Way and M31.

sources.summary: The paper measures WLM rotation, velocity dispersion, and position angle consistent with Leaman2012, including strong-evidence stellar rotation and a similar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: WLM: We measure: =14 1.6 , =15.6 0.9 , and =163_ 19 ^ 23 . The position angle we prefer agrees well with the value of =173 reported by Leaman2012 . In addition, Leaman2012 measure a velocity dispersion for WLM that is broadly consistent with our value ( 15 ), and they report a stellar rotation and that are also consistent with our measured values ( 15 ; = 1.01_ 0.15 ^ +0.17 with strong evidence).

sources.summary: The paper treats WLM as a plateauing velocity-field case, noting cored Einasto agreement with detailed modeling and a measured velocity consistent with its flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: For three of the six galaxies where the velocity fields plateau and \ is a good representation of the maximum velocity in the galaxy (i.e., NGC 3109, Sextans A, WLM), the - \ values fall somewhat below the plateau of the cored Einasto rotation curves.

Note, however, that the best-fitting value of \ for WLM from the cored Einasto profiles agrees within the uncertainties with \ derived from more detailed modeling of the stellar and gas kinematics in the galaxy Leung2021 .

For the WLM galaxy, our velocity was measured at a notably smaller radius than in Iorio2017 , but the velocity agrees within the uncertainties; this is expected as the \ data reaches the flat part of the rotation curve.

sources.summary: WLM is included as a Local Group outskirts galaxy that is gas-rich, actively forming stars, and likely has substantial rotational support.

quotes.quote: WLM WLM is a galaxy on the outskirts of the Local Group. It is gas-rich and undergoing active star formation Leaman2009 . It also likely has a large rotation component in its dynamics Leaman2009 .

gmw_00000725.name: S1189
gmw_00000725.overview: S1189 is a large wide-angle tail radio galaxy [arXiv:1005.3649, arXiv:0712.1409] associated with an elliptical-looking infrared counterpart [arXiv:0712.1409]. Located at a redshift of 0.22, the galaxy resides within a broad, unrelaxed large-scale structure and a nearby galaxy overdensity [arXiv:1005.3649].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S1189 as the largest ATLAS WAT radio galaxy, associated with a SWIRE source, a nearby galaxy overdensity, and a broad unrelaxed structure.

quotes.quote: We also present follow-up spectroscopic observations of the area surrounding the largest WAT, S1189, which is at a redshift of 0.22. The spectroscopic observations, taken using the AAOmega spectrograph on the AAT, show an overdensity of galaxies at this redshift. The galaxies are spread over an unusually large area of 12 Mpc with a velocity spread of 4500 km s ^ 1 . This large-scale structure includes a highly asymmetric FRI radio galaxy and also appears to host a radio relic. It may represent an unrelaxed system with different sub-structures interacting or merging with one another.
This WAT was first identified as radio source S1189 by Middelberg08 , and is associated with the SWIRE source SWIRE4 J003427.54-430222.5 Lonsdale03 .

sources.summary: S1189 is a large extended radio source associated with an elliptical-looking infrared counterpart, with WAT-like bent jets and no known cluster at its position.

quotes.quote: Source S1189 is a beautifully extended, large radio source. The number of constituent radio components is somewhat arbitrary, but there exists a low-SNR bridge of emission which connects the main part of the source and component C1212, 2^ north, as well as many more low-SNR patches in between. The brightest part of S1189 is centred on SWIRE4 J003427.54-430222.5 (separation 0.75'' ), which we therefore identify as IR counterpart, and which has the morphology of an elliptical galaxy in optical images. Source S1197, 0.60^ from SWIRE4 J003419.55-430151.7, is unlikely to be connected to S1189. S1189 has the shape and extent of Wide-Angle Tail galaxies (WAT, Miley1980 ) such as NGC 1265 ( Owen1978 ).

gmw_00000726.name: NGC4342
gmw_00000726.overview: NGC 4342 is a low-luminosity, edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9805324, arXiv:1311.1530] that hosts a nuclear stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/9805324, arXiv:astro-ph/0009249] and an unusually massive central black hole [arXiv:1203.1641, arXiv:1903.05659]. It is currently undergoing tidal stripping from its interaction with the neighboring galaxy NGC 4365 [arXiv:1401.5128, arXiv:2101.04895].

sources.summary: The paper argues the southwest tail near NGC 4365 was likely stripped from S0 galaxy NGC 4342, whose elongated outer isophotes align with the tail and imply major stellar mass loss.

quotes.quote: The long tidal tail can easily be seen projecting 35 \ to the southwest, with both NGC 4341 (922 ) and NGC 4342 embedded within. The brightest part of the tail, southwest of NGC 4342, reaches a peak surface brightness of =27.25 before making a 90 \ dogleg turn at its southwest limit, becoming more diffuse and extending another 14 \ to the northwest. The tail has a \ color of 0.75--0.85, somewhat bluer than the integrated color of either NGC 4365 or NGC 4342 ( =0.96 and 0.95, respectively, RC3). However, both galaxies show a marked color gradient: at 40 , NGC 4342 has a mean color of =0.8, as do NGC 4365's outer isophotes at 500 \ (Figure  N4365prof ).

While the color of the tail is consistent with tidal stripping from the outer regions of either galaxy, morphological arguments favor a scenario involving stripping from NGC 4342. The outer isophotes of NGC 4342 are elongated along the direction of the tidal tail, and the thickness of the tail (5 ) is similar to the isophotal size of NGC 4342.

Given its size and surface brightness, we estimate a total magnitude for the tail of m_V 12.5 , comparable to that of NGC 4342 itself (RC3). As such, the interaction between the galaxies has led to a significant loss of stellar material from NGC 4342, as also traced by the kinematics of the globular clusters in the tidal tail (Blom 2012a, 2014).

sources.summary: NGC 4342 is treated as a compact S0 with an oversized black hole whose stream globular clusters match its colors and velocity, supporting tidal stripping by NGC 4365.

quotes.quote: NGC 4342 is a small S0 galaxy 20 arcmin away from NGC 4365 with a recession velocity of 751 km s ^ 1 Gr98 but its distance has not been measured independently of redshift. Recently, Jia12 searched the Seven Samurai Survey Fa89 and highlighted it as a local example of a high redshift superdense `red nugget' Da05,vD08 and it has long been known to have an oversized central supermassive black hole for its bulge mass Cr99 . In the last year, Bo12a presented a very deep B filter image of NGC 4365 showing a stellar stream extending from NE of NGC 4365, across the elliptical galaxy, bridging the gap between NGC 4365 and NGC 4342, and extending further SW.

Comparing the colour distributions of GC candidates associated with NGC 4365, NGC 4342 and the stellar stream (see Figure fig:coldist ) we see a significant population of red (metal-rich) GC candidates present in NGC 4365's GC system that is absent from the colour distribution of either the stream or NGC 4342. We do not probe the central regions of NGC 4342 where red GCs are likely to be found and if the GCs are tidally stripped from the outskirts of NGC 4342 we also do not expect metal rich GCs in the stream. There is no significant difference between the GC colour distribution of the stream population and that of NGC 4342, whereas the blue colour of both stream and NGC 4342 GC candidates are not a good match for the colour of the blue GC candidates of NGC 4365. This supports the theory that the stream GCs are actually GCs from the outer parts of NGC 4342's GC system that have been stripped off the galaxy during a tidal interaction with NGC 4365.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4342 as an edge-on low-luminosity S0 with an outer and nuclear stellar disk, rapid rotation, steep central dispersion, and a required 3e8 solar-mass black hole with unusually high BH-to-bulge mass ratio.

quotes.quote: We present axisymmetric dynamical models of the edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 4342. This small low-luminosity galaxy harbors, in addition to its outer disk, a bright nuclear stellar disk. A combination of observations from the ground and with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) has shown that NGC 4342 rotates rapidly and has a strong central increase in velocity dispersion.

The three-integral models can fit all ground-based and HST data simultaneously, but only when a central BH is included. Models without BH are ruled out to a confidence level better than 99.73 per cent. We determine a BH mass of 3.0^ +1.7 _ 1.0 10^8 , where the errors are the formal 68.3 per cent confidence levels. This corresponds to 2.6 per cent of the total mass of the bulge, making NGC 4342 one of the galaxies with the highest BH mass to bulge mass ratio currently known.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4342 as an early-type galaxy with an unusually massive black hole for its stellar mass and argues that tidal stripping cannot explain the ratio.

quotes.quote: We study two nearby, early-type galaxies, NGC4342 and NGC4291, that host unusually massive black holes relative to their low stellar mass. The observed black hole-to-bulge mass ratios of NGC4342 and NGC4291 are 6.9^ +3.8 _ 2.3 and 1.9 0.6 , respectively, which significantly exceed the typical observed ratio of 0.2 . As a consequence of the exceedingly large black hole-to-bulge mass ratios, NGC4342 and NGC4291 are 5.1 and 3.4 outliers from the M_ M_ bulge scaling relation, respectively. In this paper, we explore the origin of the unusually high black hole-to-bulge mass ratio. Based on Chandra X-ray observations of the hot gas content of NGC4342 and NGC4291, we compute gravitating mass profiles, and conclude that both galaxies reside in massive dark matter halos, which extend well beyond the stellar light. The presence of dark matter halos around NGC4342 and NGC4291 and a deep optical image of the environment of NGC4342 indicate that tidal stripping, in which 90 of the stellar mass was lost, cannot explain the observed high black hole-to-bulge mass ratios.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4342 as a nearby lenticular with a massive halo and velocity dispersion comparable to NGC 4365, possibly linked to the stellar stream and low-velocity GCs.

quotes.quote: Bo12 studied the lenticular galaxy NGC 4342 V_ sys =751 km s ^ 1 , redshifts embedded in the stellar stream across NGC 4365 (see Fig. fig:schm ) and inferred a massive and extended dark matter halo around NGC 4342. The central velocity dispersions of NGC 4365 ( 261 7 km s ^ 1 ) and NGC 4342 ( 244 11 km s ^ 1 ) are consistent within errors vdisp implying similar mass galaxies. It is possible that this very massive, if relatively faint, galaxy could have passed close by NGC 4365 and dragged some of NGC 4365's own stars out from the galaxy to form the stream. It is also possible that the stream stars have been stripped from NGC 4342 itself. These scenarios both agree with our observation of low velocity GCs (at 700 km s ^ 1 , similar to the V_ sys of NGC 4342) in NGC 4365 (Fig. fig:sigma ) that are aligned with the stellar stream (Figs fig:spatial and fig:schm ).

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 4342's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 4342: We performed an Imfit bulge (function Sersic ) plus disk (function Exponential ) decomposition on a SDSS i- band image (see Figs. fig_ngc4342 and fig_imfitiso ), converting the zero-point to the Cousins I- band using the SDSS (-i) color and two equations from Jordi2006 . The parameters of the decomposition are given in Table tab:4342decomp . Our fit gives R_e=4.9'' , while Vika2012 quote R_e=0.6'' and Laesker2014 derive R_e=1.1'' for the classical fit and R_e=0.7'' for the 'best' fit. All fits have systematic residuals, but given that the resolution of the images considered by Vika2012 (FWHM= 0.5'' or 1.1 pixels) and Laesker2014 (FWHM= 0.6'' ) is too near their quoted R_e'' , we prefer our solution. The M/L is corrected for G.E. and scaled to the distance of KormendyHo2013 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4342 as a low-luminosity edge-on E7/S0 galaxy with a nuclear stellar disk, modeled with WHT/FOS kinematics to infer a roughly 3e8 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: Another example of this approach is provided by the E7/S0 galaxy NGC 4342 in Virgo. This is a low-luminosity object, seen nearly edge-on, with a prominent nuclear stellar disk. Cretton van den Bosch used ground-based major-axis kinematics from the WHT and five FOS pointings , and compared these with general axisymmetric models containing a spheroid, a stellar disk, a nuclear disk and a black hole. Free parameters in the modeling were M_ BH and the stellar mass-to-light ratio . Contours of constant ^2 in the ( , M_ BH ) plane show that the best fit is obtained for M_ BH 3 10^8 M_ , but with a significant uncertainty (Figure fig:n4342 ).

sources.summary: NGC 4342 is described as a Virgo S0 galaxy with a nuclear stellar disk, power-law brightness profile, and anomalously large black-hole-to-light ratio from HST kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4342 is an S0 galaxy in Virgo with a nuclear stellar disk (van den Bosch 1998; Scorza van den Bosch 1998). It has a power-law brightness profile, as expected for its luminosity ( =-18.49 ). NGC 4342 violates the scaling relations of Section s:trends by having an anomalously large /L . Cretton van den Bosch (1998) use HST stellar kinematics to derive = 3.0 10^8 . This yields /L = -0.63 , which exceeds the average relation in equation ( BHlumcor ) by 1.33 dex.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4342 as a Sersic galaxy repeatedly treated as an outlier, possibly heavily stripped and displaced from the black-hole luminosity relation.

quotes.quote: To the immediate lower left of this galaxy in the M_ bh - L diagram is NGC 4342. This S ersic galaxy was labelled an outlier by Laor (2001) and a potential outlier by H aring Rix (2004), both of whom excluded it from their analysis. Bogd an et al. \ (2012a) have also identified it as unusual, and it may be a heavily stripped galaxy (Blom et al. \ 2012), causing it to deviate from the M_ bh - L relation while still following the M_ bh - relation. M32 may be another such stripped galaxy (Bekki 2001; Graham 2002).

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4342, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4342 (Fig. fig:NGC4342 ) is a lenticular galaxy seen at high inclination. As other edge-on lenticulars, it presents challenges for the fitting routine, further compounded in this case by the small apparent size of the galaxy and the resulting relative lack of information in the data, which hinders a robust fit of the multiple additional components that are required to obtain acceptable residuals. Between 7 and 35 , NGC4342's profile is largely exponential, while the isophotes are disky at most radii, signaling the presence of a large-scale disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4342 as interacting with NGC 4365 and likely stripped: its GCs may feed NGC 4365, its central black hole is too massive, and it is an outlier in scaling relations.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4365 analyzed in this work still interacts with another galaxy, NGC 4342 blom12, blom12b, blom14 . There is an overdensity of GCs between the galaxies, which is aligned to the tidal features in NGC 4365. It was suggested that the GCs in this overdensity were stripped from NGC 4365. This would be supported by the fact that NGC 4365 (=VCC 731) has an unusually large amount of GCs for its luminosity peng06 . The alleged three-peaked color distribution of the GCs in NGC 4365 (e.g., blom12 ) might be a consequence of the presence of GCs coming from NGC 4342 or another galaxy. The most obvious sign that NGC 4342 lost its outer parts because of the stripping is its central black hole, which is too massive for the stellar mass of the galaxy. This galaxy is an outlier from the relations between luminosity and effective radius, luminosity and metallicity, and luminosity and the central black hole mass blom14 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4342 as the companion inside NGC 4365’s tail, with globular-cluster and diffuse-light evidence consistent with tidal stripping.

quotes.quote: A companion galaxy, NGC 4342, sits in the middle of the tail.

Their analysis of the colors and kinematics of the globular clusters suggests that they have been tidally stripped from NGC 4342. A similar conclusion was reached by , analyzing the colors and morphology of the diffuse stellar light of the tail. Moreover, NGC 4342 itself is an outlier on galaxy scaling relations in a way that is consistent with the galaxy being tidally stripped blom14 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4342 as a potentially biasing stripped galaxy whose stellar mass loss from a massive companion affects black-hole scaling-relation fits.

quotes.quote: During our linear regressions, we have excluded certain potentially biasing galaxies, which are either stripped galaxies (NGC 4342 and NGC 4486B), a single galaxy with M_ BH < 10^5 M_ (NGC 404), or more than 2 outliers (NGC 1300, NGC 3377, NGC 3998, NGC 4945, NGC 5419) in any of the correlations presented here. NGC 4342 and NGC 4486B are stripped of their stellar mass due to the gravitational pull of their massive companion galaxies NGC 4365 Blom:Forbes:2014 and NGC 4486 Batcheldor:2010:b , respectively. Hence, NGC 4342 and NGC 4486B can bias the black hole scaling relations as they have smaller n or R_e than they would have had if they weren't stripped of their mass.

gmw_00000727.name: NGC4291
gmw_00000727.overview: NGC4291 is a relatively small elliptical galaxy characterized by a pronounced core [arXiv:1311.1530]. This early-type galaxy hosts an unusually massive supermassive black hole relative to its low stellar mass and resides within a massive dark matter halo [arXiv:1203.1641].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4291 as an early-type galaxy with an unusually massive black hole for its stellar mass and argues that tidal stripping cannot explain the ratio.

quotes.quote: We study two nearby, early-type galaxies, NGC4342 and NGC4291, that host unusually massive black holes relative to their low stellar mass. The observed black hole-to-bulge mass ratios of NGC4342 and NGC4291 are 6.9^ +3.8 _ 2.3 and 1.9 0.6 , respectively, which significantly exceed the typical observed ratio of 0.2 . As a consequence of the exceedingly large black hole-to-bulge mass ratios, NGC4342 and NGC4291 are 5.1 and 3.4 outliers from the M_ M_ bulge scaling relation, respectively. In this paper, we explore the origin of the unusually high black hole-to-bulge mass ratio. Based on Chandra X-ray observations of the hot gas content of NGC4342 and NGC4291, we compute gravitating mass profiles, and conclude that both galaxies reside in massive dark matter halos, which extend well beyond the stellar light. The presence of dark matter halos around NGC4342 and NGC4291 and a deep optical image of the environment of NGC4342 indicate that tidal stripping, in which 90 of the stellar mass was lost, cannot explain the observed high black hole-to-bulge mass ratios.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4291, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4291 (Fig. fig:NGC4291 ) is a relatively small ( R_e 2 ) elliptical with a pronounced core. The ellipticity changes and peaks at 15 , followed by a minimum at 45 , to then increase again at larger radii. The second ellipticity peak may be caused by a nearby bright star, despite masking.

gmw_00000728.name: J0038--1527
gmw_00000728.overview: J0038--1527 is a luminous broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of z = 7.021 [arXiv:1810.11925, arXiv:2111.03073]. It hosts a supermassive black hole of approximately 1.3 billion solar masses [arXiv:1810.11925] and exhibits relativistic, extremely high-velocity outflows [arXiv:1810.11925, arXiv:2006.05633].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0038--1527 as a luminous z=7.021 BAL quasar with a massive black hole, strong C IV blueshift, and relativistic outflows.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we report the discovery of a luminous quasar at z=7.021 , DELS J003836.10--152723.6 (hereafter J0038--1527), selected using photometric data from DESI Legacy imaging Survey (DELS), Pan-STARRS1 (PS1) imaging Survey, as well as Wide-field Infrared Survey Explore ( WISE ) mid-infrared all-sky survey. With an absolute magnitude of M_ 1450 =--27.1 and bolometric luminosity of L_ Bol =5.6 10 ^ 13 L_ , J0038--1527 is the most luminous quasar known at z>7 . Deep optical to near-infrared spectroscopic observations suggest that J0038--1527 hosts a 1.3 billion solar mass black hole accreting at the Eddington limit, with an Eddington ratio of 1.25 0.19. The \ broad emission line of J0038--1527 is blueshifted by more than 3000 km s ^ 1 relative to the quasar systemic redshift. More detailed investigations of the high-quality spectra reveal three extremely high-velocity \ broad absorption lines (BALs) with velocity from 0.08 to 0.14 times the speed of light and total ``balnicity" index of more than 5000 km s ^ 1 , suggesting the presence of relativistic outflows.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0038-1527 as a z=7.021 BALQSO that the classifier misses because broad absorption alters its colors and depresses its Y-band relative flux.

quotes.quote: There are, at the time of writing, a total of eight known z>7 QSOs: J2356+0017 ( z=7.01 ; Matsuoka19a ), J0252-0503 ( z=7.02 ; Yang19 ), J0038-1527 ( z=7.021 ; Wang18 ), J1243+0100 ( z=7.07 ; Matsuoka19b ), J1120+0641 ( z=7.085 ; Mortlock11 ), J1007+2115 ( z=7.515 ; Yang20b ), J1342+0928 ( z=7.541 ; Banados18 ), and J0313-1806 ( z=7.642 ; Wang21 ).

However, we fail to select J0038-1527 ( P_ QSO =0.3 ). J0038-1527 exhibits strong broad absorption line (BAL) features ( Wang18 ), that can alter its colors, making it different compared to our 7 z 8 QSO models, which do not attempt to model BAL absorption. As in known_qsos , we simulated a large number of contaminants and 7 z 8 QSOs, and compare their relative fluxes with those from the real z>7 QSOs in Fig. fig:known_7 . It is evident that J0038-1527 deviates from the blue contours (deconvolved 7 z 8 QSO models) in the sub-plot displaying f_z vs. f_Y , as the absorption from the BALs impacts the Y band flux. Again, we discuss the deviations of the real QSO properties from the expected simulated ones in deviations .

sources.summary: The paper cites DELS J0038--1527 as a z > 7 quasar with several extremely high-velocity outflows, high luminosity, and a black hole mass below most DR9Q quasars.

quotes.quote: Beyond our sample, the number of known quasars decreases rapidly for z_ em > 5 : known quasars with redshifts larger than 6 are in the hundreds ( Banados16 ), and only three quasars are reported to date with z_ em > 7 (ULAS J1120+0641 in Mortlock11 , ULAS J1342+0928 in Banados18 , and DELS J0038-1527 in Wang18 ). The spectrum of DELS J0038--1527 clearly shows several outflows at extremely high velocities (see Figure 1 in Wang18 ) with v_ max 52,000 .

The presence of strong EHVOs in one of the only three quasars known to date with z_ em > 7 is a strong indication that EHVOs might be more common at larger redshifts and a fundamental component of quasars with large L_ bol : DELS J0038--1527 is the most luminous quasar with z > 7 ( M_ 1450 =-27.1 ) and Wang18 estimate that DELS J0038-1527 has a bolometric luminosity of (L_ bol ) = 47.33, similar to the median value of the EHVO quasars in our sample. The black hole mass estimate for DELS J0038--1527, log( M_ BH ) = 9.12, is, however, lower than the black hole mass estimate of most DR9Q quasars.

gmw_00000729.name: NGC 4869
gmw_00000729.overview: NGC 4869 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1002.0594] in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2009.07146] that hosts a narrow-angle-tail radio source [arXiv:1002.0594, arXiv:2207.06624]. This radio source features a weak core [arXiv:2207.06624], a bent jet [arXiv:2009.07146, arXiv:2207.06624], and a long, recollimated tail [arXiv:2207.06624] that orbits the cD galaxy NGC 4874 [arXiv:astro-ph/9809380].

sources.summary: The paper associates 5C4.81 with the giant elliptical NGC4869 and reports a narrow-angle-tail radio morphology, angular size, and tail polarization measurements.

quotes.quote: 5C4.81 - NGC 4869 This source has been studied in detail by Dallacasa et al. (1989) and Feretti et al. (1995). It is associated with the giant elliptical galaxy NGC4869. 5C4.81 has a Narrow Angle Tail radio morphology, and its angular size in the images obtained here is 55 '' (25 kpc). The mean fractional polarization in the tail is 18 at 4.535 GHz and 21 at 8.465 GHz. In Fig. fig:81_pol the radio emission is shown at 4.535 and 8.465 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper images NGC 4869 as a Coma head-tail radio galaxy with complex radio morphology, spectral steepening, jet bending, and a sheath-spine structure.

quotes.quote: The upgraded Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) has been used to image the head-tail radio galaxy NGC 4869 in the Coma cluster with an angular resolution of 6 26 at 250--500 MHz and 2 18 at the 1050--1450 MHz bands. The archival legacy GMRT data have also been used to image the source with angular resolutions from 4 9 to 21 8 at 610 MHz, 325 MHz, 240 MHz, and 150 MHz. We find that the 200 kpc scale radio morphology consists of five distinct regions with the clear presence of a pinch at 1 4 (= 38.8 kpc) and a ridge at 3 4 (= 94.2 kpc) from the head. The sharp bend by 70 ^ at 3 5 (= 97 kpc) from the head is possibly due to projection effects. The radio spectra show progressive spectral steepening as a function of distance from the head and there is possibly re-acceleration of the synchrotron electrons and perhaps also magnetic field re-generation in the 6 ^ -208 ^ (= 2.8--96.1 kpc) region of the jet. We report a steep spectrum sheath layer enveloping a flat spectrum spine, hinting at a transverse velocity structure with a fast-moving spine surrounded by a slow-moving sheath layer.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4869 as a narrow-angle-tailed radio galaxy with a weak core, long recollimated tail, bent jet, and steep integrated spectrum.

quotes.quote: 15: NGC 4869 A narrow-angle-tailed radio galaxy is hosted by an elliptical galaxy 1990AJ.....99.1381V . The radio morphology at both bands 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is typical of a head-tail radio source with a weak unresolved radio core, two oppositely directed radio jets, and a long-low-surface brightness tail, pointing away from the cluster center. The radio tail has a conical shape, which initially expands and then recollimates. The radio jet at 3 5 ( 96.1 kpc) bends by 70 deg with respect to the initial direction of propagation, after the two radio jets have undergone a twist and wrap of the two tails. A detailed study of the radio source is presented in Lal2020b,Ferettietal1990 . The largest projected linear extent of the source in the 250--500 MHz band is 6 63 ( 185.8 kpc). The host galaxy (D _ 25 = 51 24) and the tail have spectral index, (400--700 MHz), 0.62 0.01 and 1.70 0.02, respectively. The integrated spectral index is steep, (400--700 MHz) = 1.12 0.01.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4869 as orbiting NGC 4874, with radio-tail confinement implying projection effects and shielding inside the still-infalling NGC 4874 group.

quotes.quote: Radio observations of the galaxy NGC 4869, 4^ southwest of NGC 4874, by Feretti (1990) indicate that it is in orbit around the cD galaxy. They noted a major problem in the confinement of the radio tail associated with NGC 4869 due to its internal pressure strongly decreasing with distance from the center of the galaxy. One possible explanation is that, due to a projection effect, the pressure of the ambient medium is lower than that which they have computed.

If, however, NGC 4874 and NGC 4869 are part of a group still falling into Coma a significant projection effect may be at work. Given the large extent of the X-ray gas-- more than 12 \ in diameter-- found by V97 around NGC 4874, it is plausible that NGC 4869 is being shielded from the overall cluster ICM (RLB97), and the relevant ambient medium is that of the in-falling group.

gmw_00000730.name: NGC 4402
gmw_00000730.overview: NGC 4402 is an edge-on spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster undergoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:1701.02750, arXiv:astro-ph/0312061]. This interaction has truncated its atomic hydrogen and radio continuum disks [arXiv:astro-ph/0503422, arXiv:1001.3597], compressed its molecular gas [arXiv:2209.02781], and created a shrinking star-forming disk [arXiv:1701.02750].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4402 as one of three Virgo spirals with ram pressure stripping, disturbed molecular gas morphology and kinematics, upstream CO enhancement, and shrinking star-forming disks.

quotes.quote: We present 12CO (2--1) data of three Virgo spirals -- NGC 4330, NGC 4402 and NGC 4522 obtained using the Submillimeter Array. These three galaxies show clear evidence of ram pressure stripping due to the cluster medium as found in previous H i imaging studies.

At given sensitivity limits, we do not find any clear signs of molecular gas stripping. However, both its morphology and kinematics appear to be quite disturbed as those of H i . Morphological peculiarities present in the molecular and atomic gas are closely related with each other, suggesting that molecular gas can be also affected by strong ICM pressure even if it is not stripped.

CO is found to be modestly enhanced along the upstream sides in these galaxies, which may change the local star formation activity in the disc. Indeed, the distribution of H emission, a tracer of recent star formation, well coincides with that of the molecular gas, revealing enhancements near the local CO peak or along the CO compression. FUV and H share some properties in common, but FUV is always more extended than CO/H in the three galaxies, implying that the star-forming disc is rapidly shrinking as the molecular gas properties have changed.

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC873 as the giant edge-on spiral associated with the Virgo HII region 122603+130724 and argues the region may be enriched material ejected from VCC873 by a galactic fountain.

quotes.quote: This point-like source has a recessional velocity of 200 km s^ 1 , and is associated with the giant galaxy VCC873 (NGC 4402). It has a higher luminosity, star formation rate and metallicity than the extragalactic HII region recently discovered near the Virgo galaxy VCC836 by Gerhard et al. ( ).

Moreover the two giant galaxies to which the HII regions are associated have different properties. They are both edge-on star forming spiral galaxies but, while VCC836 is a Seyfert2 galaxy with a very extended emission-line region (Yoshida et al. ), VCC873 is a non active object with no evidence of extended extra-planar H emission. The mechanism responsible for the formation of the extragalactic HII region 122603+130724 is still not well understood, however the absence of a clear signature of interaction of VCC873 with another system rules out a tidal stripping scenario.

122603+130724 lies within the halo of VCC873. Its velocity differs only by 50 km s^ 1 from the velocity observed in the disk of VCC873 near its location (Rubin et al. ), suggesting that 122603+130724 is gravitationally bound to the bright galaxy. The line ratio NII 6583/ [ SII ] 6717,6731 1.2 of 122603+130724 is consistent with the metallicity obtained from the drift-scan mode spectrum of VCC873 itself, published by Gavazzi et al. ( , ), suggesting that this HII region formed from enriched material ejected from VCC873.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4402 as an observational comparison for ram-pressure stripping, noting stripped low-density ISM, surviving dense clouds, wind ablation, and extraplanar star formation.

quotes.quote: Crowl et al. (2005) have studied the Virgo cluster galaxy NGC 4402, and observe ram pressure stripping and dense cloud ablation. They find that it is possible to strip the lower density ISM without stripping dense clouds, but that the surviving clouds will eventually be ablated by the ICM wind. They also observe an HII region which has likely formed within the stripped gas, so molecular gas was either stripped or formed from stripped material.

A perfect example of a galaxy showing both the strength and weakness of our simulations that include a multiphase medium is the Virgo spiral NGC 4402 (Crowl et al 2005). The galaxy has upturned edges in it's dust and gas distribution, similar to the earliest projection of our galaxy without cooling. However, similar to our galaxy with radiative cooling, there is evidence that two dense clouds have survived longer than the lower density ISM and are being ablated by the wind.

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible tidal connection between M86 and NGC 4402, tests removal of NGC 4402 cluster candidates, and notes nearby clusters with ambiguous host assignment.

quotes.quote: We also see a small connection between the GC systems of M86 and NGC 4402 that appears to indicate a tidal interaction. We used the results from our analysis of NGC 4402's GC system (see Table table: galaxy properties ; Rhode et al. 2020, in preparation) to help us investigate whether this connection might be genuine evidence of a tidal interaction or simply the result of the two GC systems overlapping in projection on the sky. We removed the globular cluster candidates around NGC 4402 within 2 (one R_ eff for the globular cluster system) of the galaxy center; there were 23 candidates inside 2 in the full sample, and 13 blue candidates and five red candidates in the 90 color sample. We then repeated the KDE surface density map analysis but with those objects removed. Even with the removal of the GC candidates associated NGC 4402, the connection still appears in all of the KDE surface density maps, although at a reduced strength.

The barred lenticular galaxy NGC 4425 and the spiral galaxy NGC 4388 each have two clusters in close proximity with recession velocities similar to those of the galaxies, suggesting that the clusters are associated. NGC 4402 also has two clusters with similar velocities nearby, but its close proximity to M86 makes it difficult to assess to which galaxy's GC system these clusters belong.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4402 as a Virgo Cluster Sc spiral undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with truncated gas, asymmetric radio continuum, distorted dust lanes, and dust plumes shaped by an ICM wind.

quotes.quote: We present optical, HI and radio continuum observations of the highly inclined Virgo Cluster Sc galaxy NGC 4402, which show evidence for ram-pressure stripping and dense cloud ablation. VLA HI and radio continuum maps show a truncated gas disk and emission to the northwest of the main disk emission. In particular, the radio continuum emission is asymmetrically extended to the north and skewed to the west.

BVR images at 0 5 resolution obtained with the WIYN Tip-Tilt Imager show a remarkable dust lane morphology: at half the optical radius, the dust lane of the galaxy curves up and out of the disk, matching the HI morphology. Large dust plumes extend upward for 1.5  kpc from luminous young star clusters at the SE edge of the truncated gas disk. These star clusters are very blue, indicating very little dust reddening, which suggests dust blown away by an ICM wind at the leading edge of the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4402 as a Virgo spiral with leading-edge gas structures resembling Rayleigh-Taylor fingers, but argues the surviving inner gas disk suppresses RT instability and favors other origins.

quotes.quote: One example of such a case is the Virgo spiral NGC 4402. Observations of NGC 4402 ( crowl05 ) reveal structures at the leading edge of the gas disk, which resemble the characteristic finger-like structures produced by the Rayleigh-Taylor (RT) instability. We argue, however, that the RT instability is unlikely to be responsible for these structures.

Given that the RT instability is suppressed for the inner gas disk that survives ram pressure stripping, we conclude that the structures observed at the leading edge of NGC 4402 are not caused by the RT instability. These structures could be caused by different processes, one possibility being the inhomogeneous ISM as discussed by Crowl et al. (2005).

sources.summary: NGC 4402 is presented as an edge-on Virgo galaxy with blue-shifted motion, western polarized intensity aligned with the HI hot spot, and a clean disk-aligned draping case.

quotes.quote: NGC 4402 Small, blue galaxy at 2 o'clock, edge-on orientation ( = 190 km/s ). There is no stripped gas visible in HI which suggests a large LOS component of the velocity which is also consistent with the large blue-shift. The polarised intensity shows a strong emission with a maximum in the western part of the disk which coincides the the HI hot spot that ranges from the centre to the western disk ( =12:26:06-07.5 , =13:06:45 ). There is additional extraplanar polarised emission above the disk plane in the northeast of the galactic disk. This is a clean draping case with the field vectors aligned with the disk.

sources.summary: NGC 4402 is presented as a ram-pressure case: bowed gas, depleted extended-disk gas, induced recent star formation, trailing reddening lanes, and detailed stripping evidence.

quotes.quote: For example, three morphological features in NGC 4402 Hubble image: http://www.spacetelescope.org/images/heic0911c suggest strong gas interactions. First, the bowed morphology of the gas suggests a balance between the ram pressure and the lower gravitational force at larger disk radii. Secondly, the lower gas content in the extended blue stellar disk suggests a recent epoch of star formation, presumably induced by the interaction event. Finally, lanes of reddening can be seen trailing the inferred motion of galaxy in the cluster. Crowl.etal:2007 describe the observational case for ram-pressure stripping of NGC 4402 in detail.

sources.summary: NGC 4402 is treated as a leading-side ram-pressure case with kpc-scale linear dust filaments, long dust ridges, decoupled dense clouds, and polarized radio ridges.

quotes.quote: The leading side of NGC 4402 contains two kpc-scale linear dust filaments with complex substructure that have partially decoupled from the surrounding ISM. NGC 4402 also contains long dust ridges, suggesting that large parts of the ISM are being pushed out at once. Both galaxies contain long ridges of polarized radio continuum emission indicating the presence of large-scale ordered magnetic fields. We propose that magnetic fields could bind together gas of different densities, causing nearby gas of different densities to be stripped at the same rate and creating the large, coherent dust ridges and upturns. A number of factors likely play roles in determining what types of structures form as a result of ram pressure, including ram pressure strength and history, the location within the galaxy relative to the leading side, and pre-existing substructure in the ISM that may be bound together by magnetic fields during stripping.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4402 as a Virgo ram-pressure stripped galaxy whose disturbed CO plume, velocity offset, gradient, and compressed leading-edge gas indicate stripping.

quotes.quote: For example, studies have now revealed direct evidence for a marked influence on molecular gas in the Virgo cluster galaxy NGC 4402 through disturbed CO morphology with the SMA lee2017a and kinematics with ALMA at 100 pc resolution cramer2020 . The CO in the stripped plume, on the leading side of the galaxy, has a clear velocity offset of up to 60 km s^ 1 from the galaxy's normal rotation cramer2020 . The kinematic disturbance exhibits a radial gradient, with the velocity offset increasing with distance from the nucleus. This is a strong indication of the influence of ram pressure, consistent with stripping acting from the outside-in, and potentially removing the more diffuse molecular gas lee2017a, cramer2021, young2022 . Moreover, compressed CO gas along the leading edge cramer2020 of NGC 4402 is coincident with enhanced star formation traced by UV lee2017a emission (Figure fig:Virgo_407 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4402 as a ram-pressure-affected Virgo spiral with a one-sided radio-bright region and significant radio-deficit regions.

quotes.quote: The one-sided radio-bright region in NGC 4402 (Fig. fig:rcfir_spixx1c1_nice_2 ) might correspond to stripped gas (Crowl et al. 2005) or a radio halo or a thick radio disk (Krause et al. 2018), which has been pushed toward the galactic disk in the south and/or be due to CR transport together with the ISM via ram pressure. In the former scenario one would expect a radio-bright southern edge of the galactic disk due to compression. Since this is not observed, we prefer the latter scenario.
NGC 4330, NGC 4402, NGC 4522, NGC 4532, and M 51 are clear cases of CR particle advection via ICM ram pressure, galactic winds, or gravitational tides (Sect. sec:radiobright ).
These authors found significant radio-deficit regions in NGC 4254, NGC 4330, NGC 4402, and NGC 4522.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4402 as an H i deficient edge-on Virgo spiral whose continuum asymmetry and extraplanar H i are consistent with ram pressure, but whose Effelsberg offsets show no H i.

quotes.quote: NGC 4402 is another H i deficient edge-on spiral located close to NGC 4388. Crowl et al. (2005) detected an asymmetric distribution of the continuum emission and 2.7 10^7 M _ of extraplanar atomic hydrogen in the north east of the galactic disk. Both features are consistent with a scenario where ram pressure is responsible for the compressed radio continuum halo and the extraplanar H i . Our Effelsberg observations of the central position Fig. fig:n4402c do not reveal more H i than observed with the VLA (Crowl et al. 2005). We do not find any H i in the offset positions (Fig. fig:n4402eff ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4402 as having truncated radio and H I emission, a northward disk-wide extension, asymmetric polarized emission, and a strong west-edge polarization gradient.

quotes.quote: As the H i emission distribution, the 20 cm and 6 cm total power emission distribution are truncated at about half of the optical radius (Chung et al. 2009, Fig.  fig:n4402 ). In addition, we observe a disk-wide extension to the north, whereas the southern edge of the emission distributions is sharp (see also Crowl et al. 2005). Although the total power disk emission is symmetric along the major axis, the 6 cm polarized emission is more extended to the west than to the east. Moreover, we find faint extraplanar polarized emission northeast of the galactic disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4402 as ram-pressure stripped from the south, with a truncated H I disk, radio-continuum compression, and star formation following molecular and total gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4402 (Fig. fig:plot_n4402 ): the H i disk is truncated well inside the stellar disk with a small H i tail to the northwest. This galaxy also shows a compressed radio continuum halo (Crowl et al. 2005) and a polarized radio continuum ridge to the south. The galaxy thus undergoes ram pressure stripping from the south (Crowl et al. 2005). The gas disk is mostly dominated by the molecular phase and star formation is approximately proportional to the molecular and total gas surface density with a scatter of 0.2 - 0.3 dex.

sources.summary: The paper reports a western polarized-intensity maximum in NGC 4402, no eastern outer-disk detection, extraplanar polarized emission to the northeast, and ram-pressure-driven H i and radio asymmetries.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4402 we observe a strong maximum of polarized intensity in the western part of the disk where Crowl et al. (2005) detected extraplanar atomic hydrogen. There is no detection in the eastern outer disk. In addition, we observe extraplanar polarized emission above the disk plane in the north east of the galactic disk, which coincides with the extended radio continuum emission observed by Crowl et al. (2005). The observed H i and 20 cm continuum asymmetries are caused by ram pressure stripping.

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC 873 as an edge-on spiral with truncated radio emission, dust and gas stripping signs, and a possible newly observed radio continuum tail.

quotes.quote: VCC 873 (NGC 4402) This edge-on spiral is located at a distance of 700 kpc from M 87 and shows a truncated radio profile to the southeast with an extension in the opposite direction see also Murphy2009 . Furthermore, signs of ram-pressure stripping are also visible in the and the dust components of the galaxy Crowl2005Dense4402 . We speculate that in the LOFAR images, a low-surface brightness patch to the northwest of the galaxy might constitute part of a radio continuum tail of the galaxy, which was not observed previously.

gmw_00000731.name: Ton 34
gmw_00000731.overview: Ton 34 is a quasar at a redshift of 1.928 [arXiv:0710.0324, arXiv:0711.3499] that exhibits a steep continuum decline in the rest-frame far-ultraviolet [arXiv:0710.0324, arXiv:0711.3499]. Its far-ultraviolet emission lines are unusually strong, suggesting shock excitation [arXiv:0710.0324], and the source has shown short-term variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0505625].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG 1017+280/Ton 34 as an unusual redshift-1.928 quasar with a steep far-UV continuum, unusually strong far-UV lines, and shock-favored excitation.

quotes.quote: In this work, we analyze the emission lines of an unusual quasar, , which is alternatively named PG 1017+280 or J1019+2745 with redshift =1.928 . It is severely deficient in ionizing photons, since its Spectral Energy Distribution ( ) shows a remarkable steepening of the continuum in the rest-frame far-UV, shortward of 1100 \ (Binette Krongold 2007, hereafter BK07; Binette et al. 2007). If the far-UV is fitted by a powerlaw ( ^ + ), the index Among the 77 quasars whose far-UV indices could be measured by Telfer et al. 2002, there were only 3 objects with a continuum steeper than ^ 3 . is as steep as ^ 5.3 .

While the near-UV emission-line spectrum appears to be `normal', the far-UV spectrum shows low to intermediate ionization species with unusual strengths. Using the UV \ constructed by BK07 from archive data, we will quantify this statement and present photoionization and shock models for comparison.
Photoionization cannot reproduce the strengths of these far-UV lines. Shocks with 100 \ turn out to be extremely efficient emitters of these lines and are favored as excitation mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper says 1017+280 showed source variability during part of the January 2000 monitoring but no detected variation during the February 2000 night.

quotes.quote: 1017+280 (Ton34) Stalin et al. (2004) have monitored this source to look for the existence of micro-variability and their observations in three nights in R-band did not show any clear evidence of micro-variability.

The standard deviation of comparison stars is estimated at 0.006 mag, scaled value of for DLCs of the source is 0.008. The standard deviation for DLCs of qso - s1 and qso - s2 are respectively 0.019 and 0.018. The mean variance for the DLCs of the source is more than 5 . This indicates that the source is showing variability during the specific period of the night.
Hence the variance is less than 2 . Therefore, no variation is detected during the night.

sources.summary: The paper uses Ton 34 to test crystalline dust absorption, reconstructing its UV SED and finding a deep far-UV trough only partly matched by nanodiamond dust.

quotes.quote: \ ( =1.928 ) exhibits the steepest far-UV decline ( ^ 5.3 ) among the 183 quasar HST-FOS spectra analyzed by Telfer et al. It is an ideal object to test the crystalline dust hypothesis as well as alternative interpretations of the UV break. We reconstruct the UV spectral energy distribution of \ by combining HST, IUE and Palomar spectra. The far-UV continuum shows a very deep continuum trough, which is bounded by a steep far-UV rise.
Extinction by carbon crystalline dust reproduces the deep absorption trough of \ reasonably well, but not the observed steep rise in the extreme UV.

gmw_00000732.name: SBS 0909+532
gmw_00000732.overview: SBS 0909+532 is a gravitationally lensed quasar [arXiv:2201.01271] that is split into two bright, close images [arXiv:1112.0005] by a foreground early-type lens galaxy [arXiv:2201.01271]. The system exhibits both intrinsic variability [arXiv:1201.4111] and extrinsic variations attributed to gravitational microlensing [arXiv:1201.4111, arXiv:2107.06227].

sources.summary: The appendix treats QSO B0909+5312 as a z=1.377 quasar whose archival X-ray data were reprocessed; previous absorbed-power-law fitting gave a very low intrinsic column-density upper limit.

quotes.quote: QSO B0909+5312 was observed by \ in May 2003. Data were retrieved from the \ archive and reprocessed using SAS 13.5.0. Useful \ data were taken from the entire 13 ks and 17 ks exposures of pn and MOS detectors, respectively. Data were extracted following common practice and spectrally binned to 50 counts per energy channel; arf and rmf files were generated using SAS tasks.

Eitan Behar (2013) fitted the same data with an absorbed power-law model. They adopted a Galactic column density of N_ H ^ Gal =1.49 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Kalberla et al. 2005) plus and intrinsic column density at the AGN redshift. Based on their spectrum they derive an upper limit of N_ H (z)<7 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . We fit our data with a higher binning to increase statistics.

sources.summary: The paper models SBS 0909+532 as a doubly imaged quasar lens system, but finds its Hubble-constant inference unreliable because astrometry biases the lens model.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+532 is a doubly imaged quasar in which the background source (quasar) and the foreground early-type lens galaxy (main deflector) have redshifts z_ s = 1.377 and z_ d = 0.830, respectively . Our first set of observational constraints consisted of the SBS 0909+532 time delay in goico:tbl1 taking a symmetric uncertainty (50 3 days) Despite 49 3 days is fully consistent with the measurement in goico:tbl1 , initially we have preferred to keep its central value and divide the error bar into two identical halves. , the relative astrometry of B and G (their positions with respect to A at the origin of coordinates) and the flux of B in units such that the flux of A is equal to one. These last astro-photometric constraints were taken from the HST near-IR data in Table 3 of Ref. leha00 . We also considered priors on the ellipticity e and external shear of the SIE+ lens model described in goico:sec2 : e 0.5 (see Table 3 of Ref. slus12 ) and 0.1 (see Table 4 of Ref. leha00 ). Although the data fit yielded a best solution for H_0^ model of 68.4 (see goico:tbl2 ), unfortunately, the HST relative astrometry of Leh ar et al. is not so good as would be desirable.

For instance, the relative position of the faint lens galaxy G was determined with a large uncertainty of about 100 mas (1 mas = 0. ^ 001). The insufficiently accurate astrometric measures were responsible for a broad valley in the ^2 curve (see the black solid line in goico:fig2 ), so the 1 confidence interval for H_0^ model included values below 55 and above 80.
For SBS 0909+532, there are two different astrometric solutions based on the same HST near-IR data.

sources.summary: The paper monitors SBS 0909+532 as a double QSO with a faint elliptical lens, reporting first resolved brightness records, no extrinsic fluctuations, and a 45-day image delay.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+532 consists of two components (double QSO) separated by about 1 arcsec . The lensing elliptical galaxy has a large effective radius with a correspondingly low surface brightness . In Fig. 1 (top panel) we show a Maidanak subframe in the R band, where there are two close quasar components, but the very faint galaxy is not apparent. Although the presence of a faint galaxy has a positive aspect: simple photometric model with only two close point-like sources (we can avoid additional complications arising from the use of a galaxy profile), the faint and extended lenses are not a good business for cosmological studies. The relative astrometry of the quasar components (with respect to the centre of the lens galaxy) is not accurate, and this fact plays a crucial role in the estimation of the Hubble constant or the surface density of the lens (see below).

These are the first resolved brightness records of SBS 0909+532 . Our data do not show any evidence for extrinsic fluctuations, and they can be interpreted as intrinsic signals. Moreover, the direct AB cross-correlation leads to very accurate 1 determinations of the time delay ( _ BA = - 45 1 days, where the sign " " means that the intrinsic signal is observed first in B and later in A) and the flux ratio in the R band.

sources.summary: The paper uses combined UV-optical-NIR spectra of SBS0909+532, interpolates absorption, handles its peaky line profile, and provides a first H-beta-based black-hole mass estimate.

quotes.quote: SBS0909+532 --- We use the combined UV-optical-NIR spectrum of mediavilla10 of images A and B of this object, based on a combination of HST STIS and WHT INTEGRAL and LIRIS observations. The UV section of the spectrum is shown in Figure fg:all_uv_opt_spec while the NIR section is shown in Figure fg:spec_sbs0909 . The \ profile of SBS0909+532 showed a small absorption trough near observed frame 3600 \ and we interpolated over this region before measuring widths. The SBS0909+532 \ profile shape is `peaky' with broader wings at the base, and our GH fitting procedure was unable to satisfactorily fit this line profile, so we estimate errors based on the original spectra instead of a GH polynomial fit.

For this object we only measure the UV continuum luminosity on image A, as image B shows clear differential reddening with respect to A. We note that the peng06 mass quoted by greene10 is based on Mg II , so using the \ line-width measurements given in Table tab:velocities provides the first estimate of a based black hole mass for this object.

sources.summary: The paper reexamines SBS 0909+523, finding the published delay depends on two image-B points; omitting them shortens the delay, and the authors conclude the light curve is unreliable.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+523 We used the data set published by 2008NewA...13..182G , which contains 78 data points spread over two observing seasons. Their analysis leads to a time delay t_ BA = 49 6 days where B is the leading image, confirming the previously reported delay t_ BA = 45_ 1 ^ +11 of 2006AA...452...25U .

On closer inspection however, we note that this delay strongly depends on two points that are outside the general trend of the lightcurve for image B and fall right at the end of the time interval covered by the A data points for this time delay value: the 63rd and the 64th data points. Recalculating the delay while omitting these two points gives a different result of t_ BA 40 days or even lower values, which is not within the published ranges.

We conclude that this light curve does not allow a reliable determination of the time delay. To determine whether these two data points that do not follow the general trend are due to genuine quasar variations and thus crucial for the time delay determinations or whether in contrast, they are affected by large errors and contaminate the published results, we will need new observations and an independent light curve.

sources.summary: The paper treats SBS 0909+523 as a null detection for chromatic substructure effects, finding identical K and L ratios while using the measurements to constrain dust models.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+523. There is significant differential extinction in this lens 2002ApJ...574..719M ; our observations should further constrain dust models since they represent the longest wavelengths yet measured in this system. In fact, extrapolating the extinction models of 2002ApJ...574..719M (which were derived from shorter-wavelength measurements) yields predicted flux ratios of F_B/F_A=0.909 in K and 0.934 in L' , whereas we measure 0.97 0.03 in both bands. In terms of dark matter substructure, the agreement between our K and L' flux ratios is interesting as a null detection. If the K (source rest-frame 0.9 m) and L' (source rest-frame 1.6 m) sources have different effective sizes, there must be no substructure near the images with the right Einstein radius to produce a chromatic effect. Note that this does not rule out substructure in the lens --- only substructure with the right mass at the right position.

sources.summary: The monitoring found a robust 49-day image delay, little earlier flux-ratio change, intrinsic variability during LQLM I, and later microlensing-like extrinsic variation.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+532 . The LQLM I light curves in the band led to a robust time delay between its two images of t_ AB = 49 6 days ( t_ ij = t_j - t_i , B leading; ). In addition, the optical flux ratio A/B changed little in the first 10 years of observations, i.e., between the identification as a quasar pair in 1996 and our LQLM I campaign (e.g., and references therein).

For example, the band light curve of the A image and the properly shifted band light curve of B were consistent with each other throughout the LQLM I period, so the variability over this time segment was basically intrinsic to the distant quasar ( ). However, the LQLM III light curves indicate that the band flux ratio has evolved in 2010--2011. Gravitational microlensing by stars within the main lensing galaxy could account for the detected extrinsic variation.

sources.summary: The paper measures SBS0909+532's microlensed quasar continuum region sizes in g, optical, and H bands, adding long light curves to constrain accretion-disk size and wavelength scaling.

quotes.quote: We present a microlensing analysis of updated light curves in three filters, g -band, -band, and H -band, for the gravitationally lensed quasars Q0957+561 and SBS0909+532. Both systems display prominent microlensing features which we analyze using our Bayesian Monte Carlo technique to constrain the quasar continuum emission region sizes in each band. We report sizes as half-light radii scaled to a 60 inclination angle.

sources.summary: The paper describes SBS 0909+532 as an SBS quasar whose lensed nature was proposed and supported, with two close bright lensed images and a probable early-type lens.

quotes.quote: SBS 0909+532 (b) . Discovered as a quasar by Stepanyan1991 in the framework of the Second Byurakan Survey (SBS), the hypothesis of its lensed nature was first proposed by Kochanek1997 and then supported by Oscoz1997 . In Engels1998 , Engels1998 resolved the system into a pair of point sources during the Hamburg-Cfa Bright Quasar Survey. The two lensed images are bright and close to each other, which made it difficult for Lehar2000 to detect the lensing galaxy even on high-resolution near-IR images: they concluded that the lens is probably an early-type galaxy with a large effective radius and low surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper notes red-wing changes and weaker microlensing evidence for SBS 0909+532, then places it in the group of lensed quasars dominated by microlensing.

quotes.quote: Only one system (HE 0435-1223) shows a small change (0.11 mag) with S/N > 2 in the selected integration window for the blue wing as defined in Figure em1 . Visually inspecting the data, we find changes in the extreme red wings of SBS 0909+532 and SDSS J1206+0118. These changes could have their origin in the UV Fe II blend 2861-2917 located redward of Mg II, showing up with different intensities for different quasar spectra (see Kova2015 ). At odds with the results obtained from the limited sample presented in Paper I, we find, on the basis of more available epochs, that the CIII ] line seems to be significantly more affected by microlensing than the Mg II line, indicating that it arises from a more compact region inside the BLR. We detect microlensing at the 2 level within the defined integration windows in four systems (SDSS J0246-0825, HE 0435-1223, SDSS J1155+6346, WFI 2033-4723), whereas evidence of intrinsic variability (at the 2 level) can only be found in one system (Q0957+561). Also, the systems SDSS 1004-4112, SDSS J1155+6346, and HE 1104-1805 show microlensing differences at a smaller S/N ( 1.5). Notice the impact on the statistics of the quadruple systems HE 0435-1223 and SDSS J1004+4112, which have been observed in four epochs.
Additional three systems (SBS 0909+532, Q1017-207, SBS 1520+530) show evidence of microlensing at the 1.5 level, and Q 0142-100 seems to be dominated by intrinsic variability.

We derive robust estimates of both, intrinsic variability and amplitude of microlensing between the lensed images, and consistently separated a group of five objects dominated by microlensing (SBS 0909+532, FBQS J0951+2635, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1206+4332, and SDSS J1339+1310) and another group of three systems in which intrinsic variability prevails (Q 0142-100, HE 1104-1805, and WFI 2033-4723). Two systems (HE 0435-1223 and HE 2149-2745) seem to be hybrid cases with both microlensing and intrinsic variability present. Finally, the case of QSO 0957+561 may be explained by intrinsic variability combined with the large time-delay between the images plus a possible contribution from microlensing.

sources.summary: SBS 0909+532 is treated as a lensed QSO whose spectrum shows an emission line, can be fit by a broken power law, and has luminosity affected by lensing.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of SBS 0909+532 also showed evidence for a line, but it was not possible statistically to differentiate between a narrow and a broadened component; hence, the 90 per cent upper limit to the width is given in Table hard .
Only the spectrum of SBS 0909+532 can be fitted statistically as well with a broken power-law as with the power-law/blackbody model.
Note that SBS 0909+532 is a gravitational lens (Kochanek 1997; Oscoz 1997; Leh a 2002); hence, the luminosity estimated from the fits is much too high.

gmw_00000733.name: QSO 0957+561
gmw_00000733.overview: QSO 0957+561 is a gravitationally lensed, radio-loud double quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.41 [arXiv:1201.4125, arXiv:astro-ph/9812021]. The system is lensed by a giant cD galaxy within a cluster, producing two distinct images separated by approximately six arcseconds with a time delay of about 417 to 420 days [arXiv:0810.4619, arXiv:astro-ph/0609647].

sources.summary: The paper presents new two-colour monitoring of the lensed double quasar, measuring component and interband delays and attributing most observed variations to accretion-disc reverberation.

quotes.quote: We extend the gr band time coverage of the gravitationally lensed double quasar Q0957+561. New gr light curves permit us to detect significant intrinsic fluctuations, to determine new time delays, and thus to gain perspective on the mechanism of intrinsic variability in Q0957+561. We use new optical frames of Q0957+561 in the g and passbands from January 2005 to July 2007.

The gr light curves of Q0957+561 show several prominent events and gradients, and some of them (in the g band) lead to a time delay between components t_ BA = 417 2 d (1 ). We do not find evidence of extrinsic variability in the light curves of Q0957+561. We also explore the possibility of a delay between a large event in the g band and the corresponding event in the band. The gr cross-correlation reveals a time lag t_ rg = 4.0 2.0 d (1 ; the g band event is leading) that confirms a previous claim of the existence of a delay between the g and band in this lensed quasar. The time delays (between quasar components and between optical bands) from the new records and previous ones in similar bands indicate that most observed variations in Q0957+561 (amplitudes of 100 mmag and timescales of 100 d) are very probably due to reverberation within the gas disc around the supermassive black hole.

sources.summary: Q0957+561 is presented as the first gravitationally lensed z=1.41 radio-loud quasar; its delayed images enabled multiwavelength monitoring that found NUV-optical lags consistent with a centrally irradiated accretion disk.

quotes.quote: Q0957+561 was the first discovered gravitationally lensed quasar. The mirage shows two images of a radio-loud quasar at redshift z = 1.41. The time lag between these two images is well established around one year. We detected a very prominent variation in the optical brightness of Q0957+561A at the beginning of 2009, which allowed us to predict the presence of significant intrinsic variations in multi-wavelength light curves of Q0957+561B over the first semester of 2010. To study the predicted brightness fluctuations of Q0957+561B, we conducted an X-ray, NUV, optical and NIR monitoring campaign using both ground-based and space-based facilities.

From the Ugr light curves of Q0957+561B in 2010, we measured interband delays of several days that were consistent with a centrally irradiated, standard accretion disk [ 3 ] . In this scenario, a flaring source very close to the central supermassive black hole illuminates a standard accretion disk. The central flares are then thermally reprocessed into FUV-MUV variations in the inner disk to produce the observed NUV-optical variability.

sources.summary: The paper treats Q0957+561 A,B as the First Lens, using its microlensing brightness fluctuations, image separation, and inferred quasar structure to estimate rogue-planet MACHO mass-function properties.

quotes.quote: Indeed, below we clarify this from similar detections made in last decades with micro-lensing and even nano-lensing in the Q0957+561 A,B gravitational lens system (the so-called First Lens).

For one such object, the doubly imaged quasar Q0957+561 the distance observed between the A and B images is 6.26 arcsec. It has redshift of source z_S = 1.43 and of the lens z_L = 0.355 , the BH mass is 3.6 10^9 M_ (SLR06) and the gravitational radius R_G=GMc^ 2 is 5.3 10^ 14 cm. We describe the luminour ring at the inner edge of the accretion disc as a torus having two radii of importance; the outer radius, which refers to the quasar central radius to the inner edge of the accretion disc, and the sectional radius, which is half the thickness of the accretion disc and therefore the cross sectional radius. Then the outer radius is R_ outer =74 R_G=3.94 10^ 16 cm and the sectional radius is R_ '=1.52 10^ 13 cm. The main part of the quasar light comes from a structure of this radius.

A plot of the rms brightness fluctuations for the Q0957 gravitational lens system revealed that a linear relationship exists between the event duration and the amplitude of the measured fluctuations. This is represented in Fig. 8 of Schild (1999) and the best fit curve describing the rms amplitude as a function of event duration t_ ev was given as: m_ obs = 0.00133 t_ ev day ^ 1 + 0.0060 mag .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes optical light curves of the lensed quasar Q0957+561, finding multifractal signatures, band-dependent behavior, and evidence that image B is affected by microlensing in recent monitoring epochs.

quotes.quote: The quasar Q0957+561, at z = 1.41, is the first identified GLQ walsh19790957+ and one of the most studied lensed quasars (e.g., hainline2011new, nakajima2009improved, cuevas2006accurate, schild1996microlensing, rhee1991estimate, gondhalekar1980uv ); its lensing galaxy, which is known to be part of a cluster of galaxies, is located at z = 0.36 keeton2000host, rhee1991estimate . The doubly imaged quasar Q0957+561 has been observed since its discovery in 1979 walsh19790957+ in different electromagnetic bands, including the (read arm) and g (blue arm) bands, both within the optical ranges.

Here, we study the multifractal (nonlinear) behaviour of the optical observations of the lensed quasar Q0957+561 in the and g bands separately. Our questions are the following: Is there any difference in nonlinearity between the signals of the images A and B of Q0957+561? What can we learn about the intrinsic variability of the quasar and the extrinsic mechanisms distorting it?

The calculated width ( = _ max _ min ) values are _ B_ = 1.0481 and _ B_ g = 0.7739 for the and g bands of image B of Q0957+561, respectively, confirming the conclusion based on the scaling exponent function. As discussed in subsections subsec2 and subsec3 , for both quasar images, the degree of multifractality is stronger in the band. We analyse the presence of multifractal (nonlinear) signatures in the light curves of images A and B of the lensed quasar Q0957+561 in the optical and g bands using a wavelet transform modulus maxima-based multifractality analysis technique.

sources.summary: The paper models microlensing in the lensed double quasar Q0957+561, using nearly identical delayed image light curves and lens-galaxy mass models to constrain compact halo objects from absent microlensing variations.

quotes.quote: The light curves of the two images of the double quasar Q0957+561 as obtained by Kundi et al. (1997) are almost identical, except for an overall time delay and scaling factor. This allows us to put limits on the amount of microlensing that took place during the time interval corresponding to the monitoring observations. We perform numerical simulations in which we model the microlensing behaviour of the (halo of the) lensing galaxy in the system.

The double quasar Q0957+561 ( z=1.41 ) is gravitationally lensed by a galaxy ( z=0.36 ) and its associated galaxy cluster. The complex structure of the quasar in the radio regime places strong constraints on theoretical models (e.g. Gorenstein et al. ), so that well determined values for the surface mass density and the local shear at the positions of the quasar images can be obtained (Falco et al. , priv. comm., Grogin Narayan ). The normalized surface mass density = / _ crit is the projected mass density of the lensing galaxy plus cluster along the line of sight, normalized by the critical surface mass density (Schneider et al. ) _ crit = ^2 4 G D_ s D_ d D_ ds =0.92 h g / cm ^ 2 .

In two years of observational data on the gravitationally lensed double quasar Q0957+561 by Kundi et al. ( ) no microlensing variation of the quasar larger than 0.05 magnitudes was observed. In our microlensing simulations we find that this rules out a dominant population of compact objects in the halo of the lensing galaxy from 10^ 3 M_ down to 10^ 5 M_ for quasar sizes below 3 10^ 14 cm .

sources.summary: The review identifies Q0957+561 as the first well-studied double quasar lens, with two optical images, matching quasar redshifts, and a lensing galaxy in a cluster.

quotes.quote: The quasar Q0957+561 was originally found in a radio survey, subsequently an optical counterpart was identified as well. After the confirmation of its lens nature this quasar attracted quite some attention. Q0957+561 has been looked at in all available wavebands, from X-rays to radio frequencies.

In the optical light Q0957+561 appears as two point images of roughly 17 mag (R band) separated by 6.1 arcseconds (see Figure Q0957+561_hst ). The spectra of the two quasars reveal both redshifts to be z_Q = 1.41 . Between the two images, not quite on the connecting line, the lensing galaxy (with redshift z_G = 0.36 ) appears as a fuzzy patch close to the component B. This galaxy is part of a cluster of galaxies at about the same redshift. This is the reason for the relatively large separation for a galaxy-type lens (typical galaxies with masses of 10^ 11-12 M_ produce splitting angles of only about one arcsecond, see equation ( eq-angle-gal )).

sources.summary: The paper describes Q0957+561 as a giant-elliptical/cD lens where image rotation-measure differences are attributed mainly to the lensing galaxy, implying a coherent magnetic field.

quotes.quote: Both the lens systems Q0957+561 and B1422+231 have a giant elliptical galaxy as the dominant lens. While the former is part of a galaxy-cluster, the latter might be associated with a group of galaxies. An analysis of the Faraday Rotation measurements in these and similar systems could provide evidence for possible existence of coherent large--scale magnetic fields in elliptical galaxies.

Possibly the first lens system Q0957+561, with a time delay of 420 days is a good example for examination of these issues. The earlier measurements by green:85 for the system Q0957+561 reported rotation measures of A and B images to be respectively -61 3 rad m ^ 2 and -160 3 rad m ^ 2 . This gives difference in the RM between A and B images of 100 rad m ^ 2 which is at variance with the corresponding difference of 30 rad m ^ 2 measured by patn:01 .

green:85 attributed the difference between the rotation measures along the sightlines to the two images, of 100 rad m ^ 2 entirely to the lensing cD galaxy with intracluster medium making a negligible contribution.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Q0957+561 UV variability structure functions, finding comparable restframe behavior at two wavelengths and favoring reverberation from EUV/X-ray fluctuations near a jet.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed structure function analysis of the UV variability of Q0957+561 . From optical observations in the 2005 2007 seasons, we constructed normalized structure functions of the quasar luminosity at restframe wavelengths 2100 \ and 2600 . Old optical records (1995 1996 seasons) also allow the structure function to be obtained at 2100 , but 10 years ago in the observer's frame. These three structure functions are then compared to predictions of both simple and relatively sophisticated (incorporating two independent variable components) Poissonian models.

From the recent data, 100-d time-symmetric and 170-d time-asymmetric flares are produced at both restframe wavelengths. Taking into account measurements of time delays and the existence of an EUV/radio jet, reverberation is probably the main mechanism of variability. Thus, two types of EUV/X-ray fluctuations would be generated within or close to the jet and later reprocessed by the disc gas in the two emission rings, at 2100 \ and 2600 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Q0957+561 as a suitable Ukaliq monitoring target: a bright double quasar with 6 arcsec image separation, a 417-day delay, and microlensing-driven variability that remains hard to measure.

quotes.quote: One well-known gravitationally lensed system which illustrates the good fit of Ukaliq is Q0957+561. It is a doubly imaged quasar at z=1.405 lensed by a foreground, z=0.355 galaxy. Images A and B are relatively bright, V=16 , and separated by 6 arcsec on the sky; IQ of 1 arcsec PSF FWHM ensures no blending between them. Their time delay is 417 2 days, that is, a path difference causes any change in the brightness of quasar image B to lag A, which has some intrinsic color dependence inherent to the source [ 4 and references therein ] .

It is remarkable that this is not known more accurately after roughly 30 years of monitoring, but that is in part because of uncertainty due to microlensing by substructure in the lensing galaxy. Since one such 0.25 mag event in 1981 to 1986 there have been several (disputed) detections in the last 20 years with amplitudes below 20 millimag and time periods shorter than about 5 days. A possibility is that variability over various timescales are superimposed.

sources.summary: The paper uses microlensing of Q 0957+561 to infer BLR structure, finding compact C IV emission, larger C III] and Mg II zones, and possible geometry and kinematic differences.

quotes.quote: Our aim is to examine the size, kinematics, and geometry of the broad-line region (BLR) in the double-lensed quasar Q 0957+561 by analyzing the impact of microlensing on various rest-frame ultraviolet broad-emission lines (BELs). We explore the influence of intrinsic variability and microlensing on the C IV, C III ] , and Mg II emission lines through multiple spectroscopic observations taken between April 1999 and January 2017. By utilizing the line cores as a reference for no microlensing and correcting for the long time delay between the images, we estimate the sizes of the regions emitting the broad-line wings using a Bayesian approach.

Our study of the microlensing amplitudes between the lensed images of the quasar Q 0957+561 reveals differing sizes of the regions emitting the three prominent BELs C IV, C III ] , and Mg II.
