quotes.quote: We have also extended our SED modelling technique to four additional edge-on systems - NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082 and UGC 2048 - with the aim of examining whether the features of the solution we obtained for NGC 891 might be more generally applicable. In this section we mainly show and discuss the results for NGC 891, and only briefly illustrate the solution for NGC 5907. The ``two-dust-disk'' model can successfully fit the shape of the SED for both NGC 891 and NGC 5907.

The best solution for NGC 5907 (Fig. 2) has a central face-on optical depth in the optical band _ v =1.4 . The total FIR-submm re-radiated luminosity of NGC 5907, obtained by integrating the ``two-dust-disk'' model SED, is 50.5 10^ 35 W out of which 27.0 10^ 35 W is attributed to heating from the young stellar population. Thus, about 40 of the dust emission is powered by the old stellar population.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5907's 21 cm warp curve as a representative test of a magnetic warp model, fitting the observed curve and calling the match reasonable.

quotes.quote: Even though the model is highly simplified, it basically reproduces real warp curves, in particular the 21 cm warp curve of NGC 5907. If the magnetic model were considered to be correct, the fitting of warp curves could allow rough estimations of the strength and direction of the magnetic field.

Figure fig2 reproduces the observational curve for one of the best known prominent warps, in NGC 5907, adopted from Sancisi (1976). We cannot exclude that the warp of this galaxy is due to other mechanisms, but we choose this warp because it is one of the most representative and is studied very often. This figure also reproduces a fitting to the model, with parameters k=8 10^ 5 and =20^ .

The noticeable fitting of the NGC 5907 warp curve suggest that the magnetic model and the approximations considered here are not unreasonable We have studied some magnetic effects in the density distribution of the outermost disc without considering in detail the magnetic field distribution in this region.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5907 as a nearby edge-on spiral with optical, H I, and infrared warps, likely influenced by an interacting dwarf companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 5907: This is another well studied, nearby edge-on spiral galaxy in our sample. Warps in this galaxy have been observed both in optical R band (Florido et al. 1992; Schwartzkopf Dettmar 2001) and in H I (Sancisi 1976). We detect a small warp in the 4.5 micron band. Earlier thought to be a non-interacting galaxy, this galaxy has recently been discovered to be interacting with a dwarf companion PGC54419 lying at a projected distance of only 36 kpc from the center of the galaxy (Shang et al. 1998; Martinez-Delgado et al. 2008). The dwarf companion could to be the cause of NGC5907's warp.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5907 to validate SDSS stream detection, recovering the northern and southern tidal arcs while missing a bright western arc because of low-flux image limitations.

quotes.quote: NGC 5907 After processing the SDSS data of NGC 5907, we had an image which showed that the method is working, see Fig. 5907comp for a direct comparison between the deep image from Mart i nez-Delgado et al. and our own. The processed image obtained from the SDSS data shows that some parts of the NGC 5907 tidal stream are detected. Those parts are the long northern arc on the left and, even fainter, the southern arcs below NGC 5907. Curiously, one of the brightest parts, the arc near the west tip of the galaxy, is not seen in our image. This is due to the afore mentioned effects like different CCD sensitivities which degrade the image quality at low flux counts.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5907 as a symmetric galaxy with a typical TII surface-brightness profile, an inner break around 240 arcsec, a second break near 330 arcsec, and dust-reddened central colors.

quotes.quote: Another representative example (in this case a symmetric galaxy) is NGC 5907 (Fig. img:5907 ). The surface brightness profiles resemble a typical TII, with the inner break around 240". As in the case of NGC 4244, there is a second change in the exponential behavior at a radius 330" . A clear difference with NGC 4244 are the high values of the ( '-3.6 m ) color in the central region of the galaxy. Knowing that the IR band is less sensitive to dust attenuation, this red value of the ( '-3.6 m ) color could be explained by the attenuation of dust in the optical bands.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5907 scale heights measured at 20 cm, comparable to other edge-on galaxies, and treats it as likely evidence for a galactic wind.

quotes.quote: The scale heights were determined from 6 cm observations except for NGC 5907 (observed at 20 cm).

\ \ ht 9 \ The scale heights in NGC 5907 are measured from 20 cm observations taken with the VLA dumke+00 . They are similar to the values of the sample of 5 galaxies discussed above. If synchrotron losses dominate in this galaxy, its scale heights are expected to be somewhat larger than those at 6 cm, however the maps may suffer large extended structure dumke+00 .

The observations of similar scale heights for not strongly interacting galaxies (the first five galaxies in Tab. tableone and probably NGC 5907 as well) suggest the existence of a galactic wind in all of them.

sources.summary: The paper reports asymmetric UV halo flux around NGC 5907, 2-3 kpc scale heights, a modeled stellar halo, and UV halo SEDs useful for finding compact structures.

quotes.quote: We find H 2.5-3.5 kpc for NGC 5775, H 2-3 kpc for NGC 5907, and H 3-5 kpc in NGC 3079. In NGC 5907 and NGC 3079, we measure more flux on one side of the galactic center than the other. In the galaxies shown here (especially NGC 5907) most variations along the major axis are correlated between filters; this may be due to where star formation occurs in the disk or the structure of the halo or cirrus substructure.

For NGC 5907, the model predicts a bolometric stellar halo luminosity of 2 10^9 L_ , and for NGC 5775 about 5 10^9 L_ . Galaxies like NGC 5907 have such uniform UV halo SEDs (Figure figure.radial ) that bins with markedly different SEDs may identify compact halo structures.

sources.summary: The chapter presents NGC 5907 as a nearby edge-on galaxy with a spectacular rosette-like tidal stream, modeled as debris from a single low-mass satellite spanning multiple orbits.

quotes.quote: Shortly thereafter, a study of the nearby, edge-on galaxy NGC 5907 NGC 5907 by shang98 employed deep CCD images to reveal an elliptical loop in the halo of this galaxy, which they believed to be the remains of a tidally disrupted galaxy similar in size to the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy Sagittarius (Sgr) dwarf spheroidal (dSph) (Sagittarius was at that time a recent discovery in the Milky Way halo). Shang et al. also identified another Sagittarius-like dwarf galaxy dwarf galaxies that they suggested might be interacting with the disk of NGC 5907, causing the observed warp in HI.

More recently, NGC 5907 NGC 5907 was imaged by Figure n5907 MD2008 , revealing even fainter features of the stream. As shown in Figure n5907 , this stream is prominently visible as an interwoven, rosette-like structure traversing nearly 720^ around NGC 5907. Detailed N body modeling N-body simulations suggests that all of the features can be reproduced from the accretion of a single, low-mass satellite galaxy, with the stream tracing two full orbits of its progenitor.

sources.summary: The paper revisits Dragonfly imaging of edge-on NGC 5907, noting its curved tidal stream, disputing a previously claimed second loop, and improving subtraction near bright stars in the field.

quotes.quote: In a previous paper (van Dokkum et al. \ 2019) we applied the mrf methodology to a deep Dragonfly image of the edge-on galaxy NGC 5907. The galaxy has a curved tidal stream, discovered by Shang et al. \ (1998). Mart nez-Delgado et al. \ (2008) found that the stream consists not of one but of two loops, wrapping twice around the galaxy. However, the Dragonfly data, as well as recent observations by Muller et al. \ (2020), failed to confirm this second loop. There are several bright stars in the NGC 5907 field, and some of these are close to the purported second loop (see van Dokkum et al. \ 2019).

sources.summary: The paper notes background sources through NGC 5907, finds it too faint in polarized intensity across much of the halo, and detects magnetic vectors mainly along the projected disk.

quotes.quote: We have retained any radio lobe structure near AGNs (e.g. NGC 3079) and have also retained any background sources that might be seen through the disk (e.g. two background sources at the far end of the disk of NGC 5907).

This is not the case only for four of these galaxies, namely NGC 2613, NGC 3628, NGC 4302, and NGC 5907. The reason is for NGC 2613 (Fig.  n2613all ), NGC 4302 (Fig.  n4302all ), and NGC 5907 (Fig.  n5907all ) that they are too faint in linear polarization within most parts of their halos/disks, while NGC 3628 (Fig.  n3628all ) has, in addition, a strong central source.

In three galaxies the magnetic field vectors and RM values could only be detected along the projected disk. These are NGC 5907 and NGC 4013 (see also ), and also probably NGC 4565 (see schmidt+2019 ) where we detect magnetic field vectors and RM values only up to about 1.0 kpc distance from the midplane.

sources.summary: The paper reviews NGC 5907 as a late-type edge-on spiral with controversial faint extra-planar light, redder than its disk and unlike a normal thick disk.

quotes.quote: Faint light high above the plane of the well-studied, late-type, edge-on spiral NGC 5907 has further complicated the picture of extra-planar light in small- or no-bulge spirals. First detected at heights of 3 to 6 kpc above the plane in deep R band observations ( ), this extended emission is intriguing because it is unlike any known thick disk or stellar component, having instead a morphology similar to that inferred for the dark matter halo distribution of NGC 5907 ( ). Other workers have confirmed the presence of the faint light in other bands (BVRIJK), and showed that the extended light is redder than the thin stellar disk. If the faint light is due to a thick disk, it is unlike any other, with a scale length that is at least twice that of its thin disk ( ). The stellar population responsible for this faint light remains highly controversial, ranging from normal or metal-rich populations with steep IMFs ( ; ; ), old, metal-rich accreted populations with normal IMF ( ), or exceedingly metal-poor or giant-poor populations with few resolvable stars at the tip of the RGB ( ).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 5907 as an edge-on spiral where an apparent light-rotation mismatch was later explained by dust-obscured luminous material.

quotes.quote: This is well illustrated by the case of the late-type, edge-on spiral NGC 5907. Its observed rotation curve rises rapidly near the centre, whereas the rotation curve predicted from the light profile (Van der Kruit and Searle 1981) rises much less steeply (see figure 7 in Sancisi and Van Albada 1987). This seemed to indicate a clear need for an additional component, in the central parts, of matter unrelated to the light. Van Albada and Sancisi (1986) suggested that the discrepancy might be caused by a problem in the light profile and that the "missing material" might well be the luminous material obscured by the dust. Several years later, this was proved to be right by Barnaby and Thronson (1994) who used H-band surface photometry and successfully modelled the observed rotation curve in the inner parts of NGC 5907.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5907 as a nearly pure-disk edge-on galaxy with a classical H I warp and rotation-curve drop around the truncation radius.

quotes.quote: Fig1 I show two of these galaxies to illustrate the way these data have been analysed. These are NGC 4565 (in which actually the evidence for truncations was first noticed) and NGC 5907. The first has a bulge, but the second is probably almost entirely disk.

The classical example of an edge-on warp is in NGC 5907 (Sancisi, 1976). Casertano (1983) has shown that there is a drop in the rotation curve just at the truncation radius and he relates this to a sharp drop in density in the stellar disk. The radial distribution of the \ has been part of Casertano's modelling; it appears from his figures to be dropping, but behaves relatively smoothly at the transition between the inner disk and the warp at about the truncation radius.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 5907 as a prime edge-on disk example with sharp stellar truncation and an H I warp beginning at the truncation radius.

quotes.quote: Prime examples of this phenomenon of ``truncations'' (originally also called ``cut-offs'') are the edge-on galaxies NGC 4565 and NGC 5907 (Fig. 1). The truncations appear very sharp, although of course not infinitely.

Warps in the HI in external galaxies are most readily observed in edge-on systems as NGC 5907 San76 . NGC 5907 has a clear and sharp truncation vdKS82 in its stellar disk at the same radius where the warp starts.

sources.summary: The paper reports a massive tidal stream around NGC 5907, including a newly found western arm and one genuine eastern loop, while other patches are cirrus.

quotes.quote: NGC 5907 The massive tidal stream that surrounds NGC 5907, first discovered by , is discussed in . Notably, we have discovered a long western arm and detect only one eastern loop where two were previously thought to exist. This single stream appears to be the only genuine (sufficiently bright) tidal structure surrounding the galaxy. Other low surface brightness streaks and patches of note in the field match well with 100 m emission and are very likely to be cirrus.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5907 as a disputed halo case: earlier halo detections and colors are contrasted with later evidence that the extraplanar light is a tidal ring from a disrupted dwarf.

quotes.quote: After the first detection of halo around NGC 5907 claimed by , many controversial results have followed. The latest and deepest observations of this galaxy see e.g. zheng_etal99 strongly support the extraplanar emission being instead a ring which results from tidal disruption of a satellite galaxy.

After the first claim by of the detection of halo emission from NGC5907, many discrepant measurements have been made by different groups in bands from the optical to the NIR see e.g. barnaby_thronson94,lequeux_etal96,rudy_etal97, james_casali98,lequeux_etal98 . The latest observations by with intermediate-band filters, and by in optical and NIR, together with the RGB star counts derived by from NICMOS observations, seem to rule out the presence of a halo, favouring instead a luminous ring produced by the tidal disruption of a dwarf companion.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5907 as an edge-on disc galaxy, fitting optical and near-infrared profiles with star-formation history, dynamical heating, dust radiative transfer, and stellar-population synthesis.

quotes.quote: We present an evolutionary disc model of the edge-on galaxy NGC 5907 based on a continuous star formation history and a continuous dynamical heating of the stellar subpopulations. This model explains the disparate two observational facts: 1) the exponential vertical disc structure in the optical and NIR of the non-obscured part of the stellar disc and 2) the FIR/submm luminosity enhanced by about a factor of four near the obscured mid-plane, which requires additional dust and also stellar light to heat the dust component. We use multi-band photometry in U, B, V, R, and I- band combined with radiative transfer through a dust component to simultaneously fit the vertical surface-brightness and colour index profiles in all bands adopting a reasonable star formation history and dynamical heating function. The vertical distribution of the stellar subpopulations are calculated self-consistently in dynamical equilibrium and the intrinsic stellar emissivity is calculated by stellar population synthesis.

sources.summary: NGC 5907 is studied through its globular clusters and stellar stream; the paper estimates its globular-cluster population and uses imaging and spectroscopy to probe the disrupted-satellite context.

quotes.quote: Received YYY; in original form ZZZ firstpage - lastpage 2019 We study the globular clusters (GCs) in the spiral galaxy NGC 5907 well-known for its spectacular stellar stream -- to better understand its origin.
We estimate the total number of globular clusters in NGC 5907 to be 154 44 , with a specific frequency of 0.73 0.21 .
In this work, we have used wide-field imaging and multi-object spectroscopy to investigate the GCs around the spiral galaxy NGC 5907 and its associated stellar stream from a disrupted satellite.

gmw_00000738.overview: The radio galaxy 0402+379 is an extremely luminous elliptical host [arXiv:1605.05373] that contains a gravitationally bound supermassive black hole binary with a projected separation of 7.3 parsecs [arXiv:2312.07766]. This system exhibits multiple epochs of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2303.11361] and is surrounded by a cluster-scale X-ray halo [arXiv:1605.05373].

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C+37.11 as a massive radio elliptical with a resolved compact double nucleus, cored surface-brightness profile, rising central dispersion, and a scoured core.

quotes.quote: We report on IFU measurements of the host of the radio source 4C+37.11. This massive elliptical contains the only resolved double compact nucleus at pc-scale separation, likely a bound supermassive black hole binary (SMBHB). i band photometry and GMOS-N IFU spectroscopy show that the galaxy has a large r_b=1.5^ core and that the stellar velocity dispersion increases inside of a radius of influence r_ SOI 1.3^ . Jeans Anisotropic Modeling analysis of the core infers a total SMBHB mass of 2.8^ +0.8 _ 0.8 10^ 10 M_ , making this one of the most massive black hole systems known. Our data indicate that there has been significant scouring of the central kpc of the host galaxy.

found that the radio galaxy 4C+37.11 (0402+379) contains two central, compact, flat-spectrum, variable components (designated C1 and C2) with a VLBA-measured separation 7.3 pc at z=0.055 and argued that this is a gravitationally bound SuperMassive Black Hole Binary (SMBHB). Subsequent VLBA observations by 2006ApJ...646...49R have bolstered that claim and even detected a possible relative proper motion of the binary components 2017ApJ...843...14B . The binary's host is remarkable, being an extremely massive elliptical embedded in a bright X-ray halo 2014ApJ...780..149R, 2016ApJ...826...91A which represents a cluster's worth of stars and mass in a relatively isolated galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBI HI observations of radio galaxy 0402+379, modeling two high-velocity absorption clumps as a thick rotating disk around one black hole in its compact binary nucleus.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy 0402+379 was recently found to contain two central, compact, flat spectrum, variable components (designated C1 and C2), with a projected separation of 7.3 pc, a feature which had not been observed in any other compact source, making this system the most compact supermassive binary black hole pair yet imaged by more than two orders of magnitude, with an estimated system mass of a few 10^8 M_ (Maness et al. 2004; Rodriguez et al. 2006). performed spectral line observations of 0402+379 at 1.348 GHz. HI absorption at a redshift of 560 km s ^ 1 from the systemic velocity of the source (16,489 300 km s ^ 1 ; Xu et al. 1994) was observed and attributed to a high velocity gas system, possibly due to a merger.

Global VLBI observations at 1.3 GHz were performed on the radio galaxy 0402+379, the most compact supermassive binary black hole pair yet imaged. Two absorption lines were found toward the southern jet of the source, one redshifted by 370 10 km s ^ 1 and the other blueshifted by 700 10 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic velocity of the source. A model consisting of a thick disk, of which we only see a couple of clumps, was devoloped in order to reproduce the velocities measured from the HI absorption profiles. These clumps, components CW and CE, rotate in circular Keplerian orbits around an axis that crosses one of the supermassive black holes of the binary system in 0402+379, component C2.

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C+37.11’s cluster-scale X-ray halo, identifying an extremely luminous elliptical host with a parsec-scale black-hole binary and merger-driven gas sloshing.

quotes.quote: We analyzed deep Chandra ACIS-I exposures of the cluster-scale X-ray halo surrounding the radio source 4C+37.11. This remarkable system hosts the closest resolved pair of super-massive black hole and an exceptionally luminous elliptical galaxy, the likely product of a series of past mergers. We characterize the halo with r_ 500 = 0.95 Mpc, M_ 500 = (2.5 0.2) 10^ 14 \ M_ , kT = 4.6 0.2 keV, and a gas mass of M_ g,500 = (2.2 0.1) 10^ 13 M_ . The gas mass fraction within r_ 500 is f_ g = 0.09 0.01 .

4C+37.11 is a remarkable system for two major reasons. First, it hosts two compact radio nuclei, resolved by Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) observations to have a 7.3 pc projected separation, one of which currently powers relativistic jets in a Compact Symmetric Object outflow (CSO) 2004Maness,2006Rodriguez . The optical host of the SMBHB is a relatively isolated, extremely luminous elliptical galaxy with M_ K_s = -27.0, which corresponds to a stellar mass of M_ = 9.5 10^ 11 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper uses GALAXY 0402+379 as an observed VLBA supermassive black-hole binary example, estimating its orbital parameters and showing dark-energy accretion would shorten its merger time.

quotes.quote: The first example is: GALAXY 0402+379 observed in 2007 with VLBA which has the following parameters, R_0 = 10^ 17 m, T 10^ 14 s, 2 =10^6, a = 2 10^8, = 10^ 26 m . This binary thus has a frequency _0 10^ 14 Hz too small for LIGO detection and amplitude h 10^ 23 . The orbital corrections due to GW radiation y_1 and dark energy accretion y_2 differ by two orders of magnitude, y_1 y_2 10^2 10^ 24.5 t resulting in an orbital change of R (1+w) 10^6 m during each period of rotation, t = T . Since the time the signal was emitted from the binary, t_e = 10^ 14 s , the orbit has changed by 10^ 6.5 m due to the GW (Hulse-Taylor) term y_1 , and by 10^8 m due to the dark energy accretion term y_2 .

Thus the merging time for this binary, if the dark energy effect is ignored, is about another 60,000 yrs since the time the emitted signal t_e was received. Included the effect of dark energy accretion results in a merging time 1,000 yrs for (1+w) >0 , which is roughly two orders of magnitude less then the standard estimate.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0402+379 as a max-separation or merger-transition source with optical disturbance, a close companion, bent VLBI jet, diffuse VLA emission, radio variability, and thermal colors.

quotes.quote: * 0402+379 (4C 37.11) (radio galaxy, z=0.055 ): This object is both disturbed (in the optical) and has a close companion galaxy (projected distance 29 kpc). Its VLBI jet is bent. Diffuse radio emission is seen on VLA scales.
Variable in the radio. It is positioned in the thermal regime of the color-color plot. This object can also be classified as a transitory object between the max separation and merger phase.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0402+379 as a compact restarted radio source, with steep-spectrum lobes, an inner FR II-like double, and older remnant lobe pairs.

quotes.quote: The first example of an object of this kind --- 0402+379 --- was given by maness03 . On arcsecond scales this source is a core-dominated Medium-scale Symmetric Object (MSO) with very steep spectrum lobes. The multi frequency VLBA observations carried out by maness03 provide impressive evidence of intermittent activity: the innermost double ( 30 pc across) has a FR II type structure.

However, some hints of a previous active epoch are still preserved. In particular, the diffuse component 200 pc south of the centre seems to be a remnant of the past active period. Altogether there are three pairs of lobes visible in this source, two `dead' and one `alive'.

sources.summary: The paper reports 0402+379 as a radio galaxy hosting a 7.3 pc supermassive binary black hole candidate, with two compact active nuclei, jet motion constraints, and spectroscopy-based mass estimates.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of a supermassive binary black hole system in the radio galaxy 0402+379, with a projected separation between the two black holes of just 7.3 pc. This is the closest black hole pair yet found by more than two orders of magnitude. These results are based upon recent multi-frequency observations using the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) which reveal two compact, variable, flat-spectrum, active nuclei within the elliptical host galaxy of 0402+379.

Multi-epoch observations from the VLBA also provide constraints on the total mass and dynamics of the system. Low spectral resolution spectroscopy using the Hobby-Eberly Telescope indicates two velocity systems with a combined mass of the two black holes of 1.5 10^8 \ M _ . The two nuclei appear stationary while the jets emanating from the weaker of the two nuclei appear to move out and terminate in bright hot spots.

sources.summary: The paper presents 0402+369 as an elliptical galaxy hosting a compact massive black-hole binary, probably representing a late dry-merger stage before gravitational-wave losses dominate.

quotes.quote: A third example is the elliptical galaxy 0402+369 where the cleanest case of a massive BH binary has been recently discovered. Two compact variable, flat--spectrum active nuclei are seen at a projected separation of only 7.3 pc (Rodriguez et al. 2006). Arp 299, NGC 6240, and 0402+369 may just highlight different stages of the BH dynamical evolution along the course of a merger, with 0402+369 being the latest, most evolved phase (possibly related to a dry merger).

Energy and angular momentum losses due to gravitational waves are not yet significant in 0402+392, so that stellar interactions and/or material and gravitational torques are still necessary to bring the BHs down to the domain controlled by General Relativity.

sources.summary: The review describes 0402+379 as a nearby radio galaxy with two flat-spectrum nuclei separated by 7.3 pc and possible VLBA-detected relative orbital motion.

quotes.quote: A systematic study of the most luminous radio AGN in the multi-frequency VLBI archive burkespolaor2011 has produced a single case of a double flat-spectrum core, the nearby radio galaxy 0402+379 ( z=0.055 ), with two nuclei separated by just 7.3 pc (see Fig. fig:radio0402 , rodriguez2006 ). Based on the analysis of VLBA (the network of ten radio antennae located across the United States) data spanning 12 yr, recently claimed the detection of relative motion of the companion AGN. If this is due to orbital motion, and assuming a circular orbit, the authors could derive an orbital period of about 3 10^ 4 yr.
This previously known object, the nearby radio galaxy 0402+379 ( z=0.055 ), has two nuclei separated by just 7.3 pc (see Fig. fig:radio0402 , rodriguez2006 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses 0402+379 as an observed SMBHB example, citing VLBI relative-motion measurements, estimating its 230 GHz flux from redshift and mass, and proposing it for in-beam SFPR astrometry.

quotes.quote: It requires that both sources are visible and their separation and relative motions are measurable. successfully measured 1.57 \ as/yr relative motion of SMBHB 0402+379 by 12 years of VLBI observations at 5-22 GHz.
0402+379 with z=0.055 , M=1.5 10^ 10 \ M_ is estimated to be 34 mJy.

The visible SMBHB 0402+379 with a orbital separation 7.3 pc 7.3 mas in the sky, see Bansal2017ApJ843.14 would serve as an good example of the application of such observations. With the help of SFPR to remove the atmospheric errors and increase the coherent integration time galaxies11010003 , their relative motions can be precisely tracked. simultaneous multi-frequency absolute astrometry If the two sources are too separated to use in-beam observation, the relative astrometry becomes inapplicable.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C +37.11 as an unusual CSO with broad molecular lines, merger-driven radio interactions, and direct evidence for a black-hole pair.

quotes.quote: 4C +37.11 shows a highly unusual spectrum, with all lines from S(1) through S(7) detected in the LR data.

man04 show that the radio structure in 4C +37.11 may come from interactions driven by a recent merger, with a dense circumnuclear medium giving rise to shocks in the central component that would be consistent with the lines seen here.
For several CSOs, hard X-ray emission provides direct evidence for supermassive BHs; in the case of 4C +37.11, a pair of BHs has been directly detected.

sources.summary: J0405+3803 is classified as a CSO 2.0 with hot spots at its inner lobe extremities, two active nuclei, and evidence for more than one activity epoch.

quotes.quote: J0405+3803, CSO 2.0 [ 53 ] : The multifrequency observations of 2004ApJ...602..123M ^ show clearly that there are hot spots at the outer extremities of the inner lobe structure. In addition, as these authors show, there are two active nuclei in this object, so this is also a rare example of a binary supermassive black hole (SMBH). This CSO exhibits more than one epoch of activity 2004ApJ...602..123M .

gmw_00000739.name: 3C 75
gmw_00000739.overview: Located at the center of the Abell 400 galaxy cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0309441, arXiv:1612.02341], 3C 75 is a radio source powered by two interacting supermassive black holes [arXiv:2311.14830, arXiv:2001.06293]. These black holes reside in a bound binary system [arXiv:2311.14830, arXiv:astro-ph/0603129] and emit two pairs of radio jets that bend and collide [arXiv:1612.02341, arXiv:2205.04483].

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 75 as a radio source powered by two SMBHs in NGC 1128, with bent jets and X-ray/radio sources indicating a gravitationally bound binary.

quotes.quote: 3C 75 is a system of two SMBHs in the galaxy NGC 1128, which is part of the Abell 400 galaxy cluster, 300 million light-years away. In the 3C 73 image (see Figure fig:example_2 ), summing the X-ray (shown in blue) and radio (shown in pink) bands, two bright sources in the center are clearly visible -- these are two SMBHs orbiting around a common center of mass, feeding the powerful radio source 3C 75. The distance between these SMBHs is 7.6 kpc. These BHs are believed to be located in the ``new" nucleus of two merging galaxies. Hudson et al. (2006) concluded that two supermassive black holes are connected by gravitational forces and form a binary system.

They also made this conclusion for another reason. The jets ejected by the SMBHs have the same bend, which is most likely due to the fact that both SMBHs are rushing through the hot gas of the cluster at a velocity of 1200 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C75 as the central radio galaxy in Abell 400, states its redshift, uses its RM map, and notes map resolution for magnetic-field analysis.

quotes.quote: The radio galaxy 3C75 which is in the centre of Abell 400 also of richness class I has a redshift of 0.02315 1991trcb.book.....D . The X-ray properties of the cluster have been studied by 2002ApJ...567..716R using ROSAT PSPC data. They determine for the gas density profile a core radius of 3.9 arcmin, a of 0.54 and an electron density at the cluster centre of n_ e0 = 0.0016 cm ^ 3 . There are no indications of a cooling flow in Abell 400 2000MNRAS.312..663W .

The details of the radio data reduction of the RM maps for the two sources above can be found in 2002ApJ...567..202E . The typical RM values observed for them ranging between 250 rad m ^ 2 and 250 rad m ^ 2 . The beamsize for the map of 3C75 (Abell 400) is 3 arcsec which translates into 1.4 kpc and for 3C465 (Abell 2634) the beamsize is 3.8 arcsec which is equivalent to 2.3 kpc for the cosmology chosen.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 75 as a double twin-jet radio system in NGC 1128, interpreting its twisted morphology as colliding jets with different speeds.

quotes.quote: Radio observations suggest that 3C 75, located in the dumbbell shaped galaxy NGC 1128 at the center of Abell 400, hosts two colliding jets.

In the case of merging jets, the oscillations of the filaments, in projection, may show a feature which resembles a double helix, similar to the radio image of 3C 75. Thus we interpret the morphology of 3C 75 as a consequence of the collision of two jets with distinctly different speeds at a small impact parameter, with the faster jet breaking up into two oscillating filaments.

Jet collisions are also rare, but radio observations of the double twin-jet system, 3C 75, located in NGC 1128 at the center of the nearby galaxy cluster Abell 400, suggest that there are two colliding jets e.g., HudsonET06 . In Figure  F:IMAGE3C75 we show superimposed radio and optical images of the centre of 3C 75. The western jet from the northern AGN appears to collide with, and merge into, the northern jet from the southern AGN which does not seem to change its course significantly.

sources.summary: The review presents 3C75 as a radio source in Abell 400 made of two elliptical radio-jet galaxies that interact and are expected ultimately to merge.

quotes.quote: In the course of a VLA radio survey of Abell clusters, the very unusual morphology of the radio source 3C75 close to the center of the cluster of galaxies Abell 400 was discovered. It showed two pairs of radio jets (Fig. 1 of Owen et al. 1985) and was first considered an apparently single galaxy with two cores. However, optical follow-up observations showed two elliptical galaxies which each posses a radio-jet emitting core.

As regards 3C75, no distortions of the inner isophotes and no kinematic disturbances were found (Balcells et al. 1995, Govoni et al. 2000). However, the outer isophotes show an off-centering and twist (Lauer et al. 1988, DeJuan et al. 1994, Balcells et al. 1995, Govoni et al. 2000). These observations indicate, that the two galaxies are presently not bound to each other, but do interact with each other (Balcells et al. 1985).

sources.summary: The paper rejects 3C 75 as a WAT, describing it instead as twin FRI radio sources in Abell 400 with broad resolved jets and no abrupt flare point.

quotes.quote: The well-known twin source 3C 75 (Owen \ 1985), at a redshift of 0.0240, is often classed as a wide-angle tail on the basis of its large-scale structure (e.g. O'Donoghue \ 1993). It is certainly the case that the object lies at the centre of a cluster, Abell 400, and that its large-scale plumes bend in a manner similar to those of some of the WATs in our sample, although the radio luminosities of the two sources are a factor 5 lower than the typical luminosity of our WATs, and much more comparable to those of normal twin-jet FRIs . However, we have excluded it from consideration here on the basis that its inner jets have a large opening angle and that it does not show an abrupt jet-plume transition with a bright plume base; we would instead describe it as a pair of normal twin-jet FRI sources in a rich environment .

It is clear that the jets of 3C 75 are well resolved, with an opening angle much larger than those of the WATs in our sample (compare 3C 465, at a similar redshift) and have no strong side-to-side asymmetry, as is typical for normal FRI jets at more than a few kpc from the core . The N jet also displays the transition between parallel and perpendicular magnetic field characteristic of normal FRI jets, which takes place at a distance from the core about 8 arcsec (4 kpc). There is no bright flare point or abrupt change in opening angle in these jets.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 75 as the prototype twin radio galaxy, a 0.5 Mpc WAT source in Abell 400 whose jets merge and bend under intracluster crosswind pressure.

quotes.quote: The famous first example of such "Twin Radio Galaxy (TRG)" is the proto-type 3C 75, a Wide-Angle-Tail (WAT) radio source of overall size 0.5 Mpc, located near the centre of the galaxy cluster Abell 400 ( z = 0.023). It was discovered almost 4 decades ago Owen1985ApJ...294L..85O , which testifies to the extreme rarity of this cosmic phenomenon.

(i) Whilst all 3 TRGs exhibit conspicuously wiggly jets, the two northern jets of 3C 75 are seen to merge into a single entity, fairly early on (a similar situation probably exists in the other TRG, PKS 2149-158, although even its best available (VLA) map lacks sufficient clarity, see Guidetti2008A&A...483..699G .

(ii) In 3C 75, situated at the centre of the cluster Abell 400, all four jets undergo a sharp bending towards east/northeast Owen1985ApJ...294L..85O . This is attributed to dynamic pressure exerted by an intra-cluster crosswind blowing at a speed of 1200 km \ s^ 1 Hudson2006A&A...453..433H , due to the ongoing merger of the host cluster Abell 400 with a subcluster e.g., Beers1992ApJ...400..410B, Hudson2006A&A...453..433H .

sources.summary: The review calls 3C 75 a confirmed dual radio-bright AGN, with double nuclei mapped in radio, multiwavelength evidence for an 8 kpc AGN pair, and interacting jets.

quotes.quote: Probably the most spectacular example of a confirmed dual radio bright AGN is 3C 75 ( z=0.023 ). The double nuclei of the source were first mapped in the radio by owen1985 using the VLA. Later, observations conducted at various wavebands from X-rays to radio revealed that the source is indeed a pair of radio-emitting AGN separated by 8 kpc and references therein hudson2006 . The radio interferometric images of the jets of the two AGN provide a unique opportunity to study their interaction with each other, and with the intracluster medium.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C75 in Abell 400 as a double-radio-jet AGN pair whose two X-ray-detected nuclei appear physically bound and moving supersonically through the intracluster medium.

quotes.quote: As an example, we show in Figs. reip:3C75 the very center of the galaxy cluster A400 , where the well-known radio source 3C75 -- exhibiting a pair of double radio jets -- resides. Both AGNs, separated by 15 arcsec corresponding to a projected separation of 7 kpc, are detected separately for the first time in X-rays. Detailed analysis of the X-ray data reveals further evidence that the two AGNs are physically close to each other and form a bound system -- a proto supermassive binary black hole moving through the intracluster medium at the supersonic speed of about 1200 km/s.

gmw_00000740.overview: Located at a redshift of 0.66, the galaxy 812432 exhibits very compact oxygen emission lines [arXiv:1707.09838]. Its rotation curve is asymmetric and almost completely flat, showing only a slight rise on one side [arXiv:1707.09838].

sources.summary: The thesis shows object 812432 as a rejected zCOSMOS rotation-curve example with compact emission lines, an almost flat asymmetric curve, and low velocity range.

quotes.quote: The RCs of two objects, 812432 (z=0.66) and 819178 (z=0.424), are shown as well as the corresponding emission lines that were used for the RC extraction. The emission lines that were used are [ OIII ] (2.) for 812432 and H for 819178. In both cases the emission lines are very compact.

Although in the case of both objects the program gets measurements for 10 and 11 pixels, respectively, the resulting RCs are far from symmetric. There is also no distinction between a receding and an approaching side. The RC of 812432 is almost completely flat, with a slight rise on the left side.

gmw_00000741.overview: The galaxy 819178 is located at a redshift of z=0.424 [arXiv:1707.09838]. It exhibits very compact H-alpha emission and a rotation curve with a somewhat parabolic shape [arXiv:1707.09838].

sources.summary: The thesis treats object 819178 as a rejected rotation-curve case with compact H emission, a parabolic asymmetric curve, and missing inclination/position-angle information.

quotes.quote: The RCs of two objects, 812432 (z=0.66) and 819178 (z=0.424), are shown as well as the corresponding emission lines that were used for the RC extraction. The emission lines that were used are [ OIII ] (2.) for 812432 and H for 819178. In both cases the emission lines are very compact.

The RC of 819178 has a somewhat parabolic shape. In both cases the velocity values are rather low, ranging only to about 40 km/s. In three cases the RCs looked rather okay, but for one of these objects no inclination and no position angle were given (819178) and for the other two objects no scale length was available (1482306 and 1482419).

gmw_00000742.overview: The galaxy 830569 is a disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.7014 [arXiv:1707.09838]. It exhibits a symmetric, undisturbed disk structure with a small disk scale length, low B-band luminosity, and very weak [O II] emission [arXiv:1707.09838].

sources.summary: The thesis identifies galaxy 830569 as the largest velocity-size outlier, with small disk scale length, low B-band luminosity, weak [O II] emission, and a symmetric disk image.

quotes.quote: The object with the largest offset is the galaxy 830569 that has a redshift of z=0.7014 . Interstingly it is also the object with the largest offset to the local TFR towards smaller luminosities. This means that on one hand it has a considerably smaller disk scale length than would be expected from the local VSR, but on the other hand its magnitude in the B-band is also smaller than expected from the local TFR. A possible explanation for the low luminosity would be an interaction process that caused quenching.

Figure fig:830569 shows the RC, emission line and HST-image of 830569. For the RC extraction the [ OII ] emission line was used. On the cutout of the spectrum it can be seen that the emission line is very weak. Still, the resulting RC looks very nice and symmetric, and also the HST-image shows a symmetric and undisturbed looking disk.

gmw_00000743.overview: The high-redshift galaxy 837355 exhibits a clumpy morphology and bright, compact [O II] emission lines [arXiv:1707.09838]. It displays a strong offset from the local Tully-Fisher relation, which may be due to an underestimated maximum rotation velocity [arXiv:1707.09838].

sources.summary: The thesis discusses galaxy 837355 as a high-redshift object with a strong Tully-Fisher offset, compact [O II] emission, possible underestimated rotation speed, and clumpy morphology.

quotes.quote: In the case of 837355 the emission lines are bright, but also rather compact. Unfortunately, the spectrum is cut in a way that the emission lines are located at the upper edge. This could explain the seemingly missing points on the right approaching side of the RC compared to the left receding side.

The high quality object that has a rather large offset to the local relation, is the galaxy 837355 that also had a very strong offset to the local B-band TFR. Its RC, emission line and HST-image are shown in figure fig:offset_exp . As has been explained in section TFRMB , it is quite possible that v_ max is underestimated for this object.

gmw_00000744.name: NGC 5084
gmw_00000744.overview: NGC 5084 is one of the most massive disk galaxies known [arXiv:2001.01710], featuring an extended bulge [arXiv:1209.1513] and C-band radio emission extending along its disk [arXiv:1508.05153]. The galaxy possesses a globally peculiar structure with a misaligned outer ring, which likely originated from external gas accretion [arXiv:2205.10263].

sources.summary: The paper describes special scheduling for NGC 5084 and reports likely real C-band disk extensions, no apparent L-band polarization, and flat to slightly positive spectral indices.

quotes.quote: NGC 5084 Due to the low declination of this galaxy, the C-band observations were divided up into three scheduling blocks in order to a) avoid shadowing and b) increase uv-coverage. The scheduling block in which the first pointing was observed did not have a polarization leakage calibrator scan, and instead the secondary calibrator used for NGC 4192 was used to calibrate polarization leakage (the secondary calibrator of NGC 5084 did not have a sufficiently large parallactic angle span). Part of the second pointing was strongly affected by shadowing and only 21 antennas were used for these scans.

The C-band images show emission extending east and west of the point-like centre, as well as southward from the eastern extension -- these are likely real, since their intensity is greater than 10 (and they roughly follow the disk). However the extensions seen above and below the centre may be spurious since they seem to align with weak residual side lobes. In L-band, we find no apparent polarization from the source. The spectral index is flat in L-band and flat to slightly positive in C-band (but the positive trend coincides with higher error values).

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 5084 from SFR-related and correlation analyses because of missing H data and a globally peculiar disk, describing its outer tilted ring as likely external gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Because NGC 5084 lacks H observation, we exclude this galaxy from SFR related analysis. The SFR values are listed in Table table3:hHI , in which the SFR of NGC 5084 is only estimated by 22 m data.

NGC 5084 is excluded from the correlation coefficient calculation (and fitting for linear relations as well) because of the globally peculiar structure of the whole disk. Most of its distributes on an outer ring-like structure. This `ring' is following a faint optical outer ring and is tilted with respect to the main optical disk by about 5 deg 1986MNRAS.219..759G, 1990MNRAS.246..324Z . The origin of the misaligned ring is possibly external gas accretion from satellites, as NGC 5084 is one of the most massive galaxies in its group environment. As the ring is beyond the optical disk, its scale-height is unlikely to be strongly related with the stellar properties.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5084 as a massive disk-galaxy lens candidate toward GW 170817, adopting a conservative dynamical mass, distance modulus, impact parameter, and possible behind-host caveat.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies are the most plausible for large time delays: NGC 5084, M104, M83, and NGC 5128 (Centaurus A). With a dynamical mass as large as 10^ 13 M_ carignan97 , NGC 5084 is one of the most massive disk galaxies known. For a more conservative mass measurement, we use the carignan97 value excluding two possible satellites that may not be bound: (5.2 2.9) 10^ 12 M_ , which we convert to a log _ 10 (M/M_ ) measurement of 12.72 0.24 .

We take a distance modulus of 31.12 0.54 (or 16.7 Mpc, springob14 ), giving an impact parameter of 0.84 0.24 Mpc. We note that for this measured distance, there is a 0.02 chance that NGC 5084 is actually behind NGC 4993 and thus NGC 5084 could not lens GW 170817.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5084 as a high-velocity outlier whose fitted thick disc may instead be a very extended bulge, making its luminosity-profile antitruncation an artifact.

quotes.quote: The galaxy appearing at v_ 300 km s^ 1 with M_ T /M_ t 2 is NGC 5084, and what we fitted as the thick disc could actually be a very extended bulge.

Two of our total horizontal luminosity profiles -- those made for NGC 3600 and NGC 5084 -- have an antitruncation which is an artifact caused by the presence of an extended bulge like described in 15 of the galaxies in Maltby et al. (2012).
For NGC 5084, the antitruncation is an artifact caused by the presence of an extended bulge.
